nodes.title: About IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: Indian Institute of Technology Jammu (IIT Jammu) established 2016 by Act of Parliament. Permanent campus at Jagti, Nagrota, Jammu - 181221.
nodes.text: Indian Institute of Technology Jammu (IIT Jammu) established 2016 by Act of Parliament. One of the new IITs under Ministry of Education, Government of India. Mentored by IIT Delhi. Permanent campus at Jagti, Nagrota, Jammu - 181221. 250+ acres campus. Institute of National Importance. Students: 4900+. Faculty: 150+. NIRF Rank: 51-75 (2024). 12 academic departments: CSE, EE, ME, CE, CHE, Mathematics, Physics, Chemistry, HSS, Materials Engineering, Biosciences & Bioengineering, Interdisciplinary Studies.

nodes.title: About Us
nodes.summary: In July 2018, IIT Jammu embarked upon an ambitious journey to simplify its Academic, Research, and Administrative processes (Academic Affairs, Student Affairs, HR, Accounts and Audit, R and C, Procurement, CEP, etc.). Within a year, we built an…

nodes.title: About Us
nodes.summary: In July 2018, IIT Jammu embarked upon an ambitious journey to simplify its Academic, Research, and Administrative processes (Academic Affairs, Student Affairs, HR, Accounts and Audit, R and C, Procurement, CEP, etc.). Within a year, we built an…
nodes.text:
In July 2018, IIT Jammu embarked upon an ambitious journey to simplify its Academic, Research, and Administrative processes (Academic Affairs, Student Affairs, HR, Accounts and Audit, R and C, Procurement, CEP, etc.). Within a year, we built an integrated e-Governance Solution (EGS) to cater to all the processes and workflows with a vision of an almost paperless office. Entire workflow automation is achieved through an organic, bottom-up iterative process with end-user participation.

Over the past six years, our people have recognised the outstanding achievements of EGS in improving institutess ability to create and evolve high-quality software-dependent systems. However, human touch is essential. EGS has demonstrated an exceptional degree of significant, measured, sustained, and shared productivity improvements by composing a symphony of organically designed workflows. EG/SARAL is still a work in progress with evolving processes and new experiments in Academia. True to its name, SARAL helps simplify academic, research, and administrative process logistics and record keeping.

The Journey
 - e-Governance Solution brought transparency, efficiency, and accountability for all stakeholders. Our anytime, anywhere solution has helped us to reduce the paper footprint to a larger extent. During the lockdowns of 2019 and 2020, EGS came in handy in keeping all required office functionalities alive at IIT Jammu.
- We admitted the UG batch 2018 through registrations in the EGS and every admission thereafter. We have maintained every single academic course grade since inception. Course feedback from students and faculty is also available online.
- The HR information is maintained through EGS, including subsequent updates. Faculty and Staff data capture are used for review and progressions.
- In due course, Help Desk (ticketing), leave, scholarships, payroll processing, travel approvals, academic transcripts, grade sheets, admission portals, R and C projects management, including disbursals, reimbursements, and many other administrative processes made their way into this integrated e-Governance Solution.
- Multiple custom workflows have been created with tracking and time limits. Inefficiencies and bottlenecks are addressed through landing page alerts, dashboards,  triggered reports, and Auto-Escalation alerts.


Achievements
 - The list of features provisioned in EGS is huge, along with 140 workflows and several other transactional interfaces. The system is supported by robust reports, dashboards for ready references (summary data), and user messaging channels, including email, SMS, and Telegram integration.
- Processes were defined, matured, and optimized over time. Workflows were defined, iterated, and improved to help us over time. Now, it is fairly easy to add another workflow or report!
- A lot of effort has gone into the design, application building blocks, thought process, and customization with rigour. Our early investment in the e-Governance Solutio

During the lockdowns of 2019 and 2020, EGS came in handy in keeping all required office functionalities alive at IIT Jammu.
- We admitted the UG batch 2018 through registrations in the EGS and every admission thereafter. We have maintained every single academic course grade since inception. Course feedback from students and faculty is also available online.
- The HR information is maintained through EGS, including subsequent updates. Faculty and Staff data capture are used for review and progressions.
- In due course, Help Desk (ticketing), leave, scholarships, payroll processing, travel approvals, academic transcripts, grade sheets, admission portals, R and C projects management, including disbursals, reimbursements, and many other administrative processes made their way into this integrated e-Governance Solution.
- Multiple custom workflows have been created with tracking and time limits. Inefficiencies and bottlenecks are addressed through landing page alerts, dashboards,  triggered reports, and Auto-Escalation alerts.


Achievements
 - The list of features provisioned in EGS is huge, along with 140 workflows and several other transactional interfaces. The system is supported by robust reports, dashboards for ready references (summary data), and user messaging channels, including email, SMS, and Telegram integration.
- Processes were defined, matured, and optimized over time. Workflows were defined, iterated, and improved to help us over time. Now, it is fairly easy to add another workflow or report!
- A lot of effort has gone into the design, application building blocks, thought process, and customization with rigour. Our early investment in the e-Governance Solution, a continued thrust, and much-needed support from the administration have helped us to witness a digital transformation of processes - it's right here in front of us. Director IIT Jammu, an exponent of digital infrastructure for institutes, has been very supportive and inspiring and has encouraged us at each stage. EGS keeps the campus and people connected, irrespective of their physical presence.
- While we have successfully implemented our system, we are supporting other higher education institutes in their journey of e-Governance by assisting on the concept, RFP preparation, deployment discuss

nodes.summary: Jammu city, the largest city in the Jammu Division and the winter capital of the UT of Jammu and Kashmir, is situated on the banks of the Tawi river. With the recent establishment of AIIMS Jammu, IIM Jammu, and IIT Jammu, the city is expanding and…

nodes.title: About Jammu
nodes.summary: Jammu city, the largest city in the Jammu Division and the winter capital of the UT of Jammu and Kashmir, is situated on the banks of the Tawi river. Jammu city has been a centre of education for the UTs of J&K and Ladakh.
nodes.text:
Jammu city, the largest city in the Jammu Division and the winter capital of the UT of Jammu and Kashmir, is situated on the banks of the Tawi river. It is a melting pot of cultures, religions, and ethnicities from all parts of the UTs of J&K and Ladakh and other northwestern states of India. It accommodates a romantic mix of heritage and progress, the old and the new, while providing opportunities for education and work for a diverse population. It is also surrounded by holy sites and mesmerising sights, which the pilgrims/tourists find irresistible.

Despite the confluence of cultures and people it has become home to, Jammu city retains its native cultural hue, past heritage, and distinct cuisine for the ones keen to explore and experience.

Jammu city has been a centre of education for the UTs of J&K and Ladakh. Its temperate climate and politics have attracted youth from these parts for their education right from higher secondary levels and on to higher education opportunities offered by its various government schools and degree colleges, engineering colleges, and the University of Jammu. Universities like SKUAST-Jammu and Shri Mata Vaishno Devi University, Katra, have students from many parts of the nation pursuing various professional and other degrees.

With the recent establishment of AIIMS Jammu, IIM Jammu, and IIT Jammu, the city is expanding and transforming to be a national hub of education in the northernmost part of the country.

Geographical Facts about the City:

The city spreads around the Tawi river, with the old city overlooking it from the north (right bank), while the new neighbourhoods spread around the river's southern side (left bank).

Lying on the uneven ridges of the low heights of the Shivalik hills, it has an average elevation of 327 m. It is surrounded by the Shivalik range in the north, east, and southeast, while the Trikuta range surrounds it in the northwest.

It is approximately 600 km from the national capital, New Delhi.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu Breakthrough: Green Hydrogen-Fueled Vehicle Unveiled by Dr. B. Satya Sekhar's Research Group
nodes.summary: Satya Sekhar's research group at the Department of Mechanical Engineering at IIT Jammu has successfully showcased a green hydrogen-based fuel cell-powered vehicle, supported by the institute. Satya Sekhar's hydrogen research group has not only…
nodes.text:
In a groundbreaking achievement, Dr. B. Satya Sekhar's research group at the Department of Mechanical Engineering at IIT Jammu has successfully showcased a green hydrogen-based fuel cell-powered vehicle, supported by the institute. The meticulous integration process spanned over two years, resulting in a vehicle that operates seamlessly without adverse environmental effects. The vehicle incorporates a hydrogen-based Proton Exchange Membrane (PEM) fuel cell, a hydrogen storage tank, and a DC-DC converter, collectively forming an innovative and eco-friendly transportation solution.

One of the remarkable features of this development is the substantial reduction in charging time. The team has successfully brought down the charging time from an initial eight hours to a mere five minutes, enhancing the efficiency and practicality of the E-vehicle.

Hydrogen-based energy holds the promise of a closed and pollution-free chemical cycle for energy infrastructure. The PEM fuel cell, in particular, initiates the process with hydrogen and oxygen, producing water, which is then reversed to extract hydrogen and oxygen. This stands in stark contrast to traditional gasoline-fueled engines that emit carbon dioxide and other pollutants into the atmosphere.

Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi's announcement of the National Hydrogen Mission aligns with such sustainable energy initiatives, positioning India as a hub for the production and export of green hydrogen. This mission aims to foster self-reliance and energy independence for the nation.

In response to this national vision, Dr. B. Satya Sekhar's hydrogen research group has not only developed a hydrogen generation facility utilizing an electrolyzer powered by solar energy but also customized a DC-DC converter and designed a hydrogen refueling system. The ongoing work includes the development of a medium-pressure hydrogen compressor, further contributing to advancements in green hydrogen technology.

Dr. B. Satya Sekhar reflects on the continuous nature of scientific inquiry, stating, "Scientific ideas lead to ongoing research. There's always room for improvement and things that can be modified and added to improve it, and it's a great learning tool for us." This accomplishment showcases IIT Jammu's commitment to fostering cutting-edge research and innovation in the realm of sustainable energy solutions.

nodes.title: About
nodes.summary: The CWS is equipped with latest manufacturing equipment, and is expanded in 4500 sq. Machining section is equipped with wire EDM, abrasive water jet machine, CNC & conventional machines, grinders, drill machines, 3D printers and vision measuring…
nodes.text:
- About Us
- Services We Offers

The Central Workshop at IIT Jammu:

Nurturing Precision Engineering and Innovation

Central Workshop at the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jammu is the place where we work together to attain excellence and create a unique centralized facility for manufacturing by providing latest technology under one roof.  This central facility is available for designing and manufacturing of products to the faculty, staff, and students of IIT Jammu as well as for external agencies. Here, various interesting projects have been developed, and some of which have been patented. The CWS is equipped with latest manufacturing equipment, and is expanded in 4500 sq. feet area which is having two sections viz. machining and fabrication section. Machining section is equipped with wire EDM, abrasive water jet machine, CNC & conventional machines, grinders, drill machines, 3D printers and vision measuring system. Fabrication section contains carpentry, welding, sheet metal, FRP, injection moulding and other fitting facilities. Our enthusiastic educated technical team is always ready to conquer the challenges appear before them.

Our Mission:

At the Central Workshop, our mission is to provide a dynamic environment for the engineering community to explore, create, and innovate. We are dedicated to empower students with practical skills, enabling ground breaking research endeavours, and offering invaluable support for projects and coursework.

What We Offer:
 - State-of-the-Art Facilities : Our workshop is equipped with a
 wide array of advanced machinery and tools, ensuring that students and faculty
 have access to the best resources for their projects.
- Safety First : We prioritize safety above all else. Rigorous safety training and adherence to safety protocols are integral to our operations, which ensure a secure and productive environment.
- Collaborative Learning : The Central Workshop is a space for collaboration and interdisciplinary learning. We encourage students and researchers to work together, share knowledge, and push the boundaries of innovation.
- Expert Guidance : Our dedicated team of experienced and well educated staff is here to provide technical guidance, support, and training to help you achieve your goals.

nodes.title: Vision and Mission - Unnat Bharat Abhiyan
nodes.summary: Unnat Bharat Abhiyan is a flagship program of the Ministry of Education, Govt. Unnat Bharat Abhiyan is inspired by the vision of transformational change in rural development processes by leveraging the Higher Education Institutions to help build the…
nodes.text:
Unnat Bharat Abhiyan is a flagship program of the Ministry of Education, Govt. of
 India. It aims to connect the Higher Education Institutions with a set of at
 least five villages or local communities to address development challenges
 through appropriate technologies so that these institutions can contribute to
 the economic and social betterment of these village communities using their
 knowledge base.

Unnat Bharat Abhiyan is inspired by the vision of transformational change in
 rural development processes by leveraging the Higher Education Institutions to
 help build the architecture of an inclusive India.

nodes.title: About Us
nodes.summary: IIT Jammu serves as Regional Coordinating Institution (RCI) as well as Participating Institution (PI) since 2019. As Regional Coordinating Institution (RCI) , IIT Jammu act as nodal center to promote & facilitate UBA network in Jammu Province and…
nodes.text:
IIT Jammu serves as Regional Coordinating Institution (RCI) as well as
 Participating Institution (PI) since 2019. As Regional Coordinating Institution (RCI) , IIT Jammu act as
 nodal center to promote & facilitate UBA network in Jammu Province and
 Ladakh UT. With this aim, IIT Jammu handholds the Higher Education Institutes
 (HEIs) of the region in becoming a part of the UBA network and creating the
 local connect with their respective rural cluster. Presently our activities are
 centered to address the following:
 - Mapping the provision of basic amenities like food, water, shelter,
 clothing, transportation, telecommunication, health, among other.
- Mapping the reach of various Government schemes among the local inhabitants
 in the rural communities
- Providing skill development programs to rural community members of various
 education levels and across all age groups and gender
- Enabling livelihood of artisans

nodes.title: Guest Talk by Dr. Ilangovan S. A. | 05 March 2026 | 2:00–3:00 PM
nodes.summary: Ilangovan has 30+ years of experience in Energy Storage Systems, cryogenic insulation for LH₂ and LOX stages, thermal protection systems, solid and liquid propellants, special polymers, and advanced ceramics for re-entry vehicles, and combustion…
nodes.text:
Institute Lecture by Dr. Ilangovan S. A., (Ph.D. IISc Bangalore), Former Deputy Director (Retd.), VSSC (ISRO), Dr. Ilangovan has 30+ years of experience in Energy Storage Systems, cryogenic insulation for LH₂ and LOX stages, thermal protection systems, solid and liquid propellants, special polymers, and advanced ceramics for re-entry vehicles, and combustion systems.

Title: BATTERY TO SUPERCAPATTERY: An Innovative Energy Storage System Date: 05-03-2026 (Thursday) Time: 2 PM–3 PM Venue: Seminar Hall 11AC3027, Pushkar Block

Abstract: The advancement of electric mobility is contingent upon energy storage systems that bridge the gap between high-density energy reservoirs and high-rate power delivery. While traditional hybridization, paralleling discrete battery and supercapacitor modules is often proposed, which is limited by the mismatched discharge kinetics of Faradaic and non-Faradaic components and the resulting complexity of power management. This work presents the Supercapattery – an innovative, internally hybridized electrochemical cell that integrates these functionalities into a single architecture. Unlike external hybrids, the Supercapattery utilizes a precision-engineered electrode system where the battery component sustains constant loads while the supercapacitive component manages the transient peak demands through rapid power release. Developed and qualified by ISRO, this Technology has demonstrated a high pulse discharge capability with lower internal resistance value. By consolidating these features into a compact cylindrical configuration, the Supercapattery offers a transformative, mass-efficient solution for both high-performance space missions and the next generation of electric vehicles.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu: Dr Pal Receives INR 40 Lakhs Grant from MSME for Manufacturing Advancement
nodes.summary: This pioneering effort is poised to bolster IIT Jammu's initiatives in the manufacturing sector, aligning seamlessly with the resounding 'Make in India' initiative. With the support of MSME, IIT Jammu is poised to play a pivotal role in realizing…
nodes.text:
Dr Vijay Kumar Pal, Assistant Professor at the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jammu, has made significant strides in securing funding opportunities from the Ministry of Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSME). This pioneering effort is poised to bolster IIT Jammu's initiatives in the manufacturing sector, aligning seamlessly with the resounding 'Make in India' initiative.

Recognizing the pivotal role of MSMEs in nurturing and sustaining the growth of small and medium-sized enterprises, IIT Jammu is poised to forge a dynamic partnership with MSME, ushering in a new era of substantial benefits for the manufacturing industry.

This momentous funding opportunity stands as a watershed moment in IIT Jammu's unwavering commitment to spearheading innovation and excellence within the manufacturing sector. The infusion of INR 40 Lakhs will serve as a catalyst for the institution to push the boundaries of technology, research, and skill development, thereby making significant contributions to the grand vision of 'Make in India.'

The 'Make in India' initiative, championed by the Government of India, seeks to transform the nation into a global manufacturing hub by promoting indigenous manufacturing and fostering innovation. With the support of MSME, IIT Jammu is poised to play a pivotal role in realizing this vision.

IIT Jammu remains steadfast in its mission to cultivate and disseminate knowledge, fostering innovation and development that benefit the nation as a whole. This strategic collaboration with MSME exemplifies the institution's commitment to nurturing India's growth as a manufacturing powerhouse.

nodes.title: OUR MOTTO
nodes.summary: One such person was Ashi (Ashish Raj Gupta), who became part of the IIT Jammu family with the batch of 2019. His zeal of learning everything took him to various cultural, academic, and social activities.
nodes.text:
There are very few people who are good at various activities, who help people, make others happy, and live happily. One such person was Ashi (Ashish Raj Gupta), who became part of the IIT Jammu family with the batch of 2019. His zeal of learning everything took him to various cultural, academic, and social activities. He had a vision of growth and development of society and thus spent most of his time with KRITASH . Everything on the path was smooth except for the sudden cardiac arrest. Losing him is a huge loss for his family and the institute.

In order to remember his contributions to the institute and to keep his ideas alive, IIT Jammu has set up Ashi Foundation , which aims to increase the awareness on Heart related diseases and disorders.

With the motto of developing a healthy heart , we invite you to be the part of someone’s life and become someone’s smile…

nodes.title: Director, IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: Prof Manoj Singh Gaur has been serving as the founding director of the Indian Institute of Technology Jammu since June 2017 and is also a Professor at the Department of Computer Science & Engineering; he is now in his second term. Before joining IIT…
nodes.text: Prof Manoj Singh Gaur has been serving as the founding director of the Indian Institute of Technology Jammu since June 2017 and is also a Professor at the Department of Computer Science & Engineering; he is now in his second term. Before joining IIT Jammu, he was a Professor and Head of the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at Malaviya National Institute of Technology (MNIT) Jaipur, India. Prof Gaur has also been Dean of Students Affairs and Head of the Central Computer Centre at MNIT Jaipur. He also served as Professor-in-Charge (Coordinator) of IIIT Kota. In his teaching and research career of almost three decades, he has been an investigator of several funded research projects in Information Security and Networks on Chip. He has been part of the core group of Project ISEA (Information Security Education and Awareness), a major multi-institutional project in the domain of Information Security in India. His current research areas include Computer and Network Security (Network Attack Models and Countermeasures), Mobile Platform Security, and Data Privacy. He has published more than two hundred papers in reputed journals and conferences, and has supervised twenty-three PhDs to date; currently, he has two research scholars working in his research group. Prof Gaur is a leading exponent of using technology and automation to enable service delivery at higher education institutes, whether in infrastructure, teaching and learning, or e-governance. Under the leadership of the founding director, IIT Jammu is achieving new milestones in line with its vision and values to create leaders for a better tomorrow.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: To pave the way for impactful solutions in clean energy research, Dr Satya Sekhar Bhogilla, from the Department of Mechanical Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jammu, visited the National Institute of Advanced Industrial…

nodes.title: In the quest for a sustainable future, Dr Satya Sekhar visits AIST Japan
nodes.summary: To pave the way for impactful solutions in clean energy research, Dr Satya Sekhar Bhogilla, from the Department of Mechanical Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jammu, visited the National Institute of Advanced Industrial…
nodes.text:
To pave the way for impactful solutions in clean energy research, Dr Satya Sekhar Bhogilla, from the Department of Mechanical Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jammu, visited the National Institute of Advanced Industrial Science and Technology (AIST) in Tsukuba Science City, Japan.

As part of a significant collaborative project, which falls under the aegis of the New Energy and Industrial Technology Development Organization (NEDO) program: "Research and Development Program for promoting innovation clean energy technologies through international collaboration," Dr Sekhar, alongside researchers from Hokkaido University, AIST, and KTH Royal Institute of Technology, Sweden, would find clean energy solutions for a sustainable future.

The mission of the research collaboration is to advance and refine clean energy technologies through interdisciplinary investigations, encompassing mechanical engineering, materials science, and environmental studies. Dr Sekhar's expertise and dedication in the field of Mechanical Engineering align seamlessly with the project's vision, propelling the collective efforts towards greater milestones.

This collaboration will not only bolster the academic prowess of IIT Jammu but also contribute to strengthening India's position in the global research landscape. The institute's involvement in this groundbreaking initiative reaffirms its commitment to driving technological advancements that address the challenges of sustainable development.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: A Historic Milestone for IIT Jammu & India’s Semiconductor Mission! Honourable Shri Ashwini Vaishnaw, Minister of Electronics and IT, unveiled 20 semiconductor chips fabricated at India’s Semiconductor Chip Fab (SCL), Chandigarh, representing the…

nodes.title: A Historic Milestone for IIT Jammu & India’s Semiconductor Mission!
nodes.summary: A Historic Milestone for IIT Jammu & India’s Semiconductor Mission! Honourable Shri Ashwini Vaishnaw, Minister of Electronics and IT, unveiled 20 semiconductor chips fabricated at India’s Semiconductor Chip Fab (SCL), Chandigarh, representing the…
nodes.text:
A Historic Milestone for IIT Jammu & India’s Semiconductor Mission!

At the inaugural ceremony of Semicon India 2025 in New Delhi, graced by Honourable Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi, the nation celebrated a defining moment for India’s growing semiconductor ecosystem.

Honourable Shri Ashwini Vaishnaw, Minister of Electronics and IT, unveiled 20 semiconductor chips fabricated at India’s Semiconductor Chip Fab (SCL), Chandigarh, representing the remarkable contributions of 17 premier institutions across India.

Among these, two chips were proudly designed by IIT Jammu’s IC-ResQ Lab, under the mentorship of Dr. Ambika Prasad Shah. What makes this even more special? These chips are designed for pacemaker sensing, pushing the boundaries of semiconductor innovation in healthcare technology — solutions that will directly benefit mankind.

Honourable Dr. Jitendra Singh, Minister of Science and Technology, also highlighted and appreciated this achievement, recognizing the role of IIT Jammu in shaping India’s technological future.

As a young IIT, standing shoulder to shoulder with India’s most established institutions, IIT Jammu has demonstrated that with vision, talent, and dedication, it can deliver world-class innovation aligned with the India Semiconductor Mission.

This achievement is not just about technology; it is about empowering the nation, advancing healthcare, and building a self-reliant India.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: Veena Gupta, Professor (Retd.), Department of Dogri, University of Jammu. Initiatives like these reaffirm IIT Jammu’s commitment to promoting regional languages, cultural dialogue, and academic exchange beyond conventional disciplinary boundaries.

nodes.title: Dogri Language – An Introduction, Challenges and Opportunities
nodes.summary: IIT Jammu recently organized an engaging introductory talk titled “Dogri Language – An Introduction, Challenges and Opportunities” at its Jagti Campus, bringing together scholars, students, and language enthusiasts to celebrate and reflect on the…
nodes.text:
IIT Jammu recently organized an engaging introductory talk titled “Dogri Language – An Introduction, Challenges and Opportunities” at its Jagti Campus, bringing together scholars, students, and language enthusiasts to celebrate and reflect on the richness of Dogri.

The session featured an insightful lecture by Prof. Veena Gupta, Professor (Retd.), Department of Dogri, University of Jammu. Introduced by Dr. Rajendra Solanki, the speaker guided the audience through the historical roots, linguistic structure, grammatical depth, and cultural heritage that shape the identity of the Dogri language. She also addressed the contemporary challenges faced in preserving regional languages and highlighted opportunities to strengthen their relevance in modern discourse.

The talk concluded with a lively interactive session where participants shared their questions and perspectives, creating a meaningful dialogue on the literary traditions and cultural significance of Dogri.

The students, faculty and staff members of the institute along with participants even from outside the institute gained larger insights about the Dogri language.

Initiatives like these reaffirm IIT Jammu’s commitment to promoting regional languages, cultural dialogue, and academic exchange beyond conventional disciplinary boundaries.

nodes.title: Tribal Project or A Literary, Socio-Cultural, and Linguistic Study of Tribal World:
nodes.summary: On this note, an MOU was signed on 26 June 2022 between IIT Jammu and the Tribal Research Institute, J&K, in the presence of the Hon’ble Lieutenant Governor, Manoj Sinha, to establish a Tribal Chair in the institute. Director, IIT Jammu, Prof Manoj…
nodes.text:
Setting up Gujjar & Bakerwal Repository

The Tribal Project between the HSS Department, IIT Jammu, and the Tribal Research
 Institute intended to collect, interpret, and disseminate Gojri Folklore, understand the
 worldview of Gojri through their language, and study women’s socio-educational
 status in the life of Gujjars and Bakarwals living in the Pir-Panjal regions of the
 Union Territory of Jammu and Kashmir. The focus was on tangible deliverables such as
 digital repositories, reports, websites, and workshops.

On this note, an MOU was signed on 26 June 2022 between IIT Jammu and the Tribal Research
 Institute, J&K, in the presence of the Hon’ble Lieutenant Governor, Manoj
 Sinha, to establish a Tribal Chair in the institute. Director, IIT Jammu, Prof Manoj
 Singh Gaur, announced the set up of a Tribal Chair at the institute. The objectives of
 the Tribal Chair were to collect, transcreate, digitize, and disseminate the tribal
 culture of Gujjars and Bakerwals. The PIs of the Tribal Project are: Dr Quleen Kaur
 Bijral (PI), Dr Amitash Ojha (Co-PI) and Dr Sayantan Mandal (Co-PI).

Marve and Sayari, research scholars of the HSS Department, have been an integral part of
 the work.

nodes.title: Assistant Registrar Message
nodes.summary: As the Assistant Registrar, I am here to assist you with any administrative issues related to Establishment II. As Establishment II, we play a critical role in the day-to-day operations of the Institute, from managing facilities to providing all…

nodes.title: Message
nodes.summary: As Establishment II, we play a critical role in the day-to-day operations of the Institute, from managing facilities to providing all spheres of administrative support. Let us continue to work together to create a supportive and productive…
nodes.text:
I know that the past few years have been challenging for us all, with the ongoing pandemic affecting both our personal and professional lives. However, I have been continuously impressed by the resilience and adaptability shown by our team.

As the Assistant Registrar, I am here to assist you with any administrative issues related to Establishment II. As we move forward, I am confident that we will continue to face any challenges that come our way with determination and teamwork.

I want to assure you that Establishment II is committed to providing a safe and healthy workplace for all employees. As Establishment II, we play a critical role in the day-to-day operations of the Institute, from managing facilities to providing all spheres of administrative support.

I believe that communication is key to maintaining a successful academic institution, and I encourage all members of our staff to reach out to me if they ever have any questions or concerns relating to Establishment II. I am always available to provide assistance and guidance.

Thank you for your commitment to the Institute and your contributions to our shared vision. Let us continue to work together to create a supportive and productive environment.

nodes.title: About Us
nodes.summary: Establishment II Section is headed by Assistant Registrar (Establishment II). We strive to establish a strong, high on Integrity & transparent organizational foundation to recruit, hire, and retain a diverse and inclusive team of professionals.

nodes.title: About Us
nodes.summary: Establishment II Section is headed by Assistant Registrar (Establishment II). We strive to establish a strong, high on Integrity & transparent organizational foundation to recruit, hire, and retain a diverse and inclusive team of professionals.
nodes.text:
Today more than ever, Administration needs a new way of thinking---an innovative approach towards what it does and about the quality of its ideas especially in the institutes of eminence. This new way of thinking is about engaging staff in structured activities and observable behaviours to facilitate knowledge transmission in keeping with the latest developments in pedagogy in line with Institute’s Vision.

Our motive in Establishment II is to endeavour in creating a transformational experience that promotes institutes’ overall success, well-being, and resiliency at all levels in all domains of Administrative Affairs.

Establishment II Section is headed by Assistant Registrar (Establishment II). The section is concerned with the process of responsive people-centric modern administration that is an integral part of Good Governance.

The Establishment II deals with all the appointments, promotions, maintenance of Service Books, posting/transfer, pay-fixation, increments, grant of leaves/LTC, grant of various advances, children education allowance, retirements/ resignations, disciplinary proceedings, issuance of instructions and compilation of reports, etc. We strive to establish a strong, high on Integrity & transparent organizational foundation to recruit, hire, and retain a diverse and inclusive team of professionals.

nodes.text:
Algebraic extensions of function fields, subrings, local integral bases, Hurwitz genus formula.

The different and Dedekind different formula, constant field extensions, Galois extension of function field, Ramification and splitting of places in compositum field, inseparable extensions, Estimates of genus of a function field.

The Zeta function of a function field, The Hasse-Weil theorem, Some examples of function fields.

References:

1. H. Stictenoth, Algebraic Function Fields and Codes, second edition, GTM, Springer-Verlag, Berlin, 2009.

2. C. Chevalley, Introduction to the Theory of Algebraic Functions of One Variable, Mathematical Surveys, No. VI. American Mathematical Society, Providence.

3. R. Dedekind, and H. Weber, Theory of algebraic functions of one variable. Translated from the 1882 German original and with an introduction, bibliography and index by John Stillwell. History of Mathematics, 39. American Mathematical Society.

nodes.title: Institutes of Technology Act, 1961
nodes.summary: All IITs are governed by The Institutes of Technology Act (an act of the Indian Parliament - No. Gazette Notification of IIT Ammendment Act 2016 First Statutes of IIT Jammu
nodes.text:
Indian Institutes of Technology are apex institutions for engineering education and research. At present, there are twenty three Indian Institutes of Technology (IITs) viz. at Bombay, Delhi, Kanpur, Kharagpur, Madras, Guwahati, Roorkee, Hyderabad, Patna, Bhubaneshwar, Ropar, Jodhpur, Gandhinagar, Indore, Mandi, Varanasi, Tiruppati, Palakkad, Goa, Jammu, Dharwad, and Bhilai.

All IITs are governed by The Institutes of Technology Act (an act of the Indian Parliament - No. 59 of 1961, and amended in 1963) which has declared them as "Institutions of national importance", and lays down their powers, duties, framework for governance, etc. For further details, you may visit  the website of MHRD .

Gazette Notification of IIT Ammendment Act 2016

First Statutes of IIT Jammu

nodes.summary: Electrical Engineering (EE) — 75 seats 3. Mechanical Engineering (ME) — 75 seats 4.
nodes.text:
IIT Jammu B.Tech programs (4-year, JEE Advanced):
1. Computer Science & Engineering (CSE) — 75 seats
2. Electrical Engineering (EE) — 75 seats
3. Mechanical Engineering (ME) — 75 seats
4. Civil Engineering (CE) — 50 seats
5. Chemical Engineering (CHE) — 30 seats
6. Mathematics & Computing (M&C) — 40 seats
7. Engineering Physics (EP) — 20 seats
Total: 365 seats per year. 20% supernumerary seats for girl candidates.

nodes.title: M.Tech in GenAI and Data Science (Executive)
nodes.summary: - AI - Generative AI and Agentic Systems - Intelligent Systems - Data Science - LLMs Introduction: Across every industry, intelligence is no longer just an advantage; it’s the foundation. Program Highlights: - Batch Start Date :February 2026…
nodes.text:
- AI
- Generative AI and Agentic Systems
- Intelligent Systems
- Data Science
- LLMs

Introduction:

Across every industry, intelligence is no longer just
 an advantage; it’s the foundation. From automation to autonomous systems,
 the new era of AI demands engineers who can build systems that think,
 decide, and act on their own . The world is moving from predictive
 models to Generative and Agentic systems , AI that learns,
 adapts, and operates autonomously. This shift has opened a massive opportunity
 for engineers who can bridge code, cloud, and cognition. India today is entering
 one of the most exciting phases in its growth story, as AI is reshaping how
 companies operate, how governments deliver services, and how people live and
 work.

With major national initiatives like IndiaAI
 Mission and BharatGen , the country is creating
 massive opportunities for skilled professionals who can design, deploy, and
 manage AI systems at scale - where systems don’t just predict outcomes but
 generate ideas, insights, and intelligent decisions.

Addressing India’s need of a new generation of AI specialists, engineers, and innovators who can power this
 transformation — the M.Tech in Generative AI and Data Science
 (Executive) by IIT Jammu has been designed in collaboration with
 industry leaders. It combines theoretical rigor with practical hands-on labs,
 real-world problem-solving, preparing students to lead and innovate in a
 landscape where AI is all pervasive. This program by IIT Jammu is more than an
 education; it's a direct conduit to becoming a meaningful contributor to
 India’s global AI mission.

Objectives:

The MTech in GenAI and Data Science (Executive) by IIT
 Jammu is designed to develop next-generation AI engineers, data scientists, and
 applied researchers who can build, deploy, and scale intelligent systems across
 domains. Through a rigorous blend of foundational courses in programming, data
 analytics, machine learning, and computational intelligence, learners will gain
 a deep understanding of how algorithms, data, and models interact to create
 real-world impact.

In advanced semesters, the focus shifts toward
 Generative AI, Large Language Models, Agentic AI, and MLOps — enabling
 students to design, optimize, and operationalize end-to-end AI pipelines. The
 capstone dissertation provides hands-on experience in solving complex industrial
 and societal challenges using AI-driven methods. Graduates emerge as technically
 grounded, innovation-ready professionals equipped to lead AI transformation
 across sectors.

Program Highlights:
 - Batch Start Date :February 2026 (tentative)
- Duration : 24 Months (4 Semesters, 60 Credits)
- Degree : MTech in Generative AI & Data Science
 (Executive) by IIT Jammu
- Credits : 60 academic credits post successful completion
 of the program
- Alumni Status : IIT Jammu Executive Alumni Status post
 successful completion of the program
- Campus Immersion : The campus immersion phase is a
 pivotal pa

nodes.summary: - Hands-on training using industry-standard EV engineering tools, aligned with real-world electric powertrain, battery systems, and vehicle integration workflows. Programme Structure / Modules EV Core – Mandatory Courses Course 1 EV Fundamentals…

nodes.title: Post Graduate Certification Program in EV & Autonomous Vehicle Technology
nodes.summary: - Hands-on training using industry-standard EV engineering tools, aligned with real-world electric powertrain, battery systems, and vehicle integration workflows. Programme Structure / Modules EV Core – Mandatory Courses Course 1 EV Fundamentals…
nodes.text:
Programme Highlights

Master Electric Vehicle technology from fundamentals to advanced simulations, with optional specialization in Autonomous Vehicles.
 - 300+ hours of structured learning | 25+ industry-grade automotive projects | 135+ practical challenges
- Learn from experienced industry professionals and IIT Jammu–led faculty through a structured academic delivery model.
- Hands-on training using industry-standard EV engineering tools, aligned with real-world electric powertrain, battery systems, and vehicle integration workflows.
- Self-paced learning supported by recorded modules and live interactive sessions , enabling flexibility without compromising academic rigor.
- Career readiness and placement assistance facilitated by Skill-Lync , subject to eligibility and programme norms.

Programme Structure / Modules

EV Core – Mandatory Courses

Course 1 EV Fundamentals

Course 2 Matlab and Simulink Basics

Course 3 Design Concepts of Power Electronic Converters

Course 4 Fuel Cell and Ultra Capacitor for EV

Course 5 Li-ion Battery System Design in EV & ES

Course 6 PCB Design for Beginners using Altium Designer

Course 7 Electric Motor Design using MATLAB and ANSYS Maxwell

Optional Courses

EV Electives

Course 1 EV Technology Series

Course 2 Basics of Electric Vehicle Simulations using Ansys

Course 3 Hybrid Electric Vehicle Simulation Using GT-SUITE

Course 4 Automotive Wiring Harness using CATIA V5

Optional Self-Paced Track

Autonomous

Course 1 Core and Advanced Python Programming

Course 2 Data Structures and Algorithms using Python

Course 3 Introduction to ADAS & Vehicle Validation

Course 4 Computer Vision for Autonomous Vehicles

Course 5 Localization, Mapping, and SLAM

Course 6 Path Planning and Trajectory Optimization

Course 7 Autonomous Vehicle Controls

Learning Outcomes

Upon successful completion of the programme, participants will be able to:
 - Understand electric vehicle architectures, powertrain layouts, and system-level interactions across EV and hybrid vehicle platforms.
- Analyze and design key EV subsystems including battery packs, electric motors, power electronic converters, and energy storage systems using industry-aligned methodologies.
- Apply EV engineering fundamentals to model, simulate, and evaluate performance, efficiency, thermal behavior, and energy flow under real-world driving conditions.
- Develop proficiency in industry-standard EV engineering and simulation tools such as MATLAB, Simulink, ANSYS, GT-SUITE, CATIA V5, and Altium Designer.
- Build simulation-ready EV subsystem models including battery systems, motors, inverters, and powertrains through structured modeling and validation workflows.
- Design and analyze lithium-ion battery systems considering performance, safety, thermal management, and real-world integration constraints.
- Model, simulate, and evaluate electric and hybrid vehicle behavior using system-level tools to assess drivability, efficiency, and energy management strategies.
- Ex

nodes.title: Certificate Program in Data Science with AI & ML
nodes.summary: Apply Online Program Structure Modules: - Introduction to Data Science - Artificial Intelligence Foundation - Mathematical Foundations - Algorithms and Data Structures - Introduction to Big Data Technologies - SQL Fundamentals with Python -…
nodes.text:
Apply Online

Program Structure

Modules:
 - Introduction to Data Science
- Artificial Intelligence Foundation
- Mathematical Foundations
- Algorithms and Data Structures
- Introduction to Big Data Technologies
- SQL Fundamentals with Python
- Visualizing Data with Excel Spreadsheets & Cognos Dashboards
- Getting Started with Tableau
- Advanced Data Visualization Techniques in Tableau
- Project 1- Exploratory Data Analysis Project- Social Media Trend Analyzer
- Python For Data Science
- Machine Learning Fundamentals with Python
- Project 2- Predictive Analytics Project - Employee Attrition Prediction
- Deep Learning with TensorFlow
- Project 3- Deep Learning for Image or Text Project- Fake News / Deepfake Detection
- Putting it Together - Data Science for AI Applications
- Capstone Project : AI-Powered Personal Finance Advisor
- Git/DevOps
- Authorization and Managing Identity in Azure
- Managing Storage and Networking

Program Highlights
 - Earn a prestigious certification from IIT Jammu, with the option of an exclusive campus immersion.
- Learn through Virtual Instructor-Led Training (VILT) with IIT Jammu faculty and SkillUp Online mentors.
- Gain industry-recognized credentials including IBM certifications and micro-credentials from leading tech partners.
- Experience hands-on labs, guided projects, and real-world scenarios designed to strengthen your job readiness.
- Receive personalized mentoring, continuous assessments, and academic feedback to support your progress.
- Access all content, recordings, and evaluations through SkillUp’s advanced learning platform till the issuance of the Certificate.

Learning Outcomes

By completing this program, you will:
 - Strengthen your foundation in mathematics, statistics, and algorithms for AI and ML.
- Develop advanced Python programming skills for data analysis and AI application development.
- Use Pandas, NumPy, and SciPy to clean, manipulate, and visualize structured and unstructured data.
- Write optimized SQL queries to manage relational databases.
- Build interactive dashboards and visual reports using Tableau and IBM Cognos.
- Design and execute machine learning models with scikit-learn and TensorFlow.
- Apply regression, classification, clustering, and recommendation algorithms to solve business challenges.
- Explore deep learning techniques for computer vision, NLP, and generative AI use cases.
- Work with Azure AI services to deploy cloud-native AI applications.
- Follow ethical AI guidelines and ensure data privacy in your projects.
- Showcase your learning through an industry-oriented capstone project.

Governing Committee

A dedicated governing committee composed of IIT Jammu faculty and industry experts ensures that this program meets IIT Jammu’s academic standards and maintains real-world industry relevance.

Eligibility Criteria
 - Academic Background: Bachelor’s degree in any discipline.
- Preferred Profile: Working professionals and tech-oriented career switchers keen to build a pro

nodes.title: UG Programme
nodes.summary: in Civil Engineering discipline started with an initial intake of 30 students. in Chemical Engineering discipline started with an initial intake of 30 students.
nodes.text:
IIT Jammu started B. Tech. programme in the year 2016 with an initial intake of 30 students each in Computer Science and Engineering, Electrical Engineering and Mechanical Engineering discipline. In the year 2017, B. Tech. in Civil Engineering discipline started with an initial intake of 30 students. In the year 2018, B. Tech. in Chemical Engineering discipline started with an initial intake of 30 students. Starting with the 2019-20 session, B. Tech. in Materials Engineering was added with an initial intake of 30 students. IIT Jammu has updated seat intake in 2021 re-emphasizing our approach to inter-disciplinarity that is deeply rooted in our own Academic structure from 2019. Starting with the 2023-24 session, B. Tech. in Mathematics and Computing is added with an initial intake of 40 students. Starting with the 2025-26 session, B.Tech in Engineering Physics has started.

Ample opportunities and support is extended to deserving students. IIT Jammu gives full fledged on campus experience.

nodes.title: UG programme B.Tech
nodes.summary: in Electrical Engineering - B.Tech in Engineering Physics - B.Tech. IIT Jammu - IIM Jammu dual degree program Brochure
nodes.text:
The following programmes are offered by IIT Jammu for undergraduate studies.
 - B.Tech. in Chemical Engineering
- B.Tech. in Civil Engineering
- B.Tech. in Computer Science and Engineering
- B.Tech. in Electrical Engineering
- B.Tech in Engineering Physics
- B.Tech. in Materials Engineering
- B.Tech. in Mathematics and Computing
- B.Tech. in Mechanical Engineering

Note:
 - IIT Jammu offers a dual degree undergraduate program in collaboration with IIM Mumbai , allowing students to earn both a B.Tech degree from IIT Jammu and an MBA in Operational and Supply Chain Management from IIM Mumbai within five years.
- IIT Jammu UG programs include 20% seats (category-wise) for IIT Jammu - IIM Jammu dual degree program (four-year B.Tech of IIT Jammu and two-year MBA of IIM Jammu), transition to IIM Jammu MBA to be opted at the end of third year of the IIT Jammu B.Tech program. The transition to IIM Jammu MBA program is direct without CAT subject to having a minimum CGPA of 7.0 in the B.Tech program. Those not transitioning to the MBA program will get a B.Tech degree on completion of the academic requirements of IIT Jammu.
 IIT Jammu - IIM Jammu dual degree program Brochure

nodes.title: Hostel Accommodation
nodes.summary: They are requested to report to the caretaker office (Room no 132) for hostel accommodation. - Institute shall provide a bed, an almirah, a table, and a chair to each student accommodated in the hostel.
nodes.text:
- General Guidelines for the freshers (First year UG students)
- Transport Facilities
- All undergraduate students at IIT Jammu shall be provided hostel accommodation when they report at the institute. They are requested to report to the caretaker office (Room no 132) for hostel accommodation. At the security check, please show a valid id proof and seat allotment letter.
- Institute shall provide a bed, an almirah, a table, and a chair to each student accommodated in the hostel. There shall be stalls by vendors to purchase a mattress, bucket, mug, and some essential items for daily needs. Students/parents can make their own arrangements as well.

nodes.title: PG Certification Programme in Electric Vehicle Technology & Embedded Systems
nodes.summary: Apply Online Programme Highlights - 350+ hours of training 15+ industry projects Live sessions by IIT Jammu & DIYguru faculties - Hands-on practical training Home delivered embedded kit IIT Jammu campus immersion - Real hardware lab access Programme
nodes.text:
Apply Online

Programme Highlights
 - 350+ hours of training | 15+ industry projects | Live sessions by IIT Jammu & DIYguru faculties
- Hands-on practical training | Home delivered embedded kit | IIT Jammu campus immersion
- Real hardware lab access

Programme Structure / Modules

Course 1A Electric Vehicle Essentials 1: Industry Ecosystem & Battery Technology

Course 1B Electric Vehicle Essentials 2: Motors, Electrification and Charging Systems

Course 2 Advanced EV Engineering: Numerical Analysis, EVs, HEVs, & Heavy Vehicles

Course 3 MATLAB-SIMULINK, QSS & ADVISOR Toolbox for EV Design

Course 4 Certification Course in Electronics & Circuit Design for EV Applications

Course 5 Embedded Systems: Embedded C, 8051, Arduino & Simulation

Course 6 Certification Course in EV Battery Pack Design & BMS Protection Modeling

Course 7 Advanced Certification in EV Design & Simulation using MATLAB OR Certification in 2W EV Design & Modeling with SOLIDWORKS

Course 8 Certification in ANSYS for EVs: From Fundamentals to Advanced Linear Analysis

Course 9 Certification in Advanced BMS and Modeling & Simulation for EVs

Course 10 Certification Course in Embedded Systems Design with STM32 & ARM Cortex-M

Course 11 Certification in Advanced Powertrain Development for Electric Vehicles

Course 12 Developing Advanced Communication Applications Using ARM Cortex

Course 13 Certification Course in ADAS OR Certification Course in AUTOSAR

Course 14 Certification Course in Hydrogen Fuel Cell Technology for Electric Vehicles

Course 15 Certification Course in Advanced EV Charging Technology

Course 16 Certification in Motor & Drive System for EVs: Design & Optimization

Course 17 Advanced Control Systems for EV: MATLAB for Design & Implementation

Learning Outcomes

Analyse the electric vehicle industry ecosystem, regulatory framework, and vehicle architectures

Evaluate battery technologies, cell chemistries, pack configurations, and safety mechanisms

Design and analyse EV powertrains including motors, inverters, drivetrains, and power electronics

Perform EV system modelling and simulation using MATLAB-SIMULINK, QSS, and ADVISOR

Develop embedded systems using Embedded C on Arduino and STM32 microcontroller platforms

Model and implement Battery Management System (BMS) algorithms and protection logic

Conduct structural and linear analysis of EV components using ANSYS

Implement advanced control strategies for EV systems using MATLAB

Integrate vehicle communication systems and ARM-based embedded applications

Evaluate ADAS and AUTOSAR architectures for intelligent electric vehicles

Design and analyse EV charging systems including AC/DC and fast-charging technologies

Understand and apply hydrogen fuel cell systems in electric mobility

Optimise motor and drive systems for performance, efficiency, and reliability

Eligibility Criteria

Bachelor’s degree in Engineering / Technology / Science.

Final-year undergraduate students are also eligible to apply.

No prior ex

nodes.summary: - Generate Marketing Content Using AI Create engaging and brand-aligned marketing copy, product descriptions, ad creatives, blog posts, and social media content using Gen AI tools. - Integrate AI Tools into Marketing Tech Stack Seamlessly…
nodes.text:
Programme Structure:

Modules:
 - Introduction to Generative AI in Marketing
- Prompt Engineering for Marketers
- AI-Generated Content for Campaigns
- AI in Visual and Video Content Creation
- Personalization & Customer Journey Mapping
- Search Engine Optimization with Gen AI
- Social media & Influencer Strategy with AI
- AI for Marketing Analytics and Campaign Optimization
- Ethical Use of Gen AI in Marketing
- Integrated AI Marketing Stack & Automation
- Project

Programme Highlights:
 - Opportunity to gain a “Certificate of Completion/Participation” from IIT Jammu. Certificate of Completion : A Certificate of Completion will be issued to the participant who will fulfil all criteria (Attendance & Evaluation) related to completion of course.
- Course content and structure designed entirely by I3C and IIT Jammu.
- Lectures delivered by the eminent faculty from IIT Jammu and PW Experts.

Learning Outcomes:

Upon successful completion of the Professional Certificate Programme in Generative AI in Digital Marketing, participants will be able to:
 - Understand the Landscape Grasp the core concepts, capabilities, and limitations of Generative AI and its growing role in the digital marketing ecosystem.
- Generate Marketing Content Using AI Create engaging and brand-aligned marketing copy, product descriptions, ad creatives, blog posts, and social media content using Gen AI tools.
- Enhance Customer Targeting & Personalization Use AI to segment audiences, predict customer behavior, and personalize communication at scale for higher engagement and ROI.
- Leverage Data-Driven Insights Analyze marketing metrics, customer journeys, and campaign effectiveness using AI-powered analytics and dashboards.
- Master Prompt Engineering Apply effective prompt design techniques to get optimal outputs from tools like ChatGPT, DALL·E, Copy.ai, Jasper, and Midjourney.
- Integrate AI Tools into Marketing Tech Stack Seamlessly incorporate Gen AI platforms with CRM, email automation, performance marketing, and content management systems.
- Optimize Campaigns Using AI Perform AI-assisted A/B testing, performance forecasting, and campaign iteration based on real-time feedback and AI-generated recommendations.
- Apply Ethical & Responsible AI Marketing Practices Understand data privacy, brand consistency, content authenticity, and ethical use of AI in consumer-facing campaigns.
- Deliver a Marketing Project Design and present a fully AI-powered, cross-channel digital marketing campaign that solves a real-world business challenge.

Governing Committee:

A governing committee consisting of in-house faculty and renowned industry experts has been constituted to ensure compliance of the course with IIT Jammu academic standards.

Eligibility Criteria:

Professionals who are interested in building a career in analytics. This Programme would demand adequate familiarity with high-school level mathematics.

nodes.title: PG Certification Program in Automotive Design & Analysis
nodes.summary: - Hands-on training using industry-standard automotive design and CAE tools , aligned with real-world engineering workflows. Programme Structure / Modules Design Track – Mandatory Courses Course 1 Automotive Plastic Design using CATIA V5 Course 2…
nodes.text:
Apply Online

Programme Highlights

A comprehensive programme covering foundational and advanced concepts in automotive design and CAE.
 - 175+ hours of structured learning | 35+ industry-grade automotive projects | 125+ practical challenges
- Learn from experienced industry professionals and IIT Jammu–led faculty through a structured academic delivery model.
- Hands-on training using industry-standard automotive design and CAE tools , aligned with real-world engineering workflows.
- Self-paced learning supported by recorded modules and live interactive sessions, enabling flexibility without compromising academic rigor.
- Career readiness and placement assistance facilitated by Skill-Lync , subject to eligibility and programme norms.

Programme Structure / Modules

Design Track – Mandatory Courses

Course 1 Automotive Plastic Design using CATIA V5

Course 2 Automotive Sheet Metal Design using Siemens NX CAD / CATIA V5

Course 3 Automotive BIW Fixture Design using CATIA V5

Course 4 Geometric Dimensioning and Tolerancing (GD&T)

Course 5 Automotive Wiring Harness Design using CATIA V5

CAE Track – Mandatory Courses

Course 6 Preprocessor for Structural Analysis using ANSA

Course 7 LS-DYNA for Structural Mechanics / Finite Element Analysis

Optional Courses

Course 8 Tolerance Stack-Up Analysis

Course 9 Benchmarking and Costing for Automotive Product Development

Course 10 Automotive Class-A Surfacing using ALIAS

Course 11 Engineering Basics with DFM & DFA

Course 12 Sheet Metal and Welding Processes

Course 13 Crashworthiness Analysis using HyperMesh and Radioss

Learning Outcomes

Upon successful completion of the programme, participants will be able to:
 - Understand automotive product development cycles, vehicle architecture, and OEM engineering workflows.
- Design automotive plastic, sheet metal, BIW, and wiring harness components in compliance with manufacturability, safety, and industry standards.
- Apply Geometric Dimensioning and Tolerancing (GD&T) principles as per ASME standards to interpret and create functional engineering drawings.
- Develop proficiency in industry-standard automotive design and CAE tools including CATIA V5, Siemens NX, ANSA, LS-DYNA, HyperMesh, and ALIAS.
- Prepare simulation-ready finite element models through geometry clean-up, meshing, and pre-processing workflows.
- Set up, execute, and interpret structural and crashworthiness simulations for automotive components and systems.
- Perform tolerance analysis, benchmarking, and cost estimation to support engineering decision-making.
- Apply engineering fundamentals, DFM and DFA principles to optimise designs for manufacturing and assembly.
- Execute real-world automotive design and analysis projects aligned with OEM and Tier-1 industry requirements.

Eligibility Criteria

Bachelor’s degree in Mechanical Engineering, Automobile Engineering, or allied engineering disciplines.

Final-year undergraduate students and fresh graduates are eligible to apply.

Working prof

nodes.title: Master of Technology in Artificial Intelligence and Machine
nodes.summary: This committee reports to the IIT Jammu senate at regular intervals. Admission Process - Aspiring candidates should apply on the Intellipaat portal link https://intellipaat.com/m-tech-data-science-artificial-intelligence-iit-jammu/ .
nodes.text:
Learning (Executive)

Program structure:
 - Two year program consisting of 4 semesters. Refer to Annexure
 I for details.
- Program consists of 7 core courses and 3 elective courses with 42 hours
 of live lectures each along with a capstone project and a dissertation.
 Each course has a minimum 42 hours of lectures and tutorials over and
 above.
- All the lectures are offered online over the weekends (both Saturday and
 Sunday).
- Detailed course content for the courses carefully curated by the IIT
 Jammu faculty are available as per Annexure II .
- Provision for certificate (but not a degree) without Thesis is available.
- If a candidate is unable to complete course requirement, there will be
 provision for a summer semester to let him clear the backlog.

Governing Committee:

A governing committee consisting of in-house faculty and renowned industry
 experts has been constituted to ensure compliance of the course with IIT Jammu
 academic standards. This committee reports to the IIT Jammu senate at regular
 intervals.

Eligibility Criteria:

Working professionals with minimum two years experience in industry having a
 minimum of B. Tech/BE degree in any branch of Engineering or MCA or M.Sc. degree
 in related areas.

Enrolment Criterion:
 - Minimum CPI of 6.0 or 60% in qualifying degree, fulfilling specific
 requirements for different disciplines.
- Qualifying criterion for professionals with more than 10 year experience
 is relaxed to CPI of 5.5 or 55% marks.
- Shortlisted candidates will need  to clear a written test and/or
 interview before enrolment.
- Students once enrolled will be provided a student id of IIT Jammu and can
 access various services as availed by a regular student.

Sales & marketing:

M/s Intellipaat Software Solutions Pvt. Ltd. Bangalore has been roped in as our
 partner for online sales & marketing, pre-selection of candidates as per our
 norms, use of their online platform for delivery of content and exams as well as
 fee collection.

Admission Process
 - Aspiring candidates should apply on the Intellipaat portal link https://intellipaat.com/m-tech-data-science-artificial-intelligence-iit-jammu/ .
- Intellipaat will verify the documents and may seek clarification
 from candidates for any ambiguity and incompleteness. Intellipaat may
 choose to ask for a fully refundable deposit along with application from
 the candidate.
- All applications will be reviewed by IIT Jammu and If an application is
 not found to be prima facie meeting the required criterion, then IIT
 Jammu will ask Intellipaat to reject the application and refund any
 deposit given by the candidate in full.
- Intellipaat will communicate to the eligible candidates, the date for
 online proctored written test and interview after verification of
 documents. The test and interview will be conducted on regular
 intervals. Normally these will be scheduled on Saturdays and Sundays for
 the convenience of candidates.
- Selectin of a candidate will be done on the ba

nodes.title: Inauguration of Competency Development Programme in Network and Mobile Security funded by NTIPRIT, DoT
nodes.summary: IIT Jammu Launches Competency Development Programme with Inaugural Ceremony Jammu, August 21, 2024 – In a significant stride towards enhancing the nation’s cybersecurity capabilities, the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jammu today inaugurated…
nodes.text:
IIT Jammu Launches Competency Development Programme with Inaugural Ceremony

Jammu, August 21, 2024 – In a significant stride towards enhancing the nation’s cybersecurity capabilities, the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jammu today inaugurated its much-anticipated Competency Development Programme (CDP). Funded by the National Telecommunications Institute for Policy Research, Innovation, and Training (NTIPRIT) under the Department of Telecommunications (DoT), Government of India, the programme marks the beginning of a transformative journey for DoT officers and stakeholders in the cybersecurity domain.

The ceremony commenced with a warm welcome and an introduction to the training programme by Dr. Gaurav Varshney, Faculty of Computer Science and Engineering and Project Coordinator of the CDP. Dr. Varshney outlined the objectives and significance of the programme, emphasizing its role in building a robust cybersecurity framework in India. He highlighted the importance of collaboration between academia and industry to fulfill the government’s vision, stressing the critical need for competency development in today’s rapidly evolving digital landscape. He emphasized on the idea of “long term, academia lead, industry involved, government envisioned, competency development programmes”.

Following the introduction, representatives from various partner organizations shared their vision and roles in the programme. Mr. Vinayak Godse, CEO of the Data Security Council of India, spoke first, underscoring the critical role of data security in safeguarding national interests. His address was followed by Air Marshal K. Anantharaman, Vice President & Head of GMR Aero Academy and Lieutenant Colonel Nishant Singh of GRAMAX Cybersec, who discussed the importance of cybersecurity in the aviation sector and ongoing projects in aviation SCADA/IoT labs. Mr. Awadhesh K. Shukla, Principal Consultant at Quick Heal Technologies, and Mr. Pradipto Chakrabarty, Regional Director of CompTIA, provided their perspectives on the necessity of industry-academia collaboration in building solutions to the cyber threats. Prof. Nitesh Kumar, NU discussed the need of cyber skilling. Mr. Akash Agarwal, Vice President for the IMEA region at EC Council, emphasized on job role based training and the need for global partnerships to strengthen cybersecurity infrastructure.

Shri Deb Kumar Chakrabarti, Director General of NTIPRIT, and Shri Atul Sinha, Deputy Director General of ICT at NTIPRIT, delivered key insights into the government's strategic initiatives aimed at fortifying the nation’s telecommunications and cybersecurity sectors. Their discussions highlighted the collaborative efforts between the government, industry, and academia in addressing the challenges of the digital era.

Colonel Manvendra Nagaich, spoke about the techniques being employed across various verticals to combat cyber attacks.

The vote of thanks was delivered by Professor Debapriya Mandal, Dean of Research and C

Apply Online

Programme Highlights

134+ hours of training | 6+ industry projects | Live sessions by IIT Jammu and DIYguru faculty

Hands-on practical training | Real hardware lab access

IIT Jammu campus immersion (3 days)

Programme Structure / Modules

Course 1 Electric Vehicle Essentials 1: Industry Ecosystem & Battery Technology

Course 2 Electric Vehicle Essentials 2: Motors, Electrification, and Charging Systems

Course 3 Certification Course in MATLAB-SIMULINK, QSS & ADVISOR Toolbox for EV Design

Course 4 Certification Course in Electronics & Circuit Design for EV Applications

Course 5 Advanced Certification Course in EV Design & Simulation using MATLAB

Course 6 Certification Course in Advanced Powertrain Development for Electric Vehicles

Learning Outcomes

Analyse the electric vehicle industry ecosystem, regulatory environment, and battery technologies used in electric vehicles

Understand and analyse electric motors, power electronics, vehicle electrification architecture, and EV charging systems

Perform EV system modelling and performance simulation using MATLAB-SIMULINK, QSS, and ADVISOR toolboxes

Design and analyse electronic circuits and power electronics systems for electric vehicle applications

Develop and validate complete EV architectures through advanced MATLAB-based design and simulation techniques

Analyse and optimise EV powertrain systems, including motor–drive integration and efficiency evaluation

Conduct static structural analysis of EV components using ANSYS to assess strength and reliability

Design and implement advanced control strategies for EV systems using MATLAB-based control modelling

Eligibility Criteria

Bachelor’s degree in Engineering / Technology / Science

Electrical, Electronics and allied engineering backgrounds are preferred

Final-year undergraduate students are also eligible to apply

Diploma holders with relevant work experience are eligible

No prior experience in Electric Vehicles or simulation tools is required

A dedicated governing committee composed of IIT Jammu faculty and industry experts ensures that this program meets IIT Jammu’s academic standards and maintains real-world industry relevance.

Analyse the electric vehicle industry ecosystem, regulatory framework, and battery technologies relevant to automotive applications

Understand and evaluate electric motors, power electronics, electrification architecture, and EV charging systems

Develop Class-A automotive surfaces and exterior design concepts using Autodesk Alias

Design and optimise EV motor and drive systems for performance, efficiency, and reliability

Perform EV system modelling and performance analysis using MATLAB-SIMULINK, QSS, and ADVISOR toolboxes

Design and model two-wheeler and four-wheeler EV systems using SOLIDWORKS

Conduct static structural analysis of EV components using ANSYS to evaluate strength and durability

Apply advanced ANSYS techniques for engineering analysis and dynamic evaluation of electric vehicle systems

Eligibility Criteria

Bachelor’s degree in Engineering / Technology / Science

Mechanical and Automotive Engineering backgrounds are preferred

Final-year undergraduate students are also eligible to apply

Diploma holders with relevant work experience are eligible

No prior experience in Electric Vehicles or EV design tools is required

Governing Committee

A dedicated governing committee composed of IIT Jammu faculty and industry experts ensures that this program meets IIT Jammu’s academic standards and maintains real-world industry relevance.

Sales & Marketing

DIYguru has been onboarded as an official partner for online sales, marketing, learner engagement, and digital delivery.

While I3C-IIT Jammu and IIT Jammu provide academic leadership and certification, DIYguru manages admissions, program operations, and support ensuring a seamless learning experience for every learner.

Admission Process

Candidates may connect with the DIYguru admission team for guidance on the programme, eligibility, and admission process

Candidates must apply online by submitting the application form through the DIYguru platform

All applications will undergo document and eligibility verification by the DIYguru admissions team in coordination with I3C (Industry Intera

nodes.title: PG Certificate Program in AI-Driven LLM, SLM & Agentic RAG Development
nodes.summary: Program Overview This advanced postgraduate certification focuses on Large Language Models (LLMs), Small Language Models (SLMs), Context Engineering, and Agentic RAG systems —the core technologies powering enterprise AI today. Duration : 6 Months…

nodes.title: PG Certificate Program in AI-Driven LLM, SLM & Agentic RAG Development
nodes.summary: Program Overview This advanced postgraduate certification focuses on Large Language Models (LLMs), Small Language Models (SLMs), Context Engineering, and Agentic RAG systems —the core technologies powering enterprise AI today. Duration : 6 Months…
nodes.text:
Program Overview

This advanced postgraduate certification focuses on Large Language Models (LLMs), Small Language Models (SLMs), Context Engineering, and Agentic RAG systems —the core technologies powering enterprise AI today. The program equips learners to fine-tune, deploy, and productionize AI systems using real organizational data and modern MLOps practices.

Duration : 6 Months

Contact Hours : 110

Projects : 15+ AI projects with live URLs

Campus Immersion : Optional

Industry & Admission Partner: Futurense Technologies

Program Structure

Learning Journey

Months 1–2: Foundations & LLM / SLM Engineering

Module 1: Transformer Foundations
 - Attention mechanisms and tokenization
- GPT, Claude, and Gemini architectures
- Context window management and model selection
- Build attention visualizers and model comparison dashboards

Module 2: SLM, LLM & Context Engineering
 - Context engineering frameworks
- Chain-of-Thought, ReAct, Tree-of-Thought patterns
- Prompt evaluation, hallucination testing
- Build production prompt libraries and evaluation suites

Months 3–4: SLM Development & RAG Architectures

Module 3: SLM Development
 - LoRA & QLoRA fine-tuning
- Quantization and compression
- Dataset curation and benchmarking
- Build and publish 3 domain-specific SLMs on HuggingFace

Module 4: RAG Architectures
 - Vector databases and hybrid search
- Self-RAG, CRAG, GraphRAG
- Chunking strategies and reranking
- Build 5+ RAG systems across industries

Months 5–6: Production Deployment & Capstone

Module 5: Production Deployment
 - FastAPI-based LLM services
- Docker containerization
- Cloud deployment (AWS/GCP)
- CI/CD, observability, and cost controls

Module 6: Capstone Project
 - End-to-end AI system design
- SLM + RAG + production deployment
- Technical documentation and demo presentation

Program Highlights
 - Build custom SLMs published on HuggingFace
- Design and deploy multi-domain RAG systems
- Gain hands-on experience with enterprise-grade AI stacks
- Learn production deployment, monitoring, and optimization

Learning Outcomes

By completing this program, learners will:
 - Understand transformer architectures and LLM internals
- Fine-tune and deploy Small Language Models
- Design scalable RAG and context-engineering systems
- Build production-ready LLM APIs with monitoring
- Apply AI safely using evaluation and governance practices
- Showcase a multi-project AI portfolio

Eligibility Criteria
 - Education: B.Tech / B.E. / BCA /  M.Sc. / MCA (50%+ aggregate)
- Technical Skills: Python proficiency required
- AI Familiarity: Prior use of AI tools preferred

Ideal For
 - Final-year engineering students
- Early-career AI/ML professionals
- Mid-career professionals transitioning into GenAI
- Developers and engineers building AI products

Sales & Program Delivery

While I3C–IIT Jammu provides academic governance and certification, Futurense Technologies manages admissions, learner engagement, program delivery, and industry alignment.

Admissio

nodes.title: About Us
nodes.summary: Academic Affairs section is the institutional office that oversees a variety of academic programs and departments. IIT Jammu started its journey in the year 2016 with 3 UG programs and has grown now offering UG in 7 programmes, M.Tech in 8…
nodes.text:
Academic Affairs section is the institutional office that oversees a variety of academic programs and departments.

The section plays a pivotal role in advising students and assisting them as they navigate their academic pathway towards completing degree. The Academic Affairs facilitates students with a whole range of academic functions including admission, pre- registration, registration, examination & grading function and processing student requests.

The section is responsible for the implementation of the decisions taken on academic matters by the Senate.

IIT Jammu started its journey in the year 2016 with 3 UG programs and has grown now offering UG in 7 programmes, M.Tech in 8 specializations, M.Sc./MS(R) in 2 programmes and Ph.D. in 11 programmes. In addition to regular M.Tech programme IIT Jammu is also offering Executive M. Tech. for Working Professionals in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning

nodes.title: IIT Jammu selected for the MoE's Capacity Building Program on Design and Entrepreneurship
nodes.summary: The CBDE program was launched virtually by the Secretary, Department of Higher Education, Ministry of Education, GoI, Sh K Sanjay Murthy on 7th May 2024. The program is designed to equip identified Higher Education Institutions (HEIs) and faculty…
nodes.text:
The CBDE program was launched virtually by the Secretary, Department of Higher Education, Ministry of Education, GoI, Sh K Sanjay Murthy on 7th May 2024. The program is designed to equip identified Higher Education Institutions (HEIs) and faculty members to focus on Design and Entrepreneurship development in their institutions. The program will provide mentor support to the identified HEIs so they can learn from the expertise gained through years of engagement in the industry setup. 30 HEIs have been selected through a rigorous process anchored by the IIITDM Kancheepuram, a Malaviya Mission Teacher Training Center (MMTTC) as the Nodal Centre for the program.

Smt Debjani Ghosh, President, Nasscom, stressed at the launch that an entrepreneurial mindset needs to be nourished in the young minds present in educational institutions. The program's design, therefore, not only nourishes students' problem-solving skills but also has concrete steps in scaling up students' ideas through stage-wise hand-holding support provided by mentors from the industry, in addition to one-to-one mentoring of faculty. The program aims to promote generative dialogue among faculty, student teams, and HEI partners through a pool of expert mentors in order to produce actionable outcomes.
