All matters relating to following subjects are dealt by the Establishment Section:-

General Administrative Matters
 - CCS(CCA) Rules 1965, CCS(Conduct) Rules 1964, CCS (Temporary Service) Rules 1965- interpretation and application.
- Retirement/Voluntary Retirement / Pre-mature Retirement / Extension in Service
- Verification of character & antecedents
- Promotion, Seniority Departmental Examinations and Review of MACP Scheme cases
- Processing of House Building Advance, Computer Advance, Children Education Allowance, etc
- Maintenance of in-position strength and sanctioned strength
- Recruitment process for all Regular/Contractual/Third party employees including managing HR requisition, drafting and publishing advertisement, query handling, applications forms, preparation and issuance of Offer of appointments, issuing joining order and orientation training of new joinees etc.
- Pay Fixation, interpretation of FR on pay matters, special allowance, deputation allowance etc.
- General issues & interpretation of leave rules including special kinds of leave. Maintenance of leave account of Group A, B and C cadre employees
- Grant of NOC and Forwarding of applications in response to advertisements, relieving of permanent employee on lien
- Maintenance of Service Books, Personal Files and Annual Property Returns
- Joining Time/Joining Pay benefits etc.
- Holidays for IIT Jammu employees
- Scrutiny, passing and preparation of Group A, B and C - T.A., L.T.C.  and other staff related claims
- Maintenance of Annual Performance Appraisal reports
- Providing assistance in preparation of promotion panels & Departmental examinations
- Confidential matters relation to promotions, confirmations, probation in the cadre of Group 'B', and 'C'
- Disciplinary cases, RTI matters and Litigation matters pertaining to Establishment.
- Parliament questions/ data / report preparation etc. relating to Establishment Section.

Training and Development Matters:

In addition to above, Establishment II also deal with the matters under Career Progression & Training like Preparation of training plan of groups A, B and C staff

nodes.title: IIT Jammu, TimesPro offers Post Graduate Diploma in Cyber
nodes.summary: Security The Indian Institute of Technology Jammu announces its Post Graduate Diploma in Cyber Security, a comprehensive 12-month program designed to equip individuals with the necessary skills to excel in the field of cyber security. Admission to…
nodes.text:
Security

The Indian Institute of Technology Jammu announces its Post Graduate Diploma in
 Cyber Security, a comprehensive 12-month program designed to equip individuals
 with the necessary skills to excel in the field of cyber security. This program
 combines theoretical knowledge, hands-on learning, and practical projects to
 establish a strong foundation for a successful career in this rapidly evolving
 domain.

The program encompasses a wide range of topics, including the design and
 development of intrusion detection and prevention systems, security protocols
 for low powered devices, email security, anti-phishing solutions, AI-powered
 cyber defense, homomorphic encryption applications, infrastructure access
 management solutions, security solutions for cloud-based virtualized resources,
 VPNs and proxy development, PKI applications development, and much more.

This program is ideal for individuals with a background in Software Engineering
 and IT, as well as those seeking a career shift into the field of cyber
 security. IT and Engineering professionals aiming to enhance their knowledge in
 secure technology design and development, system and network security analysis,
 and application security will greatly benefit from this program.

Applicants must hold a qualifying degree in B.E, B.Tech, MCA, M.Sc./MS in IT, CS,
 ECE, EE, or related fields. A minimum of one year of work experience in IT, CSE,
 EC, EE, or related fields is required. The eligibility criteria for this program
 are a minimum of 55% or 6 CGPA in the qualifying degree, while individuals with
 over 10 years of work experience are eligible with an aggregate of 50% or 5.5
 CGPA.

Admission to the program is based on relevant academic credentials and a
 20-minute virtual interview conducted by an interview panel.

For more information and to apply for the program, please visit https://bit.ly/3C6zb5K.

View Brochure

nodes.title: Know your Counselors
nodes.summary: Himanshi Singh Himanshi Singh RCI licensed Clinical Psychologist, working at IIT Jammu since 2021. Nandita Sharma Nandita Sharma is RCI Licensed Clinical Psychologist and Educational Psychologist.

nodes.title: Know your Counselors
nodes.summary: Himanshi Singh Himanshi Singh RCI licensed Clinical Psychologist, working at IIT Jammu since 2021. Nandita Sharma Nandita Sharma is RCI Licensed Clinical Psychologist and Educational Psychologist.
nodes.text:
Himanshi Singh

Himanshi Singh RCI licensed Clinical Psychologist, working at IIT Jammu since 2021. She did her M.Phil in Clinical Psychology from PGIMS, Rohtak, Haryana. She had an experience of working with AIIMS, New Delhi.

She has been extensively trained in various forms of psychological assessments and psychotherapy including individual therapy (eg., Cognitive behavior therapy, mindfulness, behavior therapy and family therapy. She has expertise in providing therapy for depression, anxiety, academic stress, OCD, adjustment, substance use (tobacco, alcohol, cannabis, opioids, etc.), trauma, anger issues, relationship issues, issues related to transition and change, uncertainties about goals, concentration issues, etc. Along with on-site intervention, she is also well-versed with the online mode of therapy.

Her approach utilizes empathetic and a non-judgemental attitude to create a safe space for individuals. As a therapist, she incorporates individual centric and strength-based practices to provide holistic intervention, while abiding by the highest ethical principles.


Nandita Sharma

Nandita Sharma is RCI Licensed Clinical Psychologist and Educational Psychologist. She has extensive experience as an Academic Researcher and Practicing Psychologist at a medical institution, NGOs, and schools.

Her area of expertise lies in dealing with stress, restlessness, self-esteem, relationship issues, addiction,  grief, trauma, LGBTQIA+, anger issues, and procrastination. She is proficient in providing individual counseling & psychotherapy and administering and interpreting psychological assessments. She treats adolescents and adults in a unique environment while incorporating mindfulness, cognitive-behavioral, client-centric, and other strategies as appropriate.

She has also conducted seminars, awareness programs, and workshops for individuals, teachers, and working professionals.

nodes.title: Team E2S2M from IIT Jammu clinches top honours at the “Ideas to Impact Challenge 2025”
nodes.summary: Team E2S2M from IIT Jammu clinches top honours at the “Ideas to Impact Challenge 2025” held at IIT Madras. Kamakoti (Director, IIT Madras), Prof.
nodes.text:
Team E2S2M from IIT Jammu clinches top honours at the “Ideas to Impact Challenge 2025” held at IIT Madras. The team garnered significant acclaim and was awarded a prestigious cash prize of ₹1,00,000 in recognition of their impactful innovation.

Team E2S2M from IIT Jammu, consisting of Waseem Raja (Student Team Lead), Qamaruz Zaman Khaki, and Akhil Gupta, research scholars under the supervision of Dr. Pratik Kumar, has won the Ideas to Impact (i2i) Challenge 2025 organized at IIT Madras.

The winning project, titled “Decentralised Blackwater Treatment for Individual Household & Community Latrines (IHHCLs)”, is part of Waseem Raja’s ongoing research on nature-based decentralised wastewater treatment solutions in a dual plumbing system (segregation of blackwater and greywater).

More than 800 teams participated in the competition, which involved a rigorous six-month evaluation process, including virtual progress reviews and presentations, culminating in the grand finale at IIT Madras on October 25, 2025. A distinguished jury comprising academicians, researchers, industry experts, and innovators selected the winning teams. The team also interacted with Prof. V. Kamakoti (Director, IIT Madras), Prof. Indumathi M. Nambi (PhD, FNAE, IIT Madras), representatives from Ashok Leyland, Wipro, and the Special Secretary, Government of Tamil Nadu, who appreciated their innovative and practical approach.

This achievement underscores IIT Jammu’s commitment to advancing sustainable technologies, green innovation, and translating lab-scale research into real-world applications.

Congratulations to Team E2S2M, and sincere thanks to IIT Madras (i2i) for the opportunity, support, and recognition.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: A landmark achievement for IIT Jammu and a proud moment for India’s semiconductor ecosystem. Both chips were developed at IIT Jammu and fabricated at SCL Mohali under MeitY’s Chips to Startup (C2S) Programme, which plays a pivotal role in enabling…

nodes.title: A landmark achievement for IIT Jammu and a proud moment for India’s semiconductor ecosystem.
nodes.summary: A landmark achievement for IIT Jammu and a proud moment for India’s semiconductor ecosystem. We are proud that IIT Jammu contributes four of the ninety-four chips produced through SCL — accelerating India’s movement toward a resilient, indigenous,…
nodes.text:
A landmark achievement for IIT Jammu and a proud moment for India’s semiconductor ecosystem.

The IC-ResQ Lab has successfully completed the tapeout of two advanced indigenous chips — C2S0079 and C2S0080 — unveiled at a special ceremony addressed by Hon’ble Minister Shri Ashwini Vaishnaw on 28 November 2025.

This milestone is the result of five years of sustained research, strategic development, and academic rigor led by Dr. Ambika Prasad Shah. With consistent progress and growing capabilities, IIT Jammu is emerging as a strong national contender in the highly specialised domain of semiconductor design.

Each chip represents a significant leap in applied research:
 - C2S0079 – An ultra-low-power, high-gain amplifier advancing India’s capabilities in implantable medical devices and next-generation pacemakers.
- C2S0080 – A secure 4KB SRAM designed to counter leakage power attacks, strengthening hardware-level security for sensitive systems.

Both chips were developed at IIT Jammu and fabricated at SCL Mohali under MeitY’s Chips to Startup (C2S) Programme, which plays a pivotal role in enabling academic institutions and startups to design, fabricate, and test semiconductor innovations on Indian soil.

We are proud that IIT Jammu contributes four of the ninety-four chips produced through SCL — accelerating India’s movement toward a resilient, indigenous, and future-ready semiconductor ecosystem.

Gratitude to MeitY, the C2S Programme, and the SCL Mohali team for enabling this national progress.

Together, we continue to strengthen India’s scientific excellence and technological self-reliance.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu Strengthens India–Japan Academic Engagement through JIGER Initiative
nodes.summary: IIT Jammu Strengthens India–Japan Academic Engagement through JIGER Initiative Senior representatives from the Indian Institute of Technology Jammu participated in a reception hosted on 26 February 2026 at Shimane University, Japan, on the eve of…
nodes.text:
IIT Jammu Strengthens India–Japan Academic Engagement through JIGER Initiative

Senior representatives from the Indian Institute of Technology Jammu participated in a reception hosted on 26 February 2026 at Shimane University, Japan, on the eve of the Japan-India Global Education and Research Center (JIGER) Kick-off Symposium.

The IIT Jammu team included Director, Prof. Manoj Singh Gaur, Dr. Jayaramulu Kolleboyina, Dean of International Relations; and Prof. Anurag Misra, Dean (Infrastructure Planning and Management).

During the event, discussions with Hiroki Otani, President of Shimane University, and Yoshimi Kataoka, Vice President, focused on strengthening strategic partnerships between Indian Institute of Technology Jammu and Shimane University.

Interactions also explored collaboration with key academic units of Shimane University, including the Graduate School of Humanities & Social Sciences, Graduate School of Natural Science & Technology, Interdisciplinary Graduate School of Science & Engineering, and the Graduate School of Life & Environmental Science, with opportunities for joint research, joint degree programs, and faculty and student exchanges.

The International Office of Indian Institute of Technology Jammu continues to actively promote international collaborations, MoUs, and strategic partnerships with leading global institutions to advance research, innovation, and academic mobility.

The launch of Japan-India Global Education and Research Center (JIGER), led by Hemanth Noothalapati, marks an important step toward expanding India–Japan collaboration in research, education, and academic exchange

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: On 3rd March 2026, a high-level delegation from the Indian Institute of Technology Jammu, led by its Director Prof. This visit represents an important step in expanding IIT Jammu’s international academic partnerships and fostering impactful…

nodes.title: IIT Jammu Delegation Visits Tohoku University, Japan
nodes.summary: As part of the visit, the IIT Jammu delegation engaged in detailed discussions with Vice President Professor Masahiro Yamaguchi of Tohoku University and Dr. This visit represents an important step in expanding IIT Jammu’s international academic…
nodes.text:
On 3rd March 2026, a high-level delegation from the Indian Institute of Technology Jammu, led by its Director Prof. Manoj Singh Gaur, visited Tohoku University, Japan, to strengthen academic and research collaboration between the two institutions. The delegation included Dr. Jayaramulu Kolleboyina, Dean of International Relations, and Prof. Anurag Misra, Dean (Infrastructure Planning and Management).

As part of the visit, the IIT Jammu delegation engaged in detailed discussions with Vice President Professor Masahiro Yamaguchi of Tohoku University and Dr. Ankit A. Ravankar, Associate Professor in the Department of Robotics, to explore opportunities for expanding institutional collaboration.

The visit included interactions with leading research centres and facilities at Tohoku University, such as the Advanced Institute for Materials Research and the state-of-the-art NanoTerasu synchrotron radiation facility. The IIT Jammu delegation also explored collaborative opportunities in key areas including advanced materials, data-driven medicine, disaster science, Japanese studies and artificial intelligence, space research and development, and AI-driven robotics.

The discussions focused on identifying specific collaborative initiatives involving joint research projects, faculty and student exchanges, and institutional partnerships, aligned with IIT Jammu’s vision of strengthening its global research engagement. This visit represents an important step in expanding IIT Jammu’s international academic partnerships and fostering impactful collaboration with leading global research institutions.

nodes.title: Mapping Tribal Heritage
nodes.summary: Javed Ahmed Rana, who underscored that tribal heritage is a living, dynamic system rooted in language, belief structures, customary practices, and community life. The workshop reflected IIT Jammu’s continued commitment to inclusive scholarship,…
nodes.text:
The Department of Humanities and Social Sciences, IIT Jammu, organised a one-day workshop titled “Mapping Tribal Heritage”, focusing on digital documentation, archival technologies, and critical engagement with the cultural expressions, oral narratives, and socio-cultural knowledge systems of indigenous communities across India.

The workshop was inaugurated by the Hon’ble Minister for Jal Shakti, Forest, Ecology & Environment and Tribal Affairs, Mr. Javed Ahmed Rana, who underscored that tribal heritage is a living, dynamic system rooted in language, belief structures, customary practices, and community life. He emphasised that its preservation must be driven by participatory, community-led research frameworks, ensuring intergenerational transmission and cultural continuity. Highlighting the deep historical roots of Gojri, the Minister noted that language vitality lies in sustained community use supported by institutional engagement.

Commending IIT Jammu’s socially responsive academic initiatives, the Minister encouraged interdisciplinary and policy-oriented research, including studies on the Gujjar-Bakarwal legacy, climate change adaptation, seasonal migration, and sustainable livelihoods. On the occasion, IIT Jammu also launched a dedicated digital portal for tribal knowledge documentation, envisioned as an open-access repository for archival, ethnographic, and audiovisual resources.

The workshop reflected IIT Jammu’s continued commitment to inclusive scholarship, cultural preservation, and community-engaged research.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu Researchers bring laurels | bagged the 2nd prize at IEEE ICASSP 2021
nodes.summary: Vinit Jakhetiya, Assistant Professor, IIT JAMMU 4: Dr. Badri N Subudhi, Assistant Professor, IIT JAMMU 5: Dr.
nodes.text:
A team of researchers from IIT JAMMU and the University of Pennsylvania bagged the 2nd prize at the 46th edition of IEEE ICASSP 2021 organized in Toronto, Ontario, Canada between June 6th to 11th 2021. IEEE ICASSP is the flagship conference of the IEEE Signal Processing Society. The team worked on the detection of COVID 19 and community-acquired pneumonia from chest CT-scan images with deep learning; an Artificial Intelligence Technique. This challenge was held in 3 stages and 6 teams from across the World made it to the final round including; IIT Jammu, Indian Institute of Technology, Delhi, Beijing University of Posts and Telecommunications, CNR France, University of Sheffield, UK, Institute of High-Performance Computing, A*STAR, Singapore. The objective of this challenge was to design a framework that can make a distinction between covid-19, community-acquired pneumonia, and normal patient by using CHEST CT Scan images in order to help efficiently diagnose a patient for covid-19.

The team included:

1: Shubham Kumar Chaudhary, Mtech-DS,IIT JAMMU

2: Sadbhawna Thakur, Ph.D. scholar, IIT JAMMU

3: Dr. Vinit Jakhetiya, Assistant Professor, IIT JAMMU

4: Dr. Badri N Subudhi, Assistant Professor, IIT JAMMU

5: Dr. Sharath Chandra Guntuku, Assistant Professor (Research) in the Department of Computer and Information Science, University Of Pennsylvania

6: Dr. Ujjwal Baid, Postdoctoral Researcher, University Of Pennsylvania

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: Previously, during her enriching 5-month fellowship tenure at NTU Singapore, Ms. Recognized as "excellent" by her NTU Project supervisor Prof.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu: Arpita Nema receives a full-time PhD admission offer from NTU Singapore
nodes.summary: admission offer from Nanyang Technological University (NTU) Singapore. Previously, during her enriching 5-month fellowship tenure at NTU Singapore, Ms.
nodes.text:
Ms. Arpita Nema, an M.Tech. in Data Science student at IIT Jammu, after successfully completing the India Connect Research Fellowship, has achieved a remarkable milestone by receiving a full-time Ph.D. admission offer from Nanyang Technological University (NTU) Singapore.

Previously, during her enriching 5-month fellowship tenure at NTU Singapore, Ms. Nema showcased exemplary research expertise while actively participating in various extracurricular activities. Recognized as "excellent" by her NTU Project supervisor Prof. Deepu Rajan, she has also qualified for the prestigious Singapore-India Hackathon, demonstrating her outstanding aptitude in the field.

Notably, Ms. Nema exhibited remarkable leadership qualities and held a deep passion for addressing the challenges faced by women in research. She recently engaged with Prof. Sierin Lim, the esteemed Co-Chair of the Promotion of Women in Engineering, Research, and Science (POWERS) at NTU, to establish the Society of Women Engineers (SWE) at IIT Jammu.

"I am grateful to Prof. Manoj Gaur, Dr. Vinit Jakhetiya, NTU India Connect Coordinator Rahul Singh and the entire IIT Jammu family for encouragement, support, and guidance," says Ms. Nema after receiving the offer. Her accomplishment is a testament to the academic excellence fostered at IIT Jammu, enabling students like Arpita to thrive in their educational journeys.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu develops Face protection shield
nodes.summary: IIT Jammu is producing these shields in large quantities (10000 initially) to distribute among various defense professionals and doctors. These face shields would be distributed in various zones of the Jammu region.
nodes.text:
IIT Jammu is proud to announce the mass production of the face protection shields which is a very important personal protection equipment (PPE) for any professional during the current pandemic COVID-19. These shields are specially designed for Doctors, Police, Armed forces of Jammu & Kashmir and IIT Jammu students, staff, and faculties. IIT Jammu is producing these shields in large quantities (10000 initially) to distribute among various defense professionals and doctors. These shields are going to help these professionals in fighting against the COVID-19.

On 29 April 2020, in a meeting between IIT Jammu and Suram Singh JKPS, SP Rural Jammu, and Mohan Sharma JKPS, SDPO Nagrota, 250 face protection shields were handed over to the Police officials by the director of IIT Jammu, Prof. Manoj Singh Gaur. These face shields would be distributed in various zones of the Jammu region.

The event has been witnessed by various news channels and print media organizations and the related telecast and news coverage can be found in the following links:

nodes.title: IIT Jammu Chosen as Pioneering Recipient of the "100 5G Use Case Labs" Initiative
nodes.summary: In a monumental achievement that underscores its commitment to technological innovation, the Indian Institute of Technology, Jammu (IIT Jammu), has been proudly selected as a pioneer in the establishment of a 5G Use Case Lab. The 5G Use Case Lab at…
nodes.text:
In a monumental achievement that underscores its commitment to technological innovation, the Indian Institute of Technology, Jammu (IIT Jammu), has been proudly selected as a pioneer in the establishment of a 5G Use Case Lab. This prestigious initiative falls under the purview of the "100 5G Use Case Labs" scheme, generously funded by the Department of Telecommunications (DoT), Government of India.

This momentous announcement comes as a result of the dedication of IIT Jammu's team, which comprises of Dr Ankur Bansal, Dr Samaresh Bera, Dr Ankit Dubey, Dr Saurav Kushmanda, Dr Ajay Singh, Dr Ravikant Saini, and Dr Sudhakar Modem.

The institution is exceptionally proud of the team's collaborative efforts and unwavering commitment to advancing the nation's technological prowess in the field of 5G telecommunications.

The 5G Use Case Lab at IIT Jammu will serve as a pivotal center for research, development, and innovation in the realm of 5G technology. It will provide a conducive environment for conducting experiments, trials, and testing of 5G applications, propelling India to the forefront of global telecommunications innovation.

This momentous achievement stands as a testament to IIT Jammu's dedication to cutting-edge research and development and its unwavering support of the Government of India's vision for a technologically empowered future.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: "Celebrating Teacher's Day at IIT Jammu: Alumni Affairs Unites Alumni in Heartfelt Tribute to Their Favorite Professors! Alumni Affairs meticulously collected and compiled these messages into a heartwarming tribute to the dedicated teachers.
nodes.text:
"Celebrating Teacher's Day at IIT Jammu: Alumni Affairs Unites Alumni in Heartfelt Tribute to Their Favorite Professors!

At IIT Jammu, Teacher's Day took on a special significance this year, Eager to express their deep appreciation for the educators who left a lasting impact on their lives, alumni were invited to share messages of gratitude and admiration for their favorite teachers.

The response was overwhelming as alumni from around the world penned down their heartfelt thanks, sharing cherished memories and moments of inspiration. These messages were more than just words; they were tokens of gratitude nurtured over years of learning and growth.

Alumni Affairs meticulously collected and compiled these messages into a heartwarming tribute to the dedicated teachers. On Teacher's Day, these messages were presented to the educators who had shaped countless futures. It was a moving moment of connection, bridging the past and the present as alumni reached out to say, 'Thank you for lighting our path.'

This celebration served as a powerful reminder that education's impact extends far beyond the classroom, forging lifelong bonds and leaving an indelible mark on hearts and minds. As we move forward, let us carry this spirit of gratitude with us, honoring the timeless treasures of learning and the profound connections it creates."

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: Harshdev Singh, B.Tech Electrical Engineering Batch 2018-2022, received the Best New Initiative Award which was sponsored by the Alumni of IIT Jammu on 16th October 2022. During his term at IIT Jammu, he also represented IIT Jammu at Inter IIT…
nodes.text:
Mr. Harshdev Singh, B.Tech Electrical Engineering Batch 2018-2022, received the Best New Initiative Award which was sponsored by the Alumni of IIT Jammu on 16th October 2022. More than 20 applications were received from various students and around 8 were shortlisted. After transparent screening by the committee comprised of members of the Alumni Affairs Office, Dean Alumni Affairs, Faculty Members and Alumni of IIT Jammu. The committee decided to award Mr. Harshdev Singh with the ‘Best New Inititative’.

Mr. Harshdev Singh took an initiative to strengthen the robotics and coding clubs of the institute. He took responsibility in engaging and training the current students and organized various workshops on Web and Game Development under which around 200+ students participated and learned the basic and advanced concepts of Game Development. During his term at IIT Jammu, he also represented IIT Jammu at Inter IIT Sports meet in 2018 (Guwahati) and 2019 (Kharagpur) in cricket and football. He also won the title of ‘Mr. Pravaah’ in 2020 and ‘Mr. Convoquer in 2020. After graduating from IIT Jammu, he decided to launch his own organization by the name of ‘Rigel Next Games’ and working as a Co-Founder & CEO.

Alumni Team wishes him all the best and success!

nodes.text: Prior to his role at IIT Jammu, Ram served as a senior researcher at the Regional Centre of Advanced Technologies and Materials in the Czech Republic and the Technical University Munich in Germany. He is a recipient of the Alexander von Humboldt Postdoctoral Fellowship in Germany and an ICMS Postdoctoral Fellowship. Ram’s impactful contributions in the field of hybrid porous materials are reflected in his publications in renowned international journals, including Advanced Materials, Angewandte Chemie, Advanced Functional Materials, Nature Communications, and recognition in Advanced Engineering News and Advanced Science News. His research has been featured in various cover arts, showcasing the significance of his work in the scientific community.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu won the SIH 2020
nodes.summary: Vijay K Pal (Convener, IIC, IIT Jammu) Dr. Satyadev Ahlawat (Member, IIC, IIT Jammu) Dr.
nodes.text:
Our team of IIT Jammu 'Positive minds 2.0' has won the Smart India Hackathon 2020 . The problem statement of their SIH participation was "Sentiment analysis of code mixed text". Many teams from all IICs of India participated in this event.

Kudos to the following members of the team: Mentor: Dr. Yamuna Prasad 1. Akash Kumar (Team leader) 2. Vishwas Modanwal 3. Aayush Sugandhi 4. Parth Thakker 5. Shailja Sharma 6. Shreyas Arvind Gore

Smart India Hackathon is a nationwide initiative to provide students a platform to solve some of the pressing problems we face in out daily lives, and thus inculcate a culture of product innovation and a mindset of problem-solving. Like every year, this year also Prime Minister Narendra Modi interacted with the students of the final round of Smart India Hackathon ( click here to watch this year's video ).

About Institute Innovation Council (IIC) of IIT Jammu: IIT Jammu has formed its IIC under MHRD Innovation cell in Sept. 2018. Every year participation from all IICs of India are participating in SIH. At present IIC members of IIT Jammu are:

Prof. Manoj Gaur, Director (President, IIC, IIT Jammu) Dr. Vijay K Pal (Convener, IIC, IIT Jammu) Dr. Satyadev Ahlawat (Member, IIC, IIT Jammu) Dr. Yamuna and Dr. Vijay (SPOC, SIH 2020) Once again congratulations to winners.

nodes.title: Procurement Management
nodes.summary: IIT Jammu have rigorous and stringent purchasing policies to ensure fair usage of public funds and to demonstrate transparency to the public. IIT Jammu manage internal procurement requests on E governance portal which provide real-time tracking of…

nodes.title: Procurement Management
nodes.summary: IIT Jammu have rigorous and stringent purchasing policies to ensure fair usage of public funds and to demonstrate transparency to the public. IIT Jammu manage internal procurement requests on E governance portal which provide real-time tracking of…
nodes.text:
IIT Jammu have rigorous and stringent purchasing policies to ensure fair usage of public funds and to demonstrate transparency to the public.

As per GFR rules, common-use goods and services must be procured through Government e-Marketplace (GeM) and that only registered buyers may make purchases. This helps to ensure that purchases are made from trusted vendors and that due diligence is done to ensure that items available on GeM are indeed procured through GeM. Those Items not available on GeM may be procured through an open tender process.

Procurement process may be lengthy and involve multiple steps, including forming purchase committees, releasing open tenders, and conducting due diligence. It's important to be patient and allow sufficient time for these processes to be completed, particularly for expensive equipment that may take up to a year to arrive after funds have been earmarked for it and the purchasing process has been initiated.

IIT Jammu manage internal procurement requests on E governance portal which provide real-time tracking of procurement requests to ensure timely completion within the designated timeframe.

Overall, IIT Jammu is committed to ensuring fair and transparent usage of public funds through its transparent purchasing policies and procedures.

nodes.summary: Date and Time : 06.04.2024 - 5 pm onwards (Tomorrow) Venue : CR 12LH2022 His Profile: Shaik Aleem Ur Rehaman has over 10 years of Experience working in the Semiconductor/VLSI industry as an ASIC Design and Verification Engineer. During his career,…
nodes.text:
Date and Time : 06.04.2024 - 5 pm onwards (Tomorrow) Venue : CR 12LH2022

His Profile: Shaik Aleem Ur Rehaman has over 10 years of Experience working in the Semiconductor/VLSI industry as an ASIC Design and Verification Engineer. He is currently working in Microsoft designing Next Generation AI-based Accelerator Chips for Chat GPT and Azure Platforms.

He has previously worked with AMD in the Server SoC Team, working on AI Engine Chiplets and Classical Data Center Servers - comprising of high-performance x86 CPU Cores, Cache Clusters, and DDR Memory. His past experience comprises of Leading CXL/PCIe Verification team in Intel, worked with Cypress Semiconductors on Low power ARM based CPU Subsystem for driverless car and Automotives. Worked in R&D project of Boeing US Team on designing FPGA for communication between SpaceCrafts. He started his career in an US based startup named HMicro - Desiging Ultra Low Power dual band wireless ECG Chip for Remote Health Monitoring Application of Patients. During his career, starting from his B.E. days, he has presented over 40+ Research papers at various International Conferences and Journals.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: We are proud to announce that our student team SAP Aerospace from IIT Jammu has won the Boeing University Innovation Leadership Development program (BUILD 2.0) 2022-23. The program included 4 rounds of regional level competitions conducted by Boeing…

nodes.title: Build Boeing leadership and innovation challenge 2.0
nodes.summary: We are proud to announce that our student team SAP Aerospace from IIT Jammu has won the Boeing University Innovation Leadership Development program (BUILD 2.0) 2022-23. The program included 4 rounds of regional level competitions conducted by Boeing…
nodes.text:
We are proud to announce that our student team SAP Aerospace from IIT Jammu has won the Boeing University Innovation Leadership Development program (BUILD 2.0) 2022-23. The team was selected as one among the seven winners, from 829 teams. The program included 4 rounds of regional level competitions conducted by Boeing in partnership with the regional incubator FITT- IIT Delhi. Thereafter, the teams went through a bootcamp and an immersion program with FITT. The final competition was held at Boeing campus in Bangalore, in which 28 teams participated from all over the country. Winners received the award and 10 Lakh cash prize from Dr. Satheesh Reddy, scientific advisor to Defence minister. Early stage start-up entrepreneurs will be further supported by Boeing and the respective incubator partner for over six months, to help develop their ideas into market-ready and viable business solutions.

The student team is working to develop a micro gas turbine engine for combat drone applications and currently focusing to demonstrate an advanced lean-burn combustor technology. The 10 member team is being mentored by Dr. Shanmugadas K.P. and the project is in collaboration with NCCRD, IIT Madras

nodes.summary: IIT Jammu MES Lab Team Wins First Prize at Inno Spark! The recognition at Inno Spark highlights the impact of IIT Jammu’s research and innovation ecosystem in addressing global healthcare challenges.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu MES Lab Team Wins First Prize at Inno Spark!
nodes.summary: IIT Jammu MES Lab Team Wins First Prize at Inno Spark! The recognition at Inno Spark highlights the impact of IIT Jammu’s research and innovation ecosystem in addressing global healthcare challenges.
nodes.text:
IIT Jammu MES Lab Team Wins First Prize at Inno Spark!

We are proud to announce that Natish Kumar, a research scholar from the Department of Chemical Engineering, IIT Jammu, and his team member Devtulya Chander have won First Prize at Inno Spark, held on March 4, 2025, at SMVDU, Kakryal, Katra, Jammu, for their groundbreaking research on ""Portable, Quantitative, Real-time Isothermal Nucleic Acid Amplification Test for Infectious Disease Detection Using a Microfluidic Device-integrated with UV-LED Photodiode Detector.""

This innovative work, incubated within E-Sustain Power Private Limited, aims to develop a cost-effective, portable, and real-time molecular diagnostic platform to revolutionize infectious disease detection. The recognition at Inno Spark highlights the impact of IIT Jammu’s research and innovation ecosystem in addressing global healthcare challenges.

We extend our congratulations to Natish Kumar and his supervisor, Dr. Ravi Kumar Arun, for this remarkable achievement. Their dedication to advancing microfluidics and molecular diagnostics underscores IIT Jammu's commitment to cutting-edge research and societal impact.

nodes.title: Summer School 2026
nodes.summary: Summer School 2026 - Training & Internship Program launched at IIT Jammu in collaboration with I3C - IIT Jammu and Techible. We are excited to launch Summer School 2026, a hands-on Training & Internship Program designed to help students build…
nodes.text:
Summer School 2026 - Training & Internship Program launched at IIT Jammu in collaboration with I3C - IIT Jammu and Techible.

We are excited to launch Summer School 2026, a hands-on Training & Internship Program designed to help students build In-demand skills, real meaningful projects, and Industry-ready portfolios.

The program bridges the gap between academia & industry through mentorship from experts, and working with emerging technologies to develop real-world skills.

What makes this program unique?
 - Hands-on training with IIT mentors and Industry experts
- Project-based learning with real-world applications
- Exposure to emerging trends in AI & other technologies shaping the future
- Hackathons, Mentorship sessions, and Career guidance

Program Duration:
 - 4- 6 Weeks (extendable to 2 Months)
- 6 Months

Starting Date:
 - June First week, 2026

Mode : Online & Offline (On-campus at IIT Jammu)

Apply Here: https://summerschool.iitjammu.ac.in

For any queries/collaboration:

summerschool@iitjammu.ac.in, +91-9186184541.

Through Summer School'26, we aim to empower students with the skills needed to build, innovate, and lead in the technology-driven future.

(School Edition for Grades 7–12)

Indian Institute of Technology Jammu, in collaboration with I3C-IIT Jammu and NurtureNext (Techible), is pleased to announce NitiNext – The Youth Dialogue, a 3 -Day Model United Nations (MUN) conference for school students. This initiative aims to provide a national platform for young learners to engage in policy discussions, diplomacy simulations, and structured dialogue on contemporary global issues while developing critical leadership and communication skills. Dates : 8 - 10 May 2026 Venue : Indian Institute of Technology Jammu Event Highlights:
 - 3-Day Youth Policy & Diplomacy Conclave
- 6+ Diplomacy Committees for debates and discussions
- Communication & Diplomacy Masterclasses by IIT Jammu Faculty
- National Platform for School Students (Grades 7–12)
- Certification by IIT Jammu

Important Dates:
 - Last Date to Apply for School Ambassadors : 17 March 2026
- Delegate Registrations from: 20 March - 19 April 2026

Registration for School Ambassadors are live. Register at: https://nurturenext.io Join us in shaping young leaders, diplomats, and critical thinkers of tomorrow.

nodes.text:
Congratulations to Nikita Rajarwal, from team GARUDAM ( RAKSHITH VS , Vishal Dhakane, Mayura R Krishna, Aleena Manoj, Shrey Srivastva) at the Indian Institute of Technology Jammu, Jammu and Kashmir from the Department of Chemical Engineering. She received an invitation from Rashtrapati Bhavan to 'At Home' on the occasion of Independence Day on the 15th of August 2025. Last year this team participated in Smart India Hackathon (SIH) Hardware edition 2024. While participating with great enthusiasm, their team named 'GARUDAM' won the first place in the competition, under the able mentorship of Dr. Ashutosh Yadav, Assistant Professor, Department of Chemical Engineering, IIT Jammu.

The problem statement is given by the Ministry of Power, stated as " Reducing the carbon emissions from thermal power plants, developing ways of carbon capture and utilization through value-added products".

Miss. Nikita Rajarwal got the opportunity to represent the hard work of this team and also the institute on the national level on the auspicious occasion of the Independence Day,15th of August 2025.

nodes.summary: A moment of pride for IIT Jammu! Heartiest congratulations to Sumit, a PhD scholar in the Department of Civil Engineering, for winning the Best Poster Award at the prestigious 10th International Conference on Concrete under SEvere Conditions -…

nodes.title: IIT Jammu Celebrates Sumit's Achievement at CONSEC24!
nodes.summary: A moment of pride for IIT Jammu! This achievement reflects not only Sumit's dedication and research excellence but also the supportive environment fostered by the Department of Civil Engineering at IIT Jammu.
nodes.text:
A moment of pride for IIT Jammu!

Heartiest congratulations to Sumit, a PhD scholar in the Department of Civil Engineering, for winning the Best Poster Award at the prestigious 10th International Conference on Concrete under SEvere Conditions - Environment and Loading (CONSEC24)!

This A+ category international conference, held for the first time in India at IIT Madras, brought together over 400 researchers, including renowned plenary and keynote speakers, for vibrant discussions on structural and material technologies for construction and conservation.

Sumit's poster, titled "Effect of Notch Length on the Fracture Behaviour of Geopolymer Concrete Beams," impressed the judges, showcasing his impactful research on this innovative and sustainable construction material.

This achievement reflects not only Sumit's dedication and research excellence but also the supportive environment fostered by the Department of Civil Engineering at IIT Jammu. We are immensely proud of Sumit and his supervisor, Dr. Pervaiz Fathima K.M., and extend our best wishes for his future endeavors!

nodes.title: Celebrating Excellence: IIT Jammu at an International Symposium, a HATTRICK of Honours for the E2S2M (Environment Ecology Sustainable Solution and Management) Lab group.
nodes.summary: We are thrilled to share that three of our talented students have made us proud at the prestigious International Symposium cum SPARC workshop on Environmental Engineering and Sustainability (SPARC-EES-2024) held on 23rd and 24th November 2024, at…
nodes.text: We are thrilled to share that three of our talented students have made us proud at the prestigious International Symposium cum SPARC workshop on Environmental Engineering and Sustainability (SPARC-EES-2024) held on 23rd and 24th November 2024, at the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) BHU in collaboration with York University, Toronto, Canada. Their exceptional poster presentations earned them well-deserved accolades, with Mr. Waseem Raja securing the First Position, Ms. Ubhat Ali the Second Position, and Mr. Akhil Gupta felicitated with outstanding effort in poster-making among a highly competitive field of participants. All of the students are working in wastewater engineering and environmental biotechnology under the mentorship of Dr. Pratik Kumar, Assistant Professor at IIT Jammu. This remarkable achievement reflects the collaborative spirit and academic excellence that define our lab group and the institution.

nodes.title: Team from IIT Jammu has secured the First Runner-Up position at the prestigious Hackathon on Carbon Capture Technology 2025
nodes.summary: Proud to share that a team from IIT Jammu has secured the First Runner-Up position at the prestigious Hackathon on Carbon Capture Technology 2025, organized by CMPDI under the Ministry of Coal. Ravi Kumar Arun, Assistant Professor, Department of…
nodes.text:
Proud to share that a team from IIT Jammu has secured the First Runner-Up position at the prestigious Hackathon on Carbon Capture Technology 2025, organized by CMPDI under the Ministry of Coal.

The team comprised: -
 - Dr. Ravi Kumar Arun, Assistant Professor, Department of Chemical Engineering, IIT Jammu
- Keshav Dubey, Research Assistant, OPX Ai
- Mayura Krishna R, M.Tech Chemical Engineering, IIT Jammu

They presented an innovative conceptual solution on Direct Air Capture of Greenhouse Gases, in collaboration with OPX Ai Pvt. Ltd., Canada, bringing global expertise and translational potential.

The recognition included a cash prize of ₹3 lakh, awarded by Shri G. Krishna Reddy, Hon’ble Union Minister of Coal & Mines, and Shri Satish Chandra Dubey, Hon’ble Minister of State for Coal & Mines.

This milestone marks IIT Jammu’s commitment to impactful research, innovation, and sustainability for a greener future.

nodes.title: Team E2S2M from IIT Jammu has been selected among the top innovators
nodes.summary: We are happy to announce that Team E2S2M from IIT Jammu has been selected among the top innovators in the prestigious Ideas to Impact (i2I) Challenge, an initiative by IIT Madras and Wipro Foundation! As part of the program, the team will receive…
nodes.text:
We are happy to announce that Team E2S2M from IIT Jammu has been selected among the top innovators in the prestigious Ideas to Impact (i2I) Challenge, an initiative by IIT Madras and Wipro Foundation!

Their project, Decentralized Wastewater Treatment for Individual Household & Community Latrines (IHHCLs), aims to provide a sustainable sanitation solution tailored for households and community latrines. As part of the program, the team will receive funding of up to ₹50,000, mentorship, and training in business model development and customer discovery.

Led by PhD scholars Waseem Raja, Qamaruz Zaman, and Akhil Gupta, and mentored by Dr. Pratik Kumar, the team brings expertise in wastewater treatment, culture development, advanced filtration, and by-product recovery. Their innovation aligns with India’s sustainability goals and circular economy principles, reinforcing IIT Jammu’s commitment to environmental innovation and impactful research.

nodes.title: Mr. Chandra Prakash Singh, Ph.D. Research Scholar recently gave an oral presentation at the 25th NUSOD 2025
nodes.summary: Kankat Ghosh, recently gave an oral presentation at the 25th International Conference on Numerical Simulation of Optoelectronic Devices (NUSOD 2025) held at Łódź University of Technology, Poland from 14–18 September 2025. This recognition highlights…
nodes.text:
Mr. Chandra Prakash Singh, Ph.D. Research Scholar in the Department of Electrical Engineering at IIT Jammu, working under the supervision of Dr. Kankat Ghosh, recently gave an oral presentation at the 25th International Conference on Numerical Simulation of Optoelectronic Devices (NUSOD 2025) held at Łódź University of Technology, Poland from 14–18 September 2025.

His talk, titled “Mg–Si Doped Barriers for Polarization Field Screening in High-Power InGaN/GaN Green LEDs”, was selected by the International Program Committee and recognized among the winning entries for complimentary participation. His participation was fully supported under the Excellent Science II program of the Polish Ministry of Science and Higher Education.

This recognition highlights IIT Jammu’s contribution to cutting-edge research in III-nitride optoelectronic devices on an international platform.

nodes.text:
Topic: ISB's YLP Info Session at IIT Jammu

Many of the students might be willing to go for MBA after completing their degree. This session will intimate and appraise the students about the YLP entry route to the MBA program in India offered by ISB which is also ranked as No. 4 in Asia and No. 32 globally (as per the Financial Times 2022 Global MBA Rankings). It will also help them to fathom out the following :
 - Qualities that top B schools look for in a candidate
- What are the factors that count in a quality profile
- Things to keep in mind while filling out their applications for Top B Schools in the world
- The nuances of writing a good essay
- Importance of preparing for the final interviews.

The students can also ask questions to understand the concepts in a better way. Overall, it will give an idea to the students as to how they can bolster their profiles for the best B Schools.

nodes.title: NIRF 2023: IIT Jammu becomes eligible for the first time, secures 67th rank
nodes.summary: The Indian Institute of Technology Jammu (IIT Jammu) becomes eligible for the esteemed NIRF rankings for the first time, and achieves a notable milestone by securing the 67th position in the Engineering Category, thereby solidifying its position…
nodes.text:
The Indian Institute of Technology Jammu (IIT Jammu) becomes eligible for the esteemed NIRF rankings for the first time, and achieves a notable milestone by securing the 67th position in the Engineering Category, thereby solidifying its position among the top two 3G IITs.

The National Institutional Ranking Framework (NIRF) 2023 was approved by the Ministry of Education (MoE) and launched by the Honourable Minister of Education on 5th June 2023.

This framework outlines a methodology to rank institutions across the country. The methodology draws from the overall recommendations broad understanding arrived at by a Core Committee set up by MoE, to identify the broad parameters for ranking various universities and institutions. The parameters broadly cover “Teaching, Learning and Resources,” “Research and Professional Practices,” “Graduation Outcomes,” “Outreach and Inclusivity,” and “Perception”.

nodes.summary: Indian Institute of Technology Jammu was honored for its excellent execution of the Official Language Policy of the Government of India during the half-yearly meeting of the Town Official Language Implementation Committee Jammu held on 27th November…
nodes.text:
Indian Institute of Technology Jammu was honored for its excellent execution of the Official Language Policy of the Government of India during the half-yearly meeting of the Town Official Language Implementation Committee Jammu held on 27th November 2024 at the Chairing Office of the Town Official Language Implementation Committee Jammu, Council of Scientific and Industrial Research, Indian Institute of Integrative Medicine Jammu.

During the program, Director CSIR -IIIM Jammu, Dr. Zabir Ahmed, Assistant Director (Tpy./Tr.) of Hindi Teaching Scheme Institute, Jammu, Mr. Santosh Kumar, Regional Passport Officer Mr. Rajesh Kumar and Deputy Director General of National Statistics Office Mr. Palanivel were present. Official Language Nodal Officer Dr. Srishilan C collected this honor on behalf of the Institute.

nodes.title: Chandranshu Gupta and Rina Mishra from IIT Jammu has emerged victorious in Stage 1 (Ideation) and Stage 2 (MVP) of the Cyber Security Grand Challenge 2.0
nodes.summary: Gaurav Varshney, our bright minds Chandranshu Gupta and Rina Mishra from IIT Jammu has emerged victorious in Stage 1 (Ideation) and Stage 2 (MVP) of the Cyber Security Grand Challenge 2.0, sponsored by MEITY. At IIT Jammu, we foster a culture of…
nodes.text:
We are proud to share that under the guidance of our faculty, Dr. Gaurav Varshney, our bright minds Chandranshu Gupta and Rina Mishra from IIT Jammu has emerged victorious in Stage 1 (Ideation) and Stage 2 (MVP) of the Cyber Security Grand Challenge 2.0, sponsored by MEITY.

Currently advancing into the Productization Stage, the team is developing a secure hardware fingerprinting–based authentication subsystem to uniquely identify IoT/Smart devices and enable lightweight end-to-end encryption—paving the way for applications across defence and commercial sectors.

At IIT Jammu, we foster a culture of talent, openness, and collaboration, empowering our students and scholars to innovate fearlessly and make the Institute shine through such groundbreaking achievements.

Topic: Current Corporate Scenario: Strategies for Students

His Profile: Mr. Swetank Jain is a business and thought leader, passionate about building brands that consumers love. Swetank leads Praxis Home Retail Ltd, which operates a chain of large format home retail stores across 25 cities in India with the brand name HomeTown. In his 19 years of professional experience as a leader, Swetank believes in challenging set norms, creating his own path, and setting trends that are followed by others. He believes in the power of human interactions, transparent communication, and building agile organizations based on values that deliver profitability. Driven by passion, positivity, and data, Swetank has always worked with an entrepreneurial zeal in all his roles.

nodes.title: Rise and Shine: Motivating Young Minds
nodes.summary: On 24th January 2026, students from IIT Jammu visited Hee Maa Public School under “Rise and Shine: Motivating Young Minds,” a social outreach initiative by Kritash – the Social Welfare Club of IIT Jammu. Through engaging interactions with over 150…
nodes.text:
On 24th January 2026, students from IIT Jammu visited Hee Maa Public School under “Rise and Shine: Motivating Young Minds,” a social outreach initiative by Kritash – the Social Welfare Club of IIT Jammu.

Through engaging interactions with over 150 students from Classes 6 to 12, the session focused on academic guidance, exam preparedness, and building confidence for the future. While senior students received support on board exam strategies and concept clarity, younger students were encouraged to dream big, set goals, and develop a positive approach toward learning.

For our students, the experience was deeply fulfilling — a moment to give back, reconnect with their own journeys, and understand the impact of sharing knowledge beyond classrooms.

At IIT Jammu, learning goes hand in hand with responsibility — nurturing not just engineers, but empathetic changemakers.

nodes.title: EUSIPCO 2023: Ritujoy gets shortlisted for 3 MT finale
nodes.summary: Mr Ritujoy Biswas, a PhD student at IIT Jammu, working under the guidance of Dr Karan Nathwani, has been shortlisted for the final round of the 'Three Minute Thesis' [3MT] contest, being conducted by EUSIPCO 2023, a premier conference organized by…
nodes.text:
Mr Ritujoy Biswas, a PhD student at IIT Jammu, working under the guidance of Dr Karan Nathwani, has been shortlisted for the final round of the 'Three Minute Thesis' [3MT] contest, being conducted by EUSIPCO 2023, a premier conference organized by the European Association for Signal Processing.

Additionally, his research paper titled “Near-End Intelligibility Improvement through Voice Transformation in Transfer Learning Framework” has also been accepted at the same conference, for which he is planning to visit Helsinki, Finland.

Mr Biswas is working on techniques to improve speech intelligibility in noisy environments by designing optimal transformation functions that perform such speech modifications. He is also working on formulating different methods of transfer learning that are able to adapt the generated functions to varying ambient conditions.

He joined Accenture 21 years back and has played multiple roles and in his current role he is the SAP Chief Technology and Innovation officer. Prior to Accenture he worked in the strategy and finance areas with JK Industries where he brought in technology to help enable the value chain. He is based out of Delhi NCR and his weekend passion is wildlife photography.

nodes.title: Dr Shiva Sekar Co-Authors a Wiley Book: Additive Manufacturing Technologies
nodes.summary: Dr Shiva Sekar, Mechanical Engineering Department, has co-authored a Wiley book titled "Additive Manufacturing Technologies" along with Dr Anuj Kumar Shukla, National Institute of Technology Raipur. This book's matter ranges from theoretical…
nodes.text:
Dr Shiva Sekar, Mechanical Engineering Department, has co-authored a Wiley book titled "Additive Manufacturing Technologies" along with Dr Anuj Kumar Shukla, National Institute of Technology Raipur.

This book's matter ranges from theoretical concepts and principles to the applications of additive manufacturing and 3D printing. Its main objective is to disseminate knowledge about designing and creating products from an industry point of view. It will be an excellent resource for undergraduate and postgraduate students in mechanical, manufacturing, and production engineering; researchers and various industry professionals can also use it as a reference source.

To purchase the book, please follow the link: https://shorturl.at/kP078

nodes.title: Meaningful day at the Home for the Aged & Infirm
nodes.summary: On the eve of Republic Day, students from IIT Jammu, through Kritash, spent a meaningful day at the Home for the Aged & Infirm, Ambphalla. Ehsaas was a gentle yet powerful reminder that social responsibility begins with presence — with listening,…
nodes.text:
On the eve of Republic Day, students from IIT Jammu, through Kritash, spent a meaningful day at the Home for the Aged & Infirm, Ambphalla. What unfolded was not just a visit, but a shared space of warmth, stories, smiles, and quiet reflection.

Through simple conversations, cultural performances, and unspoken understanding, students connected with the elderly residents — listening to their journeys, learning from their resilience, and honoring the wisdom that time carries. Each interaction reminded us that dignity, compassion, and gratitude are values that bind generations together.

Ehsaas was a gentle yet powerful reminder that social responsibility begins with presence — with listening, caring, and showing up for those who have shaped the world we live in today.

nodes.title: About Us
nodes.summary: However, by seeking counseling services, we can address these overlooked aspects of our well-being. In one-on-one sessions, counselors provide guidance to explore and express feelings, challenge beliefs, evaluate behaviors, and work towards positive…
nodes.text:
In our journey through life, we are constantly striving to turn our personal and professional aspirations into reality. Amidst the pressure to excel, we often face emotional and psychological challenges that disturb our peace of mind. 
However, by seeking counseling services, we can address these overlooked aspects of our well-being. The center comprises a team of professional counselors and empathetic student coordinators who can assist you academically and emotionally. Through counseling, you can learn to make better choices, enhance interpersonal skills, build confidence, and improve your educational effectiveness. In one-on-one sessions, counselors provide guidance to explore and express feelings, challenge beliefs, evaluate behaviors, and work towards positive changes.

nodes.title: Nageshwar kumar and ChaitanyaTandon have developed a groundbreaking glove
nodes.summary: We are thrilled to announce that three of our institution's students have emerged as winners of the Invention Factory 2024 Program at the Indian Institute of Technology Gandhinagar Nageshwar kumar and Chaitanya Tandon from the Indian Institute of…
nodes.text:
We are thrilled to announce that three of our institution's students have emerged as winners of the Invention Factory 2024 Program at the Indian Institute of Technology Gandhinagar

Nageshwar kumar and Chaitanya Tandon from the Indian Institute of Technology Jammu have developed a groundbreaking glove that enables real-time communication for deafblind individuals.

Kritika Patidar from the Indian Institute of Technology Jammu and Stavan Mehta from the Indian Institute of Technology, Bombay have addressed complications in tooth extraction with their innovative device that uses vibrations to selectively break the periodontal ligament.

Congratulations to these brilliant minds for their remarkable achievements!

nodes.title: Ms Sheetal Ranaut, a PhD scholar under the supervision of Dr Shivnath Mazumder, delivered an invited talk
nodes.summary: Ms Sheetal Ranaut, a PhD scholar under the supervision of Dr Shivnath Mazumder, delivered an invited talk at the prestigious international conference: Sustainable Development in Chemical and Material Sciences held at Central University of Jammu from…
nodes.text:
Celebrating Academic Achievement from the Chemistry Department, IIT Jammu!

Ms Sheetal Ranaut, a PhD scholar under the supervision of Dr Shivnath Mazumder, delivered an invited talk at the prestigious international conference: Sustainable Development in Chemical and Material Sciences held at Central University of Jammu from 4th - 5th April 2024.

Ms Ranaut gave a talk on "Solvent-Dependent Regiocontrol in Oxypalladation-Initiated Cascades for Divergent Synthesis of Fused Heterocycles: Computational Prediction and Experimental Verification" to an audience comprising esteemed national and international faculty; her work was well received by chemical societies around the globe.

nodes.title: GyanSetu: Bridging Knowledge and Classroom Excellence
nodes.summary: The Teaching Learning Unit (TLU) at IIT Jammu, in academic collaboration with the CERTEx Department, IIT Kanpur, hosted the one-day pedagogy workshop “GyanSetu: Bridging Knowledge and Classroom Excellence” on 10 January 2026. Through interactive…
nodes.text:
The Teaching Learning Unit (TLU) at IIT Jammu, in academic collaboration with the CERTEx Department, IIT Kanpur, hosted the one-day pedagogy workshop “GyanSetu: Bridging Knowledge and Classroom Excellence” on 10 January 2026.

The workshop empowered Teaching Assistants and PhD scholars by strengthening key competencies essential for academic and research careers — including effective teaching methodologies, communication skills, assessment practices, and understanding learner psychology. Through interactive sessions and expert guidance, participants explored ways to enhance classroom learning and research mentorship, reflecting IIT Jammu’s commitment to academic excellence and innovation in pedagogy.

nodes.title: Balkrishna Choubey received the prestigious International Travel Support
nodes.summary: Global Recognition for IIT Jammu Research! Congratulations to Balkrishna Choubey, PhD scholar from the Department of Electrical Engineering, IIT Jammu, for receiving the prestigious International Travel Support (ITS) from the Anusandhan National…
nodes.text:
Global Recognition for IIT Jammu Research!

Congratulations to Balkrishna Choubey, PhD scholar from the Department of Electrical Engineering, IIT Jammu, for receiving the prestigious International Travel Support (ITS) from the Anusandhan National Research Foundation, Department of Science & Technology, Govt. of India.

He will be presenting his paper on High-Speed Self-Biased AlGaN-Based Deep UV MSM Photodetector at the International Conference on Nitride Semiconductors (ICNS-15) in Malmö, Sweden, from 6–11 July 2025.

Proud moment for IIT Jammu as we continue to foster research excellence and global academic engagement!

nodes.title: About Us
nodes.summary: In July 2018, IIT Jammu embarked upon an ambitious journey to simplify its Academic, Research, and Administrative processes (Academic Affairs, Student Affairs, HR, Accounts and Audit, R and C, Procurement, CEP, etc.). Entire workflow automation is…
nodes.text: In July 2018, IIT Jammu embarked upon an ambitious journey to simplify its Academic, Research, and Administrative processes (Academic Affairs, Student Affairs, HR, Accounts and Audit, R and C, Procurement, CEP, etc.). Within a year, we built an integrated e-Governance Solution (EGS) to cater to all the processes and workflows with a vision of an almost paperless office. Entire workflow automation is achieved through an organic, bottom-up iterative process with end-user participation.

nodes.title: Heartiest congratulations to our talented students –  Murtiza Ali, Pawan Kumar, Rantu Buragohain, and Tarun Bali
nodes.summary: Heartiest congratulations to our talented students – Murtiza Ali, Pawan Kumar, Rantu Buragohain, and Tarun Bali We are thrilled to announce their selection for the prestigious IISc-ICASSP 2025 Fellowship! This incredible achievement is a testament…
nodes.text:
Proud Moment for IIT Jammu!

Heartiest congratulations to our talented students –

Murtiza Ali, Pawan Kumar, Rantu Buragohain, and Tarun Bali

We are thrilled to announce their selection for the prestigious IISc-ICASSP 2025 Fellowship! This incredible achievement is a testament to their dedication, research excellence, and innovative mindset.

At IIT Jammu, we take immense pride in fostering bright minds who push the boundaries of knowledge and innovation. Wishing them all the success in their journey ahead!

Join us in celebrating their achievement!

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: Pankaj Chauhan’s Lab, Department of Chemistry, IIT Jammu, has been selected as a Scientist at CSIR–IICT Hyderabad. It also reflects the growing impact of IIT Jammu’s young researchers in the scientific community.

nodes.title: Dr. Yaseen Hussain, former PhD student of Dr. Pankaj Chauhan’s Lab, Department of Chemistry, IIT Jammu, has been selected as a Scientist at CSIR–IICT Hyderabad
nodes.summary: Yaseen Hussain, former PhD student of Dr. Pankaj Chauhan’s Lab, Department of Chemistry, IIT Jammu, has been selected as a Scientist at CSIR–IICT Hyderabad.
nodes.text:
We are proud to announce that Dr. Yaseen Hussain, former PhD student of Dr. Pankaj Chauhan’s Lab, Department of Chemistry, IIT Jammu, has been selected as a Scientist at CSIR–IICT Hyderabad.

This achievement is a testament to his dedication, perseverance, and passion for research. It also reflects the growing impact of IIT Jammu’s young researchers in the scientific community.

Heartiest congratulations to Dr. Hussain and our sincere appreciation to Dr. Chauhan for his mentorship and guidance.

nodes.summary: In view of COVID19 pandemic which has caused a paucity of hand-sanitizers in the market, a dedicated team of researchers from IIT Jammu have successfully produced a cost-effective hand sanitizer as per the guidelines laid down by the WHO. The team…

nodes.title: IIT Jammu Creates Hand Sanitizers
nodes.summary: In view of COVID19 pandemic which has caused a paucity of hand-sanitizers in the market, a dedicated team of researchers from IIT Jammu have successfully produced a cost-effective hand sanitizer as per the guidelines laid down by the WHO. The team…
nodes.text:
In view of COVID19 pandemic which has caused a paucity of hand-sanitizers in the market, a dedicated team of researchers from IIT Jammu have successfully produced a cost-effective hand sanitizer as per the guidelines laid down by the WHO.

The team has prepared 30 liters of hand sanitizer and has enough raw material to prepare 100 liters, if required. Dr Pankaj Chauhan, who led the team, thanked the Director IIT Jammu for his support and initiative and also thanked the team of researchers: Miss Namrata, Mr Yaseen, Mr Dipak and Miss Tamanna for their efforts and availability in the lab for the essential services.

nodes.title: Prof M T Arvind is the New Chief Innovation Officer at IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: Prof M T Arvind, Prof of Practice (Adjunct), Department of Electrical Engineering, has been appointed the Chief Innovation Officer at IIT Jammu. His engagement at IIT Jammu is with the goal of developing a setup to drive an incubation park with the…
nodes.text:
Prof M T Arvind, Prof of Practice (Adjunct), Department of Electrical Engineering, has been appointed the Chief Innovation Officer at IIT Jammu.

Co-founder of Amplebit Energy Solutions and Amplebit Technologies and CEO of Ramaiah Evolute, he has over 25 years of experience in the electrical, electronics, telecom, and energy domains, as well as in lean startup and entrepreneurship. His engagement at IIT Jammu is with the goal of developing a setup to drive an incubation park with the objective of self-sustenance.

nodes.title: Best poster award won by Subhajeet Sen
nodes.summary: Subhajeet Sen Participated in the 78th Annual Technical Meeting of the Indian Institute of Metals (IIM-ATM) conference (20th Nov - 22 Nov, 2024) held at GKVK – Gandhi Krishi Vignan Kendra, Bengaluru and presented his work on “Medium and high entropy
nodes.text:
Subhajeet Sen Participated in the 78th Annual Technical Meeting of the Indian Institute of Metals (IIM-ATM) conference (20th Nov - 22 Nov, 2024) held at GKVK – Gandhi Krishi Vignan Kendra, Bengaluru and presented his work on “Medium and high entropy rare earth oxides for photocatalytic hydrogen production”

Subhajeet Sen is a PhD student from the Materials Department and clinches the Best Poster Presentation Award at the conference.

Prof. B. S Murty, Director, Indian Institute of Technology Hyderabad presented him the award.

nodes.title: Arun Kaushal, Dr Dikshant Malhotra gets published in SAGE
nodes.summary: Many congratulations to Arun Kaushal and Dr Dikshant Malhotra, researchers from Mechanical Engineering Department, IIT Jammu, for their recent collaborative publication in the Journal of Materials: Design and Applications (SAGE Journal). The paper…
nodes.text:
Many congratulations to Arun Kaushal and Dr Dikshant Malhotra, researchers from Mechanical Engineering Department, IIT Jammu, for their recent collaborative publication in the Journal of Materials: Design and Applications (SAGE Journal).

The paper titled "Metallurgical, erosion, and sensitization performance of TiAlN monolayer and TiAlN/TiN multilayer ceramic coatings on AISI 304 boiler steel" reveals significant advancements in ceramic coatings for boiler steel.

To know more: https://bit.ly/3WXIePV

nodes.title: IIT Jammu's SAP Aerospace wins TDF DRDO's Dare to Dream 4.0
nodes.summary: SAP Aerospace, IIT Jammu's deep-tech start-up founded and mentored by Dr. P., has won the Dare to Dream 4.0 Innovation Contest organised under the TDF Scheme by DRDO, Ministry of Defence, Govt.
nodes.text:
SAP Aerospace, IIT Jammu's deep-tech start-up founded and mentored by Dr. Shanmugadas K. P., has won the Dare to Dream 4.0 Innovation Contest organised under the TDF Scheme by DRDO, Ministry of Defence, Govt. of India, to encourage startups and individuals to come up with innovative defence and aerospace technologies.

The team had participated in the open category and won support to further develop their aero-engine technology for UCAVs.

nodes.title: SAP Aerospace, a Deep-Tech Startup from IIT Jammu, Honoured at JK Youth Conclave 2024
nodes.summary: IIT Jammu's SAP Aerospace, led by Dr Shanmugadas KP, along with alumni Ayush Divyansh and Preetam Jamod, were awarded by LG Shri Manoj Sinha at JK Youth Conclave 2024 for their innovative work. Recognized for groundbreaking innovations, SAP Aerospace
nodes.text:
IIT Jammu's SAP Aerospace, led by Dr Shanmugadas KP, along with alumni Ayush Divyansh and Preetam Jamod, were awarded by LG Shri Manoj Sinha at JK Youth Conclave 2024 for their innovative work.

Recognized for groundbreaking innovations, SAP Aerospace is developing an indigenous micro gas turbine engine for Unmanned Combat Aerial Vehicles (UCAVs).

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: The Department of Humanities and Social Sciences (HSS), IIT Jammu, in collaboration with the Tribal Research Institute (TRI), J&K, organized a three-day workshop on “Tribal Praxis: Understanding Literary, Linguistic and Socio-cultural practices of…

nodes.title: 3-Day Tribal Workshop Organised by IIT Jammu and TRI, J&K
nodes.summary: The Department of Humanities and Social Sciences (HSS), IIT Jammu, in collaboration with the Tribal Research Institute (TRI), J&K, organized a three-day workshop on “Tribal Praxis: Understanding Literary, Linguistic and Socio-cultural practices of…
nodes.text:
The Department of Humanities and Social Sciences (HSS), IIT Jammu, in collaboration with
 the Tribal Research Institute (TRI), J&K, organized a three-day workshop on
 “Tribal Praxis: Understanding Literary, Linguistic and Socio-cultural practices of
 the tribal communities of J&K” from 4th-6th July, 2022. The main objective of
 the workshop was to provide a platform for assimilation of ideas, theory and practice
 while developing a roadmap for fruitful collaboration between IIT Jammu and TRI,
 J&K. The Inaugural session to the three day workshop deliberated on the action
 points of the MoU between IIT Jammu and TRI, J&K, Tribal chair, and the targets and
 deliverables of the Tribal Project.

The welcome address to the inaugural session was delivered by Dr Amitash Ojha, Head of
 the Department (HSS, IIT Jammu), wherein he highlighted the importance of the
 collaboration between IIT Jammu, and TRI, J&K and the establishment of a Tribal
 Chair with the goals of comprehensively gathering tribal data, transcreating,
 digitizing, disseminating, and studying the tribal culture from a research-oriented
 perspective through workshops, conferences, field trips, seminars, cultural programmes,
 and exhibitions.

Dr Quleen Kaur Bijral, Assistant Professor & FIC, Oral Narrative Center
 (Zuban-e-Kalam) HSS, IIT jammu, elucidated the theme of the workshop as how to achieve
 its targets, and provide tangible and satisfactory outcomes. Dr Quleen highlighted that
 before the initiation of any project, planning should be of primary focus. This is
 wherein the hindsight advantage of experience of the session experts can be an
 eye-opener to foster an interactive confluence of ideas enabling one to learn, reinforce
 and find the best course of action.

Professor Manoj Singh Gaur, Director, IIT Jammu talked about the vision of IIT Jammu in
 trend-setting research and the significance of the MoU with the Tribal Research
 Institute in taking it a step further. He further emphasized that IIT Jammu's
 mission has been to meet the diverse demands of various sectors from entrepreneurship,
 cultural diversity, artisanship, arts and craft of J&K, including notably the young
 brigade of students from various locales of the region and to fulfill the responsibility
 to develop strategies to strengthen the regional diversity of J&K.

Dr Javaid Rahi, Chief Editor, J&K Academy of Art and Culture, painted a vibrant
 picture of the culture and essence of the indigenous tribes of Jammu and Kashmir through
 his words. He shared his rich experience of working with the two prominent tribal
 communities of Gujjars and Bakerwals of J&K and the uniqueness of their
 Socio-economic structure.

Dr Abdul Khabir, Deputy Director, Tribal Affairs Department and Nodal Officer of the
 Tribal Research Institute eloquently summarized the very heart of this collaboration in
 the sense that both IIT Jammu and TRI are premier institutions and share the same spirit
 of research and service. Dr Khabir empha
