nodes.summary: HSS Organises 3 days Workshop on Mind and Ethics Dept. of Humanities and Social Sciences, Indian Institute of Technology Jammu organized a 3 - days Workshop on “ Mind and Ethics”” for the students of the institute.

nodes.title: Mind and Ethics
nodes.summary: HSS Organises 3 days Workshop on Mind and Ethics Dept. of Humanities and Social Sciences, Indian Institute of Technology Jammu organized a 3 - days Workshop on “ Mind and Ethics”” for the students of the institute.
nodes.text:
HSS Organises 3 days Workshop on Mind and Ethics

Dept. of Humanities and Social Sciences, Indian Institute of Technology Jammu organized a
 3 - days Workshop on “ Mind and Ethics”” for the students of the
 institute. The program was curated to foster understanding of Mental health and Ethics
 among the students keeping in view the emerging rate of mental health problems in IITs .

The program was coordinated by Dr. Suchismita Roy, Visiting Faculty, Sociology, HSS. In
 the welcome address, Dr. Roy highlighted the schedule with emphasis on the relevance as
 well as the proposed goal of the workshop. Goals of the workshop were to articulate
 rationales for integrating ethics in participants, professional and social life - what
 to consider before making decisions and principles of justice, how to handle stress,
 anxiety, substance abuse and how to be empathetic, introduce mental health awareness
 while addressing the stigma on mental health issues and awareness about relevant social
 legislation related to confidentiality, plagiarism, ethical consent, women cell.

On the First day, Dr. Abhiruchi Chatterjee, the eminent community psychiatrist, initiated
 the workshop with an interactive session discussing myths around mental health and how
 to handle stress.The second day, Mr. SS Sharma, Asst Professor of SMVDU, focused on how
 to handle ethical dilemmas with a crisp introduction on ethics and critical thinking.

Prof Renuka Devi, IIM Jammu, took a session on ethics in the organisation. In this
 session, students learned different concepts of business ethics ,do’s and
 don'ts in the organisation. She also discussed Indian perspective of ethics. On the
 last day Dr. Bhaskar Mukherjee, psychiatrist and neuroscientist explored the
 relationship between neuro-science and engineering , and how the brain works and recent
 developments in brain research.

The concluding session of the workshop was conducted by Advocate Shakeel Ahmed, who
 introduced relevant social legislation to the student in simple language. He started his
 session with the quotes of the famous poet Ghalib.

Workshop Sessions were chaired by Dr. Gaurav A Bhaduri, Dr. Bhumika R, Ms Ishita Mahajan,
 Dr. Sukanya Mandal and Dr Quleen Kaur Bijral, H.o.D, HSS. The speakers were presented
 with mementos. More than 85 % of participants said that they would like to recommend
 this course to others. Students felt this course highlighted some important aspects
 which they don't learn in regular classes of academics. Students appreciated new
 concepts, open discussion about mental health issues , friendly interaction,real life
 examples and cases used in the workshop. The event was concluded with vote of thanks by
 Dr Suchismita Roy. Special thanks to Psychologist Ms Akansha Joshi, Dr. Joby, and Mr
 Shalu, Mr Abhishek Caretaker, Mr Sunil Jamwal.

Date: Monday, December 2, 2019

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: Jammu, [09.02.2024] —Center of Essential Skills and Zuban-e-Kalam HSS, IIT Jammu in collaboration with The Kala Kendra Society, Jammu, successfully inaugurated a cultural event, "Understanding Traditional Art of Jammu," a vibrant celebration aimed…

nodes.title: A Cultural Confluence: Celebrating Traditional Art of Jammu at IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: Jammu, [09.02.2024] —Center of Essential Skills and Zuban-e-Kalam HSS, IIT Jammu in collaboration with The Kala Kendra Society, Jammu, successfully inaugurated a cultural event, "Understanding Traditional Art of Jammu," a vibrant celebration aimed…
nodes.text:
Jammu, [09.02.2024] —Center of Essential Skills and Zuban-e-Kalam HSS, IIT Jammu in
 collaboration with The Kala Kendra Society, Jammu, successfully inaugurated a cultural
 event, "Understanding Traditional Art of Jammu," a vibrant celebration aimed
 at exploring and honoring the rich cultural heritage of the Jammu region. This unique
 gathering was graced by the presence of Prof. Manoj Singh Gaur, IIT Jammu, including
 esteemed Chief Guest, Sh. Suresh Kumar Gupta, IFS, Principal Secretary to the
 Government, Department of Culture, and Dr. Javaid Rahi, Chief Editor, Gojri Department
 and Secretary of Kala Kendra, among others.

The event was set in motion with a heartfelt welcome by Dr. Quleen kaur Bijral, faculty
 and coordinator, Zuban-e-Kalam HSS, IIT Jammu in which she highlighted the importance of
 cultural interactions to boost the wholesome development of a student from academics,
 research, and re-discovery of regional roots and identity through art. Dr. Amitash Ojha,
 faculty and coordinator of CES highlighted the need for such activities designed to
 spotlight the traditional art forms that form the essence of Jammu's cultural
 identity. Dr. Rahi Ji introduced, the resource person of the event, Ms.Medhavi Sharma,
 setting the stage for her to share insights into her creative journey and the
 inspiration behind her art.

Ms. Medhavi Sharma is a distinguished artist hailing from the quaint town of Udhampur in
 Jammu and Kashmir. Through her initiative called ‘Parolaa’, she has aimed to
 explore and revive the rich local culture of the Jammu region and give it a strong
 visual vocabulary. Ms. Sharma's lecture and exhibition provided attendees with an
 immersive experience of the timeless appeal of local art forms, enriching their
 understanding and appreciation of Jammu's cultural legacy of Basholi art and Duggar
 culture.

The proceedings included a floral welcome of Sh. Suresh Kumar Gupta by the Director of
 IIT Jammu, followed by felicitations of the dignitaries in the presence of Prof. Bijoy
 H. Boruah, senior faculty at HSS, IIT Jammu. The event further engaged the audience
 through a Q&A session moderated by Sushmendra Kuril, BTech, Materials Engg offering
 an interactive platform for discussions on traditional art and its contemporary
 relevance.

The event concluded with a vote of thanks by Majid Majeed, CIO at Nucleon I3C, IIT JAMMU
 who expressed gratitude to all participants, guests, and organizers. The gathering was
 then invited for a group photo, symbolizing the unity and shared purpose among all
 present, followed by a high tea, allowing for further conversations and networking.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: Jammu, [16–2-2024] – The Indian Institute of Technology Jammu, in an initiative by the Centre for Essential Skills and HSS, has conducted a comprehensive field visit to Jambu Zoo for its students. By seeing animals in real life, students develop…

nodes.title: IIT Jammu Organizes a Field Visit to Jambu Zoo
nodes.summary: Jammu, [16–2-2024] – The Indian Institute of Technology Jammu, in an initiative by the Centre for Essential Skills and HSS, has conducted a comprehensive field visit to Jambu Zoo for its students. The visit allowed students to make connections…
nodes.text:
Jammu, [16–2-2024] – The Indian Institute of Technology Jammu, in an
 initiative by the Centre for Essential Skills and HSS, has conducted a comprehensive
 field visit to Jambu Zoo for its students. This educational excursion, meticulously
 organized by Dr. Quleen Kaur Bijral, Coordinator of Zuban-e-Kalam, HSS, and Dr. Amitash
 Ojha from the Center for Essential Skills, is designed to furnish students with a
 profound understanding of the delicate equilibrium that exists between flora, fauna, and
 human societies. The initiative aims to foster an ecocritical appreciation of wildlife,
 encouraging students to look beyond textbooks and understand the intricate connections
 in natural habitats. This field visit is part of a broader educational endeavor that
 includes heritage tours to both celebrated and lesser-known sites within Jammu and
 Kashmir, promoting the region's rich cultural diversity.

Students gained firsthand experience of ecosystem dynamics, enhancing their understanding
 of ecological balance and biodiversity. By seeing animals in real life, students develop
 empathy towards living creatures and a sense of responsibility towards environmental
 conservation and sustainable living practices.The trip offered a unique opportunity for
 students to develop essential skills such as observation, analysis, and empathy towards
 living organisms and their habitats. This initiative not only highlights IIT Jammu's
 commitment to holistic education but also underscores the importance of integrating
 practical experiences with academic learning.

The visit allowed students to make connections across various disciplines, including
 biology, environmental science, geography, and even ethics. By engaging with the
 environment around them, students are better prepared to become informed, responsible
 citizens who value and work towards a sustainable future. The tour included discussions
 on conservation efforts, the impact of human activities on wildlife, and the importance
 of protected areas like zoos in preserving endangered species.

The event saw the coordinated efforts of Dr. Nilesh, Assistant Librarian, Mr. Vishal,
 Student Welfare Office, alongside Sushmendera Kuril, Gaurav Jagarabad, and Subh Agarwal
 of IIT Jammu, ensuring a seamless and enriching experience for the participants

nodes.title: Empowering Tribal Women: A Comprehensive Analysis of Socio-Educational Status of Gujjar
nodes.summary: and Bakerwal Women in Pir Panjal Range Dr Sayantan Mandal, the co-principal investigator of the Tribal Project, along with Marve B Sofi and Sayari Mishra (PhD scholars of HSS, IIT Jammu), were responsible to examine and empirically understand the…
nodes.text:
and Bakerwal Women in Pir Panjal Range

Dr Sayantan Mandal, the co-principal investigator of the Tribal Project, along with Marve
 B Sofi and Sayari Mishra (PhD scholars of HSS, IIT Jammu), were responsible to examine
 and empirically understand the barriers to higher education and empowerment of Gujjar
 and Bakerwal women residing in the Pir Panjal Range of Jammu and Kashmir. These women
 encounter systemic discrimination, experience restricted access to education and
 healthcare and are constrained by gender roles. The main aim of this study is to gain
 insight into the socio-educational status and needs of these women while also
 identifying the primary obstacles contributing to their social, educational, and
 economic disadvantage. This study investigates the influence of cultural norms, gender
 inequality, and socio-economic factors on individuals’ lives, specifically
 examining issues such as restricted educational opportunities, limited access to
 healthcare services, disparities in technology usage, reduced political engagement, and
 diminished decision-making authority.

The study employed a mixed-method approach, incorporating interviews, focus groups,
 observations, and surveys to comprehensively comprehend the social dynamics, cultural
 practices, and gender roles within the project. The report presents a comprehensive set
 of recommendations aimed at fostering sustainable and beneficial transformations within
 the Gujjar and Bakerwal communities. The primary objective of this initiative is to
 foster enduring transformation and make a valuable contribution to the broader academic
 conversation surrounding the rights of tribal women. The achievements of the project are
 anticipated to serve as a paradigm for forthcoming endeavours and foster broader
 societal change towards the promotion of gender equality and inclusive development.

The faculty members (Dr Sayantan Mandal) and researchers (Marve B Sofi and Sayari Mishra)
 at HSS, IIT Jammu, are committed to offering additional assistance in this matter when
 required.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: - Policy brief 1 is primarily based on the research project Integrating Teaching and Learning and Digital Education (ITLDE), a large-scale, empirical, multi institutional, longitudinal study, funded by IIT Jammu. - Policy Brief 1 consists of…

nodes.title: Policy Brief 1 on Six Districts of J&K | March 2024.
nodes.summary: - The policy brief is prepared by Dr. - Policy Brief 1 consists of evidence-based policy recommendations derived from the large-scale empirical study titled ‘Integrating Teaching Learning and Digital Education’ (ITLDE) for the Higher Education…
nodes.text:
- The policy brief is prepared by Dr. Sayantan Mandal and Sheriya Sareen, Department of Humanities and Social Sciences (HSS), Indian Institute of Technology Jammu (IIT Jammu), J&K.
- Policy brief 1 is primarily based on the research project Integrating Teaching and Learning and Digital Education (ITLDE), a large-scale, empirical, multi institutional, longitudinal study, funded by IIT Jammu. The data was collected in two phases employing questionnaire-based surveys of more than 4700 students and 370 teachers, 100 focus group discussions with students, 100 interviews with teachers, 25 interviews with administrators, and 50 classroom observations in 25 higher education institutions across six districts of Jammu & Kashmir.
- Policy Brief 1 consists of evidence-based policy recommendations derived from the large-scale empirical study titled ‘Integrating Teaching Learning and Digital Education’ (ITLDE) for the Higher Education Institutions of J&K.
- The purpose of this policy brief is to inform the concerned Ministries, Policymakers, University Administrators, and Faculty members about the evidence-based, empirically grounded policy recommendations to integrate digital education with teaching and learning practices in blended spaces.
 - Download Policy Brief

nodes.title: Meenakshi Rajeev , Shafkat Dar and Karan Nathwani
nodes.summary: The PM Poshan Shakti Nirman (PM POSHAN) Scheme, earlier known as the ‘National Programme for Mid-Day Meal in Schools’ Scheme, was introduced in 2021. Meenakshi Rajeev , Dr Shafkat Dar and Dr Karan Nathwani with the support of the Directorate of PM…
nodes.text:
India has implemented various nutrition-centric and nutrition-sensitive programs to
 address malnutrition and promote better health outcomes. Pradhan Mantri Poshan Shakti
 Nirman (PM POSHAN) Scheme is one of the foremost rights-based Centrally Sponsored
 Schemes implemented in partnership with the States and UTs for providing one hot cooked
 and nutritious meal to all children up to class VIII of Government and Government-aided
 schools. The PM Poshan Shakti Nirman (PM POSHAN) Scheme, earlier known as the ‘National
 Programme for Mid-Day Meal in Schools’ Scheme, was introduced in 2021. The UT of J & K
 also adopted this scheme and reaped the benefits of the scheme. To understand the
 functioning of the scheme in the entire ten districts of Jammu region, IIT Jammu, HSS
 Department, has undertaken a social audit study led by Prof. Meenakshi Rajeev , Dr
 Shafkat Dar and Dr Karan Nathwani with the support of the Directorate of PM Poshan of
 the UT. The study covers all the ten districts of the Jammu Province. A Social Audit is
 a systematic process through which the implementation, performance, and outcomes of a
 public programme or scheme are examined with the active involvement of stakeholders.

nodes.summary: Date: Friday, September 26, 2025 The Department of Humanities & Social Sciences, IIT Jammu, successfully hosted a full-day workshop on “Mastering Academic Writing: A Workshop on Clarity, Publication, Ethics & AI Tools” on September 26, 2025, at the…

nodes.title: Mastering Academic Writing: A Workshop on Clarity, Publication, Ethics & AI Tools
nodes.summary: Date: Friday, September 26, 2025 The Department of Humanities & Social Sciences, IIT Jammu, successfully hosted a full-day workshop on “Mastering Academic Writing: A Workshop on Clarity, Publication, Ethics & AI Tools” on September 26, 2025, at the…
nodes.text:
Date: Friday, September 26, 2025

The Department of Humanities & Social Sciences, IIT
 Jammu, successfully hosted a full-day workshop on “Mastering Academic Writing: A
 Workshop on Clarity, Publication, Ethics & AI Tools” on September 26, 2025, at
 the Jagti Campus.

The event began with motivating opening remarks by Dr.
 Sartaj Ul Hasan (Dean, Administrative Affairs) and Dr. Meenakshi Rajeev (Dean, Skill
 Development and Outreach), setting an inspiring tone for the day.

The sessions featured distinguished speakers who shared
 their expertise on various dimensions of academic writing:
 - Prof. Lilavati Krishnan, IIT Kanpur – Navigating the Publication Process
- Dr. Isha Malhotra, SMVDU – Mastering Academic Writing Skills
- Dr. Deepti Malhotra, CU Jammu – The Role of AI Tools & Ethics in Academic
 Writing

Through interactive discussions, practical exercises, and
 real-world insights, participants explored the essentials of clarity, publication
 ethics, and the evolving role of AI in research writing.

This workshop not only enhanced participants’ academic
 writing skills but also fostered a culture of collaboration, learning, and innovation at
 IIT Jammu.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: Led by Dr Sukanya Mondal, the project is funded by IIT Jammu and is scheduled to be completed by 2027. The aim of this project is to examine the changes which the women's quarter in 19th Century Bengal underwent as an effect of the rapidly evolving…

nodes.title: Locating Feminist Counterpublics in 19th Century Bengal
nodes.summary: Led by Dr Sukanya Mondal, the project is funded by IIT Jammu and is scheduled to be completed by 2027. The aim of this project is to examine the changes which the women's quarter in 19th Century Bengal underwent as an effect of the rapidly evolving…
nodes.text:
Led by Dr Sukanya Mondal, the project is funded by IIT Jammu and is scheduled to be
 completed by 2027.

The aim of this project is to examine the changes which the women's quarter in 19th
 Century Bengal underwent as an effect of the rapidly evolving public sphere, literacy
 drive, and various other social-political reformations. The so-called public sphere
 (public meetings, lectures, etc., even the parlours of well-to-do families) was almost
 men-only spaces.

Therefore, this project is trying to locate the spaces or situations when women met to
 exchange ideas related to contemporary society, politics, etc. The theoretical point of
 departure for this study is Nancy Fraser's critique of Jurgen Habermas' idea of
 the Public Sphere.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: The inaugural ceremony was graced by the esteemed presence of Prof Manoj Singh Gaur, Director at IIT Jammu. It culminated in a final and remarkable session at IIT Jammu.
nodes.text:
This engaging program saw active participation from numerous schools and local colleges in Jammu, including Jawahar Navodaya Vidyalaya Gharota and Government College for Women Gandhinagar, with over 120 enthusiastic students in attendance. The inaugural ceremony was graced by the esteemed presence of Prof Manoj Singh Gaur, Director at IIT Jammu. Accompanying him were distinguished scientists from BARC, including Dr Archana Sharma, Director of Beam Technology Development; Mr S. Prabhakar, specializing in Food Irradiation Technology; Mr Sameer Mandal, expert in Nuclear Reactor technology; Mr R. K. B. Yadav, specialist in Radiation safety in health & environment; Mrs Herman Rao, Mr R. K. Mishra, and Mr Yatin Thakur, too, were present.

The IIT Jammu team was equally well-represented by Prof Kannan Iyer (Dean of Academics), Dr Vijayan Pallipattu (Dean of Administration), and Prof Debapriya Mondal. Prof Yogesh Nimdeo, the Head of the Chemical Engineering Department, also actively participated in this enlightening program. Furthermore, other institutions contributed to the success of the event, with Dr B. D. Sehgal (GWC) and Mr Awadhesh Kumar (JNVG) and their colleagues actively engaged with the attendees.

The Scientific and Technical Outreach Program, organized by the Public Awareness Section of the Scientific Information Resource Division at BARC, extended over a week, from the 9th to the 13th of October, 2023, across five colleges in Jammu. It culminated in a final and remarkable session at IIT Jammu. The overarching objectives of the program were threefold:
 - To dispel myths and misconceptions about atomic energy and radiation.
- To acquaint students and the general public with the functioning of reactors and BARC technological innovations, with a focus on Food irradiation.
- To enlighten students about the potential career opportunities in the nuclear sector and the groundbreaking innovations within it.

Eminent scientists conducted extensive discussions and interactions with students and faculty, complemented by two captivating skits on nuclear energy and BARC Technologies.

The program saw the active involvement of numerous specialists from various BARC departments, including all the aforementioned scientists, as well as technical staff members such as Mr Ram Badade, Mr Atul Likhite, Mr Umesh Gulhane, Mr Pradeep Pawar, and Mrs Samruddhi Patil. Their informative lectures and engaging skits left an indelible impact on the participants.

The response from the students who attended the workshop and participated in the quiz sessions was overwhelmingly positive and encouraging. Their enthusiasm has generated numerous requests for more such programs, reaffirming the success of this outreach initiative.

  Programme Structure and Highlights:  


	 The M.Sc.  MS (R) Chemistry course in Chemistry will provide with the necessary fundamental knowledge in both theory and laboratory practical levels followed by cutting edge and related inter-disciplinary areas of research. 
	 M.Sc. Chemistry is a 2-year masters program (77 credits) and MS (R) Chemistry program (107 credits) will be an option for up to five students of the class of M.Sc. Chemistry batch. 


  Essential Criteria:  


	 For B.Sc./B.S Students, chemistry for at least two years/four semesters 
	 B.Tech/B.E students with any specialization with four core courses in chemistry 


  To apply for spot admission, the candidates must:  


	 Qualify JAM-2022 
	 Submit JAM-2022 undertaking form 
	 Not have paid the seat booking fee to any of the institutes participated in JAM-2022 counselling 
	 Produce a valid reserved category certificate (where applicable) 
	 Send the following documents to the email:  hod.chemistry@iitjammu.ac.in  


	 The filed in registration form 
	 SSC/10th certificate 
	 12th certificate 
	 Bacherlor s latest marksheet 
	 JAM-2022 rank card 
	 JAM-2022 undertaking form 
	 Reserved category certificate (where applicable) 


  *** Mere registration does not guarantee admission. Admissions are subject to the availability of seats as per general and reserved category guidelines set by GoI.

	 Registration: 22nd - 28th July 2022 
	 Announcement of the selected candidates: 29th July 2022 
	 Fee payment: 3rd August 2022 
	 Reporting on Campus: 5th August 2022 
	 First Day of Classes: 8th August 2022 


  Tuition Fees (per Semester):  


	 GEN/OBC/EWS: INR 50,800 
	 SC/ST/PH: INR 30,800 


  ** Fee structure and details of payment will be communicated to the selected candidates at a later time  

 Accommodation: Currently, the institute may not be able to provide accommodation because of limited hostel facility. However, it is expected that the hostel accommodation will be provided on priority basis in the near future once available.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: This program also focuses on disseminating the knowledge on design of dams, tunnels, engineered landfills, deep excavations, and foundations for structures of all kinds. Research Areas: - Monitoring and mapping of landslides in the Jammu region -…
nodes.text:
The Geotechnical Engineering program at Indian Institute of Technology, Jammu, aims to educate and conduct research in the behaviour and use of soil and rock, along with other engineered and naturally available materials such as steel and geosynthetics which are used to improve their properties. This program also focuses on disseminating the knowledge on design of dams, tunnels, engineered landfills, deep excavations, and foundations for structures of all kinds.

Research Areas:
 - Monitoring and mapping of landslides in the Jammu region
- Protection works against landslides (debris flow/rockfall/mudslides) in hilly regions
- Geosynthetic solutions to stabilize landfill cover systems on slopes
- Feasibility of using low-quality soils as backfill material in reinforced slopes
- Unsaturated Soils
- Ground Engineering
- Geosynthetics
- Geoenvironmental Engineering
- Ground Improvement
- Stability of Slopes and Retaining Structures
- Application of Remote Sensing Tools for Hazard Assessment
- Application of GIS for Hazard Assessment
- Pattern Recognition and Feature Selection.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: (2) Measurable functions, simple functions, Integration of non-negative functions and integrable functions. Sheldon Axler, Measure and Integration and Real Analysis 5.
nodes.text:
(2) Measurable functions, simple functions, Integration of non-negative functions and integrable functions. The monotone and dominated convergence theorems, Fatou’s lemma, convergence in measures and Egoroff’s theorem. Characterization of Riemann and Lebesgue integrability.

(3) Product measure, Fubini-Tonelli theorem, Lebesgue integration on Rn, change of variable formula for Lebesgue integration on Rn and polar coordinates in Rn

(4) Signed measure, Hahn and Jordan decompositions, Absolute continuity, The Radon-Nikodym theorem. Monotone functions, functions of bounded variations, absolutely continuous functions and Fundamental theorem of integration for Lebesgue integrals, Lebesgue differentiation theorem and applications.

(5) Lp-spaces: basic properties, duality and convolutions, approximation theorems and applications.

(6) Convolution, Fourier transforms and Riesz representation theorem and applications.

References:

1. G.B. Folland, Real Analysis : Modern Techniques and their Applications, John-Wiley and Sons Inc., 1984.

2. H.L. Royden and P.M. Fitzpatrick, Real Analysis, Pearson publication, 4th edition, 2019.

3. W. Rudin, Real and Complex Analysis, Tata McGraw-Hill, 1974.

4. Sheldon Axler, Measure and Integration and Real Analysis

5. E. Stein, Rami Shakarchi, Real Analysis: Measure Theory, Integration and Hilbert Spaces, Princeton Lecture Notes in Analysis, Princeton University Space 2005.

6. S. Kesavan, Measure and Integration, TRIM 77, Hindustan Book Agency, 2019.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: Rudin, Principles of Real Analysis, McGraw-Hill, 1976. Rudin, Real and Complex Analysis, Tata McGraw-Hill, 2006.
nodes.text:
ii) Single and Multiple Riemann integrals, parametric surfaces, tangent plane, surface measure, surface integrals, Green’s and Gauss divergence theorems.

iii) sigma-algebra, measures and measurable sets, Borel measure. Measurable functions, simple functions, Integration of non-negative function, Mode’s of convergence, Product measure, Egoroff’s theorem.

iv) Lebesgue integral and its properties, monotone convergence theorem, Fatou’s Lemma, dominated convergence theorem, different modes of convergence and their relations, signed and complex measures, Lebesgue-Radon-Nikodym Theorem, Lebesgue-differentiation theorem, Functions of bounded variation and absolutely continuous function.

v) Elements of functional analysis: normed linear spaces, linear functionals, Bair-category theorem and its consequences, Hilbert spaces.

vi) Lp-spaces: The Minkowski’s and Holder’s inequalities, completeness of Lp, denseness results in Lp.

Text and References

(1) T. M. Apostol, Mathematical Analysis, Addison-Wesley, 1981.

(2) G.B. Folland, Real Analysis, Modern Techniques and Their Applications Second edition, A. Wiley Interscience Publication, 2007.

(3) W. Rudin, Principles of Real Analysis, McGraw-Hill, 1976.

(4) W. Rudin, Real and Complex Analysis, Tata McGraw-Hill, 2006.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: (4) Compact operators: General results on compact operators, Fredholm Alternative and Riesz-Schauder theory of compact operators. Kesavan, Functional analysis, Hindustan Book Agency, 2009.
nodes.text:
(2) The uniform boundedness principle. The open mapping and closed graph theorems and their applications.

(3) Definition and examples of Hilbert spaces, The Riesz representation theorem and its applications to variational inequalities. Projection theorem and orthonormal basis.

(4) Compact operators: General results on compact operators, Fredholm Alternative and Riesz-Schauder theory of compact operators. Compact self adjoint operators on Hilbert spaces. Weak convergence and compact operators.

References:

1. T. Nair, Functional Analysis: A First Course, Prentice Hall of India, 2008.

2. S. Kesavan, Functional analysis, Hindustan Book Agency, 2009.

3. H. L. Royden and P. M. Fitzpatrick, Real Analysis, Pearson publication, 4th ed. 2019.

4. B. V. Limaye, Functional Analysis, New Age International Pub., Reprint: 2012.

5. P.D. lax, Functional Analysis, Pure and Applied Mathematics, A Wiley-Interscience Series of Texts, Monographs, and Tracts, A John Wiley & Sons, Inc., Publication, 2002.

6. S. Kumaresan and D. Sukumar, Functional Analysis: A first course, Narosa Publishing House, 2020.

7. W. Rudin, Functional Analysis, 2nd Edition, McGraw-Hill, New York, 1991.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: Nair, Functional Analysis: A First Course, Prentice Hall of India, 2008. Kesavan, Functional analysis, Hindustan Book Agency, 2009.
nodes.text:
ii) Hilbert spaces, Dual of Hilbert spaces, Variational Inequalities, Orthonormal sets, Adjoint, Hermitian, Normal, unitary operators, Compact operators. Spectral theorem for Compact Hermitian operators.

iii) Distribution theory: test functions and distributions, Operations with distributions, Convolution of functions and distributions, Fundamental solutions, Fourier transform, Schwartz space, Tempered distributions.

iv) Sobolev spaces: definition and basic properties, approximation by smooth functions, extension theorems, compactness theorems, Sobolev inequalities, trace theory.

References

(1) P.D. Lax, Functional Analysis, Pure and Applied Mathematics, A Wiley Interscience Series of Texts, Monographs, and Tracts, A John Wiley & Sons, Inc. Publication, 2002.

(2) L.C. Evans, Partial Differential Equations, American Mathematical Society, 1998.

(3) T. Nair, Functional Analysis: A First Course, Prentice Hall of India, 2008.

(4) S. Kumaresan and D. Sukumar, Functional Analysis: A first course, Narosa Publishing House, 2020.

(5) S. Kesavan, Functional analysis, Hindustan Book Agency, 2009.

(6) S. Kesavan, Topic in Functional analysis and applications, New Age Interna-tional Publishers, 2008.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: ii) Polynomials over finite fields: Order of polynomials, Primitive polynomials, Irreducible polynomials, Construction of Irreducible polynomials, Linearized polynomials, Binomials and Trinomials. iv) Permutation Polynomials: Criteria for…
nodes.text:
ii) Polynomials over finite fields: Order of polynomials, Primitive polynomials, Irreducible polynomials, Construction of Irreducible polynomials, Linearized polynomials, Binomials and Trinomials.

iii) Exponential sums: Characters, Gauss sums, Jacobi Sums, Character sums with polynomial arguments.

iv) Permutation Polynomials: Criteria for permutation polynomials, special types of permutation polynomials, Group of permutation polynomials, Exceptional polynomials.

v) Linear Recurring Sequences: Feedback shift registers, Periodicity properties, Generating functions, The minimal polynomial, Families of linear recurring sequences, Characterization of linear recurring sequences.

Text Book:

R. Lidl and H. Niederreiter, Finite Fields, Cambridge University Press, 2009.

Reference Books

i) G. L. Mullen and D. Panario, Handbook of Finite Fields, Chapman and Hall/CRC, 2013.

ii) Z-X. Wan, Finite Fields and Galois Rings, World Scientific Publishing Co.2012.

iii) A. J. Menezes, I. F. Blake, X. Gao, R. C. Mullin, S. A. Vanstone, and T. Yaghoobian, Applications of Finite Fields, Springer, 1993.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: Munkres, Analysis on Manifolds, CRC Press A Chap- man & Hall Book, reprint-2018. Spivak, Calculus on Manifolds, CRC Press A Chap- man & Hall Book, 1965.
nodes.text:
Integration in Rn: Multiple integrals, Fubini's theorem for change of order in multiple integrations, rectifiable sets, partition of unity and change of variable formula and applications.

Manifolds: Definitions and examples, tangent and normal spaces to manifolds in Rn, differentiability and integrability for functions defined on smooth submanifolds in Rn. Surface area and volume of sub manifolds in Rn. Differential forms and their integrations. Green's Gauss's and Stoke's theorem and their applications.

References:

1. James R. Munkres, Analysis on Manifolds, CRC Press A Chap- man & Hall Book, reprint-2018.

2. S. Kumaresan, A course in differential geometry and Lie Groups, Hindustan Book Agency, 2002.

3. M. Spivak, Calculus on Manifolds, CRC Press A Chap- man & Hall Book, 1965.

4. C. Bar, Elementary Differential Geometry, Cambridge University Press, 2017.

5. W. Rudin, Principles of Mathematical Analysis, McGraw- Hill, 1976.

6. T. M. Apostol, Mathematical Analysis, Addison-Wesley, 1981.

7. K. Janich, Vector analysis, Springer, 2000.

8. M.P. do Carmo, Riemannian Geometry, Birkhauser, Boston, 1992.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: Title : Introduction to Hermite-Sobolev spaces and Tempered distributions Abstract : This set of two lectures will be an introduction to the Hermite Sobolev spaces, tempered distributions and how they are used in (stochastic) analysis. In the first…
nodes.text:
Title : Introduction to Hermite-Sobolev spaces and Tempered distributions

Abstract : This set of two lectures will be an introduction to the Hermite Sobolev spaces, tempered distributions and how they are used in (stochastic) analysis. In the first part, we will introduce tempered distributions and how they can be described in terms of certain Hilbert spaces. In the second talk we will discuss how the uniqueness of certain (stochastic) PDEs can be proved in these spaces using an inequality known as the ' monotonicity inequality '. We will try to make the presentation non technical, but a knowledge of basic Hilbert space theory, linear operators and duality would be useful.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: Title : Generalizations of Ramanujan congruence. Abstract : Ramanujan made a series of influential conjectures in his 1916 paper "On some arithmetical functions" on what is now called the Ramanujan tau function.
nodes.text:
Title : Generalizations of Ramanujan congruence.

Abstract : Ramanujan made a series of influential conjectures in his 1916 paper "On some arithmetical functions" on what is now called the Ramanujan tau function. In the same paper, he also proved a notable congruence mod 691 for the Ramanujan tau function. The existence of such congruences opened the door for many modern developments in the theory of modular forms. For example, it led to Serre and Swinnerton-Dyer developing a geometric theory of mod p modular forms.

For newforms of prime level, some partial results about the existence of such congruences are known. In this talk, we discuss and refine some of those results.

nodes.text:
Checklist – Reimbursement of Travel expenses and Travel Advances

Following points are to be kept in mind for travel advance and reimbursement process:-

| Sl. No | Checklist Item | Description/Notes | Mandatory |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | Travel Plan Approval | Prior approval of travel plan (with /without advances) | Yes |
| 2 | Travel Allowance Reimbursement  (TAD) Form | Form signed by claimant and countersigned by competent authority, if applicable | Yes |
| 3 | Filling of Travel Allowance Reimbursement  (TAD) Form | Fill complete travel description in TAD form date wise, line wise from place of origin to the end destination & back | Yes |
| 4 | Invitation Letter or email / Conference Brochure / Pamphlet (as applicable). | Relevant correspondence to be attached | Yes |
| 5 | Original Tickets & Boarding Passes | Attach all invoices, tickets / boarding passes in original. Booking of tickets through Authorized Travel Agents as per GOI guidelines. | Yes |
| 6 | Local Travel Receipts | Original receipts for local travel as per entitlement. | Yes |
| 7 | Accommodation Bills | Submit original hotel bills for reimbursement | Yes |
| 8 | Registration Fee Copy | Submit receipt of registration fee, if claimed | Yes |
| 9 | Food Bills | Optional, original bills to be submitted, otherwise entitled DA as per admissibility | No |
| 10 | Other Receipts | Travel insurance for International travel, visa fee receipts. | Yes |
| 11 | Submission Timeline | If advance is drawn | Submit travel bills within 15 days succeeding the date of completion of journey. | Yes |
| If no advance is drawn | Submit travel bills within 60 days succeeding the date of completion of journey. |

Note:
 - For project staff / student, countersign by PI /CI is mandatory.
- Self-declaration form to be submitted by employees at pay level 8 and below for those expenses for which documents are lost.

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) for Faculty and Project Staff

Q1. Is it mandatory to take prior approval before undertaking travel?

Ans: Yes. Prior approval is mandatory for every travel visit. The approval must clearly mention the source of funds. The workflow under 'My Activities → Travel Plan' shall be used for obtaining Travel Plan approval. Select Travel Category as Project in case of project related travel.

Q2. How can I submit the travel reimbursement claim?

Ans: After obtaining approval of the Travel Plan and completing the journey, the individual must submit the claim for Travel Reimbursement through the same Travel Plan module under which the approval was taken.

Q3. Which details are mandatory while submitting the reimbursement claim?

Ans: Complete journey details must be provided, including date-wise/line item-wise travel information along with DA claimed, hotel stay, and other admissible expenses.

Q4. Can I claim DA and food expenses if the host has already provided boarding and lodging?

Ans: No. If the host provides boarding and lodging, no claim towards DA or food ex

nodes.summary: alt="Mapping Tribal Heritage" / Call for Papers ICSSR-Sponsored National Conference GST Reforms: Transforming India’s Economic Landscape 14 April 2026 Organised by Department of Humanities and Social Sciences Indian Institute of Technology Jammu,…
nodes.text:
alt="Mapping Tribal Heritage" />


Call for Papers ICSSR-Sponsored National Conference GST Reforms: Transforming India’s Economic Landscape

14 April 2026 Organised by Department of Humanities and Social Sciences Indian Institute of Technology Jammu, Jammu

About the conference

The implementation of the Goods and Services Tax (GST) on 1 July 2017 marked a
 transformative milestone in India’s fiscal history. Conceived under the
 principle of “One Nation, One Tax,” GST sought to unify India’s
 fragmented indirect tax system, eliminate cascading taxation, and create a seamless
 national market.

Over the years, GST has evolved through structural reforms, technological
 integration, and policy recalibrations. The recent rationalisation of tax
 slabs—moving toward a simplified rate structure centred primarily on 5 percent
 (merit rate) and 18 percent (standard rate), effective 22 September
 2025—represents a significant step toward enhancing transparency, compliance,
 and efficiency. The Input Tax Credit (ITC) mechanism has strengthened value-chain
 neutrality, while the Composition Scheme has reduced compliance burdens for small
 enterprises with turnover up to ₹1.5 crore.

Given the central role of GST in shaping India’s economic governance,
 industrial competitiveness, and fiscal federalism, this ICSSR-sponsored national
 conference aims to bring together scholars, policymakers, practitioners, and
 researchers to critically examine the multifaceted impact of GST reforms. The
 conference will particularly focus on implications for the MSME sector,
 entrepreneurship, rural and urban livelihoods, and future reform trajectories.

Conference Themes

Submissions may address (but are not limited to) the following broad themes:
 - GST and the MSME Sector Compliance costs and formalisation Credit access and working capital dynamics Digital integration and tax administration
- Entrepreneurship and the Startup Ecosystem: Role of GST Business climate and ease of doing business Innovation, competitiveness, and supply-chain integration Sectoral case studies
- Impact of GST on Rural and Urban Livelihoods Employment and income effects Informal-to-formal transition Regional disparities
- Fiscal Federalism in the GST Era: Navigating Revenue Dynamics Centre–State fiscal relations Revenue buoyancy and tax rationalisation Inter-state trade and market integration
- Reimagining GST: The Road Ahead Rate rationalisation and structural reforms Digital governance and GSTN reforms International comparisons and policy lessons

Submission Guidelines
 - Abstract Submission: Authors are requested to submit a long abstract of approximately 400 words by 20 March 2026 to: hardeep.singh@iitjammu.ac.in
- Notification of Acceptance : 25 March 2026
- Full Paper Submission Accepted authors must submit the full paper by 31 March 2026. Length: 6,000–8,000 words (including references) Must include a 200-word abstract

All submissions should be original and unpublished work.

Venue

The Con

nodes.title: About Us
nodes.summary: - ACCOUNTING: The section is responsible for maintaining the institution's financial records. - FINANCIAL REPORTING: The section prepares financial reports that provide an overview of the institution's financial position.

nodes.title: About Us
nodes.summary: - ACCOUNTING: The section is responsible for maintaining the institution's financial records. - FINANCIAL REPORTING: The section prepares financial reports that provide an overview of the institution's financial position.
nodes.text:
The accounts section of an academic institution is responsible for managing the financial transactions and records of the institution. This section handles the financial aspects of the institution's activities, including budgeting, accounting, and financial reporting.

Some of the critical responsibilities of the accounts section in an academic institution include the following:
 - BUDGETING: The section prepares and monitors the institution's budget. It involves working with different sections to identify their financial needs and developing a budget plan that meets the institution's overall goals.
- ACCOUNTING: The section is responsible for maintaining the institution's financial records. This includes keeping track of all financial transactions, preparing financial statements, and ensuring that all financial activities comply with accounting standards and regulations.
- FINANCIAL REPORTING: The section prepares financial reports that provide an overview of the institution's financial position. These reports are typically used by management, the board of directors, and other stakeholders to make informed decisions about the institution's financial future.
- PAYROLL: The accounts section is responsible for processing the payroll for the institution's employees. This includes calculating salaries, withholding taxes, and ensuring all payroll taxes are paid on time.
- ACCOUNTS PAYABLE AND RECEIVABLE: The section manages the institution's accounts payable and receivable. This involves paying claims on time, invoicing customers, and managing collections.
- STATUTORY COMPLIANCE: The section makes all statutory payments within defined timelines, including G-TDS, GST returns, income tax, etc.

Overall, the accounts section plays a crucial role in ensuring an academic institution's financial stability and success.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: Title : Generalized Hamming weights of toric codes over hypersimplices and squarefree affine evaluation codes Abstract : Linear codes form a large family of error-correcting codes. Recently, Jaramillo, Pinto and Villarreal introduced various linear…
nodes.text:
Title : Generalized Hamming weights of toric codes over hypersimplices and squarefree affine evaluation codes

Abstract : Linear codes form a large family of error-correcting codes. Due to their algebraic properties, these are the most studied codes from the mathematical point of view. Recently, Jaramillo, Pinto and Villarreal introduced various linear codes called toric codes over hypersimplices and squarefree affine evaluation codes and calculated their basic parameters. In this talk, we will give an overview of our work on the generalized Hamming weights of these codes. At the beginning of this talk, we will define linear code and its basic parameters. Then we will see the construction of toric codes over hypersimplices and square-free affine evaluation codes and the results on their basic parameters. Then we will answer the questions on the number of zeroes of squarefree polynomials of a certain degree in the affine torus. This, in turn, answers our question on the generalized Hamming weights of toric over hypersimplices and squarefree affine evaluation codes under certain cases. This is a joint work with Dr. Sanjay Kumar Singh.

nodes.title: MCM and Other Scholarships
nodes.summary: Scholarships at IIT Jammu: 1) Merit-cum-Means (MCM): Full tuition waiver + Rs 1,000/month pocket money. 3) IITJ Need-Based: Up to Rs 50,000/year for economically weaker sections.
nodes.text:
Scholarships at IIT Jammu:
1) Merit-cum-Means (MCM): Full tuition waiver + Rs 1,000/month pocket money. Eligibility: family income below Rs 4.5 lakh/year AND CGPA >= 6.0. About 25% of students eligible.
2) SC/ST Free Studentship: Full tuition fee automatically waived.
3) IITJ Need-Based: Up to Rs 50,000/year for economically weaker sections.
4) PMRF: Rs 70,000-80,000/month for outstanding PhD scholars.
5) External: INSPIRE, CSIR-SRF, UGC-JRF, GATE-TA also available.

nodes.title: Dr Nathwani Receives DAAD Fellowship to Enhance Speech Clarity for Hearing Impaired
nodes.summary: Dr Nathwani's project, titled "Speech Intelligibility Improvement for Hearing Impaired using Artificial Bandwidth Extension in Car Noise Environment," aims to enhance speech clarity for individuals with hearing impairments. The proposed…
nodes.text:
Dr Karan Nathwani, Assistant Professor at the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jammu, has been awarded the 2024 Bilateral Exchange of Academics fellowship by the German Academic Exchange Service (DAAD). This fellowship will support his collaboration with Technische Hochschule Aschaffenburg in Germany over the summer.

Dr Nathwani's project, titled "Speech Intelligibility Improvement for Hearing Impaired using Artificial Bandwidth Extension in Car Noise Environment," aims to enhance speech clarity for individuals with hearing impairments. The proposed Artificial-Speech Bandwidth Extension (ABE) algorithm is anticipated to increase speech intelligibility and quality by more than 15% in noisy car environments. Additionally, the algorithm could be integrated into smartphones to facilitate seamless speech transmission during phone calls.

The project also explores incorporating wireless and bluetooth technologies into hearing impairment (HI) systems, enabling direct connectivity between iPhone and Android devices. This advancement is expected to mitigate the effects of background noise on sound perception for HI users.

The research outcomes have potential applications in the industry, particularly in enhancing features of existing hearing devices. The resulting product, a pioneering innovation, is slated for international market release.

nodes.title: SERB (GoI) recognizes excellence in research: Eight Faculty Members at IIT Jammu awarded SIRE-2023 Fellowship
nodes.summary: The Science & Engineering Research Board’s [SERB] International Research Experience, or SIRE, Fellowship is a coveted recognition that acknowledges outstanding researchers from India and their exceptional contributions to the field of research. The…
nodes.text:
The Science & Engineering Research Board’s [SERB] International Research Experience, or SIRE, Fellowship is a coveted recognition that acknowledges outstanding researchers from India and their exceptional contributions to the field of research. This highly competitive fellowship is awarded to exceptional individuals who have demonstrated exemplary research capabilities and possess a deep passion for advancing scientific knowledge.

This time, Dr Anup Shukla, Dr Sudhakar Modem, Dr Arun Kumar Verma, Dr Ankur Bansal, Dr Roshan Patil, Dr Vijay Kumar Pal, Dr Rimen Jamatia, and Dr Shanmugadas K P from the Indian Institute of Technology Jammu has been honored with the prestigious SIRE-2023 fellowship.

These faculty members have distinguished themselves with their remarkable research endeavors, making significant contributions to their respective fields. The recognition they have received through the SIRE-2023 Fellowship is a testament to their dedication and passion for advancing scientific knowledge.

Under the esteemed SIRE Fellowship, the awardees from IIT Jammu will collaborate with established foreign agencies to embark on innovative and meaningful research projects. This international exposure will undoubtedly enhance their research capabilities and foster academic collaborations on a global scale.

nodes.summary: Nalini Thakur, a postgraduate student from the Department of Civil Engineering at IIT Jammu, has been awarded the prestigious DAAD KOSPIE Fellowship 2025 — a testament to her academic dedication and research potential. We wish her continued success…

nodes.title: Ms. Nalini Thakur has been awarded the prestigious DAAD KOSPIE Fellowship 2025
nodes.summary: Nalini Thakur, a postgraduate student from the Department of Civil Engineering at IIT Jammu, has been awarded the prestigious DAAD KOSPIE Fellowship 2025 — a testament to her academic dedication and research potential. As part of this international…
nodes.text:
We’re proud to share that Ms. Nalini Thakur, a postgraduate student from the Department of Civil Engineering at IIT Jammu, has been awarded the prestigious DAAD KOSPIE Fellowship 2025 — a testament to her academic dedication and research potential.

As part of this international exchange program, Nalini will conduct her M.Tech thesis at RWTH Aachen University, Germany, one of Europe’s leading research institutions. Her research focuses on the seismic performance of framed structures and buckling-restrained braced frames under near-field excitations, a critical area in earthquake-resistant design.

She will be jointly supervised by Prof. Benno Hoffmeister at RWTH Aachen and Dr. Deepak Yadav at IIT Jammu.

We wish her continued success as she takes her work global and contributes to structural resilience through cutting-edge research.

nodes.title: About PMRF
nodes.summary: The Prime Minister's Research Fellows (PMRF) Scheme has been designed for improving the quality of research in various higher educational institutions in the country. National Coordinating Institute : IIT Madras
nodes.text:
The Prime Minister's Research Fellows (PMRF) Scheme has been designed for improving the quality of research in various higher educational institutions in the country. With attractive fellowships, the scheme seeks to attract the best talent into research thereby realizing the vision of development through innovation. The scheme was announced in the Budget 2018-19. The institutes which can offer PMRF include all the IITs, all the IISERs, Indian Institute of Science, Bengaluru and some of the top Central Universities/NITs that offer science and/or technology degrees. The candidates will be selected through a rigorous selection process and their performance will be reviewed suitably through a national convention.

National Coordinating Institute : IIT Madras

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: Dron Sankhala, a postgraduate student in the Department of Electrical Engineering (M.Tech in VLSI Design), has been awarded the prestigious DAAD KOSPIE Fellowship for 2025. Rohit Buddhiram Chaurasiya at IIT Jammu, Dron is focused on developing an…

nodes.title: Mr. Dron Sankhala has been awarded the prestigious DAAD KOSPIE Fellowship for 2025
nodes.summary: Dron Sankhala, a postgraduate student in the Department of Electrical Engineering (M.Tech in VLSI Design), has been awarded the prestigious DAAD KOSPIE Fellowship for 2025. Congratulations, Dron, on this remarkable achievement!
nodes.text:
We are happy to announce that Mr. Dron Sankhala, a postgraduate student in the Department of Electrical Engineering (M.Tech in VLSI Design), has been awarded the prestigious DAAD KOSPIE Fellowship for 2025. As part of this fellowship, he will be pursuing his M.Tech thesis work at the Technical University of Braunschweig, Germany, under the guidance of Prof. Dr.-Ing. Guillermo Payá Vayá.

Currently working under the mentorship of Dr. Rohit Buddhiram Chaurasiya at IIT Jammu, Dron is focused on developing an instruction set-based architecture for the efficient processing of neural network-based hearing aid algorithms, with a focus on low-power VLSI implementation.

Congratulations, Dron, on this remarkable achievement!

nodes.summary: Companies are free to decide the mode of pre-assessment tests viz. Time duration: The maximum time for any pre-assessment test (online or off-line) is 90 minutes.

nodes.title: AIPC Guidelines
nodes.summary: Companies are free to decide the mode of pre-assessment tests viz. Time duration: The maximum time for any pre-assessment test (online or off-line) is 90 minutes.
nodes.text:
Important Guidelines by the AIPC (All IIT's Placement Committee) to the Recruiters

In order to have a mutual benefit and to have a long term association, the AIPC has indicated the following guidelines to the recruiters. All the recruiters are requested to kindly stick to these positively to have a smooth placement process.

Pre-Placement:

1. PPOs/PPIs: This is for students selected as interns through on campus internship hiring process by the recruiters. After successful completion of the internship, it is highly desirable that the status of the PPOs be informed within two weeks of the completion of the internship. Companies are advised to leverage PPO instead of PPI as the latter does not provide significant benefits either to students or companies.

2. CTC parity: In order to have a healthy environment, a company is expected to quote uniform CTC for the same job profile across all IITs as well as for all students selected from the same institute.

3. Students with backlogs: If a company does not accept students with arrears/backlogs, the condition must be clearly mentioned in the Job Application Form (JAF) / Job Notification Form (JNF). Unless otherwise stated, this backlog would mean only the active backlogs.

4. Requirement on bond: Companies are strongly discouraged the use of bonds as means of retention since it acts as a deterrent to most students. If the policy of a company still requires signing a bond as part of the joining, it is mandated to specify clearly at the time of registration (on registration portal /JAF / JNF). A sample draft of the bond should also be made available during JAF/JNF registration.

5. Pre-assessment and final selection tests: Only one pre-assessment test is allowed for the final placement. Any further assessments (telephonic interview, GD, Personal Interview etc.) should be held on the same day and slot allotted to the company for the final round. Companies are free to decide the mode of pre-assessment tests viz. through virtual / online or through campus visit physically or a hybrid one. In case the mode of assessment is virtual / online, the companies are encouraged to arrange appropriate proctoring from their side. In case of campus visit, it is encouraged that representatives from the company is present in the venue during pre-assessment tests. In case invigilated examination center is required from the institutes, companies need to inform the institute well in advance.

6. Time duration: The maximum time for any pre-assessment test (online or off-line) is 90 minutes. Under certain exceptional cases another 30 minutes may be considered. Since the deadlines during final placements are very close with a sequence of companies scheduled, the process will remain smooth if a company completes the personal interview process within 6 hours window allotted to them. The duration per personal interview per candidate per company should not be more than 60 minutes for offline and 45 minutes for online interviews.

7.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: Summer Internship Program at Indian Institute of Technology, Jammu Duration: 4-6 Weeks (Annually) The Central Workshop at the Indian Institute of Technology Jammu is proud to announce that we offer a Summer Internship Program annually during the…

nodes.title: Join us in the pursuit of knowledge
nodes.summary: Internship Highlights: During this internship program, we will have the privilege of hosting and training enthusiastic interns from diverse engineering backgrounds. - Ex-Interns' Achievements Throughout the program, our interns demonstrated…
nodes.text:
At the Central Workshop, we believe that knowledge is best acquired through hands-on experience. Our training programs and workshops are designed to provide students with practical skills and insights that go beyond the classroom. Our training programs cover a wide range of technical skills, from mastering machinery and fabrication techniques to utilizing CAD-CAM software tools. These programs empower students to become adept problem solvers and innovators.

Summer Internship Program at Indian Institute of Technology, Jammu Duration: 4-6 Weeks (Annually)

The Central Workshop at the Indian Institute of Technology Jammu is proud to announce that we offer a Summer Internship Program annually during the months of June and July for which the registration process starts in the month of May. This program has marked a significant milestone in our commitment to nurturing young talent and fostering hands-on learning experiences.

Objective : The primary goal of the Summer Internship Program is to provide a platform for aspiring engineering students and researchers to gain practical insights and technical skills in a real-world workshop environment. It aims to bridge the gap between theoretical knowledge and practical application while equipping interns with valuable engineering and fabrication expertise.

Internship Highlights: During this internship program, we will have the privilege of hosting and training enthusiastic interns from diverse engineering backgrounds. The internship program feature following highlights:
 - Hands-On Experience: Interns will have the opportunity to work directly with state-of-the-art equipment and machinery available in the Central Workshop. They will gain the practical experience in machining, fabrication, 3D printing, and many more.
- Project Work: Each intern will be assigned a project tailored to their field of interest and expertise. These projects range from designing and prototyping engineering components and setups.
- Guidance from Experts: Our experienced workshop team provides mentorship and guidance throughout the internship, ensuring that interns receive the necessary technical support to excel in their projects.
- Ex-Interns' Achievements Throughout the program, our interns demonstrated exceptional dedication and creativity. They successfully completed their assigned projects and showcased their accomplishments in a final presentation to the faculty, workshop staff and fellow interns. These projects not only added value to their academic and professional growth but also contributed to the workshop's ongoing research and development efforts.

For more information about our programs and opportunities, please feel free to contact us.

nodes.title: About Us
nodes.summary: In one-on-one sessions, counselors provide guidance to explore and express feelings, challenge beliefs, evaluate behaviors, and work towards positive changes. We, as counselors, encourage you to reach out to us without hesitation for any academic,…

nodes.title: About Us
nodes.summary: In one-on-one sessions, counselors provide guidance to explore and express feelings, challenge beliefs, evaluate behaviors, and work towards positive changes. We, as counselors, encourage you to reach out to us without hesitation for any academic,…
nodes.text:
In our journey through life, we are constantly striving to turn our personal and professional aspirations into reality. Amidst the pressure to excel, we often face emotional and psychological challenges that disturb our peace of mind.

However, by seeking counseling services, we can address these overlooked aspects of our well-being. The center comprises a team of professional counselors and empathetic student coordinators who can assist you academically and emotionally. Through counseling, you can learn to make better choices, enhance interpersonal skills, build confidence, and improve your educational effectiveness. In one-on-one sessions, counselors provide guidance to explore and express feelings, challenge beliefs, evaluate behaviors, and work towards positive changes.

We, as counselors, encourage you to reach out to us without hesitation for any academic, emotional, social, or financial concerns. Our aim is to help you maintain focus and find happiness.

nodes.title: The First Visit
nodes.summary: When contacting Counseling Services for the first time, individuals can generally be seen the same day or within a day of making their appointment. Individuals coming in for their first visit will be seen for a brief (30 - 45 minute) intake appointme
nodes.text:
When contacting Counseling Services for the first time, individuals can generally be seen the same day or within a day of making their appointment. Individuals coming in for their first visit will be seen for a brief (30 - 45 minute) intake appointment with a counselor who will:
 - Provide information about what to expect from the counseling process
- Discuss the individual's presenting issues and concerns
- Develop a plan for either on-campus/online appointment, or audio/video mode, which best meets the individual's needs

nodes.title: Confidentiality
nodes.summary: Information is shared only with a person’s written permission. Exceptions to confidentiality are rare and include when a person poses a threat to his/her own safety, or the safety of someone else, or when it is ordered by the court.
nodes.text: All counseling records are kept strictly confidential and are not part of campus records. Information is shared only with a person’s written permission. Exceptions to confidentiality are rare and include when a person poses a threat to his/her own safety, or the safety of someone else, or when it is ordered by the court.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: Dr Pratik Kumar, Assistant Professor, Department of Civil Engineering, Indian Institute of Technology Jammu (IIT Jammu), has been appointed as an Associate Editor for the prestigious Journal of Hazardous, Toxic, and Radioactive Waste (HTRW). Many…

nodes.title: American Society of Civil Engineers appoints Dr Pratik Kumar as Associate Editor
nodes.summary: Earlier this year, Dr Kumar was appointed as an Associate Editor for the Journal of Nanotechnology for Environmental Engineering, published by Springer Publisher. Many Congratulations to Dr Pratik Kumar, Department of Civil Engineering, for fetting…
nodes.text:
Dr Pratik Kumar, Assistant Professor, Department of Civil Engineering, Indian Institute of Technology Jammu (IIT Jammu), has been appointed as an Associate Editor for the prestigious Journal of Hazardous, Toxic, and Radioactive Waste (HTRW). This significant recognition comes from the renowned American Society of Civil Engineers (ASCE).

This is the second Associate Editor appointment he has received this year, further validating his expertise and recognition in his field.

Earlier this year, Dr Kumar was appointed as an Associate Editor for the Journal of Nanotechnology for Environmental Engineering, published by Springer Publisher. These appointments not only showcase his academic prowess but also highlight his commitment to advancing scientific research in his respective domains.

The Journal of Hazardous, Toxic, and Radioactive Waste publishes peer-reviewed original articles on all aspects of research, planning, development and management of hazardous, toxic, and radioactive waste (HTRW). These include the traditional areas of research, design, control, restoration/remediation, construction, planning, detection/analysis, oversight, operations, regulations and policy.

The published-articles offer both the fundamental research and the practical approaches/solutions to the problems and challenges faced by engineers, scientists, process managers, practicing professionals, researchers, contractors, and government policymakers.

Many Congratulations to Dr Pratik Kumar, Department of Civil Engineering, for fetting appointed as an Associate Editor for Journal of Hazardous,Toxic and Radioactive Waste in American Society of Civil Engineers (ASCE).

For Dr Kumar, this is the second Associate Editor appointment position this year for. The first one came earlier this year in the Journal of Nanotechnology for Environmental Engineering (Springer Publisher).

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: Strengthening technical skills and expanding industry-relevant opportunities, the Centre for Essential Skills (CES) at Indian Institute of Technology Jammu inaugurated Batch 6 of the HVAC Operations & Maintenance Training Program on March 10, 2026.…

nodes.title: HVAC Operations & Maintenance Training Program
nodes.summary: Strengthening technical skills and expanding industry-relevant opportunities, the Centre for Essential Skills (CES) at Indian Institute of Technology Jammu inaugurated Batch 6 of the HVAC Operations & Maintenance Training Program on March 10, 2026.…
nodes.text:
Strengthening technical skills and expanding industry-relevant opportunities, the Centre for Essential Skills (CES) at Indian Institute of Technology Jammu inaugurated Batch 6 of the HVAC Operations & Maintenance Training Program on March 10, 2026.

This four-day hands-on training initiative aims to equip youth from Jammu & Kashmir and Ladakh with practical expertise in Heating, Ventilation, and Air Conditioning (HVAC) operations and maintenance. Organized with the support of Indian Hotels Company Limited, the program addresses the growing demand for skilled HVAC technicians across sectors such as infrastructure, hospitality, and industrial services.

The inaugural session was graced by Prof Meenakshi Rajeev, Dean – Outreach and Skill Development; Sh Gian Chand Modgil, Technical Advisor at the International Finance Corporation; and Dr Satya Sekar Bhogilla, Head of the Department of Mechanical Engineering and Program Faculty Coordinator.

Representatives from the hospitality industry, including Mr Ujjawal Bhimwal, General Manager and his team from Taj Vivanta City Centre Jammu, also participated in the inauguration, reinforcing strong industry-academia collaboration. As part of this partnership, the hotel has agreed to offer internship opportunities to 8–10 participants, providing valuable real-world exposure.

Conducted at IIT Jammu’s Cooling Innovation Lab, the program has trained nearly 140 participants in the current financial year, including ITI students, diploma holders, technicians, and early-career professionals. The current batch also welcomes 10 participants from the Union Territory of Ladakh, continuing the institute’s efforts to expand technical training opportunities across the region.

The program plays a vital role in bridging the regional skill gap and enhancing employment opportunities for young technicians.

nodes.title: About CDS
nodes.summary: Established in 2018, the Career and development Services (CDS) at IIT Jammu, provides a platform where students and recruiters can come together to explore avenues of a fruitful and synergistic alliance. The CDS conducts various activities to equip…
nodes.text: Established in 2018, the Career and development Services (CDS) at IIT Jammu, provides a platform where students and recruiters can come together to explore avenues of a fruitful and synergistic alliance. Career Development Services of the Institute centrally handles all aspects of campus placements for the graduating students of all Departments. CDS has been pushing its limits to bridge the gap between academia & industry ever since its inception. It is committed to assisting our students with career planning, Career Exploration, Job search strategies and on campus and off campus full-time employment, internships, research projects, and related opportunities. CDS helps students explore such opportunities in various industries including but not limited to Software development, Core engineering domains, Finance & Consulting, Analytics & Entrepreneurship. The CDS conducts various activities to equip the students with communication skills, interview skills, and group dynamics. Experts from diverse fields conduct sessions with the students that guide them regarding the multifaceted job requirements.

nodes.title: Message
nodes.summary: Dear Recruiters, We greatly delight in extending you a warm invitation to participate in our institute's Campus Recruitment Programme. With well thought, developed core courses, the curriculum at IIT Jammu emphasizes laying a solid foundation in…
nodes.text:
Dear Recruiters,
We greatly delight in extending you a warm invitation to participate in our institute's Campus Recruitment Programme. With well thought, developed core courses, the curriculum at IIT Jammu emphasizes laying a solid foundation in each discipline. At the same time, the choices of different elective courses going across the disciplines broaden the student's knowledge base. IIT Jammu has transformed into a Center of Excellence in technical education in just over some years to meet the growing demand for technology professionals in India and worldwide.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: The Centre for Essential Skills (CES) at the Indian Institute of Technology Jammu (IIT Jammu), in partnership with Nucleon, IIT Alumni initiative, is thrilled to announce the launch of its summer school program. To register students can visit the…
nodes.text:
The Centre for Essential Skills (CES) at the Indian Institute of Technology Jammu (IIT Jammu), in partnership with Nucleon, IIT Alumni initiative, is thrilled to announce the launch of its summer school program. The program is designed to provide an exceptional opportunity for college and school students to gain practical experience and learning across a range of domains to enhance their career prospects.

The internship & training programs offered include Web Development with Hands-On Projects, IoT & Mechatronics Foundations, Data Science, Blockchain & Web 3.0 including Growth with Professional Tools, ChatGPT and other Generative AI, Entrepreneurship & Business Essentials, Mastering No-Code Technology, and placement related guidance.

A special program is also designed for school students to build strong foundations in various domains such as IoT, Google Suite, Creativity & Design, and Communication & Personality Development including informative workshops on emerging tech such as AI, AR/VR, and Blockchain.

The summer school program is open to all students from different disciplines, and it provides a unique opportunity to gain exposure and learn from experienced professionals. The program is an excellent way for students to expand their skill sets, explore new areas of interest, and make meaningful connections with various startups incubated at IIT Jammu.

To register students can visit the website: https://ces.iitjammu.ac.in/summer-school

nodes.text:
'Gapshap' Alumni Meet in Bangalore: A Memorable Reunion of Minds and Memories!

The Agenda: Meet and greet with 40 amazing alumni, including two who traveled all the way from Hyderabad and Mysore!

The Highlight: Emphasizing the significance of Alumni SPOCs for better communication, with representatives elected from each batch:
 - Taijaswaini Agarwal (B.Tech 2020)
- Vishwas Modanwal (B.Tech 2021)
- Apurv Purohit (B.Tech 2022)
- Prince Mishra (M.Tech 2021)
- Mrigank (Thermal M.Tech 2022)
- Rahul Thakur (M.Tech 2023)
- Sufiyan (B.Tech 2023)

Chapter President: Vishwas Modanwal (B.Tech 2021)

Our Mission: Strengthening bonds, promoting excellence, and supporting current students. From mentorship to career assistance, we're here for you, IIT Jammu!

Committee Head: To be elected by the chapter, fostering collaboration and growth.

Event Funding: Alumni contributions to support our goals, with financial transparency.

Student Mentorship: Engaging alumni to mentor current students.

Support & Collaboration: A strong partnership with IIT Jammu's Alumni Affairs office.

Placement & Internships: Enhancing opportunities for students.

Compliance & Review: Periodic alignment with our mission and objectives.

The Bangalore Chapter of Alumni Affairs, IIT Jammu, is committed to strengthening bonds, building futures, and creating lasting memories!

#IITJammuAlumni #NetworkingSuccess #MemoriesMade".

Lakshay Anand AIR-101 (CSE 2021) Mr. Lakshay Anand has completed his B-Tech in Electrical Engineering from Delhi Technological University and M.A. History (Medieval India Specialization) from DU’s Ramjas College in 2020. nodes.title: Campus Location and Infrastructure
nodes.summary: IIT Jammu Permanent Campus: Jagti, P.O. Internet: 1 Gbps Wi-Fi across campus (NKN connected).
nodes.text:
IIT Jammu Permanent Campus: Jagti, P.O. Nagrota, Jammu - 181221, J&K.
Area: 250+ acres.
Established: 2016 by Act of Parliament. Mentored by IIT Delhi.
Distance: 18 km from Jammu city, 20 km from Jammu Airport, 18 km from Railway Station.
Facilities: 11 hostels, Central Library (40,000+ books), Medical Centre, Sports complex (cricket, football, basketball, volleyball, badminton, TT, gym), SBI bank branch + ATM, canteen, cafeteria.
Internet: 1 Gbps Wi-Fi across campus (NKN connected).
Phone: +91-191-257-0066. Email: info@iitjammu.ac.in. Website: iitjammu.ac.in

nodes.title: The FACILITIES OFFERED
nodes.summary: You might see a line forming, as we generally operate on a first-come, first-served basis. We know waiting isn't fun, but we try to make it as comfortable as possible.
nodes.text:
When you enter the reception area of our medical facility, you will notice a buzz of
 activity. This is where your visit begins – the patient registration center. Our staff will
 welcome you warmly and prioritize your health needs for follow-up.
 You might see a line forming, as we generally operate on a first-come, first-served basis.
 However, we always make exceptions for emergencies, giving them immediate attention.

Once you're all checked in, it's time to wait your turn in our comfortable waiting hall. We
 have chairs for you to rest in while you wait, as well as necessities like drinking water, a
 clean environment, and easily accessible toilets. We know waiting isn't fun, but we try to
 make it as comfortable as possible.

Now, when your name gets called, In OPD, you'll meet with one of our doctors for your
 clinical consultation. They'll take your medical history, give you a thorough once-over, and
 come up with a diagnosis. Don't forget to take your prescription as you may need that in the
 future.
 If necessary, they might also recommend some lab tests to get a clearer picture of your
 health.

Remember, you're in charge of your healthcare journey here. You can choose which doctor you
 want to see – we're all here to help you feel your best. So, sit back, relax, and let us
 take care of you.

nodes.title: Ambulance Services
nodes.summary: 24 hours Basic Life Support Ambulance available. The medical centre provides 24/7 support for doctor’s consultations, minor surgeries, medicines, and regular lab sample testing.
nodes.text:
24 hours Basic Life Support Ambulance available. Patients are transported from
 home (on campus) to IIT hospital and patients referred by emergency duty doctor
 to empanelled hospital for specialized care.
 Dr. Sanat Tiwari 
 Chairperson 
 Message from the Chairperson of the Medical Centre 

Dear IIT Jammu family, You and your dear ones are engaged in many important activities on campus or around.
 We encourage you all to keep a healthy lifestyle. However, when there is a need for
 care, we are here to ensure that health and well-being are the least you may have to
 worry about while making significant contributions to academics, research and
 society. The medical centre provides 24/7 support for doctor’s consultations,
 minor surgeries, medicines, and regular lab sample testing. If required, we take you
 to the nearest specialized treatment hubs for better care. Our dedicated team is committed to providing the best medical care and services to
 ensure you have a safe and healthy experience at IIT Jammu. We encourage you to
 contact us with any health-related concerns or questions. To our esteemed colleagues, your continued cooperation and dedication play a vital
 role in maintaining a supportive environment for our students. Together, we can
 foster a community where everyone thrives. Best wishes for a healthy and productive future.
