nodes.title: Why should you consider IIT Jammu as an institute of your choice?
nodes.summary: From an academic and research perspective, IIT Jammu has 12 academic departments fully equipped with the state-of-the-art equipment. IIT Jammu has campus wide ubiquitous Wi-Fi and 1 Gbps internet connectivity.

nodes.title: Why should you consider IIT Jammu as an institute of your choice?
nodes.summary: From an academic and research perspective, IIT Jammu has 12 academic departments fully equipped with the state-of-the-art equipment. IIT Jammu has campus wide ubiquitous Wi-Fi and 1 Gbps internet connectivity.
nodes.text:
Welcome to IIT Jammu!

Our campus is spread over 400 acres, at the foothills of the Shivalik range of the Himalayas, at Jagti, a forest area near Jammu City at NH 44.

The first batch of 82 students was admitted in 2016 and started functioning in a temporary campus. The operations in the Jagti campus commenced from August 2017.

Our Jagti campus is well equipped to meet all the basic needs of the students and faculty. From an academic and research perspective, IIT Jammu has 12 academic departments fully equipped with the state-of-the-art equipment. From a student's perspective it has modern hostel buildings for girls and boys with fully equipped sports facilities.

IIT Jammu has campus wide ubiquitous Wi-Fi and 1 Gbps internet connectivity. The institute has a functional e-Governance Solution ( EG/SARAL ) that caters to end-to-end lifecycle of students and employees covering all offices viz Academics, Student Affairs, Human Resources, Accounts, and Procurement. The studio-like 40 lecture halls provide an great ambience for learning.

The state-of-the-art High Performance Computing (HPC) facility known as Agastya became operational in November 2020 and serves all computing needs. The Central Instrumentation Facility (CIF) known as Saptarshi is setup in Paloura campus and houses several high-end research equipment to serve all basic research needs at molecular level.

Thus, IIT Jammu is all set to provide an all-round learning environment matching the majesty of the Himalayas, and the beauty of Jammu and Kashmir.

Salient features relevant to prospective students to consider IIT Jammu as an institution of their choice:
 - Excellent connectivity by Rail, Road, and Air.
- The current faculty strength is about 143 with 3 senior professors.
- 2024 has witnessed fifth graduating B Tech batch with six disciplines and fourth graduating M Tech batch with five disciplines. Institute is offering Ph.D in eleven disciplines.
- Overall placements have increased above 85% and remains encouraging with many students going for higher education in leading international and national institutions. More details can be obtained from Career Development Services
- Students are going for national and international internships of up to six months under various MoUs that include organizations in Europe, Asia, Australia, etc. Many of them also receive support ranging from 0.5 - 2 Lakh under different scholarships and incentive schemes.
- Students have many career development and professional skill advancement opportunities such as Prep Workshops, Leadership Conclave, Industry Conclave, etc. organized jointly by Institute and Student Body.
- There are specified entrepreneurial, research & innovation activities being made available to students early in their program under the inhouse platforms of Tinkering Workshop, I2EDC, I3C (a section 8 company), etc. IIT Jammu has created a state-of-the-art Tinkering Lab with MBF and UPL generous endowments.
- Students enjoy top of the line

nodes.title: About Us
nodes.summary: The Institute of the stature of IIT Jammu requires special and defined security procedures. - IIT Jammu has a robust, well-defined, and reliable security setup.
nodes.text:
- Security forms the basis of any organisation’s functioning and growth. A Safe and Secure environment is essential for any Education Institute to flourish. The Institute of the stature of IIT Jammu requires special and defined security procedures.
- IIT Jammu has a robust, well-defined, and reliable security setup. A qualified and experienced security officer heads the security section – fully and truly committed to the security duties of the Institute to make the IIT Jammu a safe and secure place.
- The Security Section of the Institute comprises the professional and trained manpower to manage the security setup of IIT Jammu. The security section of the Institute is guided and regulated by a duly defined set of regulations and procedures - compiled as “Security Policy - IIT Jammu.”
- The Campus remains under continuous integrated smart CCTV surveillance for the safety and security of residents and visitors.
- The local police authorities provide IIT Jammu continuous and regular support for campus security, especially during events and special requirements.

nodes.title: Paloura Campus
nodes.summary: Paloura campus is going to be a home for Ph.D. IIT Jammu started the same year with 82 undergraduate engineering students in a temporary campus at Paloura, Jammu.

nodes.title: Paloura Campus
nodes.summary: Paloura campus is going to be a home for Ph.D. IIT Jammu started the same year with 82 undergraduate engineering students in a temporary campus at Paloura, Jammu.
nodes.text:
On 1 May 2016, an MOU was signed between the department of higher education Govt. of India and the department of higher education Govt. of J K to set up an IIT at Jagti, Jammu. IIT Jammu started in the same year 2016 with 82 undergraduate engineering students in a temporary campus which is located in Paloura, Jammu, while the construction activities started at Jagti alongside. Paloura Campus has been a Kalamkari Charity Society (KCS) premises which was remodeled to facilitate the temporary campus for IIT Jammu. 

 Paloura campus has complete facilities to fulfill academic and personal potentials of students with a conducive environment to live and learn. The facilities include hostel accommodation for students, cafeteria, digital classroom, library, laboratories, laundry centers, 24 hrs WiFi, a well-equipped gym, badminton court, basketball ground, TT table and a common room for recreational activities. 

 Paloura campus is going to be a home for Ph.D. students, a research facility, and the Central Instrumentation Facility (CIF). 
On 1 May 2016, an MOU was signed between the Ministry of Higher Education, Government of India and the Department of Higher Education, Government of Jammu & Kashmir, to set up IIT Jammu at Jagti, Nagrota.

IIT Jammu started the same year with 82 undergraduate engineering students in a temporary campus at Paloura, Jammu. Paloura campus was a Kalamkari Charity Society (KCS) premise remodelled to facilitate the needs and workings of an IIT campus. At the same time, construction activities ensued at Jagti.

Facilities: Paloura campus has complete facilities to fulfil students’ academic and personal potentials with a conducive environment to live and learn. The facilities include:
 - Hostel accommodation for students
- a cafeteria
- Digital classrooms
- A library
- Laboratories
- Laundry centres
- 24-hrs Wi-Fi
- A well-equipped gym
- A badminton court
- A basketball court
- A table tennis table
- and a common room for other recreational activities.

Paloura campus is transforming into a home for PhD students and an influential research facility housing the Central Instrumentation Facility (CIF).

This Facility is a cluster of seven labs: Microscopy Lab, Spectroscopy Lab, X-ray Based Characterization Lab, optics and chromatography lab, Nanofabrication and Physical Property Characterization lab (NPPCL), Thermal Analysis Lab (TAL), and Water Quality Testing Lab (WQTL). UG/PG students, research scholars, and faculty extensively use these high-end facilities for advanced research. This facility is also extended to external users like industries, national labs, research organisations, and academic institutions of the region on a chargeable basis. The CIF also organises short-term courses, workshops, and Faculty Development Programs (FDP) on the application of instruments for scientific and technological research.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: The infrastructure part includes buildings, tunnels, bridges, and other built environment structures. - Prestressed concrete - Fatigue and Fracture - Concrete wind turbine towers - Damage assessment in existing structures - Durability of RC…
nodes.text:
The Structural Engineering division is focused on creation of new materials tailored for different uses, conventional and new methods for infrastructure design, innovative construction techniques, developing techniques for health monitoring of existing and new structures, developing methods for strengthening aged infrastructure, and smart infrastructure for natural and built environment.

The infrastructure part includes buildings, tunnels, bridges, and other built environment structures. Structural Engineering division assists with all phases of building infrastructure, from conceptualization to complete design and construction, as well as assessment and strengthening. Both traditional and new structural materials are the part of ongoing business.

Our researchers deal with advanced and innovative analysis methods, which together with experiments, lead to deeper understanding and better knowledge of the behaviour of different components, connections and the structure as a whole. Considering the terrain of Jammu & Kashmir, Structural Engineering division also closely works on the structural part of the Tunnel systems and collaboratively deals with ongoing tunnelling projects at different parts of J&K region. This makes it possible to develop safer and more effective structures to meet the future demands for sustainable development, human security and public health and above all, nation building.

Research areas:
 - Behaviour of quasi-brittle materials under non-uniform passive and active confinement.
- RC structures under monotonic and cyclic loading.
- Prestressed concrete
- Fatigue and Fracture
- Concrete wind turbine towers
- Damage assessment in existing structures
- Durability of RC structures
- Concrete Damage Plasticity
- Fatigue behaviour of polymer composites
- Fluid flow modelling in fracturing porous materials
- Fracture behaviour of polymer composites
- FRP strengthening
- Homogenization of composite materials and multiscale modelling
- Impact testing
- Application of Machine Learning
- Meta structures
- Microstructure analysis
- Multi-scale multi-phase modelling of Geomaterial
- Non-destructive Evaluation of Concrete Structures using Ultrasound
- Numerical modelling of elastic wave propagation and scattering
- Polymer composites
- Seismic analysis of RCC structures
- Spectral element method (SEM)
- Subsurface defect detection and characterization
- Subsurface imaging
- Subsurface imaging and characterization
- Surrogate modelling
- Ultra high performance concrete (UHPC)
- Ultrasonic Non-destructive evaluation
- Wave Propagation
- Wear behaviour of polymer composites
- Wind flow in complex terrains
- Wind-structure interaction
- Wind-tunnel modelling

nodes.title: Message from doctors
nodes.summary: Connect with your peers, make friends, and don't be afraid to make mistakes – they're often our greatest teachers. In the whirlwind of medical school, it's easy to doubt yourself, but remember that everyone's journey is unique.
nodes.text:
Welcome to the new chapter of your journey. Hope you all are doing well and
 prioritizing your physical and mental well-being. 

 Don't forget to engage in extracurricular activities like sports fests and cultural
 celebrations; they're not just fun but essential for maintaining balance in your
 life. 

 Connect with your peers, make friends, and don't be afraid to make mistakes –
 they're often our greatest teachers. Remember to prioritize your well-being by
 nourishing your body with regular meals and staying hydrated. 

 And speaking of well-being, make exercise a part of your daily routine; it's not
 just good for your physical health, but also your mental well-being.
 In the whirlwind of medical school, it's easy to doubt yourself, but remember that
 everyone's journey is unique. Don't compare yourself to others; instead, support
 each other and celebrate each other's successes. And above all, remember that
 learning is a lifelong journey – you're not expected to know everything from the
 start. 

 So, dear students, embrace this adventure with open arms. Cherish the friendships
 you'll make, create memories, and enjoy the ride. And if you ever need guidance or
 support, know that my door is always open. Please feel free to reach out to us for
 any medical care, advice, or attention. Together, let's navigate the highs and lows
 of medical education and emerge stronger, wiser, and ready to heal the world.

nodes.title: Jagti Campus
nodes.summary: Tech) students are residing in the Jagti campus. IIT Jammu’s Jagti campus welcomed its first resident students, 103 in number, in the academic year 2017-18.

nodes.title: Jagti Campus
nodes.summary: Tech) students are residing in the Jagti campus. IIT Jammu’s Jagti campus welcomed its first resident students, 103 in number, in the academic year 2017-18.
nodes.text:
IIT Jammu welcomed 103 students of 2017-18 batch who were the first resident students in the newly established breathtaking campus at Jagti village located on NH44. Since August 2018, all our 317 undergraduate (B. Tech) students are residing in the Jagti campus. This number is expected to cross 500 in August 2019. 

 The 400 acres lush green Jagti campus, about 20 km away from Jammu airport and Jammu Tawi Railway Station, is spread over 25 acres currently with a beautiful landscape closer to Trikuta hills. As a gift, it affords lovely vistas of the hills which gives an abundance of raw beauty in and around the Jagti campus. The hostels are well equipped for a comfortable lodging and boarding for 650 students. 

 The on-campus facilities include hostel accommodation for all students closer to academic area, cafeteria, open gym, laundry centers, digital classrooms, ubiquitous anytime WiFi, music room, TT tables, snooker table, dispensary, and a common hall with TV for indoor recreational activities. ICICI and J K Bank have set up their ATMs alongside the hostels near cafeteria.  
IIT Jammu’s Jagti campus welcomed its first resident students, 103 in number, in the academic year 2017-18. The newly established breathtaking campus at Jagti village is presently spread over 25 acres, with more than 1300 students studying various disciplines from the bachelor’s to the doctoral level. Its hostels are well equipped for comfortable lodging and boarding for 650 students. It affords lovely vistas of the Trikuta hills, surrounding the campus with abundant raw beauty.

Construction for Phases 1B and 1C are in full swing, and the campus is growing with every passing day to accommodate an even bigger strength of students and staff.

Facilities: The on-campus facilities are expanding to include hostel accommodation for all students close to the academic area and have:
 - Cafeterias
- An open gym
- An open-air theatre
- Laundry machines
- Digital classrooms
- Ubiquitous 24-hour WLAN
- Central computing facilities
- A music room
- Table tennis tables
- A snooker table
- A medical unit
- A pharmacy
- and a common hall with a TV for indoor recreational activities.

SBI, ICICI, J&K, and Canara Bank have set up their ATMs on the campus.
 - IIT Jammu, Engineering Block
- Jagti, (Hostels and Mess)
- IIT Jammu

nodes.title: About Us
nodes.summary: Welcome to the Student Affairs Section of IIT Jammu. Hostel: Campus Hostel life is one of the most important aspects of student life.

nodes.title: About Us
nodes.summary: Welcome to the Student Affairs Section of IIT Jammu. Hostel: Campus Hostel life is one of the most important aspects of student life.
nodes.text:
Welcome to the Student Affairs Section of IIT Jammu. The student affairs office is a one stop destination for all aspect of your journey as a student in this institute. Our Aim is to provide our students with a supportive and enriching environment at campus. We believe that a well-rounded student experience is critical to academic success and personal growth.

Student Affairs Office is dedicated to provide comprehensive support that cater to the diverse needs of our students. These support includes

A. Hostel: Campus Hostel life is one of the most important aspects of student life. The hostel experience is a great way to learn independence and self Sufficiency. The IIT Jammu has three hostel at main campus and Twos hostels at satellite campus. The hostel includes all basis amenities including the furniture, Water and 24*7 electricity

B. Mess: Healthy meal is key to a student's overall well-being, and our Institute provides healthy and delicious food options to ensure through Mess Canary, Mess Breag, Mess Egret.

C. Student Activities: Our Student Activities Section provides a wide range of opportunities for students to get involved in extracurricular activities through various technical clubs, Cultural Club, Social clubs, Adventure Club, EK Bharat Shrestha Bharat etc

D. Sports: Our Sports Section offers a wide range of sports facility including Cricket, football, basketball, volleyball, athletic etc. Well qualified trainer are on the staff to help the students develop their interest in games and prepare them to acceptable standards

E. Student Affairs Office: This includes providing health insurance coverage and accidental coverage, as well as travel support for representing IIT Jammu in extracurricular activities at various national events. Additionally, It also issue various certificates such as train passes, bonafide certificates, and help students receive external grants and apply for passports. It also provide financial assistance through student welfare funds, student distress funds, and external scholarships. Our office is responsible for maintaining mess accounts and facilitating awards and other recognition programs.

nodes.title: Assistant Registrar Message
nodes.summary: The Student Affairs Office at IIT Jammu is dedicated to fostering a vibrant and supportive campus environment that nurtures the holistic development of students. Through its initiatives, the Student Affairs office plays a crucial role in enriching…

nodes.title: Message
nodes.summary: The Student Affairs Office at IIT Jammu is dedicated to fostering a vibrant and supportive campus environment that nurtures the holistic development of students. Through its initiatives, the Student Affairs office plays a crucial role in enriching…
nodes.text:
The Student Affairs Office at IIT Jammu is dedicated to fostering a vibrant and supportive campus environment that nurtures the holistic development of students. It oversees a wide range of student-related activities, including Hostel, Mess, Sports & Welfare services, extracurricular programs, and community engagement initiatives. The office serves as a bridge between students and the institute administration, ensuring that student voices are heard and their needs are met. By promoting a balanced and enriching student life, the Student Affairs Office plays a crucial role in shaping well-rounded individuals ready to excel both academically and personally. Through its initiatives, the Student Affairs office plays a crucial role in enriching the student experience at IIT Jammu.

The Student Affairs Office at the Indian Institute of Technology Jammu (IIT Jammu) serves as the central hub for all aspects of student life, dedicated to ensuring a comprehensive and enriching experience for our students. We are committed to fostering a supportive environment that is integral to both academic excellence and personal development. The IIT Jammu campus provides an ideal setting for students to engage in a wide range of recreational and extracurricular activities, with a strong emphasis on cultivating these interests from the onset of their undergraduate journey. The Student Affairs Office is entrusted with the oversight and management of all dimensions of student life on campus, including the following key areas:
 - Hostels
- Mess
- Sports
- Club Activities
- Events
- Student Council
- Code of Conduct (Disciplinary Committees)
- Welfare Schemes

nodes.summary: Wellness Center Welcome to our Institute Health and Wellness Center, your go-to destination for all your medical needs on campus! We're here to keep you well at the health and wellness center!

nodes.title: Welcome to our Institute Health and
nodes.summary: Wellness Center Welcome to our Institute Health and Wellness Center, your go-to destination for all your medical needs on campus! We're here to keep you well at the health and wellness center!
nodes.text:
Wellness Center
 Welcome to our Institute Health and Wellness Center, your go-to
 destination for all your medical needs on campus! Nestled in the serene southern
 corner of our campus lies your gateway to well-being, our Health and Wellness
 Center. Here amidst the tranquil surroundings, you'll discover a haven dedicated to
 nurturing your health and vitality since 2018. Here, we've got you covered with a dedicated team of full-time
 doctors, a dentist, and even visiting specialists from renowned institutions like
 AIIMS Jammu. Our team also includes four para-medical staff including male and
 female staff, pharmacists/ drugstore, and technicians, all geared up to ensure you
 receive high-quality healthcare services. 
 Picture this: you're on campus and away from your loved ones
 needing medical attention. No worries! Our 24/7 ambulance services are just a call
 away, ready to take you to our facility hassle-free. Which is all equipped with all
 facilities. It is handled by professional drivers and emergency medical technicians
 (EMTs) . We understand how hectic life on campus can be, so we're here to ensure
 your health doesn't suffer. 
 We're here to keep you well at the health and wellness center! We
 aim to provide excellent care, helping you stay fit and avoid disruptions from
 injuries or illnesses. After all, nobody wants to miss out on campus life due to
 health issues. 
 Whether you need a check-up, have a dental concern, or need to see
 a specialist, think of us as your wellness support team. Your well-being is our top
 priority, and we're committed to keeping you in great shape so you can fully enjoy
 your campus experience.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: On November 25, 2023, a significant interaction took place with our esteemed in-campus alumni, focusing on crucial matters related to the Alumni Affairs Council Growth Plan. - Future Alumni Affairs Council Event and Fundraising Ideas: Conducted a…
nodes.text:
On November 25, 2023, a significant interaction took place with our esteemed in-campus alumni, focusing on crucial matters related to the Alumni Affairs Council Growth Plan. The discussion also encompassed upcoming Alumni Association Events and Fundraising Drives. The meeting covered various important agendas, including:
 - Alumni Affairs Council Updates: Provided an overview of the ongoing initiatives and projects led by the Alumni Affairs Council. Engaged in discussions regarding potential areas for improvement within the current framework.
- Alumni Association Elections: Discussed the formation of an election committee to ensure a transparent and fair election procedure.
- Future Alumni Affairs Council Event and Fundraising Ideas: Conducted a brainstorming session to generate innovative ideas for upcoming alumni events. Emphasized the importance of effective planning and coordination to ensure the success of these events. Explored strategies for fundraising drives to support the Alumni Affairs Council's growth plan.
- In-campus Alumni’s ID Cards:

Addressed the matter of in-campus alumni ID cards, discussing potential improvements or updates to the current system. The interaction was followed by a lunch session, providing an informal setting for further networking and discussions. The active participation of the esteemed alumni demonstrated a shared commitment to the growth and success of the Alumni Affairs Council and the Alumni Association. The outcomes of the meeting will contribute to shaping the future direction of the Alumni Affairs Council, fostering a stronger connection between the institution and its alumni community.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: Research Areas: - Characterization of pavement materials. - Intelligent transportation systems.
nodes.text:
Transportation engineering deals with the planning, functional design, operation and management of facilities for any mode of transportation in order to provide safe, efficient, and environmentally compatible movement of people and goods. The Transportation Engineering Division at IIT Jammu is one of the fastest growing departments in the country working with an aim to provide sustainable and safe solutions to transportation problems.

The Transportation Engineering Division laboratory is well equipped with advanced research facilities like Corelok Systems, automatic asphalt content tester, V-Box (Data Logger), and advanced two-wheeler driving simulator. The division is currently working in the major research areas of driving behaviour analysis, surrogate safety measures, public transport systems, traffic control and management, and pavement materials. Further, considering the shortage of good quality aggregates in the Jammu & Kashmir region, the division research group is exploring the various material treatment methods for utilizing inferior aggregates as a pavement construction material.

Research Areas:
 - Characterization of pavement materials.
- Design and performance evaluation of flexible pavements.
- Driver behaviour profiling.
- Driving behaviour analysis.
- Exclusive two-wheeler lane.
- Hot, warm and cold mix asphalt.
- Intelligent transportation systems.
- Passenger Satisfaction.
- Public transport planning and design.
- Road safety – Surrogate safety Measures.
- Traffic flow theory and capacity analysis..
- Traffic management, operations and control.

nodes.title: Heat Stress and Hospitalization in India
nodes.summary: The 13th chapter of MANTHAN at IIT Jammu featured a compelling talk on “Heat Stress and Hospitalization in India” by Abhishek Dureja from Plaksha University. The session shed light on the growing healthcare costs of climate change, revealing that…
nodes.text:
The 13th chapter of MANTHAN at IIT Jammu featured a compelling
 talk on “Heat Stress and Hospitalization in India” by Abhishek Dureja from
 Plaksha University.

The session shed light on the growing healthcare costs of
 climate change, revealing that rising temperatures significantly increase
 hospitalization rates—especially in nephrology and pediatrics—leading to
 higher public healthcare expenditure. It emphasized the urgent need for
 climate-responsive healthcare planning and policy interventions, particularly in
 developing countries.

The discussion sparked thoughtful academic dialogue on the
 intersection of climate change, public health, and healthcare systems, providing
 valuable insights for researchers, students, and policymakers alike.

Together, we explore how science and research can guide
 sustainable solutions for a climate-resilient future.

 - All the visitors to IIT Jammu are advised to note and follow the following
 points,
- Entry to campus will be with a valid photo ID card (hard copy) or soft copy only
 in the Digi locker app.
- Strictly no entry for anyone - possessing / under the influence of Liquor or
 other intoxicating items.
- Any weapon or sharp object will not be permitted inside the campus under any
 circumstance. Weapons of the security team/protection team of the Chief Guest /
 Artist / Guest will not be permitted inside the Institute.
- Visitors may be subject to physical frisking/ checking by a metal detector and
 Breathalyzer.
- The right of entry to the Institute is reserved.
- Visitors' vehicles are not permitted inside the campus.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: Kathuria did postdoctoral research at the Indian Institute of Technology in Bombay, India. in Civil Engineering (Transportation Engineering) from the Indian Institute of Technology Roorkee in 2017.
nodes.text:
About me

Dr. Ankit Kathuria is working as an Associate Professor in the Department of Civil Engineering and Humanities and Social Sciences Department at IIT Jammu. Throughout his career, he has undertaken diverse roles, including his current position as Associate Dean for E-Governance System and his previous role as Head of the Department of Civil Engineering and Associate Dean (Infrastructure Planning and Management). He received the prestigious SERB-SIRE Fellowship in 2022, allowing him to contribute to significant research related to roundabout safety at the Future Mobility Group, Newcastle University, UK, for six months.

Dr. Kathuria did postdoctoral research at the Indian Institute of Technology in Bombay, India. He obtained his Ph.D. in Civil Engineering (Transportation Engineering) from the Indian Institute of Technology Roorkee in 2017. He received a Masters in Planning degree with specialization in Transport Planning from the School of Planning and Architecture, New Delhi in 2013 and, holds a Bachelor of Technology degree in Civil Engineering from Jaypee University, India.

In the realm of research, Dr. Kathuria has led various projects funded by esteemed organizations such as the Ministry of Road Transportation and Highways, Science and Engineering Research Board, National Highway Authority of India, Central Road Research Institute, Jammu Kashmir Science Technology and Innovation Council, and Indian Institute of Technology Jammu.

Dr. Kathuria's focus on research is driven by a genuine desire to find practical solutions to various transportation planning and engineering problems. His research encompasses a range of disciplines, including behavioral and psychological aspects of traffic, road safety, public transportation system design and planning, traffic management using BT and wifi sensors, social sciences, data mining, mathematical modeling, and flexible pavements (secondary research area). Presently, he leads six research projects as the principal investigator, aligning with his research interests.

In his mentoring capacity, Dr. Kathuria guides seven Ph.D. students, out of which one student has already submitted his thesis for further evaluation. He is grateful for the opportunities to contribute to academic discourse, with over 47 peer-reviewed research and conference papers to his name. Additionally, he is honored to serve as a reviewer for respected journals such as TR part A, TR part D, Accident Analysis and Prevention, Case Study on Transport Policy, Transport Policy, Transportation Letters and Journal of Transportation Engineering. Dr. Kathuria remains deeply committed to advancing knowledge and expertise in the humble pursuit of contributing to the field of transportation Planning and engineering.

nodes.summary: - Parallel and distributed computing of computational genomic pipelines on emebedded cluster. - RISC V based System-on-Chip Design for Computational Genomic pipelines.
nodes.text:
- Parallel and distributed computing of computational genomic pipelines on emebedded cluster.
- RISC V based System-on-Chip Design for Computational Genomic pipelines.

I am actively looking for PhD students who would like to work in the following projects:
 - Many-core RISC V based SoC for computational genomic applications. The candidate should be good in coding with fundamental understanding of computer architecture, and digital logic design.
- ML based Advanced Battery Management systems. The candidate should have Bachelors in Electronics and Electrical Engineering and should, also, have done courses on ML/AI.
- Molecular diagnostics for water quality monitoring and anti-microbial resistance surveillance. The candidate should have experience in performing wet-lab experiments required for generating genomic data using sequencing machines. This will be an interdisciplinary project between Department of Computer Science and Biosciences and Bioengineering at IIT Jammu.

Feel free to discuss your background if interested. Please, email me your CV if you are interested using the subject: "PhD Application Inquiry."

nodes.title: Indian Institute of Technology Jammu scholar Sheriya Sareen and Faculty, Dr. Sayantan Mandal from the Department of Humanities and Social Sciences (HSS) recently published an article
nodes.summary: Indian Institute of Technology Jammu scholar Sheriya Sareen and Faculty, Dr. Sayantan Mandal from the Department of Humanities and Social Sciences (HSS) recently published an article titled 'Challenges of Blended Learning in Higher Education Across…
nodes.text:
Indian Institute of Technology Jammu scholar Sheriya Sareen and Faculty, Dr. Sayantan Mandal from the Department of Humanities and Social Sciences (HSS) recently published an article titled 'Challenges of Blended Learning in Higher Education Across Global North-South: A Systematic and Integrative Literature Review' in the Social Sciences & Humanities Open (Elsevier). The article is available online (DOI: https://doi.org/10.1016/j.ssaho.2024.101011 ).

This paper serves as a comprehensive resource for researchers and policymakers seeking a nuanced understanding of the barriers hindering the implementation of blended learning in higher education globally. Key highlights include a multi-stakeholder analysis of blended learning challenges, identification of learning design limitations. The paper also develops a 12-factor conceptualization of blended learning and highlights distinct barriers faced in the Global North and South and proposes a comprehensive blended learning barriers model.

nodes.title: Dr Pankaj Chauhan, Associate Professor, Department of Chemistry, IIT Jammu, J&K has been selected as an Indian National Science Academy (INSA) Young Associate.
nodes.summary: Dr Chauhan is recommended for his outstanding work in the field of organocatalysis especially on enantioselective reactions, photochemical and electrochemical organic synthesis, synthesis of new polycyclic frameworks, atrop-/enantio-selective synthes
nodes.text:
Dr Chauhan is recommended for his outstanding work in the field of organocatalysis especially on enantioselective reactions, photochemical and electrochemical organic synthesis, synthesis of new polycyclic frameworks, atrop-/enantio-selective synthesis of medium-sized bridged biaryls, generation of remote stereogenic centers, photochemical generation of triplet carbene intermediates, and electro-organic synthesis of valuable building blocks and heterocycles, as well as the merger of chiral organocatalysis with photoredox catalysis and electrochemical synthesis to develop relay catalytic transformations.

For more information kindly click on the link provided : https://insaindia.res.in/pdf/RECIPIENTS-OF-INSA-YOUNG-ASSOCIATES-(IYA)-2024.pdf

nodes.summary: RT Durai Prabhakaran, from department of Mechanical Engineering, IIT Jammu, has received funding for his project titled "Performance of Hybrid (Kevlar/Glass) Composites Subjected to High Velocity Impact: With the Innovative Sizing Formulations"…
nodes.text:
Dr. RT Durai Prabhakaran, from department of Mechanical Engineering, IIT Jammu, has received funding for his project titled "Performance of Hybrid (Kevlar/Glass) Composites Subjected to High Velocity Impact: With the Innovative Sizing Formulations" under the Scheme for Promoting Academic and Research Collaborations ( SPARC), Phase II. Dr. Durai submitted this project after joining IIT Jammu in October 2019.

Partnering with Professor Puneet Mahajan, from department of Applied Mechanics, IIT Delhi and esteemed Australian Project Partners, this project promises groundbreaking research in composite materials' performance under high-velocity impact.

nodes.title: Waseem Raja, PhD Scholar has been named an Outstanding Reviewer for 2024 by the American Society of Civil Engineers (ASCE)
nodes.summary: We are proud to share that Waseem Raja, PhD Scholar in the Department of Civil Engineering, has been named an Outstanding Reviewer for 2024 by the American Society of Civil Engineers (ASCE) for his contributions to the Journal of Hazardous, Toxic,…
nodes.text:
We are proud to share that Waseem Raja, PhD Scholar in the Department of Civil Engineering, has been named an Outstanding Reviewer for 2024 by the American Society of Civil Engineers (ASCE) for his contributions to the Journal of Hazardous, Toxic, and Radioactive Waste.

This recognition underscores his commitment to academic service and research integrity, particularly in the field of environmental and wastewater engineering. His efforts as a peer reviewer help uphold the high standards of international scientific publishing.

Waseem works under the mentorship of Dr. Pratik Kumar, whose guidance continues to inspire rigorous research and scholarly engagement within the department.

Congratulations to both Waseem and Dr. Kumar on this well-deserved recognition!

nodes.text:
Proud Moment for IIT Jammu!

Ms. Snigdha Priyadarshini, a scholar from the Department of Materials Engineering, has brought laurels to the institute as her technical paper was recognized with the Institution Award at the 64th Annual Technical Session of The Institution of Engineers (India), held on 11 May 2025 in Bhubaneswar.

Kudos to her for this remarkable achievement and contribution to the field of materials engineering!

nodes.text:
Thank you for visiting my webpage. I am an Assistant Professor in the Department of Mechanical Engineering, IIT Jammu. My research interests are in the simulation and analysis of the fluid dynamics of heated and unheated turbulent plumes which are an ansatz of clouds and forest-fires. I am also collaborating with my colleagues in the domain of heat-transfer in the materials science domain. My core teaching interest is in the intellectually challenging field of thermo-fluids. I have received my undergraduate (BE) degree from NMAMIT Mangalore in 2004, graduate degree (ME) from IIEST Shibpur in 2007 and doctoral (PhD) degree from IISc Bangalore in 2014; all of them are in Mechanical Engineering. Please do drop an email if you are interested in fulfilling your higher education ambitions with me.
 - PhD (2014), Department of Mechanical Engineering, Indian Institute of Science (IISc) Bangalore
- ME (Heat Power engineering, 2007), Department of Mechanical Engineering, Indian Institute of Engineering Science and Technology (IIEST) Shibpur
- BE (Mechanical Engineering, 2004), Department of Mechanical Engineering, NMAM Institute of Technology, Visveswaraiah Technological University, Karnataka

I have been an Assistant Professor in the at IIT Jammu since December 2017. Prior to that I was an Assistant Professor at the Mechanical Engineering Department IIITDM Jabalpur for a period of 11 months starting December 2016. I have also completed my post-doctoral research for about three years under the renowned (late) Prof. Roddam Narasimha at JNCASR Bangalore.
 - Fluid turbulence of plumes and jets
- Forest-fires in the context of climate change
- Heat transfer in materials science
 - Undergraduate: Heat transfer Engineering thermodynamics
- Graduate: Advanced heat transfer Advanced thermodynamics and fluid mechanics Numerical methods Fluid instabilities and turbulence Continuum Mechanics

Post-Doctoral Research Associate (Dec. 2013 to Dec. 2016), Jawaharlal Nehru Centre for Advanced Scientific Research, Bangalore
 - My first PhD scholar was Jalil ul Rehman Khan. For his PhD thesis which he defended in April 2024, he analysed the turbulent/non-turbulent layer on heated and unheated plumes. I am happy to say that Jalil was a diligent student.
- I was a co-supervisor for Abhishek Shrivastava. He defenced his thesis in September 2024. Abhishek was the reason for my forays into the analysis of heat-transfer in additive manufacturing processes.
 - SRG grant from SERB on Evolution and inﬂuence of coherent structures on entrainment in a transient diabatic plume - implications for cumulus cloud ﬂows (2019-2022)
- VAIBHAV grant from DST on Impact of Climate Change on the Interaction between Forest- fires and the Atmospheric Boundary Layer in the Indian Context . Prof. Kiran Bhaganagar from the Department of Mechanical Engineering, UTSA, USA is the VAIBHAV Fellow (2025 - )

Please see my Google Scholar link

I have rendered review services for Physics of Fluids, Physica D a

nodes.title: Ashutosh Bijalwan
nodes.summary: His research spans Applied Mechanics, with emphasis on Optimal Control, Computational Mechanics, and Numerical Methods, particularly applied to soft and living systems. - Department of Applied Mechanics, IIT Delhi, 2017.
nodes.text:
Ashutosh Bijalwan is an Assistant Professor in Mechanical Engineering at IIT Jammu. His research spans Applied Mechanics, with emphasis on Optimal Control, Computational Mechanics, and Numerical Methods, particularly applied to soft and living systems. He earned his PhD in Applied Mathematics with Excellent Cum Laude from CIMNE–UPC, Barcelona, Spain, where he worked on structure-preserving numerical strategies for optimal control of mechanical systems. He completed his Master’s in Engineering Mechanics from the Applied Mechanics Department at IIT Delhi, where he received the Prof. B. Karunesh Memorial Award for his thesis on visco-hyperelastic analysis of the ACL. He previously worked at Eaton and Schlumberger as a Modeling and Simulation Engineer and later as a Research Engineer (Wells) in the Geomechanics Group at ExxonMobil, where he conducted research in multiscale modeling, uncertainty quantification, and inverse problems related to the mechanics of hydraulic fracturing in unconventional reservoirs. His current research explores structure-preserving and multiscale computational frameworks for soft and living systems, with applications in tissue morphogenesis, biomedical devices, and bioinspired soft robotics.
 - Optimal control of soft and living matter
- Computational biomechanics and bioinspired soft robotics
- Geometric mechanics and variational integrators
- Stability and control of falling thin film flows

nodes.title: Department Vision
nodes.summary: In this endeavour, the Department of Mechanical Engineering aims to be globally recognized as a knowledge and technology creator. - Creating a knowledge base through research in the scientific and technological aspects of mechanical engineering.
nodes.text:
- Home

The Mechanical Engineering Department of IIT Jammu was
 established in 2016 and is one of the three founding departments of the Institute.
 The department strives to play a role in nation-building by training engineers in an
 effort to transform them into future leaders. We conduct translational research to
 enable us to surmount technological barriers so that our present and future needs
 are addressed. The department offers B.Tech., M.Tech., and Ph.D. programs. It
 currently has 19 faculty members, 6 staff members, and approximately 150
 undergraduate students (I – IV year), 28 postgraduate students (I – II
 year students of Thermal and Energy Systems Engineering), and 38 Ph.D. students.

Mechanical engineering is one of the oldest and most
 fundamental disciplines. A mechanical engineer is well-versed in the basic
 principles of dynamics, energy, and materials science and uses it to conceptualize,
 analyze, design and build mechanical systems that serve the interests of mankind and
 our planet. Hence, it is one of the most broad-ranging engineering specialities; its
 scope encompasses power plants and airplanes, cell phones, and biomedical equipment,
 to name a few. Its diversity also enables the skilled engineer to engage with other
 fundamental disciplines, such as physics and mathematics. In a nutshell, the
 discipline of mechanical engineering focuses on developing processes, systems, and
 products by harnessing energy and power across many scales, viz., from the nanoscale
 to the vast, from simple and yet elegant to complex systems. This enables its
 practitioners to achieve technological improvements and forge new domains.


Vision and Mission of the Department:

Vision of the Department Our vision is to build a strong reputation for our academic programs through
 state-of-the-art experiential curriculum, research, and technology incubation hubs.
 In this endeavour, the Department of Mechanical Engineering aims to be globally
 recognized as a knowledge and technology creator.

Mission of the Department
 - Nurture our graduates to become adaptive professionals and be successful in
 engineering practices, research, and public services.
- Creating a knowledge base through research in the scientific and technological
 aspects of mechanical engineering.
- Serving the society through innovation and excellence in teaching and research.
- Establish state-of-the-art laboratories in line with the government’s
 vision, “Viksit Bharat” through innovation and research.

nodes.title: ICCMDE-2026
nodes.summary: It featured keynote talks by eminent scientists including Naresh Bhatnagar (IIT Delhi), Bent F. Varley, RMIT, Melbourne, Australia, Giribaskar, NMIS, UK, Saju Pillai (NIIST, Trivandrum), Raguraman (IIITDM Kancheepuram), Ramadass (DRDO-RDE, Pune),…
nodes.text:
The International Conference on Composite Materials, Design,
 and Engineering (ICCMDE-2026) concluded successfully at Research and Innovation Park,
 Indian Institute of Technology Delhi from 11–13 February 2026. Jointly organized
 by Department of Mechanical Engineering, Indian Institute of Technology Jammu and
 Department of Applied Mechanics, IIT Delhi under the Scheme for Promotion of Academic
 and Research Collaboration (SPARC), the three-day event brought together leading
 researchers, academicians, industry experts, and scholars to explore emerging
 developments in composite materials, structural design, and advanced manufacturing
 technologies.

The conference was inaugurated by Bent F. Sørensen,
 Naresh Bhatnagar, R. T. Durai Prabhakaran, and Puneet Mahajan. It featured keynote talks
 by eminent scientists including Naresh Bhatnagar (IIT Delhi), Bent F. Sørensen
 (Technical University of Denmark), Anindya Deb (IISc Bengaluru), and Paul Hazell
 (University of New South Wales, Australia), and plenary talks by eminent
 scientists including Russell J. Varley, RMIT, Melbourne, Australia, Giribaskar,
 NMIS, UK, Saju Pillai (NIIST, Trivandrum), Raguraman (IIITDM Kancheepuram), Ramadass
 (DRDO-RDE, Pune), Sanjay Palsule (IIT Roorkee), Inderdeep Singh (IIT Roorkee),
 Rajnish Sharma (IIT Mandi), Pawan Kumar (CUJ, Jammu), Jayaramulu (IIT Jammu), Badri
 Prasad Patel (IIT DELHI), S.P.Singh (IIT Delhi), Pradyumna (IIT Delhi), Abhijit
 Majumdar (IIT Delhi), S Vikrant Karra (IIT Delhi), Naresh Datla (IIT Delhi), Dr.
 Gautam Jain, Endolite India Limited, New Delhi, covering advanced composite
 materials, structural performance, impact engineering, and emerging
 applications.

Participants engaged in high-quality technical sessions
 spanning military-grade composites, biomedical innovations, advanced structural
 materials, and next-generation manufacturing technologies, highlighting the
 transformative role of composites in global engineering.

The valedictory session featured Professor Sawan S. Sinha,
 Head - Department of Applied Mechanics, reflecting on key outcomes and emphasizing the
 importance of continued interdisciplinary collaboration. Selected full-length papers
 from ICCMDE-2026 will be published in a Special Issue of the Journal of Polymer
 Composites, further extending the scholarly impact of the conference.

nodes.title: Message
nodes.summary: Welcome to the Department of Mechanical Engineering website. The department is committed to excellence in teaching, research & development and outreach related to science, engineering, technology development and policy matters related to Mechanical…
nodes.text:
Welcome to the Department of Mechanical Engineering website.
 The Department of Mechanical Engineering is one of the three founding departments at IIT
 Jammu, which was established in August 2016. As of Summer 2024, the department comprises
 twenty faculty members and six staff members and is home to about one hundred forty-four
 undergraduate students and more than sixty-five graduate students. Our students come
 from various states across the union of India and, thus, contribute to the department's
 diversity.

The department houses well-equipped laboratories, which are
 used for both instruction and research. Our faculty and research scholars are actively
 involved in research in conventional and new Mechanical Engineering areas. Additionally,
 we promote interdisciplinary research and collaborate with colleagues in other
 departments, both within the institute and outside, including those in other countries,
 to identify and work on research projects common to multiple branches of science and
 engineering. Our major thrust areas in research include Applied Mechanics, Advanced
 Manufacturing, Thermal Sciences, Renewable Energy, Nuclear Energy, and Compressible
 Flows. The department is committed to excellence in teaching, research & development and
 outreach related to science, engineering, technology development and policy matters
 related to Mechanical Engineering

nodes.title: Message
nodes.summary: Welcome to the Department of Electrical Engineering, Indian Institute of Technology Jammu. In line with the Institute's focus on academics-oriented research, the department considers both teaching and research integral.
nodes.text:
Welcome to the Department of Electrical Engineering, Indian
 Institute of Technology Jammu. In line with the Institute's focus on academics-oriented
 research, the department considers both teaching and research integral. The department
 accommodates highly experienced and dedicated faculty members who are great teachers and
 researchers in their respective domains. We are also supported by invaluable staff
 members who discharge their duties efficiently and enthusiastically on the technical and
 administrative front. We look for zealous young minds for our UG, PG, and PhD programs,
 as they are our greatest strength.

Our department and the Institute's
 instrumental/computational facility are well-equipped to execute research on various
 fields of Electrical Engineering. Contemporarily and fundamentally, the overall growth
 of our department and, consequently, of the Institute and society at large drives us.
 Each member at all levels actively and innovatively contributes to reaching the utmost
 development of our department. We welcome you to be a part of our department and our
 endeavours.

nodes.title: Ankit Kathuria
nodes.summary: Kathuria did postdoctoral research at the Indian Institute of Technology in Bombay, India. in Civil Engineering (Transportation Engineering) from the Indian Institute of Technology Roorkee in 2017.
nodes.text:
Get to know the iMaSS lab, click the link for details: https://sites.google.com/view/imass About me Dr. Ankit Kathuria is working as an Associate Professor in the Department of Civil Engineering and Humanities and Social Sciences Department at IIT Jammu. Throughout his career, he has undertaken diverse roles, including his current position as Associate Dean for E-Governance System and his previous role as Head of the Department of Civil Engineering and Associate Dean (Infrastructure Planning and Management). He received the prestigious SERB-SIRE Fellowship in 2022, allowing him to contribute to significant research related to roundabout safety at the Future Mobility Group, Newcastle University, UK, for six months. Dr. Kathuria did postdoctoral research at the Indian Institute of Technology in Bombay, India. He obtained his Ph.D. in Civil Engineering (Transportation Engineering) from the Indian Institute of Technology Roorkee in 2017. He received a Masters in Planning degree with specialization in Transport Planning from the School of Planning and Architecture, New Delhi in 2013 and, holds a Bachelor of Technology degree in Civil Engineering from Jaypee University, India. In the realm of research, Dr. Kathuria has led various projects funded by esteemed organizations such as the Ministry of Road Transportation and Highways, Science and Engineering Research Board, National Highway Authority of India, Central Road Research Institute, Jammu Kashmir Science Technology and Innovation Council, and Indian Institute of Technology Jammu. Dr. Kathuria's focus on research is driven by a genuine desire to find practical solutions to various transportation planning and engineering problems. His research encompasses a range of disciplines, including behavioral and psychological aspects of traffic, road safety, public transportation system design and planning, traffic management using BT and wifi sensors, social sciences, data mining, mathematical modeling, and flexible pavements (secondary research area). Presently, he leads six research projects as the principal investigator, aligning with his research interests. In his mentoring capacity, Dr. Kathuria guides seven Ph.D. students, out of which one student has already submitted his thesis for further evaluation. He is grateful for the opportunities to contribute to academic discourse, with over 47 peer-reviewed research and conference papers to his name. Additionally, he is honored to serve as a reviewer for respected journals such as TR part A, TR part D, Accident Analysis and Prevention, Case Study on Transport Policy, Transport Policy, Transportation Letters and Journal of Transportation Engineering. Dr. Kathuria remains deeply committed to advancing knowledge and expertise in the humble pursuit of contributing to the field of transportation Planning and engineering.

Primary research interest

a) Behavioural and psychological aspects of drivers in heterogeneous disordered traffic conditions

b) Proactive ap

nodes.title: Dr Amitash Ojha
nodes.summary: Head of Department - hod.hss@iitjammu.ac.in The Department of Humanities & Social Sciences, IIT Jammu, is committed to suturing STEM (Sciences, Technology, Engineering, Math) into the fabric of Arts, Humanities and Social Sciences. - To create an…
nodes.text:
Head of Department - hod.hss@iitjammu.ac.in

The Department of Humanities & Social Sciences, IIT Jammu, is committed to suturing
 STEM (Sciences, Technology, Engineering, Math) into the fabric of Arts, Humanities and
 Social Sciences. This endeavour is being realised through an enmeshed network of cross
 and trans-disciplinary courses, projects, and collaborations. The department encompasses
 the disciplines such as English, Economics, Sociology, Philosophy, Psychology, Cognitive
 Science, Culture and Polity, and other such prospective subjects in the wide gamut of
 Humanities and Social Sciences.

The mission and goals of the Humanities and Social Sciences are consistent with those of
 IIT Jammu in that we foster innovative learning experiences that will enable our
 students/graduates to develop broad, tolerant, and cosmopolitan perspectives so that
 they will be better prepared to function in a diverse, demanding, and global society.

Our Mission
 - To train students to be highly competent and critically minded through teaching and
 research programmes that are internationally competitive and locally relevant.
- To educate students to embody holistic knowledge, inspire critical thinking, possess
 effective oral and written communication skills, and become global citizens.
- To secure a conducive educational environment with an atmosphere of collaborative
 academic research that enables faculty and students to partake in the intellectual
 life of their disciplines and contribute to the well-being of their communities and
 regions.
- To ensure, through our work performance and service, that humanistic and social
 values are pursued to benefit the individual, the institute, and the community.

Our Vision
 - To become nationally recognised for excellence in both instruction and
 research/scholarship - in which faculty members are well-rounded academics who take
 equal pride in instructional, scholarly/research, and service activities.
- To reflect the aspirations of the communities that we serve in our academic
 offerings and research outputs.
- To equip our students with the skills and knowledge necessary to excel in their
 professional careers and other spheres of life to which they are expected to
 contribute.
- To create an active cross-disciplinary intellectual environment that promotes
 creative interactions between faculty and students.

HSS Department at IIT Jammu has its roots firmly grounded in the soil of emancipatory
 learning and is on its journey to reach out for the sun. It hopes to grow its budding
 branches into a fruit-laden tree in the luminescence of the future.

nodes.title: Gendered Belonging: Women and Work in Post-Partition Kolkata
nodes.summary: Sengupta addressed the gendered impact of economic migration. These pieces vividly depict the struggles and resilience of refugee women in the post-Partition era.
nodes.text:
Date: Friday, March 21, 2025

The Department of Humanities and Social Sciences at IIT Jammu organized a workshop as part of the eighth chapter of the 'Manthan Series,' titled “ Gendered Belonging: Women and Work in Post-Partition Kolkata ,” on March 21, 2025 . The event featured Dr. Debjani Sengupta, an Associate Professor at the University of Delhi, as the resource person. The workshop aimed to explore the socio-economic and political challenges faced by refugee women in post-Partition Kolkata, emphasizing how displacement reshaped their roles in society.

The event began with a brief inauguration that set the stage for the discussions to follow. In the first session, Dr. Sengupta addressed the gendered impact of economic migration. She explained how women, previously confined to domestic roles, were compelled to seek employment in unfamiliar urban settings, competing with men in industries such as mills, factories, and offices. This shift, she argued, led to a redefinition of modernity for these women, challenging established societal norms.

Dr. Sengupta drew upon literary and cinematic representations to illustrate her points, discussing works like "Meghey Dhaka Tara" by Shaktipada Rajguru and "Bwadwip" by Sabitri Roy. These pieces vividly depict the struggles and resilience of refugee women in the post-Partition era. She emphasized how these narratives provide crucial insights into the lived experiences of displaced women, blending historical and moral dimensions with personal stories.

The second session featured an engaging interaction between Dr. Sengupta and the participants, allowing attendees to discuss the broader implications of gendered displacement. The discussion explored how refugee women not only redefined labor dynamics but also reshaped familial and social structures. Participants actively engaged in conversations about the intersections of gender, work, and migration, drawing parallels between historical displacement and contemporary issues faced by migrant women today.

The event successfully provided a platform for critical discussions on gender, displacement, and economic mobility, fostering a deeper understanding of the challenges faced by women in post-Partition Kolkata. Dr. Sengupta’s insights shed light on the resilience of these women, their struggles for survival, and their role in shaping the socio-economic fabric of the city. The session concluded with closing remarks, encouraging further academic inquiry into the themes discussed.

nodes.title: The Spectacle of AI Technology: Reflections on Ethics and Human Agency
nodes.summary: Date: Friday, February 21, 2025 On February 21, 2025, Dept. of HSS, IIT Jammu proudly hosted the fifth chapter of Manthan Series as an impactful symposium ' Spectacle of AI Technology: Reflections on Ethics and Human Agency ', bringing together…
nodes.text:
Date: Friday, February 21, 2025

On February 21, 2025, Dept. of HSS, IIT Jammu proudly hosted the fifth chapter of Manthan Series as an impactful symposium ' Spectacle of AI Technology: Reflections on Ethics and Human Agency ', bringing together prominent scholars to delve into the crucial ethical and human-centric aspects of artificial intelligence (AI).

The event showcased five compelling sessions that addressed vital issues:

1. Dr. Amitash Ojha, HoD of the HSS department, shed light on the profound influence of AI on user decision-making and personal autonomy, prompting attendees to reflect on their agency in an AI-driven world.

2. Dr. Sushruth Ravish, Assistant Professor from HSS dept, IIT Kanpur, uncovered the delicate balance between transparency and functionality in explainable AI, highlighting the need for prudent approaches in trust-building with technology.

3. Dr. Joby Verghese, Assistant Professor from HSS dept, IIT Jammu confronted the ethical challenges in healthcare associated with AI, emphasizing the importance of informed consent and shedding light on the complexities of AI's decision-making processes.

4. Dr. Sreekumar Jayadevan, Assistant Professor from HSS dept, IIT Ropar, provocatively discussed the nature of responsibility in our interactions with AI, compelling participants to ponder the implications for human relationships and moral accountability.

5. Dr. Bijoy H. Boruah, Professor from HSS dept, IIT Jammu discussed the cultural narratives surrounding AI, questioning its portrayal as an omnipotent force and advocating for a reassessment of human control in the technological landscape.

The symposium concluded with a thought-provoking valedictory session and networking opportunities, leaving participants with valuable insights and a renewed commitment to engage with the ethical dimensions of AI. This event served as a vital platform for fostering dialogue and interdisciplinary collaboration to address the societal impact of AI, urging us all to navigate the future of technology with caution and conscience.

nodes.title: HSS Organises 2-Day Research Writing Workshop
nodes.summary: “No one ever told us these things about writing - we wish we had attended something like this earlier as well!” - Participants, Academic Writing Workshop 2020. The workshop consisted of hands-on writing and reviewing sessions conducted by Dr.
nodes.text:
“No one ever told us these things about writing - we wish we had attended something
 like this earlier as well!” - Participants, Academic Writing Workshop 2020.

IIT Jammu successfully hosted a two-day Academic Writing Workshop,
 “ Work-in-Progress: Removing Roadblocks from Research
 Writing ” organized by the Department of Humanities and Social
 Sciences on 10th-11th January, 2020. The workshop consisted of hands-on writing and
 reviewing sessions conducted by Dr. Anannya Dasgupta and Mr. Sameer Thomas from the
 Center for Writing and Pedagogy, Krea University, Andhra Pradesh.

Each session was focused on generating writing output and the participants received
 detailed feedback on their work. The workshop also discussed the nuances of structuring
 the process of writing and thinking. The workshop was about strategizing research
 techniques in order to generate a strong argument. This involved a step-by-step
 engagement with the mechanics of constructing thoughts, ideas into sentences and
 building a lucid and well-structured piece of research writing.

The workshop received a great response from all the 25 participants who came from the
 following institutes:
 - IIT Roorkee
- Shri Mata Vaishno Devi University
- Central University of Haryana
- Central University of Jammu
- Lovely Professional University
- Baba Ghulam Shah Badshah University
- The University of Jammu
- Central University of Himachal Pradesh
- IIT Jammu

All the participants unanimously appreciated the relevance and the significance of the
 topics discussed throughout the workshop.

The workshop was coordinated by Ms. Ishita Mahajan, Dr. Bhumika R. and Dr. Sukanya
 Mondal. The HSS research scholars, Ms. Aditi Magotra, Mr. Pummy Sharma and Ms. Shreya
 Mudgil were immensely helpful in conducting the workshop.

Date: Friday, January 10, 2020

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: The Department of Humanities and Social Sciences, Indian Institute of Technology Jammu, is organising a one-day interdisciplinary workshop titled Reimagining Tribal Studies on 13 January, from 9:30 AM to 5:30 PM, at Sanskriti, Mansar. Bringing…
nodes.text:
The Department of Humanities and Social Sciences, Indian Institute of Technology Jammu, is organising a one-day interdisciplinary workshop titled Reimagining Tribal Studies on 13 January, from 9:30 AM to 5:30 PM, at Sanskriti, Mansar. The inaugural session, scheduled between 10:30 AM and 12:30 PM, will be honoured by the presence of the Hon’ble Minister, Mr. Javed Ahmed Rana, as the Chief Guest, and Dr. Javaid Rahi, noted scholar and cultural practitioner, as the Guest of Honour.

The workshop is conceived as a platform to critically engage with the histories, cultures, knowledge systems, and contemporary realities of tribal communities in India, with a special focus on the Gujjar and Bakerwal communities of Jammu and Kashmir. It seeks to move beyond static and stereotypical representations of tribal life and instead foreground lived experiences, narrative traditions, indigenous knowledge practices, and evolving social formations.

Bringing together more than 300 participants, including students from Gujjar and Bakerwal hostels, faculty members, researchers, policymakers, and invited experts, the workshop aims to encourage interdisciplinary dialogue across the humanities, social sciences, and allied fields. Key themes include cultural heritage and memory, oral and narrative traditions, identity and mobility, ethical community engagement, and the possibilities and challenges of digital documentation and preservation.

Through keynote addresses, panel discussions, and interactive sessions, the workshop aspires to create a reflective academic space that is both ethically grounded and socially responsive. It also seeks to strengthen collaboration between academic institutions, community stakeholders, and policymakers, contributing to more inclusive and context-sensitive approaches to tribal studies.

nodes.title: About Us
nodes.summary: - Home Welcome to the Humanities and Social Sciences Department of IIT Jammu! in Behavioural Science and Predictive Analytics, offering its students interdisciplinary expertise.
nodes.text:
- Home

Welcome to the Humanities and Social Sciences Department of IIT Jammu! We are a group of dynamic, dedicated and inquisitive faculty members and scholars, with diverse interests ranging from Philosophy to Literatures in English to Economics to Sociology, which is a testament to the vast potential of the Humanities and Social Sciences. We are committed to upholding an intellectually stimulating and inclusive environment where students and faculty engage with some of the most profound questions concerning human existence, psychology, society, economy, culture, and history. Our research queries and pursuits are in line with the larger vision of the Institute: to enable collaborative interventions that are humanistic, creative, and have a social impact.

The Department has developed a first-of-its-kind B.Sc. in Behavioural Science and Predictive Analytics, offering its students interdisciplinary expertise. The Department also offers a series of core and elective courses for the BTech students of the Institute, inculcating a spirit of curiosity and ethical considerations in budding engineers and scientists.

The Department has a steadily growing cohort of PhD students engaged in cutting-edge research, rigorously working towards broadening the scope of critical enquiry and innovative thinking within the ever-more overlapping and interdisciplinary domains of the humanities and social sciences. An Experiential Language Learning Lab to aid undergraduate students in honing their Language and Communication skills has been developed. The Department fosters a space for scientific enquiry and exchange of ideas through its workshops, conferences, cultural events and programs, apart from propagating discussions through its growing collections of books and other reading resources.

nodes.title: Marketing Analytics: Unlocking Business Performance Through Data-Driven Insights
nodes.summary: The talk focused on the areas of ‘ Marketing Analytics ’ and how business performances could be enhanced through data analytics driven insights. The talk highlighted how marketing has made a transition and evolved from an intuition to an evidence…
nodes.text:
Date: Wednesday, April 16, 2025

The Department of Humanities and Social Sciences (HSS) at IIT Jammu hosted a Research
 Talk on April 16, 2025 , as a ninth chapter of the
 ‘ Manthan Series ’. The talk focused on the areas of
 ‘ Marketing Analytics ’ and how business performances could
 be enhanced through data analytics driven insights.

The talk traced the history of marketing and how attitudinal and behavioural studies
 surrounding it have drastically changed since the adoption of technology and artificial
 intelligence systems.

Prof. Marcos Severo from the Federal University of Goiás (Brazil) discussed case
 studies from Brazil which show the multiple methods of data collection that marketing
 analytics relies on and the role of artificial intelligence in advancements like real
 time individualised strategies for developments in sales revenue and overall business
 performance enhancements. He traced the changing landscape of data collection sources
 and the different models of knowledge systems which direct the organization of these
 data sets. The talk highlighted how marketing has made a transition and evolved from an
 intuition to an evidence era relying on data and analysis.

The talk provided valuable insights to the participants through a combination of
 theoretical frameworks and specific case studies to understand the relationship between
 psychology, marketing, economics and technology.

nodes.summary: This topical and crucial research project is funded by IIT Jammu, and its principal investigator is Dr Joby Varghese. It started in April 2023 and has a three-year tenure to understand the technological basis of ethics in AI.

nodes.title: Ethics of AI
nodes.summary: It started in April 2023 and has a three-year tenure to understand the technological basis of ethics in AI. The most critical outcome of this project would be the framing of broad ethics and principles for the design, development, and deployment of…
nodes.text:
This topical and crucial research project is funded by IIT Jammu, and its principal
 investigator is Dr Joby Varghese. It started in April 2023 and has a three-year tenure
 to understand the technological basis of ethics in AI.

This project recognises AI's wide and expanding impact. Therefore, it is committed to
 taking plural perspectives in philosophy, computer science, cognitive science,
 sociology, economics, law, and public policy to understand and analyse issues related to
 AI systems and robotics. Since the philosophical literature is relatively thin on many
 important topics regarding the ethics of AI and robotics, the project’s interests
 span from establishing the preliminary and necessary clarity on key issues, such as bias
 and opacity in the decisions of AI systems, to developing these debates further and with
 rigour.

The most critical outcome of this project would be the framing of broad ethics and
 principles for the design, development, and deployment of AI in India. While it will be
 drawing on similar global initiatives, it will be grounded in the Indian legal and
 regulatory context.

nodes.title: DSS Meetings
nodes.summary: - 2025 - 2024 - 2023 - 2022 - 2021 Helmed by Prof. The broad format of these sessions provides the Speaker of the week with 35 to 45 mins of presentation time.
nodes.text:
- 2025
- 2024
- 2023
- 2022
- 2021

Helmed by Prof. Bijoy H. Boruah, Senior Faculty, HSS, the Department weekly gathers for seminar sessions on subjects and questions of “current importance and that of enduring interest” (Boruah, email). Since November 2021, Wednesday afternoons have been reserved for faculty and scholars to come together for discussions and deliberations that span the breadth of humanities and social sciences, including reflective understandings of contemporary technoscientific progress and its influence on humanity.

The broad format of these sessions provides the Speaker of the week with 35 to 45 mins of presentation time. The designated Principal Commentator responds to the presentation in 10 minutes, after which the presentation is open for all to have spirited discussions and critical insights that speculate, build on, and branch out, probing and crossing disciplinary boundaries. Apart from the Department’s faculty and research scholars, these discussions have frequently been led by distinguished academicians from and outside IIT Jammu.

nodes.title: Social Shaping of Technology
nodes.summary: Date: Wednesday, March 19, 2025 The Department of Humanities and Social Sciences (HSS) at IIT Jammu successfully hosted a workshop on March 19, 2025 , as part of the seventh chapter of the ‘ Manthan Series .’ The workshop focused on the theme of '…
nodes.text:
Date: Wednesday, March 19, 2025

The Department of Humanities and Social Sciences (HSS) at IIT Jammu successfully hosted a workshop on March 19, 2025 , as part of the seventh chapter of the ‘ Manthan Series .’ The workshop focused on the theme of ' Social Shaping of Technology .'

The event explored the interplay between technology and society, challenging the notion that technology develops independently and subsequently molds societal structures.

Dr. Sambit Mallick from the HSS department at IIT Guwahati discussed the influence of economic, political, and cultural factors on technological innovation. He highlighted how decisions made in technology development can impact various social groups and addressed the limitations of technological determinism. He also provided examples illustrating how societal dynamics shape technological advancements.

The workshop offered valuable insights into the relationship between technology and society, helping participants understand how broader social factors influence innovation.

nodes.title: Cyber Security and Privacy Awareness
nodes.summary: Date: Thursday, November 21, 2024 The Department of Humanities and Social Sciences (HSS) at IIT Jammu successfully hosted a workshop as the second chapter of the ‘Manthan Series’, which focused on ‘ Cyber Security and Privacy Awareness. An expert in…
nodes.text:
Date: Thursday, November 21, 2024

The Department of Humanities and Social Sciences (HSS) at IIT Jammu successfully hosted a workshop as the second chapter of the ‘Manthan Series’, which focused on ‘ Cyber Security and Privacy Awareness. ’ Dr. Rajendra Kumar Solanki served as the keynote speaker for the event. An expert in information security, Dr. Solanki has made significant contributions to e-governance at IIT Jammu.

The workshop consisted of two sessions. The first session addressed security practices, such as password safety, while the second session discussed privacy measures related to social media. Dr. Amitash Ojha, the Head of the Department at HSS, concluded the session by emphasizing the importance of privacy in safeguarding consumer data from targeted marketing.

nodes.title: Smart Skilling: Experimental Evidence on Vocational Training Design
nodes.summary: The 12th chapter of MANTHAN, saw an inspiring session by Dr. Tanu Gupta (University of Southampton, Delhi) on “Smart Skilling: Experimental Evidence on Vocational Training Design”.
nodes.text:
The 12th chapter of MANTHAN, saw an inspiring session by Dr.
 Tanu Gupta (University of Southampton, Delhi) on “Smart Skilling: Experimental
 Evidence on Vocational Training Design”.

The talk highlighted how blending sector-specific vocational
 skills with project-based experiential learning and digital
 competencies—especially social media for business—can significantly improve
 women’s: * Occupational alignment * Self-employment participation * Earnings and job satisfaction

The session sparked engaging discussions on evidence-based
 skilling strategies and their policy relevance, showcasing how thoughtfully designed
 training programs can empower women economically and strengthen India’s inclusive
 workforce.

nodes.title: Eye
nodes.summary: Around 250 participants gathered to discuss technology’s impact on human cognition. Ramesh Kumar Mishra highlighted eye tracking's roles in bilingualism and cognitive control.
nodes.text:
Tracking and Attention

Date: Saturday, November 16, 2024

The Department of Humanities and Social Sciences (HSS) at IIT Jammu, in collaboration with the Action Control and Cognition Lab at the University of Hyderabad, hosted the “ Eye Tracking and Attention ” workshop as the first chapter of the Manthan Series on 16 November 2024 . Around 250 participants gathered to discuss technology’s impact on human cognition.

Keynote speaker Dr. Ramesh Kumar Mishra highlighted eye tracking's roles in bilingualism and cognitive control. Sessions included insights from ACCL members on usability research and Dr. Garima Singh’s techniques for improving focus. The workshop concluded with Dr. Amitash Ojha discussing the implications of eye movements in understanding cognitive processes.
