nodes.title: Arpita Paul
nodes.summary: Cation Order Control of Correlations in double perovskite Sr 2 VNbO 6 , Arpita Paul and Turan Birol, Phys. “Phosphorene”, Arpita Paul and Umesh V.
nodes.text:
My research work focuses on first-principles quantum theory of advanced functional materials. It not only aims at achieving microscopic insights into exotic states of fundamentally interesting materials (frustrated magnets, multiferroics, superconductors, Hund’s metals and topological superconductors), but also designing materials of technological importance (multiferroics, superconductors and transparent conductors).
 - Ph.D in Physics, 2017, Jawaharlal Nehru Centre for Advanced Scientific Research (JNCASR), Bangalore, India
- M.Sc in Physics, 2012, Indian Institute of Technology Guwahati, Assam, India

Research Areas First-principles quantum theoretical modelling of advanced functional materials
 - September, 2017 - December, 2020: Postdoctoral research associate, Department of Chemical Engineering and Materials Science, University of Minnesota, USA
- February, 2021 - July, 2022: Research associate, Theoretical Sciences Unit,Jawaharlal Nehru Centre for Advanced Scientific Research, Bangalore, India
- August, 2022 - July, 2023: DST-INSPIRE faculty fellow, Department of Materials Engineering, Indian Institute of Science, Bangalore, India

Some selected publications:

1. Ab-initio statistical mechanics of Néel transition, magnetic domains and topological defects in hexagonal YMnO 3 , Arpita Paul and Umesh V. Waghmare, Phys. Rev. B 107, 174403 (2023)

2.  Pressure-induced superconductivity in higher order topological insulator candidate BiSe, Pallavi Malavi, Arpita Paul, Achintya Bera, D V S Muthu, P S Anil Kumar, Umesh Waghmare, A. K. Sood, and S. Karmakar, Phys. Rev. B 107, 024506 (2023)

3.  Cation Order Control of Correlations in double perovskite Sr 2 VNbO 6 , Arpita Paul and Turan Birol, Phys. Rev. Research 2, 033156 (2020)

4. Spin–lattice coupling and the emergence of the trimerized phase in the S = 1 Kagome antiferromagnet Na 2 Ti 3 Cl 8 , Arpita Paul, Chia-Min Chung, Turan Birol, and Hitesh J. Changlani, Phys. Rev. Lett. 124, 167203 (2020)

1. “Phosphorene”, Arpita Paul and Umesh V. Waghmare, in “2-D Inorganic Materials beyond Graphene", CNR Rao and Umesh V. Waghmare, World Scientific Publishing, 2017

DST-INSPIRE faculty fellowship (2022-2027)

nodes.title: Message
nodes.summary: Welcome to the webpage of the Department of Physics at the Indian Institute of Technology, Jammu. We eagerly anticipate your active involvement in advancing the development of our department.
nodes.text:
Welcome to the webpage of the Department of Physics at the
 Indian Institute of Technology, Jammu. We pride ourselves as an academic-oriented
 research institution where teaching and research are integral components of our mission.
 We eagerly seek enthusiastic young minds to engage in undergraduate and postgraduate
 courses, as well as pursue Ph.D. studies. Our students are the cornerstone of our
 strength.

Within our department, we house a team of highly experienced
 and dedicated faculty members who excel both as educators and researchers in their
 respective domains. Supported by a proficient group of staff members, we ensure
 efficient operations on both technical and administrative fronts. Our driving force lies
 in fostering contemporary and fundamental growth across all aspects of our endeavors.

Equipped with state-of-the-art instrumental and
 computational facilities, our department, alongside the Institute, is well-prepared to
 undertake research across various fields of Physics. We eagerly anticipate your active
 involvement in advancing the development of our department.

In closing, I extend a warm welcome to all of you, and we
 are continuously on the lookout for bright minds to collaborate with us.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: Welcome to the Department of Chemical Engineering (ChE) at IIT Jammu. program in Chemical Engineering.

nodes.title: Message
nodes.summary: Welcome to the Department of Chemical Engineering (ChE) at IIT Jammu. program in Chemical Engineering.
nodes.text:
Welcome to the Department of Chemical Engineering (ChE) at IIT Jammu. The Department of Chemical Engineering was established in 2018 with the intake of the first undergraduate batch. Since then the department has grown to the first batch graduating in 2021. The department is currently growing and consists of nine faculty members: assistant professors, visiting professors, and professors. With a unique combination of enthusiasm, experience, and adaptability, the department has expanded from modest beginnings to astounding new heights.

The vision of the department is “To be an epitome of excellence in chemical engineering science with a world-class multidisciplinary approach towards sustained growth in academics and research with impetus for service to society”. The department is working to reach new heights by bringing together faculty from different fields in order to accomplish this.

The department currently offers 4 year undergraduate, 2 year M.Tech program with specialization in Sustainable Energy Engineering and Ph.D. program in Chemical Engineering. The also contributes in Minor in Nuclear Engineering, Minor in Bioscience and Bioengineering as well as Engineering and Scientific Computation.

The department has identified different thrust areas of research ranging from Energy and Environment to Biomedical Devices, Biochemical Engineering to Catalysis and Reactor Engineering, Modelling and Simulation to Drug Design and Delivery. Having a high level of diversity in research the department has many high-end analytical and experimental facilities. The department asserts student towards interdisciplinary research that would lead new avenues of entrepreneurship that lies in between. Identification of the gaps in the market and provide sustainable solutions to those problems by students is highly encouraged.

Currently the department has identified a few thrust areas keeping in mind regional and national interests. Chemical Engineers are currently addressing the issues of Sustainable Chemical Processing, Process Intensification, Agro-chemical processing and Carbon Free Energy Systems at multiple scales (macro, conventional and micro-nano). They explore the mega possibilities at the intersection of traditional engineering and new technologies (genomics, microfluidics, nano) using Big Data Analytics and AI Predictive Models. To take part in the 21st Century “new agro” based revolution they can exploit the rich biodiversity of Union Territories of J&K Ladakh. Entrepreneurship and enterprise are one of the major ethos of the department. These skills and this approach will help the budding Chemical Engineer to achieve path breaking careers in emerging markets.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: A chemical engineering graduate has ample of opportunities to work with many national and international companies. To say, three of the top ten Fortune 500 companies are chemical companies and chemical companies always make into this list under…
nodes.text:
Chemical Engineering is one of the “BIG FOUR” disciplines of the
 Engineering Science, the rest being Civil, Mechanical, and Electrical. It is also
 the youngest of the basic branches of the engineering discipline. A chemical
 engineer works in a place like the one seen in the image above. They are responsible
 for converting any raw material to a finished product, may it be petrol from crude
 oil, synthetic rubber from rubber sap, tea bags from tea leaves, sugar from
 sugarcane, the list goes on.

Having a humble origin in 1888, under the vision of Gorge E Davis, Chemical
 Engineering has come a long way over the century. The chemical engineers, have
 provided solutions to global food problems, the environmental pollution problems,
 energy needs (in terms of gas and gasoline), the longevity of human health and so
 on. It is a dynamic discipline whose products reach and affect all life. Right from
 when you wake up to you go to bed, chemical engineering is always there with you,
 making your life that a bit more comfortable.

Over the last century the discipline has changed a lot of focus. Chemical
 Engineering, has moved from its traditional forte of Oil and Gas now to
 Pharmaceuticals to Biological production. All this is possible because a chemical
 engineer is trained in physics, chemistry, mathematics and economics including
 biological and environmental sciences. They are now being called for to make life
 simple and solve the challenges that haunt the country today, may it be the issue of
 air pollution near the capital area, valorization of solid waste generated in major
 metropolitan, solving the water pollution and provide safe drinking water, better
 power generation devices, better medical devices and infrastructure and so on. I
 would urge you to have a look at the following video that has a brief idea of what
 chemical engineers do.


A chemical engineering graduate has ample of opportunities to work with many national
 and international companies.
 All over the world there is always a shortage of good chemical engineers. To say,
 three of the top ten Fortune 500
 companies are chemical companies and chemical companies always make into this list
 under every odd. A graduate can
 choose from avid number of areas in chemical engineering to build their careers, may
 it be petroleum or
 petrochemical, polymers, water and wastewater, fine/bulk chemicals, pharmaceuticals,
 natural products, pulp and
 paper, food and beverages, nuclear energy, biomedical devices, paints and dyes, the
 list just goes on. A chemical
 engineer grows higher financially as they gain experience, in majority of the cases
 the growth is exponential.
 - https://www.flickr.com/photos/jsjgeology/48916278253 (used under creative commons
 license)

nodes.title: American Chemical Society recognises Dr Jayaramulu as the Rising Star of 2023
nodes.summary: In a momentous acknowledgment, the esteemed ACS journal has bestowed the distinction of Rising Star of 2023 in materials chemistry upon Dr Jayaramulu Kolleboyena. The news of Dr Jayaramulu’s recognition as a rising star in 2023 for his pioneering…
nodes.text:
In a momentous acknowledgment, the esteemed ACS journal has bestowed the distinction of Rising Star of 2023 in materials chemistry upon Dr Jayaramulu Kolleboyena.

The news of Dr Jayaramulu’s recognition as a rising star in 2023 for his pioneering research in the early stages of his career is indeed heartening. The imprimatur from the revered American Chemical Society (ACS) is no small feat and attests to the considerable strides made by Dr Jayaramulu in the realm of materials chemistry.

Furthermore, his invitation to contribute an article on "Hybrid Two-Dimensional Porous Materials" underscores the substantive impact of his work. The focal points elucidated in the article, encompassing structure-property relationships and applications in areas like water purification, gas storage/separation, electrocatalysis, and energy storage/harvesting, underscore the broad spectrum of potential applications for these porous materials. Such research plays a pivotal role in tackling contemporary challenges and propelling technological advancements across diverse domains.

The distinction of featuring the article with cover art adds an extra layer of prestige, as it signifies the visual representation of the research prominently adorning the journal. Cover art, carefully selected to encapsulate the essence and significance of the work, serves to make it more accessible and engaging to a wider audience.

The industrial visit to Kuantum offered an insight into production techniques, safety
 protocols, quality control, and the role of innovation in enhancing productivity. The
 students visited their in-house nursery which ensures continuous supply with a variety of
 quality raw materials for sustainable development. Students were also briefed about
 different steps involved in the process of transformation of pulp form agro-residues,
 hardwood, veneer chips, and bamboo as raw materials. Interaction of students with Engineers
 and DCS Operators form Pulp Mill and Solvent recovery section helped our students to relate
 theory with large manufacturing plant operations.

The visit also provided an opportunity for students to Interacting with professionals and
 gaining insights from industry experts. The visit concluded with a vote of thanks from HoD
 Chemical Engineering Dr. Ravi Kumar Arun and Associate Vice President HR (KPL) - Sh. Ajay
 Sharma

2 Fourier transforms : Definition and examples of Fourier transform, properties and applications, Fourier transform of Heaviside and Dirac delta, inversion formula, central slice theorem, filtered back projection and Hilbert transform. Convolution, properties of convolution and its relation with Fourier transform.

3 X-Rays and The Radon Transforms : X-Ray transform, its behavior and Beer’s law, parameterization of lines in plane and properties of X-ray transform. Inversion formula for X-ray transform. nodes.title: IIT Jammu Mathematics Colloquium
nodes.summary: 2023-24, Sem-2), the IIT Jammu Mathematics Colloquium will take place every Wednesday afternoon from 3 to 4 pm. Kousik Dhara Department of Mathematics, IIT Jammu Department of Mathematics, IIT Jammu Contact No.: 0191-274-1278 Email:…
nodes.text:
The IIT Jammu Mathematics Colloquium is directed at students, postdocs, and faculty working in the fields of pure and applied Mathematics & Statistics. It aims to present expository lectures or mini-courses that appeal to our wide range of audience at IIT Jammu. One of the main objectives of these colloquia is to inform non-specialists and graduate students about recent research trends, ideas, and results in various areas of Mathematics, rather than focusing on technical details for specialists.

This semester (i.e. 2023-24, Sem-2), the IIT Jammu Mathematics Colloquium will take place every Wednesday afternoon from 3 to 4 pm. Invited external speakers will deliver exciting talks in online mode, while the faculty, graduate students, and the visitors of IIT Jammu will present talks in offline mode in alternate weeks. Please note that the timing for a few talks may be adjusted depending on the convenience of renowned speakers from institutes around the globe. Tea and snacks will be served after the colloquium talks.

If you wish to receive emails with news about the online talks and reminders, please contact the coordinators.


Coordinators:
 - Dr. Kousik Dhara Department of Mathematics, IIT Jammu Department of Mathematics, IIT Jammu Contact No.: 0191-274-1278 Email: kousik.dhara@iitjammu.ac.in
- Dr. Soumyadip Das Department of Mathematics, IIT Jammu Contact No: 0191-274-1275 Email: soumyadip.das@iitjammu.ac.in

nodes.title: Mathematics colloquium on Local algorithms, Conway-Lagarias-Thurston tiling groups and finitely dependent processes.
nodes.summary: Mathematics colloquium on 30th October (Thursday), 4 pm IST hosted by the Department of Mathematics, IIT Jammu. - Title : Local algorithms, Conway-Lagarias-Thurston tiling groups and finitely dependent processes.
nodes.text:
Mathematics colloquium on 30th October (Thursday), 4 pm IST hosted by the Department of Mathematics, IIT Jammu.

Please find the details below:
 - Speaker : Dr. Nishant Chandgotia, TIFR-Centre for Applicable Mathematics.
- Title : Local algorithms, Conway-Lagarias-Thurston tiling groups and finitely dependent processes.
- Abstract : Suppose you were given a set of tiles T on the integer lattice Z^2 and wanted to find out whether a given region can be tiled by T. Conway, Lagarias and Thurston paved the way for answering such a question via some tools coming from combinatorial group theory and analysis. On the other hand suppose you were just given some tiles on a sparse but dense set of locations in Z^2 and you wanted to know if T can complete the tiling by looking at what is given locally. The answer to this question comes from local algorithms starting with work by Nathan Linial. Interestingly, these cover complementary cases and are related to a host of questions arising from symbolic and Borel dynamics, probability and combinatorics. We will try to give a gentle introduction to these topics and describe how they relate to finitely dependent processes. This is all in joint work with Aditya Thorat.
- Date and Time : 30th October (Thursday), 4-5 pm IST, Venue: Room 01-AC-2008.

nodes.title: Mathematics colloquium on Nonlinear Fourier analysis and connections to quantum computing
nodes.summary: Mathematics colloquium on 9th October (Thursday), 4 pm IST hosted by the Department of Mathematics, IIT Jammu. - Title : Nonlinear Fourier analysis and connections to quantum computing.
nodes.text:
Mathematics colloquium on 9th October (Thursday), 4 pm IST hosted by the Department of Mathematics, IIT Jammu.

Please find the details below:
 - Speaker : Dr. Diogo Oliveira e Silva, Instituto Superior Técnico, Lisboa, Portugal.
- Title : Nonlinear Fourier analysis and connections to quantum computing.
- Abstract : This lecture offers an introduction to the Nonlinear Fourier Transform (NLFT), a powerful analytic tool that extends classical Fourier analysis to nonlinear settings. We present the definition of the SU(2)-valued NLFT and explore its functorial properties with an emphasis on symmetries. We then focus on the nonlinear Plancherel theorem, which serves as a nonlinear analogue for the classical Plancherel identity. We also discuss a deep and elegant equivalence: up to a change of variables, the NLFT coincides with a well-known algorithm in quantum signal processing. Last but not least, we explore the very recent extension of these ideas to the SU(2n)-setting. The lecture assumes familiarity with basic (linear) Fourier analysis but aims to be accessible to a broad audience.
- Date and Time : 9th October (Thursday), 4-5 pm IST, Venue: Room 01-AC-2008

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: Title of the Talk : Square roots, character sum and Kronecker-Hecke algebra Abstract : When does a given matrix possess a square root, and if so, how many square roots does it have? In this talk, we first obtain a counting formula for square roots…
nodes.text:
Title of the Talk : Square roots, character sum and Kronecker-Hecke algebra

Abstract : When does a given matrix possess a square root, and if so, how many square roots does it have? The answer leads to profound topics in linear algebra. Investigation of this topic for permutation matrices reveals intriguing connections to the character table 'Z' of the symmetric group. Namely, the number of the square roots of such a matrix 'P' is the sum of entries of the column associated with 'P' in 'Z.' The fact stems from a classical work of Frobenius-Schur, which is relevant for any finite group.

In this talk, we first obtain a counting formula for square roots of permutation matrices. Then we propose a conjecture applicable to most finite groups 'G': " The first column sum in the character table of G dominates the sum of rest ." We shall see the essence of Frobenius-Schur's result while we validate the conjecture for symmetric groups (and other Weyl groups). Towards the end, we shall introduce the Kronecker-Hecke algebra and establish a link between tensor products of representations and simultaneous conjugacy classes.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: Title : Flow, geomechanics, and contact mechanics in fractured porous medium Speaker : Kundan Kumar, Porous Media Group, Department of Mathematics, University of Bergen, Norway. Using the tools of mathematics of contact mechanics, we analyse the…
nodes.text:
Title : Flow, geomechanics, and contact mechanics in fractured porous medium

Speaker : Kundan Kumar, Porous Media Group, Department of Mathematics, University of Bergen, Norway.

Abstract : Interaction of fluid such as CO2 with the host rock in the subsurface is of primary importance to evaluate the seismic hazard related to injection of fluids at depth. However, the role of fluid pressure in fault stability poses challenges in our understanding of seismic events. I will provide an overview of mathematical models describing flow and mechanics in the presence of fractures. The mixed dimensional, multiphysics model takes into account friction and normal forces including the effect of the fluid pressure. Using the tools of mathematics of contact mechanics, we analyse the mathematical model. Moreover, we develop and analyse temporal and spatial discretizations for numerically solving the governing equations. This is a joint work with Maarten de Hoop (Rice University), Mircea Sofonea (Perpignon), Omar Duran (Bergen), Solveig Stefansdottir (Bergen), Mary Wheeler (Austin), and Vivette Girault (Paris).

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: - Title : Counting, Curvature and Convex Duality. - Date and Time : 23rd September (Tuesday), 6:30 pm IST, Venue: Room 01-AC-2008.
nodes.text:
Please find the details below:
 - Speaker : Dr. Rajula Srivastava, Asst. Professor, University of Wisconsin-Madison.
- Title : Counting, Curvature and Convex Duality.
- Date and Time : 23rd September (Tuesday), 6:30 pm IST,  Venue: Room 01-AC-2008.
- Abstract : How many rational points with denominator of a given size lie within a specified distance from a compact, “non-degenerate” manifold? A precise answer to this question has significant implications for a host of problems including Diophantine approximation on manifolds. In this talk, I will describe how the analytic and geometric properties of the manifold critically influence this count and provide a heuristic for it. Further, I will talk about recent work which leverages Fourier analytic methods to establish the desired asymptotic for manifolds satisfying a strong curvature condition. At the heart of the proof is a bootstrapping argument that combines Poisson summation, convex duality, and oscillatory integral techniques.

Regularity and normality: regular rings are Cohen-Macaulay, the Auslander-Buchsbaum-Serre theorem, regular rings are UFDs (and thus normal), Serre’s normality criterion.

nodes.title: About Us
nodes.summary: The Department of Mathematics was started in 2016 together with the establishment of IIT Jammu. Apart from this the department is also offering a minor programme in Mathematics.
nodes.text:
The Department of Mathematics was started in 2016 together with the establishment of IIT Jammu. The vision of the department is to become a centre of excellence in Mathematics through high quality research and teaching.

Teaching

The department is offering various core and elective courses for all B. Tech. streams. Apart from this the department is also offering a minor programme in Mathematics. The minor programme in Mathematics is designed to allow undergraduate students to pursue a more rigorous education in mathematics.

The department has started its PhD program from August 2017. In addition, the department participates in the M. Tech. program in data science and information security.

Research

The department has at present energetic and dedicated faculty members in many diverse areas of mathematics. Currently, the main areas of research of the faculty members are Differential Equations, Numerical Analysis, Commutative Algebra, Group Theory, Number Theory, Finite Fields, Probability theory and Stochastic Processes.

nodes.summary: Mathematics colloquium on 16th October (Thursday), 4 pm IST hosted by the Department of Mathematics, IIT Jammu. - Title : Potential theory of invariant discretizations of diffusions in spaces of negative sectional curvature.
nodes.text:
Mathematics colloquium on 16th October (Thursday), 4 pm IST hosted by the Department of Mathematics, IIT Jammu.

Please find the details below:
 - Speaker : Dr. Debanjan Nandi, Indian Statistical Institute, Kolkata.
- Title : Potential theory of invariant discretizations of diffusions in spaces of negative sectional curvature.
- Abstract : I will discuss minimal positive harmonic, bounded harmonic and superharmonic functions associated with random walks which are obtained by discretizing invariant diffusions in a large class of discrete groups acting by isometries in spaces of negative sectional curvature. After going through some prerequisites, I will discuss results from a recent work with W. Ballmann and P. Polymerakis.

Date and Time: 16th October (Thursday), 4-5 pm IST, Venue: Room 01-AC-2008.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: - Date and Time : 25th September (Thursday), 4 pm IST, Venue: Room 01-AC-2008. - Abstract : Let $p$ and $ ell$ be primes such that $p 3$ and $p mid ell-1$ and $k$ be an even integer.
nodes.text:
Please find the details below:
 - Speaker : Dr. Shaunak Deo, IISc Bangalore.
- Title : The Eisenstein ideal of weight $k$ and ranks of Hecke algebras.
- Date and Time : 25th September (Thursday), 4 pm IST, Venue: Room 01-AC-2008.
- Abstract : Let $p$ and $\ell$ be primes such that $p > 3$ and $p \mid \ell-1$ and $k$ be an even integer. Using deformation theory of Galois representations, we will give a necessary and sufficient condition for the $Z_p$-rank of the completion of the Hecke algebra acting on the space of cuspidal modular forms of weight $k$ and level $\Gamma_0(\ell)$ at the maximal Eisenstein ideal containing $p$ to be greater than $1$ in terms of vanishing of the cup products of certain global Galois cohomology classes. We will begin with a quick review of modular forms.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: Title : Enumeration Formulae for Self-dual, Self-orthogonal and LCD Codes over Finite Commutative Chain Rings Abstract : In this talk, we will present enumeration formulae for all self-orthogonal, self-dual and linear with complementary dual (LCD)…
nodes.text:
Title : Enumeration Formulae for Self-dual, Self-orthogonal and LCD Codes over Finite Commutative Chain Rings

Abstract : In this talk, we will present enumeration formulae for all self-orthogonal, self-dual and linear with complementary dual (LCD) codes of an arbitrary length over finite commutative chain rings. These enumeration formulae are useful in classifying these three classes of codes up to equivalence, which we will illustrate in certain special cases. We will also show that the class of LCD codes over finite commutative chain rings is asymptotically good and that every free linear code over a finite commutative chain ring with at least five elements is equivalent to an LCD code. Besides this, we will explicitly determine all inequivalent LCD [n,1,d]-codes and [n,n-1,d]-codes over finite commutative chain rings for 1 \leq d \leq n. This is a joint work with Monika Yadav.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: Title : 2D V-line Tensor Tomography Abstract : The reconstruction problem of tensor fields has been studied in many classical works of integral geometry. The underlying problem involves the inversion of integral transforms of unknown tensor fields,…
nodes.text:
Title : 2D V-line Tensor Tomography

Abstract : The reconstruction problem of tensor fields has been studied in many classical works of integral geometry. The underlying problem involves the inversion of integral transforms of unknown tensor fields, including the longitudinal (Doppler) ray transform, mixed ray transform, transverse ray transform, and momentum ray transform of different orders. We consider a generalization of these transforms, in which the linear integration path is replaced either by V-lines (broken rays) or stars (a finite union of rays emanating from a common vertex). We first focus on the case of vector fields and present several exact closed-form inversion formulas along with some numerical simulations. Then we move to the case of symmetric 2-tensor fields and discuss recent developments in the area.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: - Title : Fractional derivatives and the equidistribution of Galois orbits. - Date and Time : 11th September (Thursday), 4-5 pm IST, - Venue : Room 01-AC-2008.
nodes.text:
Please find the details below:
 - Speaker : Prof. Emanuel Carneiro, ICTP, Trieste, Italy.
- Title : Fractional derivatives and the equidistribution of Galois orbits.
- Abstract : In this talk I will discuss effective versions of the celebrated Bilu's equidistribution theorem for Galois orbits of sequences of points of small height, identifying the qualitative dependence of the convergence in terms of the regularity of the test functions considered. I will present a general Fourier analysis framework that extends previous results in the literature. This is based on a joint work with Mithun Das (ICTP).
- Date and Time : 11th September (Thursday), 4-5 pm IST,
- Venue : Room 01-AC-2008.

nodes.text:
Please find the details below:
 - Speaker: Dr. Moni Kumari, Dept. of Mathematics, IIT Jammu.
- Title: Effective joint Sato-Tate distribution of Fourier coefficients of modular forms.
- Abstract: In this talk, we study the distribution of pairs of Fourier coefficients $(a_f(p),a_g(p)) $ of two non-CM, twist-inequivalent modular forms $f$ and $g$, as the prime $p$ varies. We show that for a large class of regions $U \subset [-2,2]^2$, the proportion of primes for which $(a_f(p),a_g(p))$ lies in $U$ is the joint Sato–Tate measure up to an explicit error term.

Date and Time:
 - 4th September (Thursday), 4-5 pm,
- Venue: Room 01-AC-2008.

nodes.summary: Title : GL 2 automorphic forms and associated L-function. Abstract : In this talk, we will briefly introduce the theory of GL 2 automorphic forms in the classical language.
nodes.text:
Title : GL_2 automorphic forms and associated L-function.

Abstract : In this talk, we will briefly introduce the theory of GL_2 automorphic forms in the classical language. Then we will look at the Fourier-Whittaker expansion and spectral expansion. We will attach the L-function to a Hecke-Maass cusp form. If time permits we will describe some of the unsolved problems. The basic reference for the talk will be the book of Goldfeld named Automorphic Forms and L-functions for the group GL_n(R).

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: Title : Modular representation theory of GL 2 over a finite field using calculus. Abstract : The mod p representation theory of GL 2(F q) where F q is a finite field with q = p^f elements is a classical subject going back to Richard Brauer.
nodes.text:
Title : Modular representation theory of GL_2 over a finite field using calculus.

Abstract : The mod p representation theory of GL_2(F_q) where F_q is a finite field with q = p^f elements is a classical subject going back to Richard Brauer. We use some differential operators (and even some sort of integration) to establish some new isomorphisms in the theory. One corollary of our work is that in the case of principal series we are able to deduce some new periodicity results. This is joint work with Arindam Jana.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: Title of the Talk : Galois Theory over Algebraic Curves- a brief overview Abstract : In this elementary talk, we will review some basic definitions of an algebraic curve and its 'Galois Covers'. We will mention results towards understanding the…
nodes.text:
Title of the Talk : Galois Theory over Algebraic Curves- a brief overview

Abstract : In this elementary talk, we will review some basic definitions of an algebraic curve and its 'Galois Covers'. We will establish a dictionary between such covers and certain sets equipped with the action of the fundamental groups. We will mention results towards understanding the fundamental groups, which remain an open and hard problem.

nodes.text:
The Department of Bioscience and Bioengineering (hereafter BSBE) at IIT Jammu is one of
 the seven engineering departments at IIT Jammu. Started in October 2021, the department
 aspires to cater to world-class research and training in a variety of areas that span
 engineering applications emerging from, and relevant to, the biological domain. It
 encourages strong interdisciplinary research and training across the different
 departments within the engineering and basic sciences school. Being situated in Jammu
 and proximal to the Western Himalayan region comprising the UT of Jammu and Kashmir and
 the UT of Ladakh, the department encourages research and student projects that address
 the unique challenges faced by the region in meeting sustainable development goals.


In line with the institute's vision, the department's goals focus on:
 - Pursuing impactful, world-class research in distinct areas for achieving excellence.
- Providing state-of-the-art teaching and training aimed at bioengineering-based product realization.
- Pursuing high-end interdisciplinary research through national and global collaborations.
- Addressing regional and national challenges offering sustainable solutions.
- Engaging in community outreach to popularize science to improve human health and environmental wellness.

 More](https://www.iitjammu.ac.in/bsbe/department-vision.html)

nodes.text:
The Department of Bioscience and Bioengineering (hereafter BSBE) at IIT Jammu is one of the seven engineering departments at IIT Jammu. Started in October 2021, the department aspires to cater to world-class research and training in a variety of areas that span engineering applications emerging from, and relevant to, the biological domain. It encourages strong inter-disciplinary research and training across the different departments within the engineering and basic sciences school.

Nestled amidst the breathtaking landscapes of the Jammu and Kashmir region, IIT Jammu offers a unique and inspiring setting for academic and professional pursuits. Surrounded by the majestic Himalayas, our campus provides an unparalleled environment for scholarly endeavors, fostering creativity and innovation.

We invite you to become a part of our vibrant academic community, where your expertise and contributions will play a pivotal role in shaping the future of bioscience and bioengineering. Join us in our quest for excellence as we embark on a journey towards transformative discovery and societal impact.

nodes.title: About Us
nodes.summary: The Department of Civil Engineering (CE) of IIT Jammu, one of six engineering departments, was started in the year 2017. The growing department has a faculty strength of 11, with persons active in research fields of Bridge Engineering, Seismic…
nodes.text:
The Department of Civil Engineering (CE) of IIT Jammu, one of six engineering departments, was started in the year 2017. The department offers four-year undergraduate courses, training the students in multiple core disciplines including Structural Analysis and Design, Traffic Engineering, Hydraulics, Construction Management, Environment Engineering, and Surveying, as well as exposure to interdisciplinary projects to enhance collaborative efforts and hone entrepreneurship skills. The department aligns its vision of being the collaborative innovation ambassador in Civil Engineering with the Institute’s mission objectives of passion for attempting to solve problems innovatively with a culture of mutual respect and creativity. Our mission is to offer world-class undergraduate and graduate education, research outcomes, industrial consultancy, and training in Civil Engineering.

Civil Engineering, globally, is at the verge of disruptive technology for sustainable construction methods, materials and energy efficient infrastructure. In the ever-evolving industrial scenario, the contents delivered to the students are regularly updated to successfully cope with these changes. The department also has research programs supported through government and industry funding. The growing department has a faculty strength of 11, with persons active in research fields of Bridge Engineering, Seismic Analysis, Ultra High Performance Concrete, Nondestructive Evaluation and Structural Health Monitoring, Fracture Mechanics, Novel Engineering Materials, Fatigue Modeling, Modeling of Fractured Materials, Computational Mechanics, Public Transportation, Rapid Transit Systems, Earthquake Risk Evaluation, Seismic retrofitting, Wind-structure Interaction, Groundwater and Contaminant Transport, Drought Modelling.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: IIT Jammu is situated in a prime location where lot of tunnelling activity takes place. Please refer detailed brochure Venue and Correspondence Department of Civil Engineering, IIT Jammu Jagti, Jammu.
nodes.text:
Two Days Workshop on Advances in Tunnel Engineering

Organized by Civil Engineering Department Indian Institute of Technology, Jammu

Course Objectives

Considering the increasing trend of construction of tunnels for roadways and railways, metros and hydro-projects, continuous refinement of knowledge with advances in tunnel technology is necessary.

Tectonically active Himalayan region poses difficult tunnelling problems due to high in situ stresses, mélange soil profile etc. Similarly, large amount of excavation works need to be carried out in a precise manner to prevent any damage to surrounding rock mass and structures. IIT Jammu is situated in a prime location where lot of tunnelling activity takes place. To cater the country’s need in the area of tunnel engineering including the necessary courses to educate students, carryout advanced research and knowledge distribution, a Center for Tunnel Research is planned to be opened on campus.

As a precursor, a workshop is planned to get all the experts under one roof to educate the audience with the state-ofthe-art tunnelling technology and to form a common platform for discussion. The workshop has been intended to disseminate the knowledge related to tunnelling by the experts from different academic institutes and industry for maximum benefit of the participants

For details regarding Couse Schedule, Registration, Important Dates and Registration Form. Please refer detailed brochure

nodes.title: Message
nodes.summary: My vision is to develop constructive thinking and analytical capabilities of every student of CIVIL Department. Our department is committed to provide students with a strong, broad based fundamental engineering education and prepare the students for…
nodes.text:
An affectionate welcome to the Civil Engineering discipline.Civil Engineering is a professional engineering discipline that deals with the design, construction and maintenance of physically built works like roads, bridges, canals, dams and buildings.

My vision is to develop constructive thinking and analytical capabilities of every student of CIVIL Department. Our department is committed to provide students with a strong, broad based fundamental engineering education and prepare the students for a career in the industry, teaching and national laboratories. We also plan to develop entrepreneurial skills in students so that they would drive the spirit of growth of our economy and would be able to generate employment opportunities for other qualified and skilled people. I look forward for preparing my students to face the challenges; the technology and engineering sector would offer in the future and succeed in offering technological solutions for the betterment of the society and our nation. Wishing all our students brilliant and bright future.

nodes.text:
The Department of Civil Engineering (CE) of IIT Jammu, one of six engineering departments, was started in the
year 2017. The department offers four-year undergraduate courses, training the students in multiple core disciplines
including Structural Analysis and Design, Traffic Engineering, Hydraulics, Construction Management, Environment
Engineering, and Surveying, as well as exposure to interdisciplinary projects to enhance collaborative efforts and
hone entrepreneurship skills.

nodes.summary: Many Congratulations to the students from the Civil Engineering Department, IIT Jammu, for securing the overall second position in the Inter IIT Civil Conclave 2022! The Inter-IIT Civil Conclave is an annual technical event hosted by the Civil…
nodes.text:
Many Congratulations to the students from the Civil Engineering Department, IIT Jammu, for securing the overall second position in the Inter IIT Civil Conclave 2022!

The Inter-IIT Civil Conclave is an annual technical event hosted by the Civil Engineering Consortium, Indian Institute of Technology Roorkee.

The conclave invites passionate civil engineering students from across all the IITs to compete and showcase their expertise in varied domains of Civil Engineering and gives them a forum to network with Civil Engineering Enthusiasts sharing the same interests and aspirations.

nodes.title: Message from Head of the Department
nodes.summary: Welcome to the webpage of the Department of Chemistry, IIT Jammu. Ramani Head of the Department, Department of Chemistry,

nodes.title: Message
nodes.summary: Welcome to the webpage of the Department of Chemistry, IIT Jammu. Ramani Head of the Department, Department of Chemistry,
nodes.text:
Welcome to the webpage of the Department of Chemistry, IIT Jammu. This webpage provides all the essential information about the department. In our department, teaching and research are synergistic to each other. Our research interests span various major areas of chemical sciences, ranging from synthetic organic and inorganic chemistry to computational, physical, and material chemistry. Indeed, chemistry is the central science, driving advancements across various domains to enhance human well-being. We emphasize the importance of interdisciplinary collaboration, integrating insights from physical sciences, materials science, and biological sciences to tackle complex challenges. In a short period, our department has rapidly established itself with advanced research capabilities, supported by both our dedicated departmental instrumentation facilities and the comprehensive central facility of the institute (SAPTARSHI lab). Our aim is to cultivate a collaborative and supportive environment where every member of our community can thrive and achieve their fullest potential in science and technology. We encourage you to explore the dynamic opportunities within our department and become part of our journey toward scientific and technological excellence. Should you have any questions or require further information, please do not hesitate to reach out to us. We look forward to engaging with you and welcoming you to our vibrant department

Dr. Guru B. Ramani

Head of the Department,

Department of Chemistry,

nodes.summary: About Department The Department of Chemistry came into existence since the inception of IIT Jammu in 2016. In addition to faculty members, the department has one Women Scientist.

nodes.title: About Us
nodes.summary: About Department The Department of Chemistry came into existence since the inception of IIT Jammu in 2016. In addition to faculty members, the department has one Women Scientist.
nodes.text:
About Department

The Department of Chemistry came into existence since the inception of IIT Jammu in 2016. Initially, the department was maintained by the faculty members of IIT Delhi. From 2017 onwards new faculty members started to join the department and till now the department has four faculty members and two more will be joining soon. In addition to faculty members, the department has one Women Scientist. Currently the department has one technical staff. The department is contributing to the undergraduate courses related to the B.Tech. program. The department is also running PhD program in Chemistry, which was started from July 2018. At present, the department has a total of seven PhD students including externally funded students from agencies like CSIR-UGC and DST INSPIRE. In addition, the department has one Junior Research Fellow funded by DST SERB.

Thrust areas of research of the department include asymmetric synthesis, hypervalent iodine chemistry, bio-mimetic and sustainable catalysis, organometallic chemistry, computational chemistry (photocatalytic water splitting), advanced transition-metal chemistry, colloids and surfactant chemistry. For research activities, the department is already well equipped with laboratories having several sophisticated analytical equipment and high configuration computer facilities. The department has a total of five externally funded research projects worth of approximately 1.6 crores from external funding agencies like DST.

nodes.title: Message
nodes.summary: Materials are ubiquitous, thus playing a cardinal role in enabling most engineering applications. We, at IIT Jammu, are a young and vibrant team, actively involved in developing R&D programs and teaching exciting topics in Materials, aimed at both…
nodes.text: Materials are ubiquitous, thus playing a cardinal role in enabling most engineering applications.  The famous quote by Richard Feynman "there is plenty of room at the bottom", is also apt for Materials Engineers, who strive to engineer the atomic-level structure of materials, in order to obtain desired properties.  We, at IIT Jammu, are a young and vibrant team, actively involved in developing R&D programs and teaching exciting topics in Materials, aimed at both undergraduate students and Ph.D. scholars.  The Department has made some not  insignificant strides in just over four years of its existence, by having faculty covering a range of research & teaching areas ranging from metals to polymers. In the upcoming years, we look forward to interacting with bright young minds, and making a positive impact on education as well as helping Industry and Society at large.

nodes.summary: We also encourage research in mathematical and algorithmic aspects of computing and have students and faculties working on parallel and distributed computing, optimization, compilers etc. I invite interested and motivated students and faculties to…
nodes.text: I welcome you on the behalf of the CSE Department. We are a small family today and growing at a rapid pace. We have a variety of students and young talents coming from all around India with a good mix of faculties from various fields of expertise making the department a place for quality research and teaching. We currently focus on areas that relates to Computer Security and Artificial Intelligence, Computer Vision and Machine Learning aspects of Computing. We also encourage research in mathematical and algorithmic aspects of computing and have students and faculties working on parallel and distributed computing, optimization, compilers etc. I invite interested and motivated students and faculties to look forward to IIT Jammu as a destination for high end research and teaching in India. Thank you.

nodes.text: Department of Computer Science and Engineering is from one of the very first departments established at IIT Jammu with a vision to foster teaching, research and development of technologies in areas of computing and information technology. The department has a good mix of young and experienced faculties in the domain of Cyber and Information Security, AI and Machine Learning, Software Engineering to name a few. The department runs a regular B.Tech programme and M.Tech programmes in Information Security and Data Science. Some of the focus areas of the department includes Post Quantum Cryptography, Future Internet Protocols and Security Developments, Artificial Intelligence, Data Science, Computer Vision and Machine Learning. The research scholars in the department are also working in a variety of challenging problems.

nodes.summary: Rajkumar Velu , PhD, is an Assistant Professor, Department of Mechanical Engineering and Dual Affilation: Department of Materials Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology Jammu and worked as Visiting Assistant Professor at NTUST Taiwan in…

nodes.title: Rajkumar V
nodes.summary: Rajkumar Velu , PhD, is an Assistant Professor, Department of Mechanical Engineering and Dual Affilation: Department of Materials Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology Jammu and worked as Visiting Assistant Professor at NTUST Taiwan in…
nodes.text:
Rajkumar Velu , PhD, is an Assistant Professor, Department of Mechanical Engineering and Dual Affilation: Department of Materials Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology Jammu and worked as Visiting Assistant Professor at NTUST Taiwan in 2024. Priorly worked as a visiting research associate (2023) and Research Fellow at the Centre for Laser Aided Intelligent Manufacturing (CLAIM), University of Michigan, USA, and as a Post-Doctoral Research Fellow at the Digital Manufacturing and Design Centre (DManD), Singapore University of Technology and Design, Singapore. He completed his PhD thesis at the Auckland University of Technology, New Zealand, in selective laser sintering of specific biopolymer composites for biomedical application. Dr Velu is also the recipient of the prestigious GATE scholarship, best all-rounder award, best outgoing student gold medal for his academic performances at the university level. Consequently, he has considerable insights and knowledge on different polymer, hydrogels and composite materials used explicitly for the 3D printing process. He also has substantial experience in material and component characterization targeting specific responses, evaluating the complex thermo-mechanical aspects of powder bed processing, extrusion processing, and post-mortem studies for mechanical, electrical and biological attributes. More than 30 publications were generated from his research activities, and he received a scientific research award at an international conference conducted by Philadelphia University, Amman, Jordan 2010. Also, a recipient of the “young scientist award” 2018 at the Annual Conference on 3D printing and bioprinting in Health care, Singapore. Dr Velu’s research interests encompass engineering design, optimization, and developing advanced manufacturing processes (Additive Manufacturing) and materials for designing and fabrication for fuel cell, biomedical and aerospace applications. Moreover, based on his current research practice, he is involved in developing Artificial Intelligence into an additive manufacturing process to fabricate end-use products and eliminate post-mortem analysis.

Dr. Rajkumar Velu’s research is centered on additive manufacturing (AM) and advanced materials engineering, with a particular emphasis on the development, processing, and application of polymeric, metallic, and composite materials for high-performance and functional components. His multidisciplinary research integrates experimental and computational approaches to explore the following key areas:
 - Selective laser sintering of reactive polymers
- Additive manufacturing of functionally graded structures
- Fabrication of multifunctional components using continuous carbon fibre 3D printing
- 3D bioprinting for biomedical and energy applications
- SLM and Direct energy deposition of super alloys for aerospace applications

PhD - Mechanical Engineering, Auckland University of Technology, New Zealand 2017

M.E - Engineering Design

nodes.title: Shanmugadas K.P
nodes.summary: Propulsion and Energy Research Lab Our research group specializes in the dynamic fields of aerospace engineering and propulsion. He also works closely with the National Centre for Combustion Research and Development (NCCRD) at IIT Madras, focusing…
nodes.text:
Propulsion and Energy Research Lab Our research group specializes in the dynamic fields of aerospace engineering and propulsion. Our primary mission is to develop next-generation micro gas turbine engine technology for energy production and aerospace applications.  Additionally, a key focus of our research is on the development of reusable liquid rocket engines, with a particular emphasis on utilizing throttleable METHALOX injectors. Brief Bio Dr. Shanmugadas, assistant professor within the Department of Mechanical Engineering at IIT Jammu, spearheads the Propulsion and Energy Lab, driving pioneering research and innovation related to liquid rocket engines and aero engines. With a primary focus on novel combustor concepts, his expertise extends across different areas including two-phase flow, experimental combustion, and laser flow diagnostics. Currently, his group is involved in the development of a novel gas turbine combustor capable of utilizing various fuels such as Hydrogen, methane, and SAF. Dr. Shanmugadas is recognized for his contributions to fuel injection systems with many publications and his group has also filed many patents. He is actively engaged with industry and governmental bodies like ISRO and GE Aerospace and promotes impactful collaborations. He also works closely with the National Centre for Combustion Research and Development (NCCRD) at IIT Madras, focusing on advancing gas turbine combustion research. Additionally, he collaborates with the Chair of Space Propulsion and Mobility at Technical University Munich, Germany, in the area of injection concepts for liquid rocket engines. Dr. Shanmugadas is a professional member of the American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics (AIAA) and a lifetime member of the Combustion Institute. He serves as the Associate Dean of Academic Affairs at IIT Jammu, contributing to the institution's academic excellence. Dr. Shanmugadas is the founder of SAP Aerospace, a pioneering deep-tech startup focused on developing cutting-edge aero engines for Unmanned Combat Aerial Vehicles (UCAVs).
 - BTech in Mechanical Engineering, 2006-2010, Calicut University
- MS and PhD in Aerospace Engineering, 2012-2018, IIT Madras

nodes.text:
Dr. Arvind Kumar Rajput is a faculty member in the Department of Mechanical Engineering specializing in Tribology, Fluid Film Bearings, and Advanced Lubrication Systems. His research focuses on hydrodynamic and hybrid journal bearings, porous and gas-lubricated bearings  functioing with advanced lubricants.  Dr. Rajput teaches Tribology, Solid Mechanics and Machine Design, and is a recipient of the Teaching Appreciation Award for his academic contributions for the academic year 2019-21. He also serves as a Board of Studies (BOS) Member at Government College of Engineering and Technology Jammu.

Tribological Design of Machine elements: Hydrodynamic/ hydrostatic  journal and thrust bearing), Tribological aspects in Wind turbine, TENG

nodes.title: Navneet Kumar
nodes.summary: Introduction to Mechanical Engineering 5. Energy Systems & Technologies Lab 3.
nodes.text:
Welcome to " Thermal and Natural Phenomenon Lab (TNPL) " Navneet Kumar is an Associate Professor in the Department of Mechanical Engineering at IIT Jammu. He received his Bachelor (B.Tech) & Masters (M.Tech) degrees from IIT (BHU) Varanasi in 2010 and Doctoral (PhD) degree from IISc Bangalore in 2017 all in Mechanical Engineering. His research interests lie in understanding the Natural processes and phenomena including fluid & energy flows and applying some of these to the existing and advent of cutting edge technological systems . He is further interested in developing suitable sensors (IoT based) for better and timely assessment across various disciplines inluding medical. Some of his personal interests include understanding air pollution dynamics (and devise mitigation techniques), understanding contamination of soil / aquifers / underground water (and decontaminate up to some extent). His research group explores various fundamental areas including the effect of phase changes (evaporation, transpiration, and evapotranspiration) in various systems like agriculture, surface water dynamics, and electronic cooling devices. KEY LAB (and connected) FACILITIES: Time-resolved stereo PIV PDPA (Phase Doppler Particle Analyzer) OLYMPUS Tilt Microscope (9000x magnification; -90 to +90 tilt; 3-D tomography) Contact Angle Meter (min. 1 microL drop) Infrared Camera (1280x960 pixels, with Telephoto Lens) Low, medium, and High Power Lasers (100mW to 6W) Optical Cameras Constant Temperature Bath (Convective type, 0.01 deg C) Ultra Precision Weighing Scales

Primary Area of Research Fluid mechanics & Thermal Engineering

Secondary Area of Research Evaporation, Phase change, Porous media, Multiphase flows, Capillarity, Electronic cooling devices, Agricultural technologies, Water resource management, Contamination & decontamination, Air Pollution, Atmospheric Water Generation, Wicks' Characteristics in connection to Electronic Cooling Devices.

B.Tech & M.Tech - 5 years Dual Degree programme (2005-2010): IIT (BHU) Varanasi

PhD (2017) : IISc Bangalore

nodes.title: Priyatosh Jena
nodes.summary: in Electrical and Electronics Engineering (2011–2015) International Institute of Information Technology Bhubaneswar, India Digital Control of Power Converters, Electric Vehicle Technology and Charging Systems, Network Theory, Power Converter Design,…
nodes.text:
I am Dr. Priyatosh Jena, currently serving as an Assistant Professor in the Department of Electrical Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology Jammu. I received my Ph.D. degree in Electrical Engineering from the Indian Institute of Technology (Banaras Hindu University), Varanasi, India, my M.Tech. degree in Electrical Engineering with specialization in Renewable Energy and Energy Management from the National Institute of Technology Arunachal Pradesh, India, and my B.Tech. degree in Electrical and Electronics Engineering from the International Institute of Information Technology, Bhubaneswar, India. I am a member of the IEEE Power Electronics Society (PELS) Educational Videos Committee. I was awarded the Chairman’s Gold Medal and the Institute Gold Medal from NIT Arunachal Pradesh in recognition of outstanding academic performance in the M.Tech program. I am also a recipient of the 14th GRID-INDIA Power System Awards (GIPSA) 2025–26.

Battery Charging System, Bidirectional Converter, Reconfigurable Power Converter, and Soft-Switched Converter.
 - Ph.D. in Electrical Engineering (2019–2025) Indian Institute of Technology (Banaras Hindu University), Varanasi, India Thesis Title: Soft-Switching Modulation Techniques for Bidirectional Dual Active Bridge Converter Based Battery Charging Systems Supervisor: Prof. Rajeev Kumar Singh and Prof. Vivek Nandan Lal
- M.Tech. in Renewable Energy and Energy Management (2016–2018) National Institute of Technology Arunachal Pradesh, India Thesis Title: Simulation and Study of Solar Powered Water Pumping System using BLDC Motor Supervisor: Dr. Rajen Pudur
- B.Tech. in Electrical and Electronics Engineering (2011–2015) International Institute of Information Technology Bhubaneswar, India

Digital Control of Power Converters, Electric Vehicle Technology and Charging Systems, Network Theory, Power Converter Design, Power Quality and FACTS Devices, Renewable Energy Systems, and Switch Mode Power Supplies.

1. Power Engineering (Even Semester 2026).

Bidirectional Converters, Electric Vehicle Charging Systems, Dual Active Bridge Converters Maximum Power Point Tracking, Renewable Energy Systems, Soft-Switched Converters

nodes.text:
Dr. Anup Shukla (Senior Member IEEE, Power and Energy Society, Industry Applications Society, IEEE Young Professionals). From June 2016 to Dec 2016, He was with the Department of Electrical Engineering, Indian Institute of Technology Kanpur as Senior Project Engineer. From Jan 2017 to Aug. 2017, He was with the Department of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science Engineering, Howard University, USA as Post-Doctorate. He is currently working as Visiting Faculty at Loughborough University from October 2023 to April 2024. He is currently working as an Associate Professor with the Department of Electrical Engineering, Indian Institute of Technology Jammu, India. He was the recipient of POSOCO Power System Award, 2017 and Dr. P.S. Nigam Power Sector Award, 2013.

His current research interest includes Robust and efficient system operation and planning, Renewable integration in power systems, Power system security, Power System Restructuring /Deregulation, Distribution System and Micro/Smart Grid

Indian Institute of Technology Kanpur, India July 2011-May 2016

Ph.D in Electrical Engineering

PEC University of Technology Chandigarh, India July 2007-July 2009

nodes.title: Priyanka Mishra
nodes.summary: in ENERGY SCIENCE AND ENGINEERING from INDIAN INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY KHARAGPUR (2017-2022) - M.Tech. in ENERGY SCIENCE AND ENGINEERING from INDIAN INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY KHARAGPUR (2015-2017) - B.Tech.
nodes.text:
Priyanka Mishra completed her Bachelor’s in Electrical Engineering from Galgotia’s College of Engineering and Technology, India in 2013. She received her M. Tech and Ph. D. degrees in Energy Science and Engineering from Indian Institute of Technology Kharagpur, India in 2017 and 2022, respectively. Her Masters’ work won as best post award by Indian Institute of Technology Kharagpur, India. Her Ph. D. work is awarded by POSOCO, India in doctoral category as one of the best Ph. D. Thesis in Power Systems. She worked as a Postdoctoral Associate at State University of New York at Stony Brook, New York, USA from April, 2022 to October, 2023. Key milestones during this research were development of provably resilient, programmable networked microgrids. She worked as HVDC Control and Protection Engineer at GE Renewables, Noida, India from November 2023 to January 2026. Out of her research, she published 7 journals and 5 conference papers, including one book chapter. She received outstanding reviewer award in IEEE Journal of Ocean Engineering in 2022. Her research interests include solar photovoltaic power generation and power system protection integrating inverter-based renewable energy sources.

Protection of microgrid integrating renewable energy sources (solar power, wind power, etc); Neural active fault management control of power-electronic converter-based renewable energy sources; Time-domain relaying; Converter transformer protection.

nodes.text:
Ibhan Chand Rath received the B.Tech. degree in Electrical and Electronics Engineering from the International Institute of Information Technology, Bhubaneswar, India, in 2014, and the Ph.D. degree in Electrical Engineering from IIT Bombay, India, in 2024. He worked as a System Integration and Testing Consultant at Igrenenergi Services Private Limited, Research Park, IIT BOMBAY, from May 2024 to December 2025. Currently, he is serving as Assistant Professor in the Electrical Engineering Department of IIT JAMMU from January 2026.

Grid connected hybrid multivelvel converter topology design, Flexible AC Transmission Systems (FACTs), High Voltage Direct Current (HVDC), Grid integration of Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS), Battery Management System (BMS), V2G and G2V EV charging applications,

Application of multilevel converters for simultaneous charging of multiple electric vehicles (EVs).

nodes.title: Shikha Baghel
nodes.summary: Speech and Audio Processing, Speaker Diarization, Language Diarization, Dialect/Language Identification, Applications of signal processing, Applications of Machine Learning and Deep Learning. 2024-Jul.2025 - Assistant Professor at ECE Dept.,…
nodes.text:
I am currently serving as an Assistant Professor in the Department of Electrical Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology Jammu (IIT Jammu). Prior to this, I held faculty positions as an Assistant Professor in the Department of Electrical Engineering at IIT Tirupati and in the Department of Electronics and Communication Engineering at the National Institute of Technology Karnataka (NITK), Surathkal. Before transitioning to academia, I worked as a Postdoctoral Researcher in the LEAP Lab at the Indian Institute of Science (IISc) Bangalore. I earned my Ph.D. in Signal Processing from the Department of Electronics and Electrical Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology Guwahati (IIT Guwahati). My research interests lie in the areas of speech and audio processing, speaker and language diarization, dialect and language identification, as well as the application of signal processing, machine learning, and deep learning techniques.

Speech and Audio Processing, Speaker Diarization, Language Diarization, Dialect/Language Identification, Applications of signal processing, Applications of Machine Learning and Deep Learning.

nodes.title: Gaurav Ashok Bhaduri
nodes.summary: PhD: University of Newcastle upon Tyne (Newcastle University), UK (2015) BE (Chemical), University of Pune (Now Savitribai Phule Pune University), Pune (2009) As an academic most of our time is dedicated towards students learning. Assistant…
nodes.text:
An enthusiastic and self-motivated researcher and academic. My vision is "Innovation with sustainability for society". Me and my research group look towards energy effective, bio-inspired, biochemical processes for solving current socitial problems. This is done by involving chemical engineering and biological science principles for process development using energy efficient intensification techniques.

Carbon capture storage and utilization (CCSU), Process Intensification, Environmental Engineering, Biochemical and Bioprocess Engineering, Waste to wealth, Nanomaterial synthesis and characterization, Photochemistry and Photocatalysis, Plasmonics, X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy, Heterogeneous catalysis (tri-phasic reactions), Nanofluids, Process design, Teaching and Learning in Higher Education (TLHE).

PhD: University of Newcastle upon Tyne (Newcastle University), UK (2015)

BE (Chemical), University of Pune (Now Savitribai Phule Pune University), Pune (2009)

As an academic most of our time is dedicated towards students learning. Classroom engagement is an important aspect of teaching. I work on development of new techniques of class room teaching. This is based on the epistemology represented in Indian thought and the mythological construct of the subcontinent. Within this theme we are focusing on many different aspect of Engineering Education
 - Active classroom engagement
- Development of indigenous pedagogy techniques for engineering education
- Active laboratory engagement and assessment
- Innovations in summative assessment

I have taught various course at the UG, PG and PhD level in Chemical Engieering and other desiplines (such as Bioscience and Bioengineering and Humanities and Social Sciences).

I have worked in various institutes over the world to learn and grow. Below is a list of the places where I have worked.

Assistant Professor- Department of Chemical Engineering, IIT Jammu (July, 2019- till date)

Assistant Professor- Department of Chemical Engineering, Manipal University Jaipur, (July 2017- Jun 2019)

Institute Post-doctoral Fellow, Indian Institute of Technology Kharagpur, (Apr -Jun 2017)

Research Assistant, School of Chemical Engineering and Advanced Materials, Newcastle University, UK (Dec 2013- Aug, 2014)

Visiting Scholar, The Institute of Advanced Energy and Powertrain Technology, Shanghai Jiao Tong University, PR China (May-June 2012)

Teaching Scholar, School of Chemical Engineering and Advanced Materials, Newcastle University, UK (Sept 2011- Jun 2014)

Lecturer, Department of Chemical Engineering, AISSMS College of Engineering, Pune (Affiliated to Pune University), (Jan 2010- Sept 2010)

Research Trainee, Department of Chemical Engineering and Process Development, National Chemical Laboratory, Pune (Jun 2008- Mar 2009)

nodes.title: Shirsendu Ghosh
nodes.summary: Chemistry (Hons), Jadavpur University, 2006-2009, 2009 - M.Sc. Chemistry, IIT Kharagpur, 2009-2011, 2011 - Ph.D.
nodes.text:
Dr. Shirsendu Ghosh did his graduation from Jadavpur University with honors in Chemistry (2009). Then he obtained his master’s degree in Chemistry from IIT Kharagpur (2011). He received his Ph.D. degree from the Indian Association for the Cultivation of Science (IACS), Kolkata in 2016. After a Postdoctoral stint at Weizmann Institute of Science, Israel (2016-2021) he joined as a research associate at Cornell University, USA (2021-2022). After that, he served as an Assistant Professor in the Dept. of Chemistry, GITAM Hyderabad for two years (2022-2024). Then he joined as an Assistant Professor in the Dept. of Chemistry with a joint affiliation in the Department of Bioscience and Bioengineering at IIT Jammu on December 2024. His area of research revolves around the development and use of new optical spectroscopy and microscopy techniques to decipher the dynamics of biological systems and live cells. More specifically, he developed total internal reflection fluorescence microscopy-based super-resolution microscopic techniques to investigate the role of plasma membrane topography in cancer biology and immunology (such as T-cell signaling pathways and Mast cell signaling pathways). He is also interested in deciphering the consequences of long-range charge reorganization within a protein on the allosteric signaling of proteins which may lead to the development of a pathway to control allosteric signaling. He employs single-molecule fluorescence microscopy and time-resolved confocal microscopy techniques to decipher the changes in polarity, viscosity, and dynamics of different regions of biomolecules (such as proteins, DNA, lipid vesicles, etc.) and live cells. He is also interested in the development of low-cost protein-coated copper nanoclusters for drug delivery and ion sensing. He is the recipient of the prestigious Ramanujan Fellowship (SERB), DST-inspired faculty fellowship, Koshland Prize (Koshland Foundation, Israel), and CSIR-Research Fellowship.

Development of Super/high resolution optical microscopy and Single Molecule Spectroscopy technique, Membrane Biophysics, Immunological signaling pathways, Allosteric Signaling in Proteins
 - B.Sc. Chemistry (Hons), Jadavpur University, 2006-2009, 2009
- M.Sc. Chemistry, IIT Kharagpur, 2009-2011, 2011
- Ph.D. (Biophysical Chemistry), Indian Association for the Cultivation of Science, Kolkata (Jadavpur University), 2011-2016, 2016 (Supervisor: Late Prof. Kankan Bhattacharyya)
 - Assistant Professor (Dec 2024- till date): Department of Chemistry with a joint affiliation in the Department of Bioscience and Bioengineering, IIT Jammu
- Assistant Professor (Dec 2022- Dec 2024): Department of Chemistry, GITAM School of Science, GITAM University, Hyderabad, India. (MURTI Faculty)
- Research Associate (Nov 2021- Nov 2022): Department of Chemistry and Chemical Biology, Cornell University, USA
- Academic Consultant (March 2021- Oct 2021): Department of Chemical and Biological Physics, Weizmann Institute of Scienc

nodes.title: Uday Kiran Roopavath
nodes.summary: - Subingya Pandey, Chinmaya Panda, Lalit M Pandey, Uday Kiran Roopavath, Pamu Dobbidi. Biomedical Materials & Devices (2024).
nodes.text:
Dr. Uday Kiran Roopavath has interdisciplinary expertise in biomedical engineering, nanotechnology, and biotechnology. He has research interests in advanced biofabrication techniques such as 3D printing, 3D bioprinting and scaffold free fabrication techniques involving spheroids and organoids. Currently his research focuses on developing functional materials for bone regeneration, fabrication of tissue engineered blood vessels and organ on chip devices for disease modeling applications. He has supervised multiple Master’s and currently supervising 3 Ph.D. students, founded startup (Medoja Bio Private Limited) in MedTech, and has active collaborations with premier international institutes. His contributions span from biomaterials research to translational medical devices.

Tissue engineering, Biofabrication, Biomaterials, Bioengineered organoids and Biodesign

Ph.D. Biomedical engineering from IIT Hyderabad (2015-2020)

Assistant Professor in the Department of Medical Devices, NIPER Guwahati from July 2023 to May 2025.

Biomaterials science, Biodesign and biofabrication of artificial organs, and Regulatory for medical devices.

Bioinspired design of medical devices like cardiovascular stents, Self healing hydrogels for organoid fabrication and Organ on a chip devices, Assistant Professor in the Department of Medical Devices, NIPER Guwahati from July 2023 to May 2025.

Regenerative bone fillers and custom scaffolds with a inherent angiogenic ability for periodontal diseases, Principal investigator, BIRAC BIG Call 14, Dec 2019 to Dec 2022.

nodes.title: Jigmi Basumatary
nodes.summary: Institute: Indian Institute of Science, Bengaluru, India Research area: Single molecule localization microscopy. (2024 – 2025) Institute: California Institute of Technology, Pasadena, USA.
nodes.text:
Dr. Jigmi Basumatary has been an Assistant Professor in the Department of Biosciences and Bioengineering at the Indian Institute of Technology Jammu since February 2026. His research focuses on the development and advancement of diverse optical imaging and manipulation tools for studying biological systems.

Dr. Jigmi’s research interests include key technologies such as single-molecule localization microscopy (SMLM), light-sheet microscopy, optical tweezers, and photoacoustic/ultrasound imaging. He adopts a multidisciplinary approach that integrates optical engineering with other science and engineering disciplines, along with rigorous qualitative and quantitative analysis. The overarching goal of his research is to develop and extend imaging techniques to address fundamental biological questions.

nodes.text:
An enthusiastic and self-motivated researcher and academic. My vision is "Innovation with sustainability for society". Me and my research group look towards energy effective, bio-inspired, biochemical processes for solving current socitial problems. This is done by involving chemical engineering and biological science principles for process development using energy efficient intensification techniques.

Carbon capture storage and utilization (CCSU), Process Intensification, Environmental Engineering, Biochemical and Bioprocess Engineering, Waste to wealth, Nanomaterial synthesis and characterization, Photochemistry and Photocatalysis, Plasmonics, X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy, Heterogeneous catalysis (tri-phasic reactions), Nanofluids, Process design, Teaching and Learning in Higher Education (TLHE).

PhD: University of Newcastle upon Tyne (Newcastle University), UK (2015)

BE (Chemical), University of Pune (Now Savitribai Phule Pune University), Pune (2009)

As an academic most of our time is dedicated towards students learning. Classroom engagement is an important aspect of teaching. I work on development of new techniques of class room teaching. This is based on the epistemology represented in Indian thought and the mythological construct of the subcontinent. Within this theme we are focusing on many different aspect of Engineering Education
 - Active classroom engagement
- Development of indigenous pedagogy techniques for engineering education
- Active laboratory engagement and assessment
- Innovations in summative assessment

I have taught various course at the UG, PG and PhD level in Chemical Engieering and other desiplines (such as Bioscience and Bioengineering and Humanities and Social Sciences).

I have worked in various institutes over the world to learn and grow. Below is a list of the places where I have worked.

Assistant Professor- Department of Chemical Engineering, IIT Jammu (July, 2019- till date)

Assistant Professor- Department of Chemical Engineering, Manipal University Jaipur, (July 2017- Jun 2019)

nodes.title: Dnyaneshwar Ramloo Bhawangirkar
nodes.summary: Dnyaneshwar Bhawangirkar is presently serving as an Assistant Professor in the department of chemical engineering at IIT Jammu in India. - Ph.D., Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Bombay, India - M.Tech, Indian Institute of Technology (IIT)…
nodes.text:
Dr. Dnyaneshwar Bhawangirkar is presently serving as an Assistant Professor in the department of chemical engineering at IIT Jammu in India. His research interest is in ‘Gas Hydrates or Clathrate Hydrates’ focusing on various applications, including methane recovery from the natural gas hydrates present beneath the seafloor, carbon sequestration in geological marine sedimentary locations, hydrogen and natural gas storage, and flow assurance through both modelling and experimental approaches. He is also exploring different strategies for improving the oil recovery via novel EOR technologies. Kindly email at dnyaneshwar.bhawangirkar@iitjammu.ac.in for any possible collaborations.

Gas hydrates or clathrate hydrates for their applications in NGH development, CCS, energy storage and flow assurance (Modelling and Experiments)
 - Methane recovery from natural gas hydrates (NGH development)
- Carbon capture and sequestration in porous medium as hydrates (CCS)
- Natural gas and hydrogen storage as hydrates (Energy storage)
- Hydrate inhibition in oil and gas pipelines for flow assurance

Additionally, we are also equally interested in developing strategies for enhancing the oil recovery (EOR) through different approaches.
 - Ph.D., Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Bombay, India

nodes.summary: Faculty selection at IIT Jammu is by rolling advertisement. For the present special drive, 31st October 2021 shall be the cutoff date for the batch of application processing.
nodes.text:
Indian Institute of Technology Jammu, one of the leading third generation IITs in India engaged in teaching, research and development is seeking faculties for various academic units. The institute invites application from qualified Indian Nationals, Persons of Indian Origins (PIOs), Overseas Citizens of India (OCIs) and Foreign Nationals belonging to SC/ST/OBC/EWS/PwD category possessing excellent and consistent academic credentials with a passion for teaching and cutting edge research for the faculty positions, for its various departments and centers.

Faculty selection at IIT Jammu is by rolling advertisement. Usually all the applications shall be processed in multi stage screening and interaction stages after submission at the portal. For the present special drive, 31st October 2021 shall be the cutoff date for the batch of application processing. Candidates those who have submitted their applications in last 6 months in any of the following disciplines at our portal, need not apply again.

The applications received through IIT Jammu online portal shall only be considered.

To submit an application please visit: https://apply.iitjammu.ac.in and select the tab Regular Faculty Search to apply.

Note :-

Regular Faculty, Visiting Faculty (including Retired Faculty)

IIT Jammu invites applications from energetic meritorious qualified Indian Nationals, Persons of Indian Origins (PIOs) and Overseas Citizens of India (OCIs) and Foreign Nationals having passion for teaching and research for the faculty positions at the level of Professor, Associate Professor, Assistant Professor (Grade I) and Assistant Professor (Grade II) and Visiting Professor (on contract and deputation) / Retired Professors (on contract) for its various academic programs.

Disciplines: Chemical Engg., Chemistry, Civil Engg., Computer Science and Engg., Electrical Engg., Humanities and Social Science, Material Science and Engg., Mathematics, Mechanical Engineering, and Physics.


This is a rolling advertisement and there is no last date . The applications received through IIT Jammu online portal shall only be considered. Usually, all the applications shall get processed as soon as they are submitted to the portal. However, the applications received by November 30, 2019, or before will be considered in the present round of the selection process.

Note :-
