nodes.text:
Faculty Search Abroad

IIT Jammu invites applications from Indian Nationals, Persons of Indian Origins (PIOs), Overseas Citizens of India (OCIs), and Foreign Nationals with proven academic credentials and who have a passion for teaching and research, for faculty positions at the level of Professor, Associate Professor, Assistant Professor (Grade I & II) and Visiting Professor in the following disciplines:

Chemical Engineering, Chemistry, Civil Engineering, Computer Science & Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Humanities & Social Science, Material Science & Engineering, Mathematics, Mechanical Engineering, and Physics.

Applicants should hold a Ph.D. degree in one of the above disciplines. Industry experience and/or post-doctoral experience is highly desirable. Candidates must have a passion for excellence in both research and teaching.

The venue is to be finalized shortly and shall be shared with the shortlisted candidates via their registered email IDs. For the faculty search-cum-selection committee interaction, the shortlisted candidates will be required to give an e-seminar (skype). Candidates shortlisted at this stage will be invited for interaction with the faculty search-cum-selection committee. The interaction shall assess research and teaching potential. We would also like to look for your value edition and possible contribution for building IIT Jammu as a world class Institution. Each candidate should regularly check their registered email ID for information regarding the schedule of interaction.


For online application submission please visit: https://apply.iitjammu.ac.in and select the tab Faculty Search Abroad to apply.

Archived advertisements

Faculty on Deputation

IIT Jammu invites applications from well qualified Indian Nationals having a passion for teaching and research and who are already employed at Central Educational Institutes (CEIs) in India for faculty positions on deputation basis at the level of Professor, Associate Professor, and Assistant Professor (Grade I) in the following disciplines:

Chemical Engg., Chemistry, Civil Engg., Computer Science & Engg., Electrical Engg., Humanities & Social Science, Material Science & Engg., Mathematics Mechanical Engineering, and Physics.

For online application submission please visit: https://apply.iitjammu.ac.in and select the tab Faculty on Deputation to apply.

This is a rolling standing advertisement and there is no last date as such. The applications received through IIT Jammu online portal shall only be considered. Usually, all the applications shall get processed as soon as they are submitted to the portal.

Note :-

(a) Maximum age – up to 60 years of age.

(b) The appointment of faculty on deputation shall be governed by MHRD guidelines for the mobility of faculty to the newly established Central Educational Institutes (CEIs). http://mhrd.gov.in/sites/upload_files/mhrd/files/MohilityofFaculty.pdf

(c) Applications received till May 01, 2019 for On-Deputation Faculty have been processed and shortlisted applicants were notified.

nodes.title: Faculty Search Cum Selection
nodes.summary: Our application system for faculty positions is completely online. Please submit a fresh application for a particular round to be considered in that round itself (Rounds : January - June and July - December) - For any technical query regarding…
nodes.text:
Our application system for faculty positions is completely online. Applications submitted through IIT Jammu online portal shall only be considered.
 - Please read detailed advertisement first for important announcements and then visit Application Portal using the link provided. New users are required to register first using a valid email address. This email address cannot be changed later, therefore, it is recommended to register using your personal email id.
- Once registered, please activate your login and fill out the required information on the portal.
- Applications are processed at regular intervals. No interim communication shall be entertained about the status of the submitted application
- If the candidate created the application in one round and wants to submit in the next round, due to regular portal enhancements this is not allowed. Please submit a fresh application for a particular round to be considered in that round itself (Rounds : January - June and July - December)
- For any technical query regarding submission of application, please contact : 0191-257-0630 (Mon - Fri, between 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM)
- For any query related to the faculty position advertisement, write an email to faculty [DOT] recruitment [AT] iitjammu [DOT] ac [DOT] in

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: My research focuses on Utilization of Wastes in Concrete, Durability of Concrete, Concrete Repairs and Non-destructive Testing of Concrete. I have been involved in supervising projects in the area of Durability of High-Volume Fly ash Concrete,…
nodes.text:
Before taking up this industrial assignment, I worked in academia as Professor & Head of Structural Engineering, Coordinator of Centre for Building Technology and Construction Materials at Malaviya National Institute of Technology, Jaipur and Director of Anand International College of Engineering, Jaipur. I also worked at Liverpool John Moores University, Liverpool (U.K.) and Curtins Consulting Engineers (U.K.) under U.K. India REC’s Project for six months in the area of Structural Design and Construction.

My research focuses on Utilization of Wastes in Concrete, Durability of Concrete, Concrete Repairs and Non-destructive Testing of Concrete. I have been involved in supervising projects in the area of Durability of High-Volume Fly ash Concrete, Self-compacting Concrete, Use of Wollastonite in Concrete and Durability of Concrete Containing ISF Slag, Geopolymer Concrete, Use of Jarosite in Concrete, etc. I have also been associated with collaborative research project "High Performance Sustainable Concrete Infrastructure" jointly with University of Dundee (U.K.) and eight Indian Institutions, funded by UK India Education and research Initiative (UKIERI). I also executed more than 200 consultancy projects including Jaipur Metro Project.

Presently I am looking for motivated Post-Doctoral/ PhD/ Internship candidates who would be interested in working on development of low carbon cement, Non-destructive Testing with IoT applications and Prestressed Composite bridges, High Strength Steel welding. Interested persons may send an email: anurag.misra@iitjammu.ac.in

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: His research focuses on enhancing the recyclability of end-of-life vehicle (ELV) plastics , optimizing pre-treatment processes , and improving feedstock quality to drive sustainable material recovery . Keywords: ELV Plastics; Resource recovery;…
nodes.text:
His research focuses on enhancing the recyclability of end-of-life vehicle (ELV) plastics , optimizing pre-treatment processes , and improving feedstock quality to drive sustainable material recovery . He is particularly interested in addressing the identification challenges of black plastics , a growing concern in automotive and waste electrical and electronic equipment ( WEEE) plastic recycling industries , and assessing the performance indicators of compounded materials .

His earlier work extensively explored the formation, distribution, and impact of microplastics (MPs) in technical and environmental systems. He is dedicated to developing efficient methodologies for detecting, identifying, and quantifying MPs , aiming to systematically understand their fate, transport, and interactions with organic and chemical pollutants .

Through his research and academic engagement, he strives to contribute to circular economy solutions, sustainable waste management strategies, and innovative recycling approaches that bridge scientific advancements with industry applications .

Keywords: ELV Plastics; Resource recovery; Circular economy; Solid waste management; Plastic waste management; Recyclability assessment; Fate and Transport of Microplastic; Standardization of Microplastic Assessment

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: Navneet Kumar is an Associate Professor in the Department of Mechanical Engineering at IIT Jammu. He received his Bachelor (B.Tech) & Masters (M.Tech) degrees from IIT (BHU) Varanasi in 2010 and Doctoral (PhD) degree from IISc Bangalore in 2017 all…
nodes.text:
Navneet Kumar is an Associate Professor in the Department of Mechanical Engineering at IIT Jammu. He received his Bachelor (B.Tech) & Masters (M.Tech) degrees from IIT (BHU) Varanasi in 2010 and Doctoral (PhD) degree from IISc Bangalore in 2017 all in Mechanical Engineering.

His research interests lie in understanding the Natural processes and phenomena including fluid & energy flows and applying some of these to the existing and advent of cutting edge technological systems . He is further interested in developing suitable sensors (IoT based) for better and timely assessment across various disciplines inluding medical. Some of his personal interests include understanding air pollution dynamics (and devise mitigation techniques), understanding contamination of soil / aquifers / underground water (and decontaminate up to some extent).

His research group explores various fundamental areas including the effect of phase changes (evaporation, transpiration, and evapotranspiration) in various systems like agriculture, surface water dynamics, and electronic cooling devices.

KEY LAB (and connected) FACILITIES:

Time-resolved stereo PIV

PDPA (Phase Doppler Particle Analyzer)

OLYMPUS Tilt Microscope (9000x magnification; -90 to +90 tilt; 3-D tomography)

Contact Angle Meter (min. 1 microL drop)

Infrared Camera (1280x960 pixels, with Telephoto Lens)

Low, medium, and High Power Lasers (100mW to 6W)

Optical Cameras

Constant Temperature Bath (Convective type, 0.01 deg C)

Ultra Precision Weighing Scales

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: The Alumni Meetup with the Bangalore chapter took place on December 9, 2023, at the Indian Institute of Science (IISC) in Bangalore. An especially notable guest was Anil Pandit ji, a distinguished individual from the 1970 batch of IIT Bombay alumni,…
nodes.text:
The Alumni Meetup with the Bangalore chapter took place on December 9, 2023, at the Indian Institute of Science (IISC) in Bangalore. The event was a great success, bringing together alumni from various batches. An especially notable guest was Anil Pandit ji, a distinguished individual from the 1970 batch of IIT Bombay alumni, who graced the occasion with his presence.

The meetup provided a platform for reconnecting with old friends, reminiscing about shared experiences, and celebrating the enduring bond among the alumni. Anil Pandit ji's participation added a special touch to the event, and his insights and experiences from his time at IIT Bombay in the 1970s enriched the gathering.

The venue, IISC Bangalore, provided an ideal setting for the reunion, fostering a sense of nostalgia and camaraderie. aLUMNI’S had the opportunity to interact with esteemed faculty members, including Prof. Kannan Iyer, Dr. Gaurav Varshney, and Mr. Manoj Aggarwal. These interactions allowed alumni to gain insights into the current developments and achievements of their alma mater.

Overall, the Meetup was a memorable occasion, creating a vibrant atmosphere of unity and shared history. The event not only facilitated networking but also served as a platform for the exchange of ideas and experiences between alumni and the distinguished faculty members present.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: He completed his Master’s in Engineering Mechanics from the Applied Mechanics Department at IIT Delhi, where he received the Prof. Karunesh Memorial Award for his thesis on visco-hyperelastic analysis of the ACL.
nodes.text:
He earned his PhD in Applied Mathematics with Excellent Cum Laude from CIMNE–UPC, Barcelona, Spain, where he worked on structure-preserving numerical strategies for optimal control of mechanical systems. He completed his Master’s in Engineering Mechanics from the Applied Mechanics Department at IIT Delhi, where he received the Prof. B. Karunesh Memorial Award for his thesis on visco-hyperelastic analysis of the ACL.

He previously worked at Eaton and Schlumberger as a Modeling and Simulation Engineer and later as a Research Engineer (Wells) in the Geomechanics Group at ExxonMobil, where he conducted research in multiscale modeling, uncertainty quantification, and inverse problems related to the mechanics of hydraulic fracturing in unconventional reservoirs.

His current research explores structure-preserving and multiscale computational frameworks for soft and living systems, with applications in tissue morphogenesis, biomedical devices, and bioinspired soft robotics.

nodes.text:
Recent news from the group:

* Our new work on resonant Edelstein and inverse Edelstein effects, charge-to-spin conversion, and spin pumping from chiral spin modes has been accepted for publication in Physical Review B [DOI: https://doi.org/10.1103/5p54-96d7] as a contribution to a special issue dedicated to the memory of late Emmanuel Rashba.

* Our recent work on a tight-binding and density-functional investigation of the Raman tensor in 2D massive Dirac fermion systems has been published in Physical Review B [https://link.aps.org/doi/10.1103/m1p1-w3w4].

He received his Ph.D. in 2010 from the Indian Institute of Technology Bombay under the supervision of Professor Sudhir R. Ghorpade.

His research interests include various aspects of finite fields and their applications.

For more details, please visit his homepage: https://sites.google.com/site/sartajulhasan/

nodes.title: Dr. Arun Kumar Verma, Associate Professor, Electrical Engineering, has been appointed as an Associate Editor for the IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Industrial Electronics
nodes.summary: Arun Kumar Verma, Associate Professor, Electrical Engineering, has been appointed as an Associate Editor for the IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Industrial Electronics (JESTIE). Verma’s contributions to advancing research and…
nodes.text:
We are proud to share that Dr. Arun Kumar Verma, Associate Professor, Electrical Engineering, has been appointed as an Associate Editor for the IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Industrial Electronics (JESTIE).

JESTIE publishes pioneering work on emerging trends and innovations in industrial electronics, shaping the future of this dynamic field.

This recognition reflects Dr. Verma’s contributions to advancing research and knowledge in electrical and industrial electronics.

nodes.summary: I was a visiting fellow at TIFR Mumbai from September 2020 to July 2022. Subsequently, I joined CMI with NBHM in August 2022, and transferred to ISI Kolkata in August 2023.
nodes.text: After finishing my integrated MSc from NISER Bhubaneswar I joined ISI Bangalore as a PhD student under Prof. Manish Kumar. My PhD work was on understanding covers of curves in positive characteristic, leading to my dissertation `On the Inertia Conjecture and its Generalizations' that generalizes and provides numerous evidence for a long standing conjecture posed by Abhyankar. I was a visiting fellow at TIFR Mumbai from September 2020 to July 2022. Subsequently, I joined CMI with NBHM in August 2022, and transferred to ISI Kolkata in August 2023.

nodes.text: Dr. Ambika Prasad Shah, Assistant Professor, Electrical Engineering Department is elected as a Fellow IETE. The Institution of Electronics and Telecommunication Engineers (IETE) is India's leading recognized professional society devoted to the advancement of Science and Technology of Electronics, Telecommunication & IT. Dr. Shah becomes one of the youngest Fellows of IETE. The IETE Fellow is conferred to a person who has achieved eminence through the quality of his work in the field of Electronics Engineering.

nodes.title: Abhishek Kumar
nodes.summary: Quantum polar phase transition Transport in correlated electron systems Fermi-liquid with spin-orbit coupling Theory of Raman, electron spin resonance (ESR) and electric-dipole spin resonance (EDSR) spectroscopies Strongly correlated systems:…
nodes.text:
I am a condensed matter theorist interested in exploring dynamical and transport properties of systems away or close to quantum critical point of certain types. I joined IIT Jammu as an Assistant Professor in March, 2025. I look forward to doing exciting science in collaboration with enthusiastic students and postdocs. Recent news from the group: * Our new work on resonant Edelstein and inverse Edelstein effects, charge-to-spin conversion, and spin pumping from chiral spin modes has been accepted for publication in Physical Review B [DOI: https://doi.org/10.1103/5p54-96d7] as a contribution to a special issue dedicated to the memory of late Emmanuel Rashba. * Our recent work on a tight-binding and density-functional investigation of the Raman tensor in 2D massive Dirac fermion systems has been published in Physical Review B [https://link.aps.org/doi/10.1103/m1p1-w3w4].

Quantum polar phase transition

Transport in correlated electron systems

Fermi-liquid with spin-orbit coupling

Theory of Raman, electron spin resonance (ESR) and electric-dipole spin resonance (EDSR) spectroscopies

Strongly correlated systems: cuprates high temperature superconductors, flat-band systems

PhD (Aug 2013 - Aug 2020): University of Florida, Gainesville, USA

Integrated BS-MS (Aug 2008 - Jun 2013): IISER Kolkata, India

Assistant Professor, IIT Jammu (Mar 2025 - present)

Research interests - Theoretical condensed matter physics

Teaching interests - Solid State Physics, Statistical Mechanics, Electromagnetic theory and Quantum Mechanics

Autumn (2025-26) - Semester 25-01: PH104 (TO) Waves and Oscillations

PH101 (PO) UG Physics laboratory

Spring (2025-26) - Semester 25-02: PH103 (TO) Electrodynamics

PH621 (TO) Advanced Mathematical Physics (Multi-faculty course)

Postdoctoral experiences:

Florida State University and National High Magnetic Field Lab (Quantum Initiative Postdoctoral Fellow) (Sept 2024 - Mar 2025) , Area of work - Dynamical and Transport properties of electronic systems

Université de Sherbrooke (Aug 2022 - Sept 2024) , Area of work - charge nematicity in cuprates

Rutgers, The State University of New Jersey, USA (Sept 2020 - July 2022) , Area of work - Polar metals

Selected publications:

1) Abhishek Kumar , D. Sénéchal, and A.-M. S. Tremblay, “Cluster dynamical mean-field study of intra-unit-cell charge nematicity in hole-doped cuprates,” Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences, vol. 122, no. 10, e2419534122 (2025).

2) Abhishek Kumar , V. I. Yudson, and D. L. Maslov, “Quasiparticle and nonquasiparticle transport in doped quantum paraelectrics,” Phys. Rev. Lett., vol. 126, p. 076601 (2021).

3) A. S. Mishchenko, L. Pollet, N. V. Prokof’ev, Abhishek Kumar , D. L. Maslov, and N. Nagaosa, “Polaron mobility in the “beyond quasiparticles” regime,” Phys. Rev. Lett., vol. 123, p. 076601 (2019).

4) Abhishek Kumar , P. Chandra, and P. A. Volkov, “Phonon-induced collective modes in spin-orbit coupled polar metals,” (Editor’s suggestion) Phys. Re

nodes.title: Ankita Yadav
nodes.summary: Ankita Yadav is an Assistant Professor of Law at Dr. Ankita Yadav, Right to Privacy and Data Protection: Special Reference to India, Satyam Law International, 2023.(Book) 2.
nodes.text:
Dr. Ankita Yadav is an Assistant Professor of Law at Dr. RMLNLU. She completed her LL.B.(Hons.) from Banaras Hindu University in 2013 and her LL.M. from Dr. RMLNLU in 2015, and she was awarded a silver medal in her LL.M. She cleared the NET(JRF) and All India Bar Council in 2015 and joined Dr. RMLNLU as an Assistant Professor in 2016. She also completed her Doctorate in 2021 and worked on Right to Privacy in India with a special focus on Digital Privacy. She also completed a short-term certificate course in 2019, organised by the International Union for Conservation of Nature. In April 2023, she was part of a round table meet on ChatGPT-Beginning of the End of Juris Copyright, organised by Hidayaullah National Law University. She worked as a trainee in the Corporate Law Department of Bharat Heavy Electricals Limited, Delhi and worked under the able guidance of Senior Advocate Rana Mritunjay Singh at the High Court (Lucknow Bench).

Digital Divide, AI and Data Protection, Judgement and Decision Making, Critical Thinking and Interpretation

nodes.title: Garima Singh

nodes.text:
Neural mechanisms of reward processing, decision-making and aberrant behaviours in clinical versus healthy populations through neuroimaging methods, delay discounting, metacognition, and memory dynamics.

PhD in Psychology, University of New Mexico, USA (2019-2023)

Master of Science in Psychology, University of New Mexico, USA (2019-2021)

Bachelor of Science in Psychology, Idaho State University, USA (2016-2019)

Dr. Garima Singh’s academic interests lie at the intersection of cognitive neuroscience and psychology, with a particular focus on the neural mechanisms underlying reward processing, decision-making, and aberrant behaviors in clinical versus healthy populations. Using advanced neuroimaging methods, including EEG, her work aims to deepen our understanding of how neural pathways impact cognitive and behavioral health, especially in relation to psychiatric conditions affecting the reward system.

In addition to these areas, Dr. Singh pursues separate research in delay discounting, metacognition, and memory dynamics, exploring how these processes influence decision-making and cognitive function across various contexts. Her broader research includes examining the role of stress, depression, and substance misuse on brain function, and investigating social factors such as memory distortion from social media and social influences on memory recall.

Dr. Singh’s research ultimately aims to develop effective diagnostic tools and contribute to a comprehensive understanding of cognitive health, advancing interventions for mental health challenges and aberrant behaviors.

1. Research Scholar, 2019-2023, University of New Mexico, USA

Area of work: Induced changes in the brain via affective imagery, decision-making dynamics, reward positivity, reward learning, and attentional bias in clinical populations.

2. Research Assistant, 2017-2019, Idaho State University, USA

Area of work: Sexual impulsivity and monetary delay discounting, Metacognition health and memory (perceptive memory, procedural memory, semantic memory).

nodes.title: Integrating Teaching-Learning and Digital Education: A Study of Selected Higher
nodes.summary: Education Institutions in Jammu & Kashmir and Ladakh This project, headed by Dr Sayantan Mandal, is funded by IIT Jammu. It employs a mixed-methods approach to investigate the integration of digital technologies with traditional teaching and…
nodes.text:
Education Institutions in Jammu & Kashmir and Ladakh

This project, headed by Dr Sayantan Mandal, is funded by IIT Jammu.

It is the first large-scale empirical longitudinal study conducted in multiple
 institutions of Jammu & Kashmir and Ladakh UTs. It employs a mixed-methods approach
 to investigate the integration of digital technologies with traditional teaching and
 learning methods in Higher Education Institutions (HEIs) of these regions.

This project aims to identify empirically supported strategies for incorporating digital
 technologies into the classroom and recommend evidence-based policy briefs.
 Additionally, the project seeks to explore innovative teaching approaches and ensure
 equitable access to high-quality higher education for all individuals, taking into
 account the distinct characteristics of the region, as well as the socio-economic,
 educational, and other diversities among learners and educators.

The primary aims of this study encompass familiarizing oneself with current teaching and
 learning methodologies, examining obstacles related to academic and socio-economic
 aspects, identifying regional and institutional factors that impact the incorporation of
 online education alongside traditional classroom instruction, investigating disparities
 and difficulties within and between institutions when integrating online education with
 in-person teaching and offering evidence-based recommendations for suitable frameworks
 that combine classroom and online teaching and learning in a context-specific manner.

The primary goal is to make a significant contribution to the growth of IIT Jammu as a
 central institution in facilitating the integration of teaching, learning, and digital
 technologies for higher education institutions in the northernmost region of India. This
 will be achieved by enhancing teaching and learning through rigorous research and policy
 interventions based on dynamic evidence.

nodes.title: Arun Kumar Verma
nodes.summary: Power electronics, converters for renewable energy and EV chargers, bi-directional power flow, and electric transportation. Electrical Engineering Indian Institute of Technology (I.I.T.) Delhi, New Delhi-1110016.
nodes.text:
Dr. Verma earned his master’s and Ph.D. from I.I.T. Delhi, New Delhi, India. He served as a visiting professor at Ontario Tech University, Oshawa, Ontario, Canada, in 2024 . He was a Postdoctoral research fellow (PDF) at the Energy Research Institute (ERI@N), Nanyang Technological University (NTU) Singapore, from 2015 to 2016. Before joining NTU Singapore, he worked as a visiting graduate researcher (VGR) at the Smart Grid Energy Research Center (SMERC), University of California, Los Angeles (UCLA), California, USA, from 2014 to 2015. He won the prestigious BASE fellowship for advanced solar energy research in 2014. He has been conferred with the prestigious POSOCO Power System Research Award 2016. He was also selected for the SERB International Research Experience (SIRE) award in 2023. He is an associate Editor for IEEE Transactions on Transportation Electrification and an associate editor for IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Industrial Electronics, as well as an assistant editor of the Smart Grid journal and a member of the editorial board for the EPE Journal. He has been invited by Defence University College, Ethiopia, and Metal Engineering Corporation, Ethiopia, as an instructor for the M.Tech program in Renewable Energy Engineering from 7 May 2016 to 27 May 2016. To name a few. He has/completed DST research funding of 7.5 cr., including Mission Innovation (MI), SPARC, ECR, SERD, and CRG. He is also the mentor director of a startup, Rishi Agastaya Technologies Pvt. Ltd. Dr Verma has guided 10 PhD and 18 M.Tech theses. Six PhD candidates are currently working under his supervision.

Power electronics, converters for renewable energy and EV chargers, bi-directional power flow, and electric transportation. Sustainable and intelligent Off-Grid solar PV system, On-board EV chargers, and  Fast EV chargers

nodes.title: Ambika Prasad Shah
nodes.summary: - PhD from Indian Institute of Technology Indore in March 2020. - Assistant Professor in the Electrical Engineering Department, Indian Institute of Technology Jammu, India, from March 2020 to February 2026.
nodes.text:
Dr. Ambika Prasad Shah is currently working as an Associate Professor in Electrical Engineering Department at the Indian Institute of Technology Jammu, India. He received Ph.D. degree from the Electrical Engineering Department, Indian Institute of Technology Indore, India. Before joining IIT Jammu, Dr. Shah worked as a Postdoctoral Fellow at the Institute for Microelectronics, TU Vienna, Austria. He is the recipient of the Young Scientist Award from the M.P. Council of Science and Technology Bhopal, M.P. India. He received a Young Faculty Research Fellowship (YFRF) from the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY), Government of India in 2025. He was nominated for the 10 th BRICS Young Scientist Forum 2025 in Brazil by DST, Govt. of India. He is the recipient of the Best Paper Award for two consecutive years at the VDAT-2024 and VDAT-2025, Second Best Award at the 7th International Conference on Microelectronics, Circuits and Systems (Micro-2020), and was nominated for the Best Paper from 31 st IEEE Conference on Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI-SoC 2023). He has authored/co-authored more than 110 research papers in peer-reviewed international journals and conferences and authored two books. He was the Conference Organizing Chair for VDAT-2022, Fellowship Chair for VLSID-2022, Publicity Chair for iSES 2023, and Fellowship Chair for VDAT 2025. He coordinates the VLSI Society of India, Himalayan Region Chapter. He is the associate editor of IEEE Transactions on VLSI Systems and IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing, editor of Nature Scientific Reports and IETE Journal of Research, and guest editor of Springer and Elsevier journals. He is a fellow of IETE, a senior member of IEEE, and a member of ACM, ISTE, and IEI. His current research interest includes reliability analysis of digital circuits, circuits and systems for biomedical applications, fault-tolerant circuits, low-power high-performance circuit designs, and Hardware security primitives.

Reliability analysis of digital circuits, circuits and systems for biomedical applications, low-power high-performance circuit designs, In-memory computing, SRAM, and Hardware security primitives.

nodes.title: 3-Day National Conference
nodes.summary: HSS IIT Jammu Conducts 3-Day National Conference HSS IIT Jammu organized a 3-day National Conference on “Conflict and the Everyday: Narratives from the Personal and Public Realms” from 21st- 23rd February 2020. Ashok Kumar Ahuja Dean, Student…
nodes.text:
HSS IIT Jammu Conducts
 3-Day National Conference HSS IIT Jammu organized a 3-day National Conference on “Conflict and the
 Everyday: Narratives from the Personal and Public Realms” from 21st- 23rd
 February 2020.

Prof
 Shiv VisvanathanProfessor & Vice Dean and Executive Director, Centre for the
 Study of Science, Society, and Sustainability, OP Jindal Global University,
 Sonepat was the Keynote Speaker of the conference. The plenary session of the
 conference had eminent speakers Dr KB Veio Pou, Prof. Meera Velayudhan,Dr.
 Amitabh Vikram Dwivedi, Adhik Kadam, who disseminated their knowledge to the
 participants for a better understanding of the notions of conflict and the
 everyday.

Dr.
 Amitash Ojha, H.O.D. HSS, welcomed the esteemed dignitaries, faculties,
 participants while briefing on the significance of the Conference. Dr. Quleen Kaur Bijral, Convener, addressed the attempt of re-thinking of the
 term ‘Conflict’ by listening to the ‘insignificant’ and
 ‘everyday’ narratives. Dr. Bhumika, co-convener, gave the vote of thanks while expressing gratitude
 towards - Director, IIT Jammu, Prof. Manoj Singh Gaur, Dean, Academic Affairs, Prof. Ashoke Kumar Sarkar Dean, Continuing Education and Outreach, Prof. Ashok Kumar Ahuja Dean, Student Welfare, Prof. Chandan Gosh Div. Chairperson, Prof. Uma Shankar OSD, Cmde Naresh Kumar and other eminent members of the institute and outside.

On the
 second day, there was a theatrical performance by Natrang, which is a Theatre
 & Performing Arts Institution of Jammu. Under the direction Shri Balwant
 Thakur, Padmashree awarded theatre director, two plays or Nukkad Drama were
 enacted:

'Girgit' in Hindi based
 on a Russian short story by Anton Chekhov. A hard-hitting satire on the present
 socio-political system. 'Gatt' a multilingual play based on an Urdu story by Kishan Chander
 called ‘Khadda’. The play reflects the plight of the poor masses who
 continue to suffer for want of help from those at the helm of affairs.

The
 third day concluded with a valedictory session wherein Prof. Ashoke Sarkar
 distributed the best paper presenter award. The session was anchored by Dr.
 Suchismita Roy, co-convener of the conference in coordination with Dr. Sayantan
 Mandal.

Date: Friday,
 February 21, 2020

nodes.title: International Conference
nodes.summary: Venue and Date The Conference will be fully online using various digital platforms. IIT Jammu, Jammu and Kashmir, INDIA on 19th and 20 March 2021.
nodes.text:
Objectives The major objectives of this proposed International Conference are:
 - To provide a platform for experts, researchers and practitioners to share their experiences on the present and future of HSS.
- To discuss innovative practices in the area of HSS in Technical and Professional Higher Education for the improvement of the learners, the subjects and of the higher education sector(s).
- To explore possibilities of promoting interdisciplinary research studies and,
- Bring hi-quality academic publications (Edited Volume from an internationally recognized publishing house).

In addition to the above, the Conference will create an excellent opportunity to form an international network of partners, researchers, experts and institutional leaders for sustainable knowledge-building.

Venue and Date The Conference will be fully online using various digital platforms. IIT Jammu, Jammu and Kashmir, INDIA on 19th and 20 March 2021.

For more details, see Brochure

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: In a groundbreaking research effort, scientists from IIT Jammu have shed light on the growing concern of Glacial Lake Outburst Floods (GLOFs) in the Himalayas. The IIT Jammu team's groundbreaking research serves as a wake-up call, drawing attention…

nodes.title: New research by IIT Jammu's Dr. Divyesh Varade & Mr. Hemant Singh gets featured on TIMES NOW!
nodes.summary: In a groundbreaking research effort, scientists from IIT Jammu have shed light on the growing concern of Glacial Lake Outburst Floods (GLOFs) in the Himalayas. The IIT Jammu team's groundbreaking research serves as a wake-up call, drawing attention…
nodes.text:
In a groundbreaking research effort, scientists from IIT Jammu have shed light on the growing concern of Glacial Lake Outburst Floods (GLOFs) in the Himalayas. Dr. Divyesh Varade, Assistant Professor of Geomatics, and Mr. Hemant Singh, a PhD student from the Department of Civil Engineering, IIT Jammu, along with their team, have conducted a comprehensive study on the cascaded extreme flood events caused by GLOFs in the Hunza valley of the Karakoram ranges.

Their recent publication, titled "Assessment of potential present and future glacial lake outburst flood hazard in the Hunza Valley: A case study of Shisper and Mochowar Glacier," in the esteemed journal Science of the Total Environment, has brought attention to the dire situation. The study analyzes the glacial lake outbursts that have already occurred in the Hunza Valley, including those in 2019, 2020, and 2022.

The research team focused on the bifurcation of the Shisper glacier, which has led to the formation of an ice-dammed lake. This lake, originating from the ice-melt of the Mochowar glacier, has been identified as susceptible to GLOFs in the past. Dr. Divyesh and Mr. Hemant's study reveals that the lake has been expanding since its origin in 2018. Notably, the team investigated a critical GLOF event that took place on 7th May 2022, stemming from the Shisper glacier ice-dammed lake. The analysis determined a release volume of 6.23×106 m3 and a peak discharge of approximately 1505 m3s−1.

Collaborating with scientists from various institutions, including Maximillian VDW Vries from the School of Geography and the Environment at the University of Oxford and Mr. Kirtan Adhikari from the Royal University of Bhutan, the research team emphasized the urgent need for hydrometeorological monitoring of glacial regions in the Himalayas. Furthermore, they highlighted the critical importance of assessing climate change impacts and enhancing disaster response mechanisms in the region.

The study's findings raise concerns about the possibility of similar glacial hazards occurring in other parts of the Himalayas. The researchers stressed the necessity for government agencies to develop monitoring and early warning systems in these vulnerable regions. The proactive implementation of such systems will play a crucial role in mitigating potential disasters and safeguarding the lives and livelihoods of the communities residing in these high-altitude areas.

The IIT Jammu team's groundbreaking research serves as a wake-up call, drawing attention to the escalating threat of glacial lake outburst floods in the Himalayas. As climate change continues to impact these fragile ecosystems, urgent action is needed to protect both the environment and the communities that depend on them.

Please follow the link to read more: https://bit.ly/3nAqMnH

nodes.title: IIT Jammu Scholars Shine at EASTS 2023, Garnering International Recognition
nodes.summary: IIT Jammu's participation in EASTS 2023 culminated in outstanding recognition for their scholars. Ms Yadav's recognition underscores the pivotal role her research plays in advancing road safety infrastructure.
nodes.text:
In a remarkable display of academic excellence, two research scholars from the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jammu have earned prestigious awards at the 15th International Conference of the Eastern Asia Society for Transportation Studies 2023 (EASTS 2023), held in Shah Alam, Malaysia.

EASTS is renowned for its biennial international conferences, which serve as a pivotal platform for the exchange of cutting-edge transportation research and practices. The conferences play a vital role in advancing transportation knowledge across various modes and aspects. IIT Jammu's participation in EASTS 2023 culminated in outstanding recognition for their scholars.

Mr Harish Kumar Saini, working under the guidance of Dr Ankit Kathuria from the Department of Civil Engineering at IIT Jammu, clinched the prestigious "Outstanding Poster Presentation Award for Young Researchers." His research, focusing on Exclusive Motorcycle Lanes as a strategy to mitigate motorcyclist crashes, received acclaim for its potential to enhance motorcycle safety—a critical concern in many regions. This award underscores the significance of Mr Saini's work in addressing road safety challenges.

Adding to the accolades, Ms Anamika Yadav, also under the mentorship of Dr Ankit Kathuria, secured the "Honourable Mention Award sponsored by Volvo for the best poster presentation." Her research centers on the safety evaluation of road facilities, including intersections and roundabouts, using surrogate safety measures. Ms Yadav's recognition underscores the pivotal role her research plays in advancing road safety infrastructure.

The EASTS 2023 conference featured over 400 oral presentations and 85 poster presentations, showcasing a diverse array of transportation research and innovation by scholars from across the globe. The fact that only 5 posters received esteemed awards highlights the rigorous scrutiny and exceptionally high standards maintained for these recognitions.

These awards not only acknowledge the dedication and tireless efforts of IIT Jammu's young researchers but also underscore the invaluable contributions they are making to the field of transportation studies. IIT Jammu takes immense pride in its scholars and remains committed to fostering academic excellence and impactful research.

nodes.title: ICCMDE-2026
nodes.summary: It featured keynote talks by eminent scientists including Naresh Bhatnagar (IIT Delhi), Bent F. Varley, RMIT, Melbourne, Australia, Giribaskar, NMIS, UK, Saju Pillai (NIIST, Trivandrum), Raguraman (IIITDM Kancheepuram), Ramadass (DRDO-RDE, Pune),…
nodes.text:
The International Conference on Composite Materials, Design, and Engineering (ICCMDE-2026) concluded successfully at Research and Innovation Park, Indian Institute of Technology Delhi from 11–13 February 2026. Jointly organized by Department of Mechanical Engineering, Indian Institute of Technology Jammu and Department of Applied Mechanics, IIT Delhi under the Scheme for Promotion of Academic and Research Collaboration (SPARC), the three-day event brought together leading researchers, academicians, industry experts, and scholars to explore emerging developments in composite materials, structural design, and advanced manufacturing technologies.

The conference was inaugurated by Bent F. Sørensen, Naresh Bhatnagar, R. T. Durai Prabhakaran, and Puneet Mahajan. It featured keynote talks by eminent scientists including Naresh Bhatnagar (IIT Delhi), Bent F. Sørensen (Technical University of Denmark), Anindya Deb (IISc Bengaluru), and Paul Hazell (University of New South Wales, Australia), and plenary talks by eminent scientists including Russell J. Varley, RMIT, Melbourne, Australia, Giribaskar, NMIS, UK, Saju Pillai (NIIST, Trivandrum), Raguraman (IIITDM Kancheepuram), Ramadass (DRDO-RDE, Pune), Sanjay Palsule (IIT Roorkee), Inderdeep Singh (IIT Roorkee), Rajnish Sharma (IIT Mandi), Pawan Kumar (CUJ, Jammu), Jayaramulu (IIT Jammu), Badri Prasad Patel (IIT DELHI), S.P.Singh (IIT Delhi), Pradyumna (IIT Delhi), Abhijit Majumdar (IIT Delhi), S Vikrant Karra (IIT Delhi), Naresh Datla (IIT Delhi), Dr. Gautam Jain, Endolite India Limited, New Delhi, covering advanced composite materials, structural performance, impact engineering, and emerging applications.

Participants engaged in high-quality technical sessions spanning military-grade composites, biomedical innovations, advanced structural materials, and next-generation manufacturing technologies, highlighting the transformative role of composites in global engineering.

The valedictory session featured Professor Sawan S. Sinha, Head - Department of Applied Mechanics, reflecting on key outcomes and emphasizing the importance of continued interdisciplinary collaboration. Selected full-length papers from ICCMDE-2026 will be published in a Special Issue of the Journal of Polymer Composites, further extending the scholarly impact of the conference.

nodes.title: 1. Development of low-emission combustor technology for micro gas turbine engines
nodes.summary: Currently, his group is involved in the development of a novel gas turbine combustor capable of utilizing various fuels such as Hydrogen, methane, and SAF. He also works closely with the National Centre for Combustion Research and Development…
nodes.text:
Our research group specializes in the dynamic fields of aerospace engineering and propulsion. Our primary mission is to develop next-generation micro gas turbine engine technology for energy production and aerospace applications.  Additionally, a key focus of our research is on the development of reusable liquid rocket engines, with a particular emphasis on utilizing throttleable METHALOX injectors.

Brief Bio

Dr. Shanmugadas, assistant professor within the Department of Mechanical Engineering at IIT Jammu, spearheads the Propulsion and Energy Lab, driving pioneering research and innovation related to liquid rocket engines and aero engines. With a primary focus on novel combustor concepts, his expertise extends across different areas including two-phase flow, experimental combustion, and laser flow diagnostics. Currently, his group is involved in the development of a novel gas turbine combustor capable of utilizing various fuels such as Hydrogen, methane, and SAF.

Dr. Shanmugadas is recognized for his contributions to fuel injection systems with many publications and his group has also filed many patents. He is actively engaged with industry and governmental bodies like ISRO and GE Aerospace and promotes impactful collaborations. He also works closely with the National Centre for Combustion Research and Development (NCCRD) at IIT Madras, focusing on advancing gas turbine combustion research. Additionally, he collaborates with the Chair of Space Propulsion and Mobility at Technical University Munich, Germany, in the area of injection concepts for liquid rocket engines.

Dr. Shanmugadas is a professional member of the American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics (AIAA) and a lifetime member of the Combustion Institute. He serves as the Associate Dean of Academic Affairs at IIT Jammu, contributing to the institution's academic excellence.

Dr. Shanmugadas is the founder of SAP Aerospace, a pioneering deep-tech startup focused on developing cutting-edge aero engines for Unmanned Combat Aerial Vehicles (UCAVs).

nodes.title: Innovative Research by Dr Jayaramulu Kolleboyina Recognized in "The Journal of Physical Chemistry C"
nodes.summary: The paper titled "Lewis Acid Catalyzed Amide Bond Formation in Covalent Graphene–MOF Hybrids" has been accepted for publication in the esteemed ACS journal, "The Journal of Physical Chemistry C". Notable contributors include: Indian Institute of…
nodes.text:
In a remarkable display of collaboration, the groundbreaking research conducted by Dr Jayaramulu Kolleboyina in conjunction with institutions from the Czech Republic has achieved significant recognition. The paper titled "Lewis Acid Catalyzed Amide Bond Formation in Covalent Graphene–MOF Hybrids" has been accepted for publication in the esteemed ACS journal, "The Journal of Physical Chemistry C". Adding to the accolades, this article has also been selected as the cover art for the publication. \

The collaborative efforts of multiple renowned institutes have been instrumental in the realization of this pioneering research endeavor. Notable contributors include: Indian Institute of Technology Jammu (IIT Jammu), Czech Advanced Technology and Research Institute, and IOCB Prague - Institute of Organic Chemistry and Biochemistry.

The focal point of the cover art is a vivid representation of the graphene hybrids, a foundation with boundless potential for forthcoming advancements in the realms of energy and the environment. The research delves into the mechanics of the formation of a covalent hybrid material, leveraging the inspiration provided by nature's own amide bond. This endeavor was substantiated through comprehensive molecular dynamics simulations that employed force-field-based methodologies to evaluate the hybrid's structural compatibility and steadfastness.

Dr Jayaramulu Kolleboyina's expertise, coupled with the collaborative synergy between the aforementioned institutions, has illuminated new horizons in the field of materials science. This achievement underscores the significance of cross-border partnerships in propelling scientific progress and innovation. The publication not only enriches the academic landscape but also offers profound insights into the development of materials with a potential to revolutionize energy and environmental technologies.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: He has authored/co-authored more than 110 research papers in peer-reviewed international journals and conferences and authored two books. He was the Conference Organizing Chair for VDAT-2022, Fellowship Chair for VLSID-2022, Publicity Chair for iSES…
nodes.text:
He is the recipient of the Young Scientist Award from the M.P. Council of Science and Technology Bhopal, M.P. India. He received a Young Faculty Research Fellowship (YFRF) from the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY), Government of India in 2025. He was nominated for the 10 th BRICS Young Scientist Forum 2025 in Brazil by DST, Govt. of India. He is the recipient of the Best Paper Award for two consecutive years at the VDAT-2024 and VDAT-2025, Second Best Award at the 7th International Conference on Microelectronics, Circuits and Systems (Micro-2020), and was nominated for the Best Paper from 31 st IEEE Conference on Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI-SoC 2023).

He has authored/co-authored more than 110 research papers in peer-reviewed international journals and conferences and authored two books. He was the Conference Organizing Chair for VDAT-2022, Fellowship Chair for VLSID-2022, Publicity Chair for iSES 2023, and Fellowship Chair for VDAT 2025. He coordinates the VLSI Society of India, Himalayan Region Chapter. He is the associate editor of IEEE Transactions on VLSI Systems and IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing, editor of Nature Scientific Reports and IETE Journal of Research, and guest editor of Springer and Elsevier journals. He is a fellow of IETE, a senior member of IEEE, and a member of ACM, ISTE, and IEI.

His current research interest includes reliability analysis of digital circuits, circuits and systems for biomedical applications, fault-tolerant circuits, low-power high-performance circuit designs, and Hardware security primitives.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: Dr Ankur Bansal, a distinguished faculty member at the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jammu, has been awarded a generous grant for his project titled "Design, Optimization and Performance Evaluation of Hybrid Underwater-Terrestrial Edge…

nodes.title: IIT Jammu: Dr Ankur Bansal secures grant to improve computational efficiency in underwater communication
nodes.summary: The project focuses on developing a hybrid underwater-terrestrial edge computing network (UT-ECN), which will revolutionize the field of computational efficiency in underwater communication. Dr Ankur Bansal, along with Co-PI Dr Ankit Dubey, aims to…
nodes.text:
Dr Ankur Bansal, a distinguished faculty member at the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jammu, has been awarded a generous grant for his project titled "Design, Optimization and Performance Evaluation of Hybrid Underwater-Terrestrial Edge Computing Network Through Satellite Communication." The funding has been provided by the NMICPS Technology Innovation Hub on Autonomous Navigation (TiHAN) Foundation, IIT Hyderabad.

The project focuses on developing a hybrid underwater-terrestrial edge computing network (UT-ECN), which will revolutionize the field of computational efficiency in underwater communication. Dr Ankur Bansal, along with Co-PI Dr Ankit Dubey, aims to leverage autonomous water surface vehicles (WSVs) as an integral part of the network. Through this innovative approach, multiple underwater users will be able to efficiently offload their computationally intensive and delay-sensitive tasks to the edge server, also known as the base station, using the WSVs.

One of the key objectives of the project is to optimize task-offloading procedures, ensuring non-overlapping trajectories for different WSVs and mitigating collision risks. To achieve this, the team will employ a cutting-edge multi-agent Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) algorithm. Furthermore, the project will involve the design and implementation of a prototype module, along with onboard controllers for the autonomous WSVs, facilitating seamless sharing of information and control signals for efficient task offloading.

The Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jammu, renowned for its cutting-edge research and academic excellence, continues to foster innovation and empower its faculty members to pursue groundbreaking projects that contribute to the advancement of science and technology.

nodes.title: SERB (GoI) awards Dr Vijay Kumar Pal SIRE Fellowship 2023-24
nodes.summary: Dr Vijay Kumar Pal, Assistant Professor in the Department of Mechanical Engineering, IIT Jammu, has been honored with the prestigious SIRE Fellowship for the year 2023-2024. Driven by the pressing need to develop efficient and sustainable hydrogen…
nodes.text:
Dr Vijay Kumar Pal, Assistant Professor in the Department of Mechanical Engineering, IIT Jammu, has been honored with the prestigious SIRE Fellowship for the year 2023-2024. The fellowship is supported by the Science and Engineering Research Board (SERB), a prominent statutory body operating under the purview of the Department of Science and Technology (DST), Government of India (GoI).

The SIRE Fellowship is a coveted recognition that acknowledges outstanding scholars and their exceptional contributions to the field of research. This highly competitive fellowship is awarded to exceptional individuals who have demonstrated exemplary research capabilities and possess a deep passion for advancing scientific knowledge.

Under the esteemed SIRE Fellowship, Dr Vijay Kumar Pal will undertake an innovative and significant research project titled "Hydrogen storage: Nanofabrication of metal nanoparticles with Carbon allotropes." Collaborating with Dr Suprem Ranjan Das, a distinguished faculty member at the Industrial and Manufacturing Systems Engineering department of Kansas State University, USA, Dr Pal aims to explore groundbreaking advancements in the domain of hydrogen storage.

Hydrogen storage plays a pivotal role in various sectors, including renewable energy, transportation, and green technology. Driven by the pressing need to develop efficient and sustainable hydrogen storage methods, Dr Pal's research will focus on the nanofabrication of metal nanoparticles utilizing carbon allotropes. This pioneering approach has the potential to revolutionize hydrogen storage technologies, leading to significant advancements in clean energy systems.

Dr Pal's work in the field of hydrogen storage is poised to make significant contributions to the global scientific community and advance India's position in clean and sustainable energy technologies.

nodes.title: IHUB IIT Roorkee Awards Pioneering Consultancy cum Research Project to Dr Karan Nathwani of IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: In a significant stride towards addressing hearing impairment, IHUB IIT Roorkee, the innovation hub of the Indian Institute of Technology Roorkee, is delighted to announce the grant of a groundbreaking Consultancy cum Research Project to Dr Karan…
nodes.text:
In a significant stride towards addressing hearing impairment, IHUB IIT Roorkee, the innovation hub of the Indian Institute of Technology Roorkee, is delighted to announce the grant of a groundbreaking Consultancy cum Research Project to Dr Karan Nathwani, Assistant Professor at IIT Jammu.

The primary objective of this groundbreaking project is to revolutionise the lives of individuals with hearing impairments by enhancing hearing intelligibility and mitigating hearing loss. Dr. Karan Nathwani, a distinguished academician and researcher, will lead this ambitious initiative.

The project is poised to achieve its objectives through the development of an innovative hardware prototype. This prototype is designed to significantly improve hearing intelligibility for individuals with hearing impairments. By utilising cutting-edge technology and scientific insights, the project aims to provide a practical solution to a pressing healthcare concern.

In addition to hardware development, the research endeavours to contribute valuable insights into the field of audiology. The team will analyse hearing loss among individuals with hearing impairments using behavioural audiometry evaluation techniques. This in-depth analysis will facilitate a comprehensive understanding of the challenges faced by those with hearing loss.

Furthermore, the project will employ speech-in-noise testing utilising the developed prototype. This testing methodology will play a pivotal role in assessing the efficacy of the hardware solution in real-world scenarios, thereby ensuring its practicality and effectiveness.

This collaboration between IHUB IIT Roorkee and Dr Nathwani exemplifies their dedication to advancing scientific knowledge for the betterment of society.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: Dr Ambika Prasad Shah, Assistant Professor at the Department of Electrical Engineering, IIT Jammu, in collaboration with researchers from IIT Indore, ABV-IIITM Gwalior, IET-DAVV Indore, and SGSITS Indore, has secured a significant funding of INR 4.8…

nodes.title: MeitY C2S Programme: Dr Ambika Prasad Shah receives a funding of INR 4.8 Cr.
nodes.summary: Dr Ambika Prasad Shah, Assistant Professor at the Department of Electrical Engineering, IIT Jammu, in collaboration with researchers from IIT Indore, ABV-IIITM Gwalior, IET-DAVV Indore, and SGSITS Indore, has secured a significant funding of INR 4.8…
nodes.text:
Dr Ambika Prasad Shah, Assistant Professor at the Department of Electrical Engineering, IIT Jammu, in collaboration with researchers from IIT Indore, ABV-IIITM Gwalior, IET-DAVV Indore, and SGSITS Indore, has secured a significant funding of INR 4.8 Cr for their joint project titled "Implantable Pacemaker Chip (iPACE-CHIP)". The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY), Government of India (GoI), has awarded this grant under the Chip to Startup (C2S) Programme.

The primary objective of the project is to design an advanced chip for an Implantable Pacemaker utilizing SCL 180nm / TSMC 130nm CMOS High Voltage, Low power, BCD PLUS technology. The iPACE-CHIP, once successfully developed and tested, will be incorporated into the next-generation commercial product of Shree Pacetronix Ltd., with an array of enhanced features.

The MeitY C2S Programme is an integral part of the Indian government's vision to establish the nation as a global hub for Electronic System Design and Manufacturing. The initiative aims to foster a robust semiconductor ecosystem encompassing various facets such as design, manufacturing, packaging, testing, and related services.

The successful collaboration between Dr Shah, IIT Jammu, and esteemed institutions such as IIT Indore, ABV-IIITM Gwalior, IET-DAVV Indore, and SGSITS Indore highlights the spirit of cooperation and innovation that drives the Indian academic and research community. The successful outcome of the iPACE-CHIP project has the potential to revolutionize the field of cardiac care and implantable medical devices, showcasing India's technological prowess on the global stage.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: The Science and Engineering Research Board (SERB), Government of India, has provided the prestigious International Travel Support (ITS) to Mr Ritujoy Biswas to present his research at the EUSIPCO 2023 [European Signal Processing Conference],…

nodes.title: EUSIPCO 2023: SERB (GoI) offers International Travel Support to Ritujoy
nodes.summary: The Science and Engineering Research Board (SERB), Government of India, has provided the prestigious International Travel Support (ITS) to Mr Ritujoy Biswas to present his research at the EUSIPCO 2023 [European Signal Processing Conference],…
nodes.text:
The Science and Engineering Research Board (SERB), Government of India, has provided the prestigious International Travel Support (ITS) to Mr Ritujoy Biswas to present his research at the EUSIPCO 2023 [European Signal Processing Conference], scheduled to take place from 4th to 8th September 2023 at Aalto University in Finland.

Mr Biswas, a PhD student at IIT Jammu, under the guidance of Dr Karan Nathwani, is working on techniques to improve speech intelligibility in noisy environments by designing optimal transformation functions that perform such speech modifications. He is also working on formulating different methods of transfer learning that are able to adapt the generated functions to varying ambient conditions.

Besides his participation at the 3MT thesis contest, his research paper titled “Near-End Intelligibility Improvement through Voice Transformation in Transfer Learning Framework” has also been accepted at the same conference.

EUSIPCO is one of the most influential conferences in the field of signal processing, attracting leading researchers, academics, and industry professionals from around the globe. With its strong tradition of knowledge exchange and cutting-edge research presentations, EUSIPCO provides an unparalleled platform for researchers like Mr Biswas to disseminate their findings and engage in fruitful discussions with peers in the scientific community.

With the generous support from SERB, Mr Ritujoy Biswas is all set to represent India on the international stage and contribute to the advancement of knowledge in the field of signal processing.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: Durai Prabhakaran, Department of Mechanical Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jammu, has been awarded a research fund of ₹2.38 crores by the Indian Government's Textile Ministry in New Delhi. Under the auspices of the Special…

nodes.title: GoI: Ministry of Textiles Awards Research Grant to Prof Prabhakaran for Innovative Prosthetics Project
nodes.summary: Durai Prabhakaran, Department of Mechanical Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jammu, has been awarded a research fund of ₹2.38 crores by the Indian Government's Textile Ministry in New Delhi. Under the auspices of the Special…
nodes.text:
In a groundbreaking development, Dr. R.T. Durai Prabhakaran, Department of Mechanical Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jammu, has been awarded a research fund of ₹2.38 crores by the Indian Government's Textile Ministry in New Delhi. The research project, spanning three years and boasting a total budget of ₹2.87 crores, is a collaborative effort with IIT Madras, AIIMS Rishikesh, and IIPO, Chennai, alongside two esteemed industrial partners—NANOCOMPS, Pune, and PCI Limited, New Delhi.

Under the auspices of the Special Call of Textile Ministry, the industrial partner NANOCOMPS, Pune, is contributing ₹10 lakhs in financial support to the IIT Madras project partner.

This joint venture aims to address a critical need in the field of prosthetics through the project titled "Low-cost Prosthetics Design and Fabrication using Advanced Textile and Composites Technology." The overarching objective is to leverage advanced design and kinematic mechanisms to create low-cost and lightweight composite prosthetics. The project will harness textile fiber and sensor technologies to develop highly beneficial below-knee prosthetics suitable for both the general public and military personnel.

This collaboration brings together the expertise of leading academic institutions and industry partners, fostering innovation and contributing to the advancement of prosthetics technology in India. The project's success holds the potential to transform lives and set new standards in the field of assistive technologies.

nodes.title: About Us
nodes.summary: The Research and Consultancy Section at IIT Jammu is an integral part of the institute. These include research projects, industry collaborations, and consulting services.

nodes.title: About Us
nodes.summary: The Research and Consultancy Section at IIT Jammu is an integral part of the institute. These include research projects, industry collaborations, and consulting services.
nodes.text:
The Research and Consultancy Section at IIT Jammu is an integral part of the institute. This section works to support research and development activities in the institute, with a goal of enhancing the intellectual property and knowledge base of the institution. It also aims to promote collaboration between the institute and industry, as well as between different departments within the institute.

The Research and Consultancy Section provides a wide range of services to both internal and external clients. These include research projects, industry collaborations, and consulting services. The department often works closely with other departments within the institute, including the various engineering departments, as well as with external partners in industry and academia.

The main functions of Research and Consultancy Section include administrative and accounting support for:
 - Sponsored Research Projects
- Consultancy Jobs
- Institute Sponsored Projects
- Research Grant for New faculty
- Mid-Career Research Grant (PRAISE)
- Continuing Education Programs
- Recruitment of Project staff
- Professional Development Fund (PDF)
- Departmental Development Fund (DDF)
- Institute Development Fund (IDF)
- Sponsored Fellowships (PMRF, N-PDF, Ramanujan, INSPIRE etc)
- Student Assistantships from Projects.
- Fellowships and Travel Assistance to Research Scholars.
- Monitoring and tracking of budgets and utilization of funds received from sponsors.
- Regulating expenditures and administration of projects (project staff appointment, travel, etc.) on behalf of the Institute.
- Preparation of Utilization Certificates and Statement of Expenditures.

nodes.title: Research Scholar Surya Narayanan Sakthivel Clinches Best Paper Award at 16th World Conference on Transport Research
nodes.summary: Mr Surya Narayanan Sakthivel, a research scholar under the guidance of Dr Ankit Kathuria, from the Department of Civil Engineering, has secured the Best Paper Award at the 16th World Conference on Transport Research. The 16th World Conference on…
nodes.text:
Mr Surya Narayanan Sakthivel, a research scholar under the guidance of Dr Ankit Kathuria, from the Department of Civil Engineering, has secured the Best Paper Award at the 16th World Conference on Transport Research. The event took place in July, in the vibrant city of Montreal, Quebec, Canada.

He has been diligently conducting pioneering research under the mentorship of two mentors – Dr Ankit Kathuria from IIT Jammu and Dr Bhupendra Singh from IIT Jodhpur. The research endeavors of Mr Sakthivel delve into the intricate domain of civil engineering, with a specialized focus on the evaluation of Moisture Induced Damage of Hot Mix Asphalt through the ingenious application of Chemically Treated Inferior Quality Aggregates.

The 16th World Conference on Transport Research, a congregation of brilliant minds from across the globe, saw the presentation of a staggering 949 research papers, each contributing to the enrichment of the transportation engineering landscape. Amidst this formidable competition, a mere nine papers emerged victorious, emerging as the cream of the intellectual crop, securing the much-coveted "Best Paper Award."

This remarkable achievement showcases the pinnacle of scholarly excellence that is consistently fostered within the corridors of the Department of Civil Engineering. Surya Narayanan Sakthivel's accomplishments will undoubtedly inspire countless others to explore the frontiers of knowledge and endeavor to make their mark in their respective fields.

nodes.title: Ministry of Textiles (GoI) Supports Dr Pratik's Research Propelling Eco-Friendly Packaging Solutions
nodes.summary: Dr Kumar, along with his team, will meticulously assess the blend composite for various properties inherent in lightweight packaging materials. By doing so, IIT Jammu aims not only to curb import dependency on lightweight packaging materials but…
nodes.text:
In a pioneering initiative, Dr Pratik Kumar from the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jammu sets on a groundbreaking research project aimed at the development of poly-hydroxybutyrate-based bioplastic, heralding a new era for the packaging industry. This initiative seeks to present an environmentally conscious alternative, fostering sustainability and reducing the dependence on conventional materials.

The primary focus of the research revolves around the identification of a novel microorganism capable of naturally producing polymers within their cellular structure. The extracted biopolymer will undergo rigorous validation to ensure its compatibility, achieving an optimal balance of strength and flexibility. Sustainable solid materials, including algal biomass and recycled polylactic acid, will be employed in this innovative blend composite.

Dr Kumar, along with his team, will meticulously assess the blend composite for various properties inherent in lightweight packaging materials. The objective is to create a resilient, eco-friendly solution that aligns with global standards.

The ultimate deliverable of this avant-garde project is twofold: to bioengineer the production of polymer and validate the biodegradability index of the composite plastic material post short-term usage. By doing so, IIT Jammu aims not only to curb import dependency on lightweight packaging materials but also to stimulate local revenue through the adoption of a sustainable, green economy.

nodes.title: IEEE NEWCAS 2023: Ms Farah Naz receives SERB International Travel Support
nodes.summary: The Science and Engineering Research Board (SERB), Government of India, has provided the prestigious International Travel Support (ITS) to Ms Syed Farah Naz to present her research at the 21st IEEE NEWCAS 2023 conference, scheduled to take place…
nodes.text:
The Science and Engineering Research Board (SERB), Government of India, has provided the prestigious International Travel Support (ITS) to Ms Syed Farah Naz to present her research at the 21st IEEE NEWCAS 2023 conference, scheduled to take place from June 26 to June 28, 2023, in the beautiful city of Edinburg, Scotland.

Under the expert guidance of Dr Ambika Prasad Shah, Assistant Professor at IIT Jammu, Ms Farah Naz has been making significant contributions in her field. Her exceptional research work has earned her recognition and support from the prestigious SERB, which seeks to foster and promote scientific endeavors of Indian researchers on the global stage.

The SERB's International Travel Support (ITS) Scheme aims to provide financial assistance to Indian researchers for presenting their research papers at international scientific events held abroad, such as conferences, seminars, and workshops. This assistance not only enables researchers to showcase their work but also facilitates collaboration, knowledge exchange, and the development of networks with global experts in their respective fields.

Ms Farah Naz's selection for the SERB International Travel Support serves as a testament to her dedication and the immense potential she possesses to make significant contributions to her field of study. Her achievement serves as an inspiration to aspiring researchers and further highlights the excellence and potential within IIT Jammu’s scientific community.

nodes.title: MoE's STARS: Dr. Divyesh Varade receives an estimated funding of INR 1.26 cr
nodes.summary: Varade, along with researchers from IISER Bhopal and Indian Institute of Technology, Kanpur, has received acceptance for their joint proposal titled "Climate change impacts on glacio-fluvial and hydrogeomorphic flood vulnerability of the upper…
nodes.text:
Dr. Divyesh Varade, Assistant Professor at the Department of Civil Engineering, IIT Jammu, has achieved a significant milestone.

Dr. Varade, along with researchers from IISER Bhopal and Indian Institute of Technology, Kanpur, has received acceptance for their joint proposal titled "Climate change impacts on glacio-fluvial and hydrogeomorphic flood vulnerability of the upper Bhagirathi catchment, West-Central Himalayas." This proposal has been granted an estimated funding of INR 1.26 Cr in the second call for proposals under the Ministry of Education (MoE)-Transformational and Advanced Research in Sciences (STARS) 2023 scheme.

The collaborative effort aims to conduct a comprehensive assessment of flood vulnerability in the upper Bhagirathi catchment, which includes the critical Maneri dam reservoir in Uttarakhand, India. Dr. Varade and his team plan to employ remote sensing-based inputs to precisely estimate snow and glacier melt runoff, thereby determining glacio-fluvial flood vulnerability accurately. Additionally, the research will explore the sediment connectivity and propose mitigation measures for flood-related challenges.

Once again, congratulations to Dr. Divyesh Varade and his team for this remarkable achievement. We look forward to witnessing the impactful outcomes of their research and the positive influence it will have on flood management strategies in the Bhagirathi catchment and beyond.

nodes.title: 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends & Advances in Water Science, Remote Sensing & Climate Change (WSRSCC-2)
nodes.summary: 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends & Advances in Water Science, Remote Sensing & Climate Change (WSRSCC-2) Date: 5th – 7th February 2026 Venue: Indian Institute of Technology Jammu The Department of Civil Engineering at IIT Jammu invites…
nodes.text:
2nd International Conference on Recent Trends & Advances in Water Science, Remote Sensing & Climate Change (WSRSCC-2)

Date: 5th – 7th February 2026 | Venue: Indian Institute of Technology Jammu

The Department of Civil Engineering at IIT Jammu invites researchers, academicians, policymakers, and industry experts to a three-day international symposium dedicated to addressing the pressing global challenges of climate change through innovation, collaboration, and research.

Conference Themes:
 - Geospatial Applications in Environmental Management
- Sustainable Engineering and Climate Change
- Impact Studies on Himalayan Ecosystem and Communities
- Water Pollution / Solid Waste Management: Impact & Mitigation
- Advances in Risk Assessment of Natural Hazards
- AI in Geospatial Sciences and Environmental Engineering

The symposia aims to exchange ideas, share breakthroughs, and create actionable roadmaps for tackling environmental and climatic challenges through science, technology, and policy innovation.

Important Dates:
 - Abstract Submission: 10 December 2025
- Early Bird Registration: 20 December 2025 – 10 January 2026
- Registration Ends: 20 January 2026

Know More: https://sites.google.com/iitjammu.ac.in/wsrscc-2/home

Join us in shaping a sustainable future through science, collaboration, and innovation.

nodes.title: Dr Chembolu Vinay Secures Funding from IFCPAR for Indo-French Seminar on River Corridor Research and Management
nodes.summary: The collaborative seminar proposal, titled "A Workshop on State of the Art Techniques in River Corridor Research and Management: An Indo-French Perspective," has received approval under the Collaborative Scientific Research Programme (CSRP). They…
nodes.text:
In a noteworthy collaboration, Dr Chembolu Vinay from IIT Jammu and Dr Benoit Camenen from INRAE France have successfully secured funding from the Indo-French Centre for the Promotion of Advanced Research (IFCPAR/CEFIPRA). The collaborative seminar proposal, titled "A Workshop on State of the Art Techniques in River Corridor Research and Management: An Indo-French Perspective," has received approval under the Collaborative Scientific Research Programme (CSRP).

With an allocated budget of 15 Lakhs, the seminar underscores the commitment to fostering joint scientific endeavors between India and France. Set to unfold at IIT Jammu, the seminar is scheduled to take place from February 29 to March 2, 2024. This event aims to provide an immersive platform for the exchange of ideas and the exploration of cutting-edge techniques in river corridor research and management.

The collaborative effort signifies the dedication of both Indian and French institutions to advancing scientific understanding, particularly in the crucial field of river corridor studies. Dr Chembolu Vinay and Dr Benoit Camenen, the organizers, express their gratitude for the support from IFCPAR/CEFIPRA. They anticipate that the seminar will play a pivotal role in contributing to the global discourse on river corridor research.
