nodes.summary: Upasana Gupta for her recent publication in the International Journal of Biological Macromolecules (IF 8.02), titled "Bio-inspired Self-pumping Spiral Shaped Microfluidic Device for Cleaning of Urea Using Reduced Graphene Oxide (rGO) modified…

nodes.title: Ms. Upasana Gupta gets her article published in the International Journal of Biological Macromolecules
nodes.summary: Upasana Gupta for her recent publication in the International Journal of Biological Macromolecules (IF 8.02), titled "Bio-inspired Self-pumping Spiral Shaped Microfluidic Device for Cleaning of Urea Using Reduced Graphene Oxide (rGO) modified…
nodes.text:
Congratulations to Ms. Upasana Gupta for her recent publication in the International Journal of Biological Macromolecules (IF 8.02), titled "Bio-inspired Self-pumping Spiral Shaped Microfluidic Device for Cleaning of Urea Using Reduced Graphene Oxide (rGO) modified Polymeric Nanohybrid Membrane." The article discusses a self-pumping microfluidic device that utilizes a nanohybrid membrane modified with reduced graphene oxide (rGO) to efficiently clear urea biomolecules in a single pass.

Ms. Gupta is currently working as a research scholar under the guidance of Dr. Ravi Kumar Arun, Assistant Professor, Department of Chemical Engineering, Indian Institute of Technology Jammu.

IIT Jammu extends congratulations to the co-authors of this article, Mr. Natish Kumar and Mr. Akash Lata, for their valuable contributions.

About the journal The International Journal of Biological Macromolecules is an established international journal of research into chemical and biological aspects of all natural macromolecules . It presents the latest findings of studies on the molecular structure and properties of proteins, macromolecular carbohydrates, glycoproteins, proteoglycans, lignins, biological poly-acids, and nucleic acids.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: The innovative organic synthesis research work from the research group of Dr Pankaj Chauhan (ASCRG), IIT Jammu has recently been published in an esteemed journal of chemistry i.e. Angewandte Chemie International Edition (Impact Factor: 16.6).

nodes.title: IIT Jammu Researchers' Work Published in Prestigious Journal of Chemistry
nodes.summary: The innovative organic synthesis research work from the research group of Dr Pankaj Chauhan (ASCRG), IIT Jammu has recently been published in an esteemed journal of chemistry i.e. The research work discloses a solvent-dependent light-assisted…
nodes.text:
The innovative organic synthesis research work from the research group of Dr Pankaj Chauhan (ASCRG), IIT Jammu has recently been published in an esteemed journal of chemistry i.e. Angewandte Chemie International Edition (Impact Factor: 16.6). The experimental work was carried out by Mr Yaseen Hussain, PhD students of ASCRG.

The research work discloses a solvent-dependent light-assisted divergent reactivities of cyclic diazo imides and thiols. In dichloromethane, an indane-fused pyrrolidine is formed via triplet a carbene intermediate. By switching the solvent to acetonitrile, the aryl thiol acts as a reductant and leads to the reduction of the diazo functional group to a hydrazone. The mechanism of the developed reaction is supported with experiments (carried out at IIT Jammu) and DFT studies (carried out by Prof. Rene Koenigs’ group at RWTH Aachen University, Germany).

This achievement underscores IIT Jammu’s position as a hub of excellence in scientific research and its commitment to producing knowledge that transcends boundaries.

To learn more, please follow: https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1002/ange.202309184

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: His basic research interest lies in experimental and computational geomechanics with focus on foundation engineering, dam-foundation system on jointed rock, ground improvement, and soil dynamics. He is involved in various professional societies,…
nodes.text:
His basic research interest lies in experimental and computational geomechanics with focus on foundation engineering, dam-foundation system on jointed rock, ground improvement, and soil dynamics. Currently, his research group at IIT Jammu is working on applied geomechanics domain to better understand the transportation geotechnics in hilly regions, application of geosynthetic-based solution for rock-fall hazard, tunnel & underground space, and cold-weather geotechnics .

Dr. Halder publishes extensively in journals and conferences of international repute and has successfully secured multiple research projects from funding agencies such as SERB (presently ANRF), Ministry of Textiles (NTTM), and MoRTH. He has received several awards and recognitions from reputed research agencies. He is involved in various professional societies, including the Indian Geotechnical Society and the International Society of Soil Mechanics and Geotechnical Engineering. ​

nodes.title: Rohit Anand, an MTech student from the Civil Engineering Department (Batch 2023-2025) at IIT Jammu, has been awarded the Best MTech Research Award
nodes.summary: We are proud to share that Rohit Anand, an MTech student from the Civil Engineering Department (Batch 2023-2025) at IIT Jammu, has been awarded the Best MTech Research Award at the prestigious iGrip Conclave 2025, hosted by IIT Gandhinagar for the…
nodes.text:
We are proud to share that Rohit Anand, an MTech student from the Civil Engineering Department (Batch 2023-2025) at IIT Jammu, has been awarded the Best MTech Research Award at the prestigious iGrip Conclave 2025, hosted by IIT Gandhinagar for the Assessment of the impact of rockfall on geosynthetic-reinforced unpaved roads!

This conclave, focused on Geotechnical Research and Innovative Practices, brought together top minds from IITs, NITs, and premier institutions across India. Competing among some of the brightest researchers, Rohit’s outstanding research work earned him this well-deserved recognition.

Under the expert guidance of Assistant Professor Dr. Prasun Halder, Rohit showcased the spirit of academic excellence, innovation, and dedication that defines IIT Jammu.

A big thank you to IIT Gandhinagar for fostering a culture of research and innovation! We look forward to seeing more of our students excel on such national platforms.

Join us in congratulating Rohit on this incredible achievement!

nodes.title: Dr Mandal Secures ICSSR Project Grant
nodes.summary: In a significant academic achievement, Dr Sayantan Mandal, Assistant Professor in the Department of Humanities and Social Sciences at the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jammu, has been awarded an ICSSR [Indian Council of Social Science…
nodes.text:
In a significant academic achievement, Dr Sayantan Mandal, Assistant Professor in the Department of Humanities and Social Sciences at the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jammu, has been awarded an ICSSR [Indian Council of Social Science Research] Project Grant for the research project titled "Examining Teachers’ Competencies in Generative AI-Enabled Higher Education: An Empirical Study in Jammu & Kashmir and Ladakh UTs."

This pioneering research aims to bridge the knowledge gap surrounding teachers' competencies and perceptions in integrating Generative AI into higher education, particularly in the unique contexts of Jammu & Kashmir and Ladakh. Employing an innovative framework, the study delves into various dimensions of teacher competencies related to Generative AI, offering practical policy implications.

This endeavor is aligned with IIT Jammu's commitment to contributing to the development of Jammu & Kashmir and Ladakh through research-driven, evidence-based policy interventions. Dr Mandal's project promises to be a valuable step towards advancing knowledge and fostering positive educational changes in the region.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu Researchers' Breakthrough in Zinc Ion Batteries Published in Journal of Materials Chemistry A
nodes.summary: In a recent development, researchers from the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jammu have made significant strides in the field of energy storage with the publication of their research paper on zinc ion batteries in the esteemed Journal of…
nodes.text:
In a recent development, researchers from the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jammu have made significant strides in the field of energy storage with the publication of their research paper on zinc ion batteries in the esteemed Journal of Materials Chemistry A.

Led by the innovative efforts of Dr Rahul R Salunkhe's research group, the study sheds light on the advancements and potential of zinc ion batteries, a promising avenue for energy storage technologies.

The experiments, pivotal to the paper's findings, were conducted by PhD students Mr Prakash Kumar Pathak and Dr Nitish Kumar, a postdoctoral researcher at Chalmers Institute of Technology, Sweden, who played a crucial role in the success of the project.

The research paper provides valuable insights into the experimental methodologies and outcomes, offering a comprehensive view of the team's findings. To delve into the details of this groundbreaking research, interested readers can access the paper via the following link: https://doi.org/10.1039/D3TA05805K.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu Research Scholar Concludes Successful Research Visit to NSYSU Taiwan
nodes.summary: Mr Uday Raj Singh, a Research Scholar from the Department of Mechanical Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jammu, has recently completed a fruitful research expedition at the Hydrogen Energy and Fuel Cell Research Laboratory.…
nodes.text:
Mr Uday Raj Singh, a Research Scholar from the Department of Mechanical Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jammu, has recently completed a fruitful research expedition at the Hydrogen Energy and Fuel Cell Research Laboratory. The laboratory is situated within the Department of Mechanical and Electro-Mechanical Engineering at the prestigious National Sun Yat-sen University in Kaohsiung, Taiwan.

Mr Singh actively engaged in a collaborative endeavor titled "Renewable Energy Storage based on Reversible PEM Fuel Cell for Grid Applications." The project was spearheaded by the esteemed Dr Satya Sekhar and Prof Jenn-Kun Kuo from NSYSU, Taiwan. Noteworthy is the endorsement and support received for this joint research initiative from the Department of Science and Technology (DST) in India and the National Science and Technology Council (NSTC) in Taiwan.

This significant research collaboration marks another milestone in fostering international partnerships for advancements in renewable energy and fuel cell technologies.

nodes.title: STAEBM’23: Lalit Singh gets honored with 2nd Best Paper Presentation Award
nodes.summary: Mr Lalit Singh, a dedicated Research Scholar in the Department of Civil Engineering, under the esteemed guidance of Prof Anurag Misra, has been recognized for his outstanding contribution at the 3rd International Conference on Sustainable…
nodes.text:
Mr Lalit Singh, a dedicated Research Scholar in the Department of Civil Engineering, under the esteemed guidance of Prof Anurag Misra, has been recognized for his outstanding contribution at the 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Technologies and Environmental Building Materials (STAEBM’23). The event, held on May 13th-14th, 2023, at NIT Srinagar, witnessed the remarkable presentation by Mr Singh on "Development of low-carbon cementitious material using magnesium oxide and fly ash as partial replacement of Portland cement."

Mr Singh's research focuses on the crucial endeavor of developing sustainable and environment-friendly cementitious materials with reduced carbon footprint. His diligent efforts and innovative approach have earned him the 2nd Best Paper Presentation Award, acknowledging the significance and potential impact of his work.

The STAEBM’23 conference aimed to bring together academia, researchers, and students in order to exchange their experiences and research results about all aspects of specialized and interdisciplinary fields.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu's SAP Aerospace Wins Yukti Innovation Challenge Grant
nodes.summary: IIT Jammu's SAP Aerospace, a pioneering deep-tech startup focused on developing cutting-edge aero engines for Unmanned Combat Aerial Vehicles (UCAVs), founded and mentored by Dr Shanmugadas K. To learn more about the work of the SAP Aerospace group,…
nodes.text:
IIT Jammu's SAP Aerospace, a pioneering deep-tech startup focused on developing cutting-edge aero engines for Unmanned Combat Aerial Vehicles (UCAVs), founded and mentored by Dr Shanmugadas K. P., Mechanical Engineering Department, has received funding from the Innovation Cell of Ministry of Education, GoI, and the All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE) under the Yukti Innovation Challenge.

This MoE initiative aims to create a repository of ideas, innovations, and startups developed in academic institutions. It provides invaluable support, including mentorship, grant assistance, and connections with incubation units, investors, and knowledge agencies.

With this grant and support, the SAP Aerospace team- Ayush Divyansh and Preetam Jamod -is geared to strengthen indigenous UCAV engines through continued development and technology demonstration.

To learn more about the work of the SAP Aerospace group, visit www.sapaerospace.com/

nodes.title: We are delighted to share a significant development in the realm of advancement in Sodium-Ion Battery research
nodes.summary: We are delighted to share a significant development in the realm of advancement in Sodium-Ion Battery research. Salunkhe, Prakash Kumar Pathak, Kavya Prakash Agrim, and the entire research team for their commendable achievement!
nodes.text:
We are delighted to share a significant development in the realm of advancement in Sodium-Ion Battery research.

Under the supervision of Prof. Rahul R. Salunkhe, Associate Professor in the Department of Physics, IIT Jammu, PhD scholars Prakash Kumar Pathak and Kavya Prakash Agrim have made remarkable strides in augmenting the efficiency of sodium-ion batteries. A research paper on the topic "Enhancing the Performance of Prussian Blue Analogue Cathode for Sodium-Ion Batteries through Low Spin Fe Redox Adjustments" has been published in the prestigious journal of ACS Applied Energy Materials.

Heartfelt congratulations to Prof. Rahul R. Salunkhe, Prakash Kumar Pathak, Kavya Prakash Agrim, and the entire research team for their commendable achievement!

For more information kindly visit: https://pubs.acs.org/action/showCitFormats?doi=10.1021/acsaem.3c03262&ref=pdf

nodes.summary: The Science and Engineering Research Board (SERB), a Statutory Body Established Through an Act of Parliament : SERB Act 2008, has granted substantial support to Dr Karan Nathwani, Assistant Professor at IIT Jammu, for his research project titled…

nodes.title: SERB Extends Support to Dr Nathwani's Research Endeavor
nodes.summary: The Science and Engineering Research Board (SERB), a Statutory Body Established Through an Act of Parliament : SERB Act 2008, has granted substantial support to Dr Karan Nathwani, Assistant Professor at IIT Jammu, for his research project titled…
nodes.text:
The Science and Engineering Research Board (SERB), a Statutory Body Established Through an Act of Parliament : SERB Act 2008, has granted substantial support to Dr Karan Nathwani, Assistant Professor at IIT Jammu, for his research project titled "Applications of Convex Optimization Algorithms in Underwater Acoustics for Source Localizations."

The project aims to detect and pinpoint underwater targets in challenging conditions such as rain, wind, mammal noise, and ship noise. Dr Nathwani's objective is to achieve localization of contiguous targets with fewer sensors compared to state-of-the-art methods in non-Gaussian and unknown noise fields. The final phase involves testing the algorithms with real-time data to attain a Technology Readiness Level (TRL) of 4.

The outcomes of the project are anticipated to be valuable in advancing India's underwater defense technologies.

nodes.summary: Ambika Prasad Shah has been awarded the prestigious Young Faculty Research Fellowship (YFRF) by the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY), Government of India. Ambika Prasad Shah on this remarkable milestone!
nodes.text:
We are proud to announce that Dr. Ambika Prasad Shah has been awarded the prestigious Young Faculty Research Fellowship (YFRF) by the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY), Government of India.

His groundbreaking research on “Cryogenic SRAM-based In-Memory Computing for Deep Space Applications” has earned this recognition under the Digital India Corporation initiative. This fellowship provides a personal monthly fellowship and research contingency grants for up to five years, empowering innovation in deep-space technology.

This achievement highlights IIT Jammu’s commitment to cutting-edge research and technological advancements.

Join us in congratulating Dr. Ambika Prasad Shah on this remarkable milestone!

nodes.title: Dr Uday Raj Singh joined as a Postdoctoral Fellow at the Global Zero Emission Research Center (GZR)
nodes.summary: Dr Uday Raj Singh, a graduated research scholar under the guidance of Dr Satya Sekhar in the Mechanical Engineering Department, joined as a Postdoctoral Fellow at the Global Zero Emission Research Center (GZR), Department of Energy and Environment,…
nodes.text:
Dr Uday Raj Singh, a graduated research scholar under the guidance of Dr Satya Sekhar in the Mechanical Engineering Department, joined as a Postdoctoral Fellow at the Global Zero Emission Research Center (GZR), Department of Energy and Environment, AIST, Tsukuba Science City, Japan. Dr Uday Raj Singh also received the Prime Minister Research Fellowship for his doctoral research at IIT Jammu.

He will be involved in creating innovations to achieve global carbon neutrality as well as a reduction of the accumulated stock of CO2 in the earth’s atmosphere, called the “Beyond-Zero” initiative, by 2050. IIT Jammu takes immense pleasure in congratulating Dr Uday Raj Singh for taking a big step in his career and wishes him well in his future endeavours.

nodes.title: Dr. B. Satya Sekhar, Dept. of Mechanical Engineering, IIT Jammu, received a research fund
nodes.summary: of Mechanical Engineering, IIT Jammu, received a research fund of ₹ 170 L from the Indian Government's Department of Science & Technology. This is a 3-year joint research collaboration project (total budget ₹ 317 L) with IIT Jodhpur under the…
nodes.text:
Dr. B. Satya Sekhar, Dept. of Mechanical Engineering, IIT Jammu, received a research fund of ₹ 170 L from the Indian Government's Department of Science & Technology. This is a 3-year joint research collaboration project (total budget ₹ 317 L) with IIT Jodhpur under the Technology Mission Programme on Water and Clean Energy.

The objective of the proposed project is the "Development of a mini hybrid solar desalination and power plant," which will be a multipurpose plant aimed at producing clean power and potable water supply using the abundantly available solar energy to satisfy the needs of a cluster of households.

nodes.text: Out of the 50 sanctioned projects, Dr Kumar's project will evaluate the impact of the Smart City mission in the domain of wastewater monitoring and its reuse. His project aims to understand and assess the advancement so far in the field of sewage and industrial effluent treatment focusing on 4-5 major Indian cities including Surat, Indore, and Visakhapatnam with the help of the CEOs of the respective smart cities.

nodes.text:
Mr Deepak Sharma, a PhD scholar under Dr Pankaj Chahuhan's guidance, received acclaim for his poster at the Indo-French conference "FCSB-2024" at University of Hyderabad.

His research on "Relay Photoredox-or Electro-/Organocatalysis Enabled Asymmetric Synthesis of Dihydrodibenzo-oxazepine Derivatives" was hailed as the best by the Royal Society of Chemistry UK's "Organic & Biomolecular Chemistry" journal.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: Ankit Garg concluded the session by encouraging students to pursue research in China. Vinod Kushvaha (Assistant Professor, IIT Jammu, India) Research Area - ‘fundamentals of academic writing’
nodes.text:
The students were also briefed about the various research funding opportunities in China. The workshop lasted for two days. In the Q&A session, the students inquired about the various possibilities of research in the various fields and collaboration with different researchers at different universities in China.

Dr. Ankit Garg concluded the session by encouraging students to pursue research in China. It was followed by a discussion on MoU between Shantou University, China and IIT Jammu, India. The session was coordinated by PhD research scholars Insha Wani, Aanchna Sharma and Bhargav Reddy.

Scholars name - Insha Wani, Aanchna Sharma and Bhargav Reddy.

Supervisor Name - Dr. Vinod Kushvaha (Assistant Professor, IIT Jammu, India)

Research Area - ‘fundamentals of academic writing’

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: Scope: The Indian Institute of Technology, Jammu, will stage the KC75:International Conference on Innovative Materials Research (KC75:IC-IMR) in Jammu, India from 27th February to 1st March, 2025 to celebrate the 75th birthday of Professor Kamanio…
nodes.text:
Scope: The Indian Institute of Technology, Jammu, will stage the KC75:International Conference on Innovative Materials Research (KC75:IC-IMR) in Jammu, India from 27th February to 1st March, 2025 to celebrate the 75th birthday of Professor Kamanio Chattopadhyay.

The conference will showcase invited presentations by distinguished materials scientists and professionals worldwide to explore the most recent breakthroughs in materials processing, structural analysis, and computational methods and their pertinent uses in advanced alloys, energy materials, and environmental applications. The platform will also strive to stimulate discourse on the challenges associated with the implementation of innovative processing and corresponding microscopy methods for characterization. This conference will prioritise the promotion of scientific collaboration among members of the materials community.

For more details, please visit website

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: Our research focuses on membrane proteins, particularly G protein-coupled receptors (GPCRs). Research Interests G protein-coupled receptors (GPCRs) act as membrane gatekeepers and are the pleiotropic mediators of cellular signaling.

nodes.title: Mithu Baidya
nodes.summary: Our research focuses on membrane proteins, particularly G protein-coupled receptors (GPCRs). Research Interests G protein-coupled receptors (GPCRs) act as membrane gatekeepers and are the pleiotropic mediators of cellular signaling.
nodes.text:
Our research focuses on membrane proteins, particularly G protein-coupled receptors (GPCRs). Our primary goal is to unravel the molecular basis of GPCR function and leverage this knowledge to develop improved medicines for the future. Our research aims to enhance our understanding of membrane proteins and their role in cellular communication, paving the way for targeted and effective therapeutic interventions. Lab site: https://sites.google.com/view/receptorbiologylaboratory

The Receptor Biology laboratory at IIT Jammu focuses on understanding the molecular mechanisms of G protein-coupled receptors (GPCRs) in physiological and pathophysiological processes. GPCRs play a vital role as mediators of cellular signaling and are the largest receptor superfamily encoded by the human genome. Recognizing their involvement in numerous biological functions and their significance as drug targets, the lab aims to unravel the intricate workings of GPCRs at the molecular level to develop safer and more effective therapeutics.

To achieve this, the lab employs a range of research techniques, including biochemical, biophysical, chemogenetic, and pharmacological approaches. By utilizing multiple methods, we strive to comprehensively understand GPCR function and its molecular interactions and signaling pathways.

The lab's research themes focus on two key areas. Firstly, to investigate the molecular basis of interaction and modulation between GPCRs and pore-forming toxins. The insight into toxin influence on GPCRs can yield underappreciated signaling responses and immunomodulatory effects on our physiology. A holistic understanding of the dynamics of toxin-receptor interaction and signaling will open up new opportunities to develop novel therapeutic interventions.

Secondly, the lab is dedicated to unraveling the mechanisms behind signaling bias in GPCRs. GPCR biasing involves selectively activating or inhibiting specific signaling pathways downstream of a GPCR while sparing others. GPCR biasing holds great promise for developing next-generation therapeutics with enhanced efficacy and reduced side effects. Our continuing research effort in this direction is expected to uncover new insights into GPCR signaling and facilitate the design of novel drugs that can selectively modulate specific signaling pathways, leading to more precise and personalized treatments for various diseases.
 - PhD, Indian Institute of Technology Kharagpur, 2016

2016-04 - 2023-01: Postdoctoral Fellow, Indian Institute of Technology Kanpur

Research Areas Area: G protein-coupled receptor (GPCR) biology and Cell signaling Approaches: Biochemical, biophysical and chemogenetic based methods.


Research Interests G protein-coupled receptors (GPCRs) act as membrane gatekeepers and are the pleiotropic mediators of cellular signaling. nodes.title: Santhilal Subhash
nodes.summary: Key Focus Areas: - Reproductive Biology - Genomics & Single Cell Biology - Multi-omics - Bioinformatics - Computational Biology - Bigdata Analysis - Cancer Biology - Developmental Biology - Epigenetics - RNA Biology - AI/ML in Healthcare - Biomarker…
nodes.text:
Bioinformatics & Computational Biology (BCB) Laboratory focuses on improving female reproductive health by decoding regulatory elements in the genome using next-generation sequencing approaches. Key Focus Areas: Reproductive Biology Genomics & Single Cell Biology Multi-omics Bioinformatics Computational Biology Bigdata Analysis Cancer Biology Developmental Biology Epigenetics RNA Biology AI/ML in Healthcare Biomarker Discovery Visit lab website: https://www.subhashlab.in

Goal

Our lab at IIT Jammu focuses on understanding the complexities of female reproductive tissues, particularly in the context of gynecological diseases and cancers. This research emphasizes the significance of both coding and noncoding regulatory elements in the genome (REGULOME), which drive cellular heterogeneity and plasticity. By employing advanced Next-Generation Sequencing (NGS) techniques and computational models, we aim to map these regulatory elements to uncover mechanisms underlying disease progression and treatment resistance. The ultimate goal is to develop targeted therapies that address tissue heterogeneity and improve women's reproductive health.

Key Focus Areas:
 - Reproductive Biology
- Genomics & Single Cell Biology
- Multi-omics
- Bioinformatics
- Computational Biology
- Bigdata Analysis
- Cancer Biology
- Developmental Biology
- Epigenetics
- RNA Biology
- AI/ML in Healthcare
- Biomarker Discovery

Lab website: https://www.subhashlab.in/
 - 2014 - 2020 - Ph.D. in Medical Science (Bioinformatics & Computational Biology) | University of Gothenburg (Sweden).
- 2004 - 2008 - B.Tech. in Bioinformatics | SRM University (India).

Our goal is to integrate high-dimensional data from genomics, transcriptomics, and epigenomics to predict how regulatory elements—such as enhancers, promoters, and long noncoding RNAs—govern gene expression in specific tissue contexts. To accomplish this, our lab is focusing on exploring the following research areas.
 - Identifying tissue-specific regulatory elements in the genome using next-generation sequencing approaches.
- Using Single Cell Multiomic Techniques to Understand Complex Regulation of Female Reproductive Tissues.
- Regulome Trained Computational Models to Predict Female Reproductive Health Outcomes.
- More Information: https://www.subhashlab.in/Research-Areas

nodes.text:
Three-year grant for post-doctoral studies in medical science. This grant aims to provide younger researchers with the opportunity to achieve qualifications leading to the establishment of independent research.


Available Positions We're inviting applications to join our Bioinformatics Team! We’re looking for passionate individuals who are eager to translate genomics into clinical applications. This position offers extensive exposure in analyzing advanced multiomic datasets from cutting-edge genomic technologies, as well as opportunities to engage in research and development and collaborate with scientists across India and internationally. Minimum Qualifications/Requirements: Master’s (M.Sc./M.Tech. or equivalent) or Bachelor’s (B.Tech.) degree in Bioinformatics, Computational Sciences with an interest in Life Sciences research, Computational Biology, or Life Sciences. Candidates should be proficient in R, Python, or similar programming languages. Who are we looking for? Postdoctoral researchers willing to apply for any of the following fellowships such as DBT-BioCARe, SERB-National Post Doctoral Fellowship, or D. S. Kothari Post Doctoral Fellow and any others fellowships. We are happy to assist with preparing project proposals. Students interested in pursuing doctoral research in Bioinformatics and Computational Biology. Candidates must have valid GATE score, CSIR/UGC NET, DST-INSPIRE, or PMRF are encouraged to apply through the IIT Jammu Ph.D. program . Students who are interested in undergraduate/postgraduate internships or projects are welcome to contact. Location: IIT Jammu If interested, please contact us at: santhilal.subhash@iitjammu.ac.in

Start-Up: Xandrax pharma AB - Gothenburg, Sweden August 2022 - January 2024 - Deputy Member of the Board of Directors Provider of long noncoding RNA therapeutic strategies designed to treat cancer. The company offers treatment that helps to reduce the medical and social consequences of aggressive cancers, enabling healthcare providers to improve the lives of their patients.

Our research group focuses on nanotechnology applications in bioengineering, with an emphasis on developing innovative technologies at the nanoscale and exploring their biomedical applications. Key areas of research include: Technology Development at Nanoscale: Portable Diagnostics, Biosensors, Single molecule/Single Cell Techniques etc. Biomedical Applications of Nanostructures: Bioimaging, AMR, Cancer Therapy, Gene Therapy etc. With a strong interdisciplinary approach, our lab integrates biotechnology, nanotechnology, electronics, chemistry, and materials science to create healthcare solutions that are both accessible and affordable. Details can be accessed at https://medsenselab.com/

nodes.text:
Water quality testing lab houses many number of equipment like Atomic absorption spectrophotometer (AAS), Water Quality Analyzer, colorimeter, BOD Respirometric sensor system, COD Reactor and many more.

Gas Chromatography Mass Spectrometry (GC-MS)

Instrument charges updated

Instrument request form
 - Thermo Scientific gas chromatography (GC) equipped with single quadrupole mass spectrometry (QMS) systems enables the identification of volatile and semi-volatile compounds at trace levels. It is used to identify the organic compounds by their mass-to-charge ratio.
- GC-MS have wide applications in environmental analysis, food and beverage analysis, pharmaceutical analysis, and clinical analysis.

Gas Analyzer (Gas Chromatography)


 - A gas analyzer equipped with a pulsed discharge detector (GC-PDD) is used to analyze natural gases like hydrogen, oxygen, and nitrogen in their low-level concentrations in parts per million (ppm).
- A gas analyzer equipped with a flame ionization detector (GC-FID) is used to analyze methane, ethane, ethylene, and acetylene in their low-level concentrations in parts per million (ppm).
- The low level concentration of carbon monoxide (CO) and carbon dioxide (CO2) can be detected using methanizer.

Atomic absorption spectrophotometer (AAS)

Make : Shimadzu Model : AA-6880 Equipment Description : Atomic Absorption Spectroscopy is an instrumental analysis technique for rapid trace metal analysis. HVG assembly is also attached with the instrument for the detection of metals like mercury, arsenic and selenium. Besides the specific characteristics of AA-6880 system are as AA-6880 Series supports a wide range of analysis applications such as environment, chemistry, food etc. 8-lamp turret. Easy to use user-friendly software. Advances in optics and a new graphite furnace design achieve improved lower limits of detection for furnace analysis. Trace Element Analysis by using Micro Sampling Technology.

Fourier Transform Infra-red Spectrometer (FTIR)

Make : Thermo Scientfic Model : NICOLET iS50 FTIR Equipment Description : Chemical identification of liquids, and powders, Quality verification of incoming/outgoing materials, Microanalysis of small sections of materials to identify contaminants, Analysis of thin films and coatings, Monitoring of chemical adsorption/absorption properties of materials.

High-resolution Mass Spectrometer (HRMS)

Make : waters (India) Pvt Ltd Model : Xevo G2-XS QToF 4k Equipment Description : High-Resolution Mass Spectrometer (MS) is used to measure the masses within a sample as well as the purity of the unknown compound. It is one of the most frequently used instruments, used by Organic, and Inorganic chemistry, Biology, and material science. Room temperature measurement possible.

 - Possible to measure in the wavelength of 190-1100 nm

nodes.summary: Program Highlights: - Batch Start Date: February 2026 (tentative) - Duration: 24 Months - M.Tech by IIT Jammu - 60 academic credits post successful completion of the program - IIT Jammu Alumni Status post successful completion of the program - Visit…
nodes.text:
- AI
- Intelligent Systems
- Sensors

Introduction:

At the forefront of technological innovation, the need for advanced expertise in
 VLSI Design and Intelligent Systems is more pronounced than ever. With the tech
 industry's rapid evolution and integration of AI with electronic design, IIT
 Jammu introduces a ground-breaking program dedicated to moulding experts in
 these intersecting domains. This program is tailored for those aspiring to
 master the complexity of VLSI circuits and the ingenuity of intelligent systems
 — a dual proficiency that's becoming indispensable in sectors like
 telecommunications, smart electronics, and IoT.

The curriculum, designed in collaboration with industry leaders, combines
 theoretical rigor with practical hands-on labs, real-world problem-solving,
 preparing students to lead and innovate in a landscape where AI and VLSI design
 are rewriting the rules of technological capability. This program by IIT Jammu
 is more than an education; it's a direct conduit to becoming a tech
 visionary in the high-demand field of intelligent electronic systems.

Objectives:

IIT Jammu's two-year M.Tech in VLSI Design is a pioneering academic venture
 that marries the theoretical underpinnings of academia with the dynamic
 pragmatism of the semiconductor, sensors and AI industries. This advanced
 program is meticulously structured to navigate the intricate landscape of
 microelectronics and smart technologies, fortifying students with the skills
 needed to master complex integrated circuit design and intelligent system
 innovation.

Emphasizing a dual focus, the program encompasses a comprehensive VLSI curriculum
 that includes CMOS circuits, FPGA systems, and advanced electives like Edge AI
 Computing Chips. Simultaneously, it delves into the realm of Intelligent
 Systems, exploring the integration of AI with hardware design. The expanded
 two-year timeline allows for an in-depth engagement with core subjects, a range
 of electives, and a significant emphasis on practical application through
 extensive project work and industry internships.

Graduates of this program, empowered by IIT Jammu's commitment to educational
 excellence and interdisciplinary learning, will emerge as industry-ready experts
 equipped for leading-edge roles in the rapidly evolving domains of VLSI and
 intelligent system design.

Program Highlights:
 - Batch Start Date: February 2026 (tentative)
- Duration: 24 Months**
- M.Tech by IIT Jammu
- 60 academic credits post successful completion of the program
- IIT Jammu Alumni Status post successful completion of the program
- Visit IIT Jammu campus for 5-7 Days, every year for Immersion sessions and
 examinations. The first year of campus immersion is mandatory, while the
 second-year campus immersion is optional.
- Live online sessions by IIT Jammu faculty, and industry experts.
- 300+ Hours of Overall Learning Experience.
- Multiple hours of online lab exercises and project work.

Programme Structure / Modules

Course 1 Electric Vehicle Essentials 1: Industry Ecosystem & Battery Technology

Course 2 Electric Vehicle Essentials 2: Motors, Electrification, and Charging Systems

Course 3 Certification Course in MATLAB-SIMULINK, QSS & ADVISOR Toolbox for EV Design

Course 4 Certification Course in Advanced Driver Assistance Systems (ADAS) for EVs

Course 5 Certification Course in AUTOSAR for EVs: Standards & Software Architecture

Learning Outcomes

Analyse the electric vehicle industry ecosystem, regulatory landscape, and battery technologies used in electric vehicles

Understand and analyse electric motors, power electronics, electrification architecture, and EV charging systems

Perform EV system modelling and simulation using MATLAB-SIMULINK, QSS, and ADVISOR toolboxes

Understand ADAS architectures, sensor technologies, and functional safety concepts in electric vehicles

Understand AUTOSAR layered architecture, standards, and software abstraction for automotive systems

Eligibility Criteria

Bachelor’s degree in Engineering / Technology / Science

Diploma holders with a minimum of three years of relevant work experience are eligible

Relevant industry experience of 3–5 years is preferred

Prior experience in EV systems, modeling, ADAS, or automotive software is preferred

Governing Committee

A dedicated governing committee composed of IIT Jammu faculty and industry experts ensures that this program meets IIT Jammu’s academic standards and maintains real-world industry relevance.

Sales & Marketing

DIYguru has been onboarded as an official partner for online sales, marketing, learner engagement, and digital delivery.

While I3C-IIT Jammu and IIT Jammu provide academic leadership and certification, DIYguru manages admissions, program operations, and support ensuring a seamless learning experience for every learner.

Admission Process

Candidates may connect with the DIYguru admission team for guidance on the programme, eligibility, and admission process

Candidates must apply online by submitting the application form through the DIYguru platform

All applications will undergo document and eligibility verification by the DIYguru admissions team in coordination with I3C (Industry Interaction and Innovation Cell) – Indian Institute of Technology Jammu

Eligible applicants may be required to appear for an online entrance assessment

Based on eligibility and assessment results, shortlisted candidates will receive an Offer Letter issued by I3C–IIT Jammu

Upon acceptance of the offer, candidates must complete the fee payment as per the approved structure

Enrollment will be formally confirmed by I3C–IIT Jammu after verification of payment

The final Admission Letter will be issued by I3C–IIT Jammu, confirmin

nodes.title: IIT Jammu: I-DAPT-HUB-Foundation (IITBHU) Grants Coveted Support to Dr Nathwani’s Pioneering Underwater Surveillance Research
nodes.summary: 10.45 lacs to Dr Karan Nathwani, Assistant Professor at the Department of Electrical Engineering, Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jammu. The grant provided by I-DAPT-HUB-Foundation underscores the foundation's commitment to advancing…
nodes.text:
The Chairman of the Board of Directors at I-DAPT-HUB-Foundation, Indian Institute of Technology (BHU) Varanasi, has announced the allocation of a grant totaling Rs. 10.45 lacs to Dr Karan Nathwani, Assistant Professor at the Department of Electrical Engineering, Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jammu. This grant has been extended to support Dr Nathwani's groundbreaking research project titled, "Underwater Surveillance of Contiguous Objects Through Drones via Differential Subspace Re-weighted Modeling."

Recognizing the critical importance of this research endeavor to the Defense sector, with specific relevance to underwater acoustics, including the Indian Navy, this grant will enable Dr Karan Nathwani and his team to achieve the following key objectives:

Precise Target Localization in Noisy Underwater Environments: Dr Nathwani's research aims to develop innovative techniques for localizing targets with unparalleled precision in challenging and noisy underwater environments. Drones equipped with advanced technology will be utilized for tracking and surveillance purposes, offering invaluable support to defense and security operations.

Correction of Direction of Arrival (DOA) Estimates: The movement of drones, particularly hydrophone arrays, within underwater settings can introduce complexities in DOA estimation. Dr Nathwani's project will address these challenges by implementing state-of-the-art methodologies to correct DOA estimates, enhancing the effectiveness of surveillance efforts.

Localization in Non-Gaussian or Unknown Noise Fields: One of the distinctive features of underwater environments is the presence of diverse and often unpredictable noise sources, such as mammal noise, shrimp noise, shipping noise, and ambient noise. Dr Nathwani's research will develop algorithms capable of localizing targets even in Non-Gaussian noise fields, ensuring robust surveillance capabilities in the face of complex acoustic conditions.

The grant provided by I-DAPT-HUB-Foundation underscores the foundation's commitment to advancing cutting-edge research in the field of underwater surveillance, with a focus on strengthening national security efforts. Dr Karan Nathwani's pioneering work at IIT Jammu promises to contribute significantly to the advancement of technology in underwater acoustics and will have far-reaching implications for defense and security applications.

 medical services and continuity of care. Medanta Hospital, Gurugram: World-class medical attention for highly specialized
 treatments. Renowned Hospitals in Jalandhar: Broadened scope of medical services for
 efficient treatment plans. These partnerships ensure comprehensive and reliable care, offering
 timely and effective medical attention. We coordinate closely with our
 partner hospitals to manage appointments, facilitate emergency transfers,
 and keep families updated, guaranteeing a smooth and stress-free experience. With our robust network of collaborations, you are in safe and

nodes.title: Dr Kolleboyina and Team Gears Up for Scientific Collaboration with Japan
nodes.summary: In a significant development, Dr Jayaramulu Kolleboyina's application to the prestigious Japan Science and Technology - Sakura Science Program (JST-SSP) under the esteemed "Japan-Asia Youth Exchange Program in Science" has received approval. Guided…
nodes.text:
In a significant development, Dr Jayaramulu Kolleboyina's application to the prestigious Japan Science and Technology - Sakura Science Program (JST-SSP) under the esteemed "Japan-Asia Youth Exchange Program in Science" has received approval. This collaboration, in partnership with Prof Hemanth Noothalapati from the Faculty of Life and Environmental Sciences at Shimane University, marks a momentous achievement for our team at IIT Jammu.

As Asia witnesses a period of remarkable progress, the promotion of science and technology stands as the driving force for realising a brighter future for the continent. Guided by this vision, the "Japan-Asia Youth Exchange Program in Science" seeks to foster closer collaboration between industry, academia, and government. It does so by facilitating short-term visits by talented Asian youths to Japan, fostering exchanges that enhance their understanding of leading Japanese science and technologies. These exchanges occur at Japanese universities, research institutions, and private companies.

The central focus of this visit by Dr Kolleboyina is to engage in cutting-edge collaborative research activities at Shimane University, with a specific emphasis on 2D materials for biological applications. This research includes pioneering spectroscopic studies with the potential to push the boundaries of scientific knowledge.

The institute envisions that this exchange will not only facilitate the sharing of invaluable knowledge but also lay the groundwork for enduring partnerships between IIT Jammu and prestigious Japanese universities. This collaborative effort is set to pave the way for innovative discoveries and advancements in the field of science.

nodes.title: Tinkerer's Lab
nodes.summary: This lab will be located in the institute's academics block, to be open 24 7 for the students and faculty members. Tinkerer’s lab will be a significant part of this centre.
nodes.text:
Enter with an Idea leave with a product

Mission and Vision

IIT Jammu Tinkering Lab's mission is to create workspaces where young minds can learn innovation skills, sculpt their ideas through hands-on activities, social and cross-cultural collaboration, and ethical leadership. Creative thinkers would get a place to transform their imagination into reality. Students will learn to build their models and enhance their product development skills, and thus the ability to make products indigenously would enhance and thus promote Atma Nirbhar Bharat and build innovative solutions to India’s unique problems. All in all, we aim to create an Ecosystem that nurtures Futuristic skills like complex problem solving, creative thinking, Entrepreneurial skills, adaptive thinking, and Computational skills.

Tools and Resources

Currently, 1800 sq. ft. of the area is available. This lab will be located in the institute's academics block, to be open 24*7 for the students and faculty members. The lab is located at an optimum distance of 170m from student hostels to ensure that all interested students can reach the facility within 5-10 minutes from their place of stay. Also, the lab will be in close proximity of classrooms and faculty offices which will enable the students to seek any help they require while completing a project and will enhance the teaching experience of faculty members who aim to conduct design and innovation courses or any other task as a part of the tinkering lab. Also, IIT Jammu is developing a new facility within the next 18 months and around 8000 -10000 ft 2 is earmarked for Institute Innovation & Entrepreneurship Development Center. Tinkerer’s lab will be a significant part of this centre.

nodes.summary: In a notable announcement, Dr Karan Nathwani's submission, titled "Hardware Prototype for drone detection via Exploitation of Wavelet Scattering and other Acoustic Features with 1D-Convolutional Neural Network," has been chosen for presentation at…

nodes.title: Dr Nathwani's Cutting-Edge Drone Detection Prototype Selected for IInvenTiv-2024 Showcase
nodes.summary: In a notable announcement, Dr Karan Nathwani's submission, titled "Hardware Prototype for drone detection via Exploitation of Wavelet Scattering and other Acoustic Features with 1D-Convolutional Neural Network," has been chosen for presentation at…
nodes.text:
In a notable announcement, Dr Karan Nathwani's submission, titled "Hardware Prototype for drone detection via Exploitation of Wavelet Scattering and other Acoustic Features with 1D-Convolutional Neural Network," has been chosen for presentation at IInvenTiv-2024 following a comprehensive review by the Technical Evaluation Committee (TEC).

The pioneering nature of Dr Nathwani's innovation left a strong impression on the TEC, and they are enthusiastic about featuring his work on the IInvenTiv-2024 platform scheduled for January 19-20, 2024, at the Convention Centre, IIT Hyderabad.

Key aspects of the proposed concept include:
 - Introduction of a hybrid network for drone detection, seamlessly integrating Wavelet Scattering Transform (WST) and 1D-CNN.
- UAV detection is achieved by inputting the network with unprocessed raw audio waveform, eliminating the need for extracted acoustic features like MFCC, mel spectrum, etc.
- Acoustic-based detection is advantageous, as it operates without requiring a line of sight and remains unaffected by weather conditions or clutter at low altitudes.
- Additionally, the implementation cost is lower compared to vision-based or alternative techniques.

The proposed UAV detection technology was demonstrated to Hon’ble J&K LG Shri Manoj Sinha during the Northtech Army Symposium held at IIT Jammu last year.

nodes.title: About us
nodes.summary: The SAPTARSHI lab is a conglomeration of seven labs, namely; Microscopy Lab, Spectroscopy Lab, X-ray Based Characterization Lab, optics and chromatography lab, Nanofabrication and Physical Property Characterization lab (NPPCL), Thermal Analysis Lab…

nodes.title: About us
nodes.summary: The SAPTARSHI lab is a conglomeration of seven labs, namely; Microscopy Lab, Spectroscopy Lab, X-ray Based Characterization Lab, optics and chromatography lab, Nanofabrication and Physical Property Characterization lab (NPPCL), Thermal Analysis Lab…
nodes.text:
Central Instrumentation Facility (CIF) named SAPTARSHI at IIT Jammu houses a wide array of high-end instruments for pushing the boundaries of research in science and technology to a higher level. The SAPTARSHI lab is a conglomeration of seven labs, namely; Microscopy Lab, Spectroscopy Lab, X-ray Based Characterization Lab, optics and chromatography lab, Nanofabrication and Physical Property Characterization lab (NPPCL), Thermal Analysis Lab (TAL), and Water Quality Testing Lab (WQTL). This is the heart of the institution, which helps the faculties, research scholars, and students to carry out globally competitive research in basic, applied, and medical sciences and publish their research findings in peer-reviewed high-impact factor journals for a sustainable society.

The center also hopes to expand the facilities each year, making it a core facility in the country. By realizing CIF (SAPTARSHI), we expect a prominent hub for pioneering and collaborative analytical research in our country. This facility is extensively used by internal doctoral students and faculty members and extended to external organizations, academic institutions as well as the industry in the country on a chargeable basis. This facility is also used for organizing short-term courses/workshops on the use and application of various instruments and analytical techniques. The CIF (SAPTARSHI) is expected to self-sustain by revenue generation for the upkeep and maintenance of the instruments.

View CIF Brochure

nodes.title: Inventory Management
nodes.summary: Inventory management is an important aspect of managing educational institutions like IITs. Recording all non-consumable equipment/items in the inventory database using E governance provides an efficient way to track inventory levels and ensure that…

nodes.title: Inventory Management
nodes.summary: Inventory management is an important aspect of managing educational institutions like IITs. Recording all non-consumable equipment/items in the inventory database using E governance provides an efficient way to track inventory levels and ensure that…
nodes.text:
Inventory management is an important aspect of managing educational institutions like IITs. IIT Jammu has a wide range of inventory items, including laboratory equipment, furniture, computers and peripherals, books, and other educational materials.

The main function of Inventory section is to accurately track inventory levels and keeping accurate records of the goods in stock. Recording all non-consumable equipment/items in the inventory database using E governance provides an efficient way to track inventory levels and ensure that all inventory items are properly cataloged and labeled. Having an inventory database with information on each asset's owner can also help in identifying who is responsible for maintaining the asset and ensuring its proper usage.

To remove old and non-functional items from the inventory, Institute follows the write-off procedure as per Govt. of India guidelines which ensures the inventory is up to date and the institution does not have to allocate resources to maintain or repair non-functional items.

In addition to this, there is a separate form for consumable items (C-form) and non-consumable items (NC form) to ensure proper classification of Assets/Inventory, and employees can view their Assets/Inventory in the ERP system which provides transparency and accountability.

nodes.text: The Central Instrumentation Facility (Saptarshi Labs) of IIT Jammu at Paloura campus is well equipped with highly sophisticated state of the art instruments, in order to enable researchers to peep into the unexplored domains of basic sciences and engineering. The facility includes microscopy lab, spectroscopy lab, X-ray characterization lab, thermal analysis lab, Optics and chromatography lab, nano fabrication and physical property characterization lab and water quality testing lab. The high end facilities are extensively used by UG/PG Students, Research Scholars and Faculty for Advanced Research. This facility is also extended to External Users like industries, National labs, Research Organizations and academic institutions of the region on chargeable basis. The CIF also organizes short term courses, workshops and Faculty Development Programs (FDP) on the application of the instruments for scientific and technological research.

nodes.title: Dr Kolleboyina and Team Explores Scientific Collaborations in Japan Under Sakura Science Exchange Program
nodes.summary: In a recent update, Dr Jayaramulu Kolleboyina, Assistant Professor in the Department of Chemistry, led a group of students to Shimane University in Japan from December 10th to 24th, 2023, as part of the Sakura Science Exchange Program. Dr…
nodes.text:
In a recent update, Dr Jayaramulu Kolleboyina, Assistant Professor in the Department of Chemistry, led a group of students to Shimane University in Japan from December 10th to 24th, 2023, as part of the Sakura Science Exchange Program.

Dr Kolleboyina expresses gratitude to the program and Prof Hemanth Noothalapati for facilitating meaningful scientific discussions at Shimane University. Highlighting their collaborative work on 2D materials for biological applications using Raman spectroscopy at the Faculty of Life and Environmental Science, he emphasizes the value of the experience.

This exchange program not only fosters current research collaborations but also lays the groundwork for future endeavors, including student exchanges, solidifying the bond between IIT Jammu and Shimane University.

nodes.text:
IIT Alumni Centre's permanent facility in Bengaluru with office and meeting rooms is NOW OPEN.

The facility has off campus offices of every IIT including IIT JAMMU which got inaugurated on 9th September 2023.

The facility is at Bommasandra Industrial Area, across from Anthem BioSciences, Bommasandra Village, Attibele Hobli Anekal Taluk, Bengaluru.

The facility is an important landmark for IITACB and the IIT community in Bengaluru. .

We now have a platform and location from where we can plan programs, collaboration with IIT's and industry and provide a common meeting and networking location for IITian's.

The centre has multifaceted facilities including an auditorium, seminar rooms, meeting rooms, conferencing area, informal event organisation possibilities, a 180 seat incubator and mane more.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: Advances in Diagnostics" We are delighted to share the successful conclusion of our two-day workshop, "Micro-fabrication and Biosensors: Advances in Diagnostics," held from January 18th to 19th, 2025, at the Department of Chemical Engineering, IIT…
nodes.text:
Advances in
 Diagnostics"

We are delighted to share the successful conclusion of our two-day workshop, "Micro-fabrication and
 Biosensors: Advances in Diagnostics," held from January 18th to 19th,
 2025, at the Department of
 Chemical Engineering, IIT Jammu. The event brought together an exceptional panel of speakers
 from renowned
 institutions, including IIT-KGP, IIT BHU, GMC Jammu, AIIMS Jammu, Max Healthcare,
 CSIR-IIIM, CSIR-CMERI,
 CSIR-NPL, MNNIT Allahabad, and KIET Delhi.

Our esteemed speakers captivated the audience with insightful sessions on cutting-edge topics
 like MEMS
 devices, clinical biosensors, AI in healthcare, innovative materials, and advanced
 diagnostics.

We extend our heartfelt gratitude to our Convener, Dr. Ravi Kumar Arun , and
 Co-convenor, Dr.
 Vinay Sharma , for their visionary leadership. A special thanks to our
 sponsors— @JKSTIC,
 @BharatScientific, @BiotechnicalResource,@Batsolequipments, and
 @IIT-JAMMU —for their generous
 support, which made this event possible.

Participants had the unique opportunity to engage in hands-on lab sessions and explore
 the Paloura campus,
 Receptor Biology Lab, Microfluidic Design and Bioengineering Lab, Advanced Manufacturing
 Lab, Thermal and
 Natural Phenomenon Lab, Microfluidics and Energy Systems Lab , and the CIF facility of
 IIT Jammu. These experiences provided invaluable practical insights into biosensor
 integration and advanced
 diagnostic technologies. We sincerely thank everyone who contributed to the success of this
 workshop. Together, we
 are fostering innovation, collaboration, and excellence in the field of diagnostics.

Stay connected for more exciting initiatives and opportunities! Prof. Pranjal Chandra, Asha Chaubey, Pawan Gupta, Sandeep Dogra, Shashank Kr. Singh,
 Surinder P. Singh,
 Pravesh Singh, Shivani Gandhi, Girijesh Kumar Patel, Vinay Patel, Soumen Mandal,
 Dharitri Rath, Vijay Kumar Pal,
 Shiva S, Mithu Baidya, Navneet Kumar

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: With an intent to reimagine the language learning process, a mobile (non-static) Experiential Language Learning Lab has been newly developed at HSS, IIT Jammu. This Experiential Language Lab, constituted in 2025, is a consolidation of various…

nodes.title: Language Experiential Lab
nodes.summary: With an intent to reimagine the language learning process, a mobile (non-static) Experiential Language Learning Lab has been newly developed at HSS, IIT Jammu. This Experiential Language Lab, constituted in 2025, is a consolidation of various…
nodes.text:
With an intent to reimagine the language learning process, a mobile (non-static)
 Experiential Language Learning Lab has been newly developed at HSS, IIT Jammu. This
 Experiential Language Lab, constituted in 2025, is a consolidation of various
 methods and modes of Language instruction and training, supplemented by dedicated
 language learning software systems. The Lab then, is primarily a system of learning
 that is highly adaptable to different teaching spaces and classrooms, without being
 fettered to contingent infrastructural conditions.

At HSS IIT Jammu, we also have a dedicated space– a Multipurpose Hall to refine
 the academic English and interpersonal Communication skills of the students. The
 English language proficiency course offered by the HSS for the new cohort of BTech
 students would enable them to excel in all the dimensions for developing the
 essential skills i.e. LSRW with a student-centered approach.

Apart from the dedicated software systems, we guide students in their learning
 journey through Open-Access digital platforms and applications, operated through
 spaces with projecting facilities and AV equipment, as well as outdoor spaces, to
 encourage creative productions and disseminations, recitations and performances by
 the students, within a moderated participatory environment.

The Lab caters to the level based needs of the learners by providing them with an
 immersive experience, with AI tools and software and a variety of interactive
 activities led by the Instructors.

The Hall is a forum for the confluence of numerous ideas and thoughts with Debate,
 Film and Reading Clubs catering to the spirited target group and indulging in
 stimulating discussions.

The Language Experiential Lab is being implemented along with classroom engagement
 features and dedicated AI enhanced software to impart self paced learning modules.
 The software consisting of levels mapped with CEFR guidelines issues certifications
 to the students that they can link with their professional achievements.

The vision of this lab ensures that students actively participate and hone their soft
 skills. The language proficiency course offered here is a dedicated credit course to
 set the foundation for their future career paths.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: Here we shall mainly focus on eigenvalues of distance matrix associated with distance regular graphs and in particular minimal cages. Distance regular graphs were introduced by Biggs, in the year 1974, while studying so called distance transitive…
nodes.text:
Title : Some spectral results of distance-regular graphs and minimal cages

Abstract : From a given graph G, several matrices are constructed by researchers to study different properties within graph theory as well as in other areas. Computation of eigenvalues and eigenvectors of these matrices comes under the area of spectral graph theory. Here we shall mainly focus on eigenvalues of distance matrix associated with distance regular graphs and in particular minimal cages. Distance regular graphs were introduced by Biggs, in the year 1974, while studying so called distance transitive graphs. Random walks on certain distance regular graphs correspond to important models for the diffusion of particles. The Ehrenfests’ urn model that was proposed to explain the second law of thermodynamics corresponds to random walks on the hypercube, which is an example of distance regular graphs. For positive integers k and g, a minimal (k, g)-cage graph is a k-regular graph with girth g and minimum possible number of vertices. We obtain a quotient matrix Q(G)(which is of smaller order) of distance matrix D(G) of a distance regular graph G so that all the eigenvalues of Q(G) are eigenvalues of D(G). We determine a polynomial p(x) for minimal cages so that p(λ) is an eigenvalue of distance matrix of a minimal cage, where λ is an adjacency eigenvalue of the same minimal cage. Finally, we discuss some open problems associated with the above results.

nodes.summary: The Office of Alumni Affairs is dedicated to strengthening and enriching the relationship between IIT Jammu and its alumni. We plan to fix an Alumni Day as a way of celebrating the achievements of all alumni in various fields of life, and drawing…
nodes.text: The Office of Alumni Affairs is dedicated to strengthening and enriching the relationship between IIT Jammu and its alumni. This relationship will be a foundation for organizing various outreach activities, such as Alumni Reunion , Campaign for New Resources to Create Scholarship for Students , Creating Faculty Chairs , and a Distinguished Lecture Series , amongst others. We plan to fix an Alumni Day as a way of celebrating the achievements of all alumni in various fields of life, and drawing from these achievements inspiration for furthering the progress of IIT Jammu in innovative directions.

nodes.title: Message
nodes.summary: As the Assistant Registrar for Finance & Accounts at IIT Jammu, I would like to welcome all our users warmly. My team and I are here to assist you with any accounts-related queries, including billing, fee payments, financial aid, and scholarships.
nodes.text:
As the Assistant Registrar for Finance & Accounts at IIT Jammu, I would like to welcome all our users warmly.

My team and I are here to assist you with any accounts-related queries, including billing, fee payments, financial aid, and scholarships. We understand that managing finances can be challenging, and we are committed to ensuring that our students, faculty, staff, and strategic partners (vendors) have a seamless experience when it comes to managing their accounts.

We are available during regular working hours, and you can contact us via email at accounts@iitjammu.ac.in. Our team will respond to your queries immediately and provide you with the necessary support.

Please feel free to reach out to us if you have any concerns or queries, and we will be happy to help you.

nodes.summary: We are proud to announce that Poorvi Joshi, 4th Year EE, IIT Jammu, working on Luneburg Metasurface Millimeter Wave Lens got best B.Tech Student initiative award in MAPCON 2022 , held in Bangalore from 13 to 15 th December 2022. Rajlakshmi Menon…
nodes.text:
We are proud to announce that Poorvi Joshi, 4th Year EE, IIT Jammu, working on Luneburg Metasurface Millimeter Wave Lens got best B.Tech Student initiative award in MAPCON 2022 , held in Bangalore from 13 to 15 th December 2022.

The conference was attended by Prof. Stefano MACI (Professor at the University of Siena), Dr. Rajlakshmi Menon (Director, CABS/DRDO), Prof. Yashwant Gupta (Center Director, National Center of Radio Astronomy), and various experts in the field of RF and Microwave.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: - FAQs - What is the Central Workshop at IIT Jammu? - Who can access the Central Workshop at IIT Jammu?

nodes.title: Frequently Asked Questions
nodes.summary: - FAQs - What is the Central Workshop at IIT Jammu? - Who can access the Central Workshop at IIT Jammu?
nodes.text:
- FAQs
 - What is the Central Workshop at IIT Jammu? The Central Workshop at IIT Jammu is a specialized facility equipped with tools, machinery, and resources for hands-on learning, research, and project work for students and faculty.
- Who can access the Central Workshop at IIT Jammu? Access to the Central Workshop is typically available to registered students, faculty, and staff of IIT Jammu (through SARAL portal). Charge basis services are also provided to the internal as well as external users. Specific access policies may apply.
- How external user can use the Central Workshop facilities? External user may contact in-person or through e-mail given in the contact information of CWS.
- How can I gain access to the Central Workshop at IIT Jammu? To access the Central Workshop, students usually need to take permission from FIC CWS or from AWO and adhere to specific guidelines.
- What safety measures are in place at the Central Workshop at IIT Jammu? Safety is a top priority. The Central Workshop has safety measures in place, including safety training, protective gear requirements (e.g., goggles, gloves, safety shoes), and strict safety protocols for equipment use.
- Is there a fee for using the Central Workshop facilities at IIT Jammu? The fee structure for using Central Workshop facilities may vary. Some services may be offered free of charge as part of the educational experience, while others may have associated fees. Refer to the workshop's guidelines/policy for details.
- Are the Central Workshop facilities at IIT Jammu open during holidays and weekends? Workshop hours may vary, but generally, they are open during weekdays. Limited weekend hours may also be available upon the request from students. Refer to the workshop's schedule for specific timings.
- What types of projects can I work on in the Central Workshop at IIT Jammu? You can work on a wide range of projects, including academic assignments, research projects, and personal endeavours. The workshop is designed to support various engineering and fabrication needs.
- Can I reserve specific equipment or workspace in advance at the Central Workshop? Some equipment and workspaces in the Central Workshop may be available for reservation.
- Are there restrictions on the materials I can use in the Central Workshop at IIT Jammu? Certain materials may have restrictions due to safety or equipment compatibility concerns. Always consult with the Central Workshop staff for guidelines on materials usage.
- What should I do if I accidentally damage equipment or tools in the Central Workshop? If you damage any equipment, it's crucial to report it immediately to the Central Workshop staff. They will provide guidance on the necessary steps to address the issue.
- Can I collaborate with other students or researchers in the Central Workshop? Collaboration is encouraged. You can collaborate on group projects or seek assistance from Central Workshop staff and fellow students.

nodes.summary: MESH is now present in 4 institutes and many schools of Jammu region. Along with this he was able to manage a decent CGPA of 8.3, and won third prize for IIT Jammu in inter IIT Tech Fest.
nodes.text:
A renowned honour given by IIT Jammu

The "Best Initiative Award" recognises exceptional efforts and innovations made by individuals within the IIT Jammu community. This honour demonstrates the university's dedication to fostering social responsibility, leadership, and creativity in its faculty, staff, and students.

The "Best Initiative Award" recognizes a initiative taken by a student that is novel, impact the community in and around IIT Jammu and is proven to be sustainable

We proudly announce that this year Lakshya Devani, B.Tech Electrical Engineering Batch 2019-2023, won the Best New Initiative Award 2023 on 21st October 2023 because of his contribution in MESH. MESH - MESH of Engineering Science and Humanities is a forum which promotes Interdisciplinary Thinking. Interdisciplinary Thinking is an attempt to re-think the boundaries between engineering, medicine, basic sciences, humanities and in general all aspects of human knowledge. For the last two years Lakshya Devani was the convenor of MESH which was successfully able to organise 50+ sessions including fests like Udyamitav and National level completions and also hosted flagship events like management of EBSB Yuva Sangam. MESH is now present in 4 institutes and many schools of Jammu region. Along with this he was able to manage a decent CGPA of 8.3, and won third prize for IIT Jammu in inter IIT Tech Fest.

Best wishes from Alumni Affairs Council

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: Celebrating Research Excellence at IIT Jammu! Humara Iqbal (2023RCE2014), a fellow research scholar, and Mr.

nodes.title: Ms. Masoom Mairaj has been awarded the Best Poster Award at the prestigious International Conference on GLS-2025
nodes.summary: Masoom Mairaj (2024RCE1013), a research scholar from the Department of Civil Engineering, has been awarded the Best Poster Award at the prestigious International Conference on “Ground Improvement, Landfills and Sustainability (GLS-2025)”, held at…
nodes.text:
Celebrating Research Excellence at IIT Jammu!

We are proud to share that Ms. Masoom Mairaj (2024RCE1013), a research scholar from the Department of Civil Engineering, has been awarded the Best Poster Award at the prestigious International Conference on “Ground Improvement, Landfills and Sustainability (GLS-2025)”, held at the Indian Institute of Science (IISc), Bangalore on 18–19 July 2025.

Her award-winning poster, titled “Comparative Study on the Reinforcement of Sandy Soils Using Polypropylene and Natural Root Fibers”, is the result of a collaborative research effort with Ms. Humara Iqbal (2023RCE2014), a fellow research scholar, and Mr. Kalyanam Lokesh Kumar (2021UCE0060), a B.Tech. student from the 2021–2025 batch.

Selected among the top five posters out of 45 presentations across four technical sessions, their work was lauded for its innovative approach to sustainable soil reinforcement, technical rigor, and clear, impactful presentation.

This recognition reflects IIT Jammu’s growing impact in interdisciplinary and sustainability-driven research, and we congratulate the team and their mentors, Dr. Riya Bhowmik and Dr. Prasun Halder, for this outstanding achievement!

nodes.title: Global Recognition for IIT Jammu – Best Paper Award at VDAT 2025
nodes.summary: Notably, IC-ResQ Lab has received this prestigious Best Paper Award for two consecutive years 2024 and 2025 – a testament to the consistent excellence of our research community. Heartiest congratulations to the authors for this remarkable…
nodes.text:
We are proud to announce that the research paper titled “Low-Power NBTI Resilient 12T SRAM Cell with Enhanced Reliability”, authored by Vipul Sahu, Aryan Kannaujiya, Pranjal Verma, and Ambika Prasad Shah – all associated with the IC-ResQ Lab (Integrated Circuit Reliability, Security and Quality Laboratory) at IIT Jammu – has won the Best Paper Award at the 29th

International Symposium on VLSI Design and Test (VDAT 2025), held at Chandigarh, India.

The prestigious event was jointly organized by IIT Ropar, IIT Mandi, Semi-Conductor Laboratory

(SCL) – an autonomous body under the Ministry of Electronics & Information Technology

(MeitY), Government of India – Chitkara University, Panjab University, and Punjab Engineering College.

This award holds special significance as papers from across India and abroad were evaluated, and among all, this innovative work from IIT Jammu was selected as the Best Paper.

Notably, IC-ResQ Lab has received this prestigious Best Paper Award for two consecutive years 2024 and 2025 – a testament to the consistent excellence of our research community.

Heartiest congratulations to the authors for this remarkable achievement, bringing global recognition and pride to IIT Jammu!

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: Alumni with faculty members came together to celebrate the beginning of a new chapter in their lives. The evening was a perfect blend of nostalgia, fun, and inspiration, making it a memorable start to their post-graduation journey
nodes.text:
The campus was buzzing with energy and excitement as the Alumni Affairs Office hosted a grand welcome meetup for the new alumni batch during the convocation weekend. The evening was filled with a vibrant and pulsating music, setting the perfect backdrop for a memorable gathering. Alumni with faculty members came together to celebrate the beginning of a new chapter in their lives.

Fun activities were abundant, from photo capturing candid moments to alumni sharing their fond memories and experiences. The atmosphere was electric as alumni engaged in friendly banter and rekindled old friendships while forging new ones.

Short and exciting challenges were also organized, adding a thrilling element to the evening.

The event not only provided a chance for the new alumni to feel a sense of belonging but also offered them an opportunity to connect with their faculty and friends, who shared invaluable insights and advice for their post-graduation journey.

The new alumni batch left the event with a strong sense of camaraderie and anticipation for the exciting adventures that awaited them in the professional world. The evening was a perfect blend of nostalgia, fun, and inspiration, making it a memorable start to their post-graduation journey

The SC/ST Students Cell addresses academic and non-academic issues and complaints received from students in reserved categories.

The institute strives to maintain an environment where all communities can participate in academic and research activities without any sort of discrimination with respect to caste or creed. In order to maintain such an atmosphere, the SC/ST students cell is responsible for sensitizing the campus community about the importance of having diversity.

nodes.title: Prestigious 2024 IEEE AP-S Mojgan Daneshmand Award to Research Scholar “Suchitra Tiwari”
nodes.summary: Prestigious 2024 IEEE AP-S Mojgan Daneshmand Award to Research Scholar “Suchitra Tiwari” for her research titled “3D-Printed Dielectric Bifunctional Metasurface Antenna for Millimeter-Wave 5G Communication”. Suchitra Tiwari pursuing her Ph.D.
nodes.text:
Prestigious 2024 IEEE AP-S Mojgan Daneshmand Award to Research Scholar “Suchitra Tiwari” for her research titled “3D-Printed Dielectric Bifunctional Metasurface Antenna for Millimeter-Wave 5G Communication”.

Suchitra Tiwari pursuing her Ph.D. at the Department of Electrical Engineering, Indian Institute of Technology Jammu, India under the supervision of Dr. Amit Kumar Singh (Assistant Professor, IIT Patna) and Dr. Ankit Dubey (Associate Professor, IIT Jammu) received prestigious 2024 IEEE AP-S Mojgan Daneshmand Award for her research work to present at 2024 IEEE International Symposium on Antennas and Propagation and ITNC-USNC-URSI Radio Science Meeting, which was held at Fortezza da Basso Convention Center, Florence, Italy from July 14 to 19, 2024. This award is given in the memory of Late Prof. Mojgan Daneshmand, whose research contributions serves as a testament to the hard work and dedication in the field and this year 12 women researchers worldwide were recognized for their research contribution in the field of Antenna and Wave Propagation through this prestigious award.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: At the Central Workshop-IIT JAMMU, we envision ourselves as the heart of practical knowledge and academic excellence. We encourage local industries to explore the possibilities of collaboration with us.

nodes.title: Message
nodes.summary: At the Central Workshop-IIT JAMMU, we envision ourselves as the heart of practical knowledge and academic excellence. We encourage local industries to explore the possibilities of collaboration with us.
nodes.text:
At the Central Workshop-IIT JAMMU, we envision ourselves as
 the heart of practical knowledge and academic excellence. We are committed to providing
 students with a dynamic and immersive learning experience that transcends traditional
 education. Our goal is to nurture not only their theoretical understanding but also
 their hands-on skills, making them well-rounded professionals prepared to conquer the
 challenges of the real world. Our mission is to equip them with the knowledge and
 practical skills that are essential for their success both in academia and in the
 industry. Collaboration with local industries is at the core of our vision. We recognize
 the tremendous value that can be derived from such partnerships. Central Workshop is not
 just a place for learning; it's a strategic partner in your quest for innovation and
 growth. We encourage local industries to explore the possibilities of collaboration with
 us. Together, we can drive innovation, stimulate economic growth, and cultivate the
 talent of the future. Together, we can create a brighter future.
