nodes.title: Sneha Choudhury has received the Best Paper Award at the 18th International Conference on Communication Systems and Networks (COMSNETS 2026)
nodes.summary: We are proud to share that Sneha Choudhury, M.Tech student from the Department of Electrical Engineering, has received the Best Paper Award at the 18th International Conference on Communication Systems and Networks (COMSNETS 2026) held in Bangalore.…
nodes.text:
We are proud to share that Sneha Choudhury, M.Tech student from the Department of Electrical Engineering, has received the Best Paper Award at the 18th International Conference on Communication Systems and Networks (COMSNETS 2026) held in Bangalore.

Her research, titled “Nonlinear Energy Harvesting Based Relaying for Efficient Multi-Rate SWIPT in Energy-Constrained Networks”, was carried out under the guidance of Dr. Atul Banotra and Dr. Sudhakar Modem. Sneha was felicitated by eminent academics including Prof. Parimal Parag (IISc Bengaluru) and Prof. Abhiav Kumar (IIT Hyderabad).

COMSNETS 2026 brought together leading global experts from institutions and organizations such as Google, KTH Royal Institute of Technology, RIT, University of Tokyo, Delft University of Technology, Samsung R&D India, and HPE India, highlighting the conference’s international stature.

This recognition underscores IIT Jammu’s commitment to fostering world-class research, innovation, and academic excellence.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: IIT Jammu is proud to host the 12th Aditya-L1 Support Cell (AL1SC) Workshop from December 22–24, 2025, at its Jagti (Main) Campus. Know more: https://al1ssc.aries.res.in/workshop-12

nodes.title: Twelfth Aditya-L1 Support Cell (AL1SC) Workshop at IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: IIT Jammu is proud to host the 12th Aditya-L1 Support Cell (AL1SC) Workshop from December 22–24, 2025, at its Jagti (Main) Campus. Know more: https://al1ssc.aries.res.in/workshop-12
nodes.text:
IIT Jammu is proud to host the 12th Aditya-L1 Support Cell (AL1SC) Workshop from December 22–24, 2025, at its Jagti (Main) Campus. The workshop, jointly organized by ISRO and ARIES, focuses on advancing research and capacity building in solar physics and heliophysics.

India’s Aditya-L1 mission, launched in January 2024, marks the nation’s first dedicated mission to study the Sun. Positioned in a halo orbit around the Lagrange Point 1 (L1), the mission aims to deepen our understanding of solar dynamics and their influence on space weather.
 - The upcoming workshop will feature:
- Expert lectures by scientists from ISRO and ARIES
- Hands-on project sessions on SUIT payload data
- Interactive discussions and presentations on solar observations

Designed for students and early-career researchers, this three-day workshop will offer participants an opportunity to gain practical insights into Aditya-L1 data analysis and contribute to India’s growing community of solar researchers.

Know more: https://al1ssc.aries.res.in/workshop-12

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: Ambika Prasad Shah at the IC-ResQ Lab, IIT Jammu, have received the Best Paper Award at the prestigious 28th International Symposium on VLSI Design and Test (VDAT-2024) held at VIT Vellore. Satya Gupta, President, VLSI Society of India, and Chitra…

nodes.title: Best Paper award to Aryan Kannujiya and Vipul Sahu at VDAT-2024
nodes.summary: Congratulations to Aryan Kannujiya and Vipul Sahu! Ambika Prasad Shah at the IC-ResQ Lab, IIT Jammu, have received the Best Paper Award at the prestigious 28th International Symposium on VLSI Design and Test (VDAT-2024) held at VIT Vellore.
nodes.text:
Congratulations to Aryan Kannujiya and Vipul Sahu!

We are thrilled to announce that Aryan Kannujiya, PhD scholar and Vipul Sahu, BTech student working under the guidance of Dr. Ambika Prasad Shah at the IC-ResQ Lab, IIT Jammu, have received the Best Paper Award at the prestigious 28th International Symposium on VLSI Design and Test (VDAT-2024) held at VIT Vellore.

The award was presented by Dr. Satya Gupta, President, VLSI Society of India, and Chitra Hariharan, Secretary, VLSI Society of India (VSI). This recognition is particularly remarkable considering the conference saw a total of 327 submissions from leading institutions of many IITs and NITs.

Their paper, titled "High-Performance Stacked Dynamic Comparator for Analog to Digital Converters," stood out for its excellence in VLSI design. This achievement not only highlights their outstanding research but also uplifts the name and prestige of the IC-ResQ Lab and IIT Jammu on a global stage.

Awardee website: https://vit.ac.in/vdat2024/awardees.html

nodes.title: International Conference on Sustainable Materials and Infrastructure
nodes.summary: Date: 16ᵗʰ -18ᵗʰ December 2025 The International Conference on Sustainable Materials and Infrastructure serves as a platform for researchers, industry professionals, and practitioners to drive progress in sustainable construction. Aligned with the…
nodes.text:
Date: 16ᵗʰ -18ᵗʰ December 2025

The International Conference on Sustainable Materials and Infrastructure serves as a platform for researchers, industry professionals, and practitioners to drive progress in sustainable construction. This event offers an engaging forum to explore cutting-edge advancements in eco-friendly materials, energy-efficient technologies, and innovative design approaches. Participants can exchange knowledge, present groundbreaking solutions, and build collaborations to accelerate the adoption of sustainable practices in the construction industry.

Aligned with the principles of the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) , the conference addresses critical global challenges, including climate resilience, resource efficiency, sustainable urban development, and access to clean energy. By fostering dialogue and cooperation, this event aims to advance the creation of a sustainable and inclusive built environment that balances environmental, social, and economic priorities

nodes.title: IIT Jammu proudly congratulates Ms. Dharishti Thappa, M.Tech scholar on her remarkable achievement at the MAPCON 2025
nodes.summary: IIT Jammu proudly congratulates Ms. Dharishti Thappa, M.Tech scholar from the Department of Electrical Engineering, on her remarkable achievement at the IEEE Microwaves, Antennas and Propagation Conference (MAPCON) 2025, held at the Lulu…
nodes.text:
IIT Jammu proudly congratulates Ms. Dharishti Thappa, M.Tech scholar from the Department of Electrical Engineering, on her remarkable achievement at the IEEE Microwaves, Antennas and Propagation Conference (MAPCON) 2025, held at the Lulu International Convention Center, Grand Hyatt, Bolgatty, Kochi, Kerala.

Under the guidance of Dr. Archana Rajput (IIT Jammu), Dharishti secured Second Place in the highly prestigious Three Minute Thesis (3MT) Competition—a globally recognized academic platform originally developed by the University of Queensland. Her impactful three-minute presentation titled “Small Sheet, Strong Signal” impressed both the jury and the audience through its clarity, innovation, and scientific depth.

The award was conferred in the distinguished presence of eminent representatives from DRDO, ISRO, and leading universities from across the world, highlighting the global stature of the competition.

nodes.title: Jal Shakti Abhiyan: IIT Jammu students present posters on water conservation
nodes.summary: IIT Jammu’s third-year Civil Engineering (B.Tech) students designed and presented nine posters on “Water Conservation.” The exhibition was open to the entire campus community to learn about the rich and various water conservation methods practiced…
nodes.text:
IIT Jammu’s third-year Civil Engineering (B.Tech) students designed and presented nine posters on “Water Conservation.” The exhibition was open to the entire campus community to learn about the rich and various water conservation methods practiced across the country.

This poster presentation competition was organized under the Ministry of Jal Shakti’s nationwide campaign “Jal Shakti Abhiyan: Catch the Rain,” which focuses on saving and conserving rainwater. For the pre-monsoon and monsoon periods of 2023 (4th Mar. to 30th Nov. 2023), the theme “Source sustainability for drinking water” has been delineated, covering both urban and rural areas of all the districts in the country.

Among the nine posters, the poster on the topic “Ground Water Harvesting Practices in Different Parts of India” won the first prize. Second and third prizes were awarded to posters “Interlinking of Rivers” and “Water Conservation Strategies in Urban Areas,” respectively.

nodes.title: IIT Jammu
nodes.summary: Ajaz Mir on winning the prestigious Buti Young Scientist Award 2022 at the 37th National Symposium on Plasma Science & Technology. The award seminar was held at IIT Jodhpur during 12-14 December 2022.

nodes.title: PhD student Ajaz Mir won the Buti Young Scientist Award
nodes.summary: Ajaz Mir on winning the prestigious Buti Young Scientist Award 2022 at the 37th National Symposium on Plasma Science & Technology. Bimla Buti, a renowned Indian plasma physicist, confers this award to young researchers in the field of plasma physics…
nodes.text:
Congratulations to Mr. Ajaz Mir on winning the prestigious Buti Young Scientist Award 2022 at the 37th National Symposium on Plasma Science & Technology. The award seminar was held at IIT Jodhpur during 12-14 December 2022. The award included a citation and a cash prize of Rs. 10,000/-.

The Buti foundation, established by Prof. Bimla Buti, a renowned Indian plasma physicist, confers this award to young researchers in the field of plasma physics for significant research contributions.

Mr. Mir received the award for his theoretical research on " Synchronization of Dust Acoustic Waves in a forced Korteweg-de Vries-Burgers model. " He has done this work under the supervision of Dr. Sanat Kumar Tiwari in collaboration with Prof. Abhijit Sen from the Institute for Plasma Research India, Prof. John Goree from the University of Iowa USA, and Dr. Chris Crabtree and Dr. Gurudas Ganguli from Naval Research Laboratory USA.

nodes.title: Ms Nisa Secures Best Paper Award at IIChE-CHEMCON 2023 for Energy Transition Research
nodes.summary: In a notable achievement, Ms Saimatun Nisa, under the mentorship of Dr Gaurav Ashok Bhaduri, clinched the Best Paper Award in the "Energy Transition: Challenges and Opportunities" category at IIChE-CHEMCON 2023. The International Conference was held…
nodes.text:
In a notable achievement, Ms Saimatun Nisa, under the mentorship of Dr Gaurav Ashok Bhaduri, clinched the Best Paper Award in the "Energy Transition: Challenges and Opportunities" category at IIChE-CHEMCON 2023.

The International Conference was held at Heritage Institute of Technology, Kolkata during 27-30 December 2023, and was organized by Indian Institute of Chemical Engineers and co-sponsored by American Institute of Chemical Engineers.

Ms Nisa's accolade stems from her presentation on "Investigation of water retention in sandy soil using chemically treated and untreated walnut shell char." This noteworthy research was conducted at the Process Intensification and Nanoscale Advanced Materials Laboratory, overseen by Dr Bhaduri, situated at IIT Jammu.

nodes.title: Best paper award- FMFP conference
nodes.summary: BVSSS Prasad Memorial best paper award amongst the papers presented in the 9th International Conference on Fluid Mechanics and Fluid Power- FMFP 2022 . The conference was held at IIT Roorkee on 14-16 December 2022 .
nodes.text:
Mr. Vivek Sahu's paper has been selected for Prof. BVSSS Prasad Memorial best paper award amongst the papers presented in the 9th International Conference on Fluid Mechanics and Fluid Power- FMFP 2022 . The conference was held at IIT Roorkee on 14-16 December 2022 . The award included a citation and a cash prize of Rs. 10,000.

The award was constituted by the National Society of Fluid Mechanics and Fluid Power in the memory of Late Prof. BVSSS Prasad who had made great contributions to Fluid Mechanics and turbomachinery research. Vivek's work focuses on the development of advanced lean-burn fuel injection systems for gas turbine engines.

He has done the present work under the supervision of Dr. Shanmugadas K. P. in collaboration with NCCRD, IIT Madras

nodes.title: Mr. Lomas Rishi was awarded the Best Paper Presentation Award
nodes.summary: Lomas Rishi was awarded the Best Paper Presentation Award in the Catalysis & Reaction Engineering (CRE) theme at the 75th Chemical Engineering Congress, CHEMCON 2022 . The conference was organized by Indian Institute of Chemical Engineers and…
nodes.text:
Mr. Lomas Rishi was awarded the Best Paper Presentation Award in the Catalysis & Reaction Engineering (CRE) theme at the 75th Chemical Engineering Congress, CHEMCON 2022 . This International Conference was held at Harcourt Butler Technical University Kanpur during 27-30 December 2022. The conference was organized by Indian Institute of Chemical Engineers and co-sponsored by American Institute of Chemical Engineers.

Mr. Lomas Rishi received the award for presenting his research work on "DFT study of Fe based bimetallic catalysts for thermocatalytic decomposition of methane." He has done this work under the supervision of Dr. Aditya Sandupatla Shankar in collaboration with Dr. José G. Rivera de la Cruz, Total Energies, France .

nodes.summary: IIT Jammu congratulates Abhishek Panwar, Department of Materials Engineering, for winning the Best Poster Award at the 89th Annual Session of the Indian Ceramic Society and International Conference on Research Advancements and Industrial Challenges…
nodes.text:
IIT Jammu congratulates Abhishek Panwar, Department of Materials Engineering, for winning the Best Poster Award at the 89th Annual Session of the Indian Ceramic Society and International Conference on Research Advancements and Industrial Challenges in Glass and Ceramics (RAICGC 2025) held from 27–29 November 2025 at IIT Bombay.

Working under the guidance of Dr. Sunil Kumar Kashyap, Abhishek presented his research through a poster presentation and emerged as one of the standout contributors at this prestigious international conference.

IIT Jammu applauds his dedication and scholarly excellence, and we wish him continued success in his research journey.

There shall be trained experts who shall be taking the workshop.

The workshop will cover: Crafting a Standout Presence & CV : Experts will delve into creating compelling resumes that make employers take notice, helping each individual stand out from the crowd.

Interview Prep : This module will cover how to present effectively through body language and communication skills, answer tricky questions with poise, and leave a lasting impression on potential employers.

nodes.title: Ms. Aparna C, Ph.D. has received the Best Presentation Award
nodes.summary: Divyesh Varade, has received the Best Presentation Award at the International Conference on Mapping the Future of Sustainable Landscapes: Geospatial Innovations for People, Places and Practices, organized by IUST, Kashmir, and ISPRS ICWG V/IV. Her…
nodes.text:
We are proud to share that Ms. Aparna C, Ph.D. scholar from the Department of Civil Engineering, IIT Jammu, under the supervision of Dr. Divyesh Varade, has received the Best Presentation Award at the International Conference on Mapping the Future of Sustainable Landscapes: Geospatial Innovations for People, Places and Practices, organized by IUST, Kashmir, and ISPRS ICWG V/IV.

Her research, titled “Retrieval of Surface Soil Moisture Over Bare Soil from Sentinel-1 SAR Data Using the TU-Wien Change Detection Approach,” reflects IIT Jammu’s commitment to advancing sustainable and data-driven research in geospatial sciences.

Congratulations on this remarkable achievement!

nodes.title: Vanisha Sodhi Clinches Poster Prize at IIT Kharagpur's International Conference
nodes.summary: Ms Vanisha Sodhi, a PhD student, under the guidance of Dr Pankaj Chauhan, has been honored with the Poster Prize at the International Conference on ‘Emerging Trends in Catalysis & Synthesis 2024’ hosted at the Indian Institute of Technology…
nodes.text:
This recognition was bestowed by “Organic & Biomolecular Chemistry,” a distinguished journal of the Royal Society of Chemistry UK.

Ms Vanisha Sodhi, a PhD student, under the guidance of Dr Pankaj Chauhan, has been honored with the Poster Prize at the International Conference on ‘Emerging Trends in Catalysis & Synthesis 2024’ hosted at the Indian Institute of Technology Kharagpur, India from March 7th to 9th, 2024.

Vanisha's presentation delved into the topic of "Organocatalyzed Enantioselective 1,6-Addition Reaction: A Key Step In The Desymmetrization of Coumarin Tethered 2,5-Cyclohexadienones Involving A Domino Sequence."

nodes.summary: Ritujoy Biswas supervised by Dr. Karan Nathwani from the Department of Electrical Engineering will be representing the institute at INTERSPEECH CONFERENCE 2021 at Brno, Czech Republic.

nodes.title: Research Papers presented at Interspeech Conference
nodes.summary: Ritujoy Biswas supervised by Dr. Karan Nathwani from the Department of Electrical Engineering will be representing the institute at INTERSPEECH CONFERENCE 2021 at Brno, Czech Republic.
nodes.text:
Mr. Murtiza Ali and Mr. Ritujoy Biswas supervised by Dr. Karan Nathwani from the Department of Electrical Engineering will be representing the institute at INTERSPEECH CONFERENCE 2021 at Brno, Czech Republic. About 4000 papers from around the world were submitted with an acceptance rate of less than 50%. The scholars made great efforts to achieve this feat.

Their paper titles are as follows:

1. Group Delay-based Re-weighted Sparse Recovery Algorithms for Robust and High-Resolution Source Separation in DOA Framework by Murtiza Ali. 2. Transfer Learning for Speech Intelligibility Improvement in Noisy Environments by Ritujoy Biswas.

nodes.text: **Sheetal Ranaut** , recently participated in the prestigious international conference "Current Trends in Theoretical Chemistry (CTTC-2024)" held at Bhaba Atomic Research Center, Mumbai, from 26th September - 28th September, 2024. She presented her work on "Pd-Catalyzed Oxypalladation-Initiated Domino Reactions:Why does Solvent Polarity Control the Regioselectivity?"She has won the best poster award sponsored by American Chemical Society (ACS). In the picture, Sheetal is receiving her award from Prof. Avesh K. Tyagi, Director of the Chemistry Group at Bhaba Atomic Research Center.

nodes.title: FCASI-2023: Ms. Namrata Kotwal won the Best Poster Award.
nodes.summary: Pankaj Chauhan, won the Best Poster Award at the international conference on Frontiers at the Chemistry-Allied Sciences Interface (FCASI-2023). Kotwal presented her research work entitled "Organocatalytic Asymmetric Synthesis of…
nodes.text:
Ms. Namrata Kotwal, a Ph.D. student in the Department of Chemistry supervised by Dr. Pankaj Chauhan, won the Best Poster Award at the international conference on Frontiers at the Chemistry-Allied Sciences Interface (FCASI-2023). The American Chemical Society has sponsored this recognition.

The conference was held on the 20th-21st of April 2023 at the University of Rajasthan, Jaipur, where Ms. Kotwal presented her research work entitled "Organocatalytic Asymmetric Synthesis of Dibenzocyclohepatanes Bearing Multiple Elements of Chirality."

nodes.title: Ms. Saandra Sharma has been awarded the Best Poster Award in the field of CO₂ Reduction to Fuels
nodes.summary: Saandra Sharma from the Hybrid Porous Materials Lab, Department of Chemistry, has been awarded the Best Poster Award in the field of CO₂ Reduction to Fuels at the 35th CRSI National Symposium in Chemistry held at IIT Gandhinagar. Jayaramulu…
nodes.text:
Proud moment for IIT Jammu!

Ms. Saandra Sharma from the Hybrid Porous Materials Lab, Department of Chemistry, has been awarded the Best Poster Award in the field of CO₂ Reduction to Fuels at the 35th CRSI National Symposium in Chemistry held at IIT Gandhinagar.

Her groundbreaking research, guided by Prof. Jayaramulu Kolleboyina, was recognized and awarded by Prof. Uday Maitra, President of CRSI, marking a significant step forward in sustainable chemical research.

nodes.text:
Congratulations to Miss Vanisha Sodhi from the research group of Dr. Pankaj Chauhan for winning the Best Poster Award at the Fostering Chemical Science (FCS) Conference 2025, University of Delhi.

This marks her third Best Poster Prize at international conferences in the past two years, recognizing her outstanding work in stereoselective desymmetrization reactions.

IIT Jammu is proud of her remarkable achievement and research excellence!

nodes.summary: Jammu, [01.03.2024] — The Center for Essential Skills and Zuban-e-Kalam, Humanities and Social Sciences (HSS), IIT Jammu, successfully organized an enlightening workshop cum interaction session with the distinguished Padma Shri awardee, Shri Balwant…

nodes.title: IIT Jammu Hosts Workshop on Performing Arts
nodes.summary: Jammu, [01.03.2024] — The Center for Essential Skills and Zuban-e-Kalam, Humanities and Social Sciences (HSS), IIT Jammu, successfully organized an enlightening workshop cum interaction session with the distinguished Padma Shri awardee, Shri Balwant…
nodes.text:
Jammu, [01.03.2024] — The Center for Essential Skills and Zuban-e-Kalam, Humanities
 and Social Sciences (HSS), IIT Jammu, successfully organized an enlightening workshop
 cum interaction session with the distinguished Padma Shri awardee, Shri Balwant Thakur.
 The event, aimed at emphasizing the significance of performing arts in education,
 witnessed an enthusiastic participation from students and faculty alike.

Dr. Quleen Kaur Bijral opened the session with a warm welcome address, highlighting the
 dynamic delivery of performing arts and its indispensable role in transforming students
 into eloquent speakers and impactful individuals. Dr. Amitash played a key role in the
 event, felicitating Shri Balwant Thakur with a memento, expressing gratitude for his
 invaluable contributions to the field of performing arts.

Shri Balwant Thakur, an esteemed figure in the realm of performing arts, shared his
 insights on how performance art is pivotal in shaping students into holistic leaders of
 tomorrow. His discourse illuminated the path for students, guiding them to harness the
 power of arts in personal and professional development.

The session also featured a workshop-based activity designed to test the listening and
 speaking abilities of the students, facilitating a hands-on learning experience.
 Highlighting the talents within IIT Jammu, Vipul, a B.Tech student, captivated the
 audience with a mesmerizing poetry recitation, showcasing the artistic_ prowess of the
 institute's students.

The event was seamlessly coordinated by Kuril and Vipul, ensuring an engaging and
 interactive session for all attendees. The workshop concluded on a high note with a high
 tea, fostering an environment of camaraderie and intellectual exchange among
 participants.

The Center for Essential Skills and Zuban-e-Kalam, HSS, IIT Jammu, remains committed to
 organizing such enriching sessions, underlining the importance of arts in education and
 its role in nurturing well-rounded individuals capable of leading with empathy,
 creativity, and effectiveness

 from Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel's Ideology" on July 25-26, 2025.

This collaborative venture with the ICSSR, convened by Dr. Sanchita Srivastava, provided
 an invaluable platform for engaging discussions on the vital themes of empowerment and
 social justice.

The seminar kicked off with a dynamic inauguration, featuring a distinguished lineup of
 guests. Ms. Indu Kanwal Chib, JKAS, Chairperson of the J&K SSB, honored the event as
 the Guest of Honor, while Prof. Pragati Kumar, Vice Chancellor of SMVDU, served as the
 Chief Guest. Their insights, along with contributions from Prof. Manoj Singh Gaur,
 Director of IIT Jammu, and Prof. Bijoy Boruah, Dean at IIT Jammu, enriched the
 discussions, fostering a constructive atmosphere for meaningful dialogue on the
 seminar's core themes.

This collaborative effort not only highlighted the importance of women's empowerment
 and social justice but also drew inspiration from Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel's
 enduring ideology, making it a truly impactful event.

nodes.title: Introductory Workshop on Systematic Reviews
nodes.summary: Date: Wednesday, January 21, 2026 The Department of Humanities & Social Sciences at IIT Jammu successfully hosted a Two-Day Introductory Workshop on Systematic Reviews, as part of the MANTHAN series (Chapter 11) , on January 21–22, 2026 , at the…
nodes.text:
Date: Wednesday, January 21, 2026

The Department of Humanities & Social Sciences at IIT
 Jammu successfully hosted a Two-Day Introductory Workshop on Systematic Reviews, as part of the MANTHAN series (Chapter 11) , on January
 21–22, 2026 , at the Jagti Campus.

Led by Dr. Prachi Pundir from The George Institute for
 Global Health, New Delhi, the workshop offered participants a comprehensive
 journey through the systematic review process.

Day 1 focused on foundational concepts, helping attendees
 distinguish between systematic, narrative, and other review types, formulate precise
 review questions, and develop protocols with hands-on database search exercises.

Day 2 emphasized the analytical and interpretative stages,
 including screening, data management, risk of bias assessment, narrative synthesis, and
 meta-analysis, reinforced through interactive group activities and practical Excel
 exercises.

The workshop concluded with discussions on project management,
 ethical considerations, team coordination, and next steps, equipping participants with
 actionable insights to conduct high-quality systematic reviews and strengthen
 evidence-based research skills.

nodes.title: Film Appreciation Workshop
nodes.summary: HSS Conducts Film Appreciation Workshop (21st-23rd January) HSS IIT Jammu organized a three-day Film Appreciation workshop from 21st to 23rd January 2020. Date: Tuesday, January 21, 2020
nodes.text:
HSS Conducts Film Appreciation Workshop (21st-23rd January)

HSS IIT Jammu organized a three-day Film Appreciation workshop from 21st to 23rd
 January 2020. Padma Shri and Padma Bhushan awardee regional film director Mr Jahnu
 Barua and Mrs Gayatri Barua were the experts on the occasion. Around 50 students from
 and outside the institute participated in the workshop. During the workshop, multiple
 films directed by Jahnu Barua were screened in the auditorium. Each screening was
 followed by the question and answer session. Film enthusiasts from the institute and
 other colleges asked Mr and Mrs Barua pertinent questions about their craft and other
 secrets of the trade.

Dr. Quleen Kaur Bijral (H.O.D. HSS) addressed the significance of the workshop as it
 inculcates the aesthetic as well as didactic expressions of films that continue to
 disseminate the system of values, ethics, morals, and critique of the society through
 ages. Pertinent extrapolations were also offered by the present HSS faculty: Dr. Amitash
 Ojha, Dr. Suchismita Roy, Dr. Bhumika R, Dr. Joby, Dr. Sayantan Mandal, Dr. Sukanya
 Mondal, and Ms. Ishita Mahajan.

Date: Tuesday, January 21, 2020

nodes.title: BSBE Students Shine at XIV ISLS Annual Meet
nodes.summary: Ms Khushboo Warjurkar and Mr Rajan Patyal, both mentored by Dr Vinay Sharma, secure the Best Oral Presentation Award at The International Society of Life Sciences (ISLS) Annual Meeting for their outstanding research. Ms Warjurkar presented Tradescant
nodes.text:
Ms Khushboo Warjurkar and Mr Rajan Patyal, both mentored by Dr Vinay Sharma, secure the Best Oral Presentation Award at The International Society of Life Sciences (ISLS) Annual Meeting for their outstanding research.

Ms Warjurkar presented Tradescantia pallida-derived Cu-doped carbon dots as Fe3+ sensors, while Mr Patyal showcased his cutting-edge research on NIR-active carbon dots: A dual-sensor approach for selective Cu2+ and Hg2+ detection.
